name: Acappology
description: Acappology is the premiere student run, mixed voice a cappella group at NCSU. We pride ourselves on our unique arranging style and we generally cover a broad array of musical styles. We are continually seeking strong new voices. Throughout the year we develop new music, compete in the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella [Yes, it's like pitch perfect ;) ], record for streaming services, and perform throughout the community.
summary: Acappology is the premiere student run, all gender a cappella group at NCSU. We are continually seeking strong new voices. Throughout the year we develop new music, record, and perform throughout the community.

name: Accounting Society
description: The Accounting Society is an organization designed for all undergraduate accounting students. Accounting Society creates a structured network of students, their professors and professionals. This network allows students to maximize their education at NC State. Members face many opportunities to practice communication and networking skills that are vital in today's professional environment. Additionally, members are offered advice and guidance relevant to the interviewing process and business etiquette. Participating in the NCSU Accounting Society truly is rewarding. In addition to our monthly meetings, Accounting Society also performs several service projects throughout the year. There are several social events and fundraisers as well. Professionals are invited to take part in nearly every event held by the organization. Annual dues are only $15.00. It includes a one year membership, food provided at the meetings, social events, and a t-shirt. All accounting majors are encouraged to attend.
summary: Edit your profile to add a summary!

name: Aerial Robotics Club at NC State
description:
We're a multidisciplinary engineering club that competes in the student unmanned aerial systems (SUAS) competition each summer. We build an autonomous fixed-wing drone we call Kavik. There are four subteams that work together to build our plane: airframe, drop, electrical, and software. This is a very hands-on club where you can gain experience in designing aircraft, manufacturing, coding, and electrical systems. 

https://aerialrc.mae.ncsu.edu/
summary: We build planes that fly themselves! If you are interested in joining either drop by our lab in room 2003 EBIII right off of the main lobby and/or attend one of our meetings on Wednesdays at 6:30pm in room 2003, EBIII.

name: African American Textile Society at NC State
description: The purpose of the organization shall be to provide support for and serve as a network with the minority population in the College of Textiles. In addition to the above, The African American Textile Society is responsible for NC States' longest-running fashion show dating back to 1997! The Annual Fashion Expose hosted by AATS allows students to showcase their talents in garment design and construction while competing for minimal scholarship funds.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide minority students enrolled in the Wilson College of Textiles as well as outside programs a network of social, and professional opportunities that will enhance their overall college experience.

name: African Student Union
description: African Student Union is an organization that promotes African culture on the campus of North Carolina State University, as well as the community at large. We stress the importance of scholarship, service, and over all enlightenment of African culture to our members and to the community we inhabit. Our greatest strength is unity expressed through individuals committed to ensuring that Africans blaze a trail of excellence and foster a legacy all people can be proud of.
summary: African Student Union promotes African culture on campus, as well as the community at large. We stress the importance of scholarship, service, and over all enlightenment of African culture.

name: Agapé Campus Ministry
description:
We are a Christian church-based campus ministry desiring to establish worship on the NC State campus. We have weekly meetings where we pray, worship, read God's word, and spend time together. It is in those times we experience the reality of God’s love and truth drawing us into lives of discipleship.

Bible Study: Thursdays 7:30pm, Talley rm 4265
Rides to Sunday Worship: 10:50am at Harris Hall lot off of Dan Allen Drive
summary: We are a Christian church-based campus ministry desiring to establish worship on the NC State campus, for both undergraduate and graduate students. We meet together for prayer, worship, Bible study, and community.

name: The Agri-Life Council at North Carolina State University
description: The Agri-Life Council is an umbrella organization consisting of representatives from student organizations in CALS. The vision of the Agri-Life Council is to make a positive contribution to the College of Agriculture & Life Sciences by unifying our student representatives while promoting leadership through fellowship, scholarship, and service. The Agri-Life Council's mission is to serve as the voice of all students in the College of Agriculture & Life Sciences by promoting academics and student life.
summary: The Agri-Life Council is an umbrella organization consisting of representatives from student organizations in CALS. The vision of the Agri-Life Council is to make a positive contribution to CALS by unifying our student representatives.

name: Agribusiness Club
description: In the Agricultural Business Management Club we aim to provide students with opportunities to develop career skills and connections that will allow them to be successful in the industry following graduation. As a club, we hear from industry representatives at all of our meetings. We aim to provide members with the opportunity to gain internships and career opportunities on a regular basis. We also provide assistance to the department for events and career opportunities.
summary: We promote agribusinesses and have speakers who educate us about the agriculture world today as well as provide assistance to the program for event coordinating.

name: FFA Alumni and Supporters at NC State
description:
The object of this chapter shall be to support and promote the FFA, FFA activities, and agricultural education on local, state and national levels; to provide engagement opportunities to former FFA members and supporters of FFA and agricultural education; to promote greater knowledge of the agricultural industry and support education in agriculture; to cooperate with the local FFA chapter, and FFA at the state and national level; to promote and maintain an appreciation of the American free enterprise system; and to promote the personal development aspect of FFA.

Meetings are held on the first and third Wednesday of every month at 6:00pm in 113 Ricks Hall, room subject to change.

Dues are $20 a semester and can be paid with cash at one of our meetings, or you can pay online using CheddarUp.

You can also purchase a club t-shirt using our online payment system. T-shirts will be distributed at the meeting following the time of your purchase.

All up-to-date club information can be found via our FFA Bulletin. If you have questions, please contact the sitting president using the email address below.
summary: Supports and advances agricultural education programs, FFA chapters, agricultural outreach programs, and organizations associated with agricultural education and FFA.

name: Agromeck
description: First published in 1903, Agromeck has served as the archive of NC State culture and history as the official school yearbook. The name Agromeck originates from the name of the University at the time of first publication, when the school was known as “North Carolina College of Agriculture and Mechanic Arts,” with the “Agro-“ symbolizing agriculture and the “-meck” honoring the mechanic arts. As the oldest and most award-winning student publication on campus, Agromeck is always looking for writers, designers and photographers to help cover on-campus, national and international events
summary: Since 1903, the Agromeck yearbook has provided a historical record of events on campus. Agromeck is always looking for writers, designers and photographers. No experience required.

name: Aikido Club
description:
Do you ever wish you could deflect basic attacks with as little effort as possible? Ever wish you could improve on your cat-like reflexes and martial awareness? Or how about a club that functions as a tight knit and dependable community? Well, that basically sums up Aikido club! We strive to perfect our fundamentals in order to build on and improvise in large setting scenarios. 

We welcome everyone! During Spring 2026, we are planning to have practices on Tuesday and Thursday from 6pm to 8pm at Carmichael Gym Court 4. You can join our practices with your work out clothing. It is recommended that you wear pants that cover the knees.

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to our email or Instagram page!
summary: Aikido: a martial art based on defense and deflection. It ensures your attacker's effort is redirected back at them with as minimal effort as possible on your part. Aikido will train you for better discipline, physical condition, and defensive instinct.

name: Air & Waste Management Association - Student Chapter at NC State University
description: We are the NC State student chapter of the Air & Waste Management Association. The AWMA is a global non-profit, nonpartisan professional organization. The mission of the AWMA is to assist in the professional development and critical environmental decision-making of our members to benefit society. The core purpose of AWMA is to improve environmental knowledge and decisions by providing a neutral forum for exchanging information.
summary: We are the NC State student chapter of the Air & Waste Management Association, a global professional organization. A&WMA promotes global environmental responsibility and increases the effectiveness of orgs to make critical decisions that benefit society.

name: Alexander Hamilton Scholars Program
description: The Alexander Hamilton Scholars Program is a unique opportunity for undergraduate students offered jointly by the Poole College of Management and the College of Humanities and Social Sciences at North Carolina State University. Hamilton Scholars are a select group of highly motivated students. To enrich their experience at NC State, they participate in activities such as special lectures and networking opportunities with potential employers, mentoring international business students, and scholarship banquets. Activities are designed to facilitate sharing of experiences by the Scholars. Each Hamilton Scholar completes a Business Administration, Accounting or Economics degree as well as an International Studies degree, which focuses on a specific area of the world or a thematic area of interest, along with study of a foreign language.
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Scholars Program is a unique opportunity for undergraduate students offered jointly by the Poole College of Management and the College of Humanities and Social Sciences at North Carolina State University.

name: All-Girl Cheerleading Club
description: The All-Girl Cheerleading Club at NC State University was founded in January 2002. It was created for students on campus who enjoy competitive cheerleading and want the privilege of wearing our university’s name on a uniform. In addition to the competitive element, our club serves as a place for young athletes with similar interests to make new friends, stay in shape, and have fun. We show our Wolfpack pride in competing nationally against the best cheerleading programs in the country at NCA Nationals in Daytona Beach, Florida. Along with the thrill of competing, we participate in countless events and community service projects throughout the year. As we continue establishing our presence and fine-tuning our organization, we look forward to stepping onto the competition floor. Go Wolfpack!
summary: All-Girl cheerleading at NC State University was created in January 2002; founded for females on campus who enjoy competitive cheerleading and wanted the privilege of putting our school's name on a uniform they would be proud to wear.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma- Gamma Xi Chapter
description: Alpha Chi Sigma is a professional chemistry fraternity, devoted to the advancement of chemistry as a science and as a profession. We are dedicated to performing outreach and service endeavors that are related to chemistry, as well as other philanthropy. We have ample opportunities for brothers to be involved in professional, social, and outreach events.
summary: Professional Chemistry Fraternity

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description:
First, Finest, Forever. Founded on May 15, 1851, Alpha Delta Pi is the oldest secret society for college women in the world. Alpha Delta Pi was established at Wesleyan College in Macon, Georgia, by Eugenia Tucker Fitzgerald and five of her friends. As the foundation of today's sorority system, it is only fitting that our founding took place at the first college in the world chartered to grant degrees to women. The principles established by our founders in 1851 are enduring standards exemplifying the highest ideals of sisterhood, service, character, and knowledge.

The Zeta Beta chapter of Alpha Delta Pi was founded at North Carolina State University in 1971. We are the proud 7-time recipient of the prestigious Golden Lion award - an honor that is earned over numerous years and presented by the national level of Alpha Delta Pi. Zeta Beta is honored to have won our 8th Golden Lion Award in 50 years of being chartered at NC State University. This award is the highest honor an Alpha Delta Pi chapter can receive, designating us as one of the highest performing chapters nationally. 

At the 2025 ADPi Academy, our chapter was recognized with nine programming awards: Operations Excellence, Facility Management Excellence, Finance Excellence, History Reporting Excellence, Marketing Excellence, Membership Experience Excellence, Panhellenic Excellence, Philanthropy Excellence, and Recruitment Excellence. We’re also incredibly honored to have been named the 2024 Most Outstanding Sorority of the Year by NC State Fraternity and Sorority Life!

The national philanthropy of Alpha Delta Pi is the Ronald McDonald House. Our chapter is lucky to have a Ronald McDonald house located nearby in Durham, NC, where we frequently volunteer to have fun with the families through activity nights, birthday parties, and cooking meals for the families. We support our philanthropy through a variety of fundraisers and fundraising events. We have continued to support Ronald McDonald House throughout the COVID-19 pandemic, by ongoing donations and virtual philanthropy events. Zeta Beta hosts two philanthropy events each year: Ram Roast in the fall semester and an additional event in the spring semester. Ram Roast, an event we host at our house, is a barbecue with proceeds from ticket sales going to the Ronald McDonald House. We also collect pop-tops from soda cans to raise money, and we additionally host percent of purchase nights at popular restaurants to conduct fundraising. These events provide an excellent opportunity to raise funds, engage with the community, and spread awareness about the Ronald McDonald House Charities. While our sisters are very active with our philanthropy, they also volunteer for a variety of other charities and programs throughout the year such as the Miracle League, the Special Olympics, and the Salvation Army. 

While our sisters are involved with our chapter and our philanthropy, they are also very involved on campus and in their academics. From holding positions on a variety of committees in their respective majors, club sports, club cheerleading teams, academic honor societies, and university programs, our sisters stay very active! Zeta Beta not only loves Alpha Delta Pi, but we also love North Carolina State University. Sisters can always be found cheering on the Pack at football games, basketball games, a variety of other sporting events, and by actively participating in the annual Homecoming Competition. ADPi always backs the Pack!

Alpha Delta Pi is something you are, not just something you become. At North Carolina State University, by living by this motto our lives are enriched by true friendships and unselfish service to our community. Through Alpha Delta Pi our sisters establish not only close friendships, but also a strong sisterhood that lasts a lifetime. We live for each other, and for Alpha Delta Pi.

 

NC State University's Policy on Discrimination: 

Discrimination based upon race, color, religion, creed, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or sexual orientation is in violation of federal and state law and North Carolina State University policy, and will not be tolerated.
summary: At North Carolina State University, by living our motto, our lives are enriched by true friendships and selfless service to our community. Through Alpha Delta Pi our sisters establish a strong sisterhood that lasts a lifetime.

name: Alpha Gamma Rho
description:
Alpha Gamma Rho (ΑΓΡ) is the fraternity sharing a common bond within a dynamic, global agriculture committed to fostering the highest values and providing each and every brother with superior lifelong social and professional success to make better men.

Status: Organizational Probation through January 21, 2026.

Violations: Violation of University Policies, Regulations, or Rules (FSL Social Events), Alcohol (b)
summary: Alpha Gamma Rho is an agricultural based fraternity that was charted at NC State on March 15, 1919. AGR is a social and professional fraternity, the only one of its kind at NC State. Our mission to make better men at North Carolina State University.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated.
description:
Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is an international service organization that was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. on January 15, 1908. It is the oldest Greek-lettered organization established by African-American college-educated women. 

On April 27, 1976, the Kappa Omicron Chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated was chartered on the campus of North Carolina State University.

Chapter Website: 

http://kappaomicron.wixsite.com/kappaomicronaka
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha is an international service organization, founded at Howard University in 1908, and is the oldest Greek organization established by African-American college-educated women. The Kappa Omicron Chapter was charted on April 27, 1976 at NCSU.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description:
"Alpha Kappa Psi is recognized as the premier developer of principled business leaders." We are a co-ed professional business fraternity with a deep history throughout the United States and around the world. We pride ourselves on participating in numerous professional development events such as speakers, workshops and seminars in addition to philanthropic activities such as the V Foundation, Adopt-a-Highway, Service Raleigh and Operation Christmas Child. We also host many social activities throughout the year such as a camping trip, beach trip, and formals to build a lasting bond within our brotherhood. While many of our brothers are business majors or minors, our brotherhood is composed of majors from nearly every college. So whether you are studying engineering, communications, or all of the above, Alpha Kappa Psi may have something for you! Every semester we hold a week-long RUSH, which is a great time to find out more about us and meet our brothers. Visit our website or contact one of our officers to learn more about our chapter.

 

akpsincsu.com
summary: We are a co-ed professional business fraternity with a deep history throughout the United States and around the world. Whether you are studying engineering, communications, or all of the above, Alpha Kappa Psi may have something for you!

name: Alpha Omega Epsilon Gamma Chapter
description: Alpha Omega Epsilon is a professional and social STEM-oriented sorority that promotes a set of Ideals and Objectives that we hope will further the advancement of female engineers and scientists in our fields of study. While doing so, we also encourage our Sisters to develop bonds of lifelong friendship with each other and others in the community. Alpha Omega Epsilon strives for scholarship and academic achievement. The Sorority pursues relationships with other Greek organizations, helps discover a wide variety of extracurricular activities and leadership roles, and aids in networking with engineers and scientists in the industry.
summary: Professional and Social STEM Sorority

name: Alpha Phi Alpha
description: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. was founded on December 4, 1906 at Cornell University in Ithaca, New York. It is the first Black Intercollegiate Greek-letter organization. The mission of Alpha Phi Alpha is to develop leaders, promote brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for our communities. The Eta Omicron Chapter was chartered on the campus of NC State University on April 7, 1971. With the help of Dr. Augustus M. Witherspoon and the Beta Rho Chapter at Shaw University, the Eta Omicron Chapter was the first Black Greek Letter Organization on NC State's campus.
summary: The first Black Intercollegiate Greek-letter organization. The mission of Alpha Phi Alpha is to develop leaders, promote brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for its communities.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
Check out our website https://www.ncsuapo.org/ to learn about who we are and how to join!

Alpha Phi Omega is a co-ed national service fraternity that was originally founded in 1925 at Lafayette College in Easton, PA by Frank Reed Horton. The fraternity was founded on the principles of the Boy Scouts of America and is committed to providing leadership, friendship and service to the colleges, communities and nations that we are a part of. Currently there are over 750 Alpha Phi Omega chapters in college throughout the United States. The Iota Lambda Chapter at NC State was founded in 1950 and today has over 120 active members. We volunteer throughout the NC State community and the Raleigh area, as well as helping out with service projects around campus. In APO you will find a diverse group of people from many different backgrounds and majors. We strive to help each individual member develop leadership skills, experience friendship on many levels and provide service to others.

If you are interested in rushing for Spring '26, reach out to education@aponcsu.org, and follow our Instagram at @apo_ncsu to see our rush schedule.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega is a national, co-educational service fraternity. Its purpose is to develop leadership, promote friendship, and provide service to humanity in an inclusive environment where all are welcomed!

name: Alpha Pi Mu at NC State
description: Throughout the field of Industrial Engineering, Alpha Pi Mu is a highly recognized society, identifying its members as being the top of their class. The society of Alpha Pi Mu serves to promote the field of Industrial Engineering through monthly meetings, guest speakers, information sessions, community service, and social activities. Moreover, the society offers students the opportunity to interact among their peers and form relationships to last beyond college. Finally, potential employers recognize Alpha Pi Mu students as leaders, able to bring their high level of academic achievement to the work force. With the possibilities available as an Alpha Pi Mu member, it is no surprise that its students have entered and succeeded in industry, such as Edward Woolard, CEO of Dupont Industries and member of the Alpha Rho Chapter at NC State in 1956.
summary: Throughout the field of Industrial Engineering, Alpha Pi Mu is a highly recognized society, identifying its members as being the top of their class. Alpha Pi Mu serves to promote the field of Industrial Engineering through various activities.

name: Alpha Pi Omega Sorority, Inc.
description: Alpha Pi Omega Sorority Inc., is the country’s oldest Native American Greek-letter organization. Founded at the University of North Carolina-Chapel Hill on Sept. 1, 1994, the sorority has more than 700 sisters representing more than 100 tribes nationwide and 20 chartered chapters. We are a united sisterhood of Indigenous women who are committed to each other, our communities, tribes, families, academic excellence, and self-empowerment.
summary: The Eta Chapter of Alpha Pi Omega Sorority, Inc. at NC State.

name: Alpha Sigma Phi
description: Restarted in Fall 2015, the Beta Zeta chapter is currently comprised of 87 undergraduates. Since its re-chartering, the Beta Zeta chapter of Alpha Sigma Phi has grown and improved each semester and was awarded a Silver Cup from Alpha Sigma Phi Inc. in the summer of 2018 - recognizing the chapter as one of the top 10% of Alpha Sigma Phi's +150 chapter nationally.
summary: Originally founded in 1930, Beta Zeta Chapter of Alpha Sigma Phi was restarted in the Fall of 2015 with a goal of Bettering the Man. Alpha Sigma Phi looks to improve the undergraduate experience with opportunity in leadership, service, and philanthropy.

name: Alpha Tau Omega
description:
We are an American Leadership fraternity that annually ranks among the top ten national fraternities for number of chapters, and total number of members. ATO gives you the opportunity to find a family away from home and connect with people in a meaningful way. In ATO you have people to support you, people to turn to when you need help, and a camaraderie that is impossible to duplicate on campus.

For those interested, we are always recruiting! Reach out to us on our Instagram or by using this link.

 

"To bind men together in a brotherhood based upon eternal and immutable principles, with a bond as strong as right itself and as lasting as humanity; to know no North, no South, no East, no West, but to know man as man, to teach that true men the world over should stand together and contend for supremacy of good over evil; to teach, not politics, but morals; foster, not partisanship, but the recognition of true merit wherever found; to have no narrower limits within which to work together for the elevation of man than the outlines of the world: these were the thoughts and hopes uppermost in the minds of the founders of the Alpha Tau Omega Fraternity"

-- Otis Allen Glazebrook
summary: An American Leadership fraternity that annually ranks among the top ten national fraternities for number of chapters, and total number of members. ATO gives you the opportunity to find a family away from home and connect with people in a meaningful way.

name: Alpha Zeta Agricultural Honors Fraternity
description: The purpose of Alpha Zeta Fraternity is to bind together a group of men and women interested in the continued advancement of agriculture and to foster and develop high standards of scholarship, character, leadership, and a spirit of fellowship among its members. Our mission is to do the greatest good for the greatest number while advancing agriculture and its allied fields. The fraternity does this through service to our local community, raising awareness about agriculture and the goods and services it provides, and taking farm tours to continually stay engaged with agriculture and its direction.
summary: The purpose of Alpha Zeta Fraternity is to bind together a group of men and women interested in the continued advancement of agriculture and to foster and develop high standards of scholarship, character, leadership, and a spirit of fellowship.

name: Student Alumni Association Ambassadors
description: The purpose of SAA is to engage the NC State community by fostering traditions. SAA strives to provide opportunities for the active involvement of students and to promote a vibrant campus culture. Chosen through a closed membership process, the Ambassadors are a diverse group of student leaders passionate about their alma mater and committed to bridging the gap between past and present members of the Wolfpack.
summary: The purpose of SAA is to engage the NC State community by fostering traditions. SAA strives to provide opportunities for the active involvement of students and to promote a vibrant campus culture.

name: American Concrete Institute - Student Chapter
description: The American Concrete Institute - Student Chapter serves to advance NC State students by fostering an interest in concrete and facilitating professional development. This is accomplished by completing a semester project that focuses in concrete design and application as part of an international student competition and by forging relationships with professionals in the concrete industry. Each semester, students travel to the ACI International Convention to present their work, compete in the student competition with other teams from universities around the world, and create meaningful professional connections with industry leaders.
summary: The American Concrete Institute - Student Chapter, or ACI, is a student organization focusing on concrete structures and design. The team travels to international ACI certified competitions to present their projects against other ACI student chapters.

name: American Indian Science and Engineering Society at NC State
description: The purpose of AISES is to promote the recruitment, admission, and retention of Native American students, mainly in the science and engineering disciplines, to support Native American students in all degree programs at NC State University at the undergraduate and graduate levels. As well as other reasonable efforts which will support such a purpose including, but not limited to, appropriate counseling, financial, academic, educational, and social resources.
summary: The purpose of AISES is to promote the recruitment, admission, and retention of Native American students, mainly in the science and engineering disciplines, to support Native American students in all degree programs.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description: The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics is the student chapter of the national organization of the same name. Our student group has the same goals as the parent organization which is to "ignite and celebrate aerospace ingenuity and collaboration, and its importance to our way of life." This organization is comprised of students of all backgrounds who are interested in the aerospace field. The parent organization is the world's largest technical society dedicated to the global aerospace profession. At NC State, we promote professional development, provide networking opportunities, and volunteer in all aspects related to the aerospace industry.
summary: The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) is the world's largest technical society dedicated to the global aerospace profession.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
description:
The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is a student-run organization that has been advancing leadership, service, and design among Architecture students for over 50 years. Get involved in our chapter! Through AIAS design competitions, skills workshops, firm crawls, professional-development events, and national and regional conferences, the AIAS will help prepare you for your life as an Architect, as a Designer, and as a well-rounded Citizen.

The mission of the AIAS is: (1) To promote excellence in architecture education, training and practice. (2) To foster an appreciation of architecture and related disciplines. (3) To enrich communities in a spirit of collaboration. (4) To organize students and combine their efforts to advance the art and science of architecture.
summary: The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is a student-run organization that has been advancing leadership, service, and design among Architecture students for over 50 years. Get involved in our chapter!

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description: The American Institute of Chemical Engineers Student Chapter at North Carolina State University is one of the most active chapters in the nation. AIChE is a professional student-led organization that focuses on professional development, leadership, networking, as well as social service and outreach. Please visit our website for more information: https://www.ncsuaiche.com/
summary: AIChE is a professional student-led organization for chemical engineers that focuses on professional development, leadership, networking, continuing education, as well as social service and outreach.

name: American Marketing Association at NC State
description:
Our Chapter

The NC State AMA is an internationally recognized chapter of the American Marketing Association, and the largest business organization at NC State. We are NC State's #1 source for marketing and professional development topics, including networking, LinkedIn, interviewing, and more!

 

Our Mission

Our mission is to empower NC State students to achieve the careers of their dreams. We accomplish this through professional development, real-world marketing experience, leadership opportunities, and meaningful connections.

 

 

Visit our website for information about our upcoming events and to learn how to join NC State AMA!

 

2025 Interest Meeting: Tuesday, August 26th 6:00PM-7:00PM Nelson Hall Room 3400
summary: The NC State American Marketing Association is the largest business organization at NC State. We are the university's #1 source for marketing and professional development topics, including networking, LinkedIn, interviewing, and more!

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description:
American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) is a professional organization that serves the civil engineering profession and the public. Through the expertise of its active membership and staff, ASCE is the world's largest publisher of civil engineering content in the world, a leading provider of technical and professional conferences and continuing education, and a frontrunner in developing codes and standards that protect the public.

The objective of this ASCE Student Chapter is to introduce Civil, Construction, and Environmental Engineering students to the infrastructure industry through social and professional development events. We supplement this with community service opportunities, competition teams, and participation in the annual ASCE Carolinas Student Symposium.

Join our mailing list here. Contact us at ascestudentchapter@ncsu.edu!
summary: This organization is a student chapter of the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE). ASCE is the United States' oldest civil engineering society.

name: The American Society of Mechanical Engineers North Carolina State University Section
description:
Click here to visit our website:

asmencstate.wordpress.ncsu.edu

 

 

The American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) is an international organization founded to help "the global engineering community develop solutions to real world challenges... ASME is a not-for-profit professional organization that enables collaboration, knowledge sharing and skill development across all engineering disciplines, while promoting the vital role of the engineer in society. ASME codes and standards, publications, conferences, continuing education and professional development programs provide a foundation for advancing technical knowledge and a safer world." The NCSU student chapter of ASME was founded in 1920. We give students an opportunity to understand how their classes can be applied practically by competing in design competitions, as well as holding resume workshops, professional seminars with company representatives, social events, and service events. We also attend several professional development conferences every year.

 

Upcoming Events:

Fall 2026 events to come soon!

Meeting Times:

Meeting times will be determined soon!

 

 


*Food is provided by ASME@NCSTATE for free during each professional meeting. 

*You DO NOT have to be a member to participate in our professional development meetings. 

 

 

 

Joining:

*If you are interested in joining ASME@NCSTATE, the first step is to join here on Get Involved!

 If you would like to receive weekly email updates, please visit our website by clicking here (you will be redirected to our new member sign-up page).

*To become a national member, go to www.asme.org and sign up. 

    -Free for freshman!

    -$25 per year for upperclassman.

 

 



Join our Discord at https://discord.gg/wTgc4JuwRE

Follow us on Instagram at https://www.instagram.com/ncsu.asme/

Follow us on LinkedIn at https://www.linkedin.com/company/asme-at-nc-state-university/

For more information, visit our Website at asmencstate.wordpress.ncsu.edu
summary: The ASME Student Section At NC State is an organization that focuses on providing professional development and practical engineering opportunities to students through networking, design competitions, and social events.

name: Animal Science Graduate Student Association
description:
Objectives:

(1) To disseminate information of interest or importance to the graduate students involved in ANS programs

(2) To provide a forum for discussion of problems and other matters of importance to the students of North Carolina State University

(3) To organize and conduct professional and social activities for ANS graduate students, including the Annual Invited Guest Lecture series
summary: The organization provides a platform to disseminate information of interest or importance to the graduate students involved in Animal Science programs and to organize and conduct professional and social activities for Animal Science graduate students.

name: Anime Club
description:
Room for Spring 2026: Williams Hall Room 1404
Anime Meetings: Thursdays 7:00 PM ~ 9:00 PM (9:00 PM ~ 10:00 PM dinner at Golden Dragon)
Hang-out Meetings: Mondays 7:00 PM ~ 9:00 PM (9:00 PM ~ 10:00 PM dinner at Golden Dragon)
 

 

Weekly shows:

takt op.Destiny (Anilist, MAL)

Girls' Last Tour (Anilist, MAL)

 

 

Club AniList: AnimeClubAtNCSU

Club Discord: discord.gg/m6C3bNhueQ

Club Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ncsuanimeclub

 

Mission:

The purpose of this club is to provide a social environment to watch anime.  Those who are interested in manga and video games are also encouraged to join.  Anime Club also strives to provide a place for people to interact with others who are passionate about niche Japanese culture.

 

How Things Roll

  • Our club aims to have a chill and relaxed atmosphere for anyone who would like to come by.  Feel free to just come to meet people, have a different atmosphere to study/do work, or whatever else you'd like.  If the club isn't to your liking, you are welcome to leave whenever you wish.
  • Our other objective is to expose anime lovers to shows they may have easily missed or are not well known.  This being said, we try to avoid showing mainstream or other popular anime that most people have likely seen (Ex: Naruto, One Piece, Bleach, Fairy Tail, FMA/FMAB, Death Note, Attack on Titan, My Hero Academia, etc.).  Feel free to discuss these shows, but we will most likely not be showing them in club.
  • On the first day of club, the officers and president will select 4 shows we deem appropriate for watching on a weekly basis.  After watching the first episode of each, members will vote on the two they would like to watch every week and the two highest voted shows will make the cut.
  • Our formal meeting on Thursdays consists of watching 4 episodes of anime. The first and last episodes we watch will be our weekly series.  The other 2 episodes will be the first episodes of member-voted series.
  • Every week after watching the first 2 episodes, we will have a brief intermission for socializing.  We will then pick 8 people to recommend 8 different shows, of which we will then vote upon.  Next week, we will watch the 2 shows with the greatest number of votes.
  • Once a semester, we will have an Anime Trivia Night or Name That Tune.  Typically, Anime Trivia Night is held in the fall around Fall Break or Thanksgiving Break, while Name That Tune is held around Spring Break or Easter Vacation.  Anime Trivia Night and Name That Tune are always held on a Monday night.  Winners will receive grand prizes; other participants will receive consolation prizes.
  • On the last Thursday of each semester, we watch a movie. 
  • There are a few notable rules:
  1. Respect one another.  This is quite important, especially because not all people have the same taste in anime.  Let's keep things civil.  No bashing shows/waifus/husbandos/ships/etc.
  2. Any show suggestion that the officers and/or the president deem inappropriate is subject to immediate revocation.  These include (but are not limited to) hentai or other overly sexual/ecchi anime (Ex: High School DxD, To LOVE-Ru, Monster Musume, etc.) and anime that attack sensitive topics such as race, sexual orientation, and/or religion (Ex: Panty & Stocking with Garterbelt).  Fanservice here and there is inevitable in most anime, so some will be overlooked, but those that are too sexually gratuitous will be vetoed.
  3. Please avoid talking/commenting on the shows we watch until halftime.  An occasional comment to a friend whispered here or there is fine, but otherwise try not to chat during the shows.
  • Following Thursday meetings, we go out to eat dinner at Golden Dragon on Hillsborough Street.  However, feel free to order take-out anywhere we don't go and join us afterwards.  Drinks are free for club members because we bring in such a large crowd every Thursday.  
  • The informal Monday meetings are primarily for hanging out with everyone.  Video games will be set up (generally anime-style fighting games such as Smash or Project Diva) for anyone to enjoy.  We also play Weiss Schwarz, the anime trading card game, so if you already play or are interested in getting into the game, come join us!  Funny/Interesting anime videos and/or trivia sites are often explored as well.  Feel free to come and go as you please.  We usually go eat somewhere on Hillsborough Street afterwards.  Some of the officers and club members have cars and are willing to provide transportation.
  • Above all else, we hope you enjoy the club!
summary:
The purpose of this club is to provide a social environment to watch anime. Those who are interested in manga and video games are also encouraged to join!

Click Read More to see exactly what we do and when/where we meet.

name: Antioch College Ministry at North Carolina State University
description:
We are a people with a passion for Jesus and His purposes in the earth! Because of God’s great love for us, we love Him, love others, and make disciples at NC State, in Raleigh, and to the ends of the earth!
 
We gather weekly in Lifegroups on and around NC State’s campus and would love for you to join us!
 
To join a Lifegroup please fill out this Google Form and someone will be in touch with more information!
 
Lifegroup form: https://forms.gle/yNMqzvhyPVJEhjYh9 

Antioch College Ministry is a part of Antioch Community Church. We gather on Sunday mornings at 10:30am at Wiley Magnet Elementary School (301 St. Mary's Street).
summary: We have a passion for Jesus and His purposes in the earth! We desire to passionately love and glorify God, love one another in authentic community and reproduce the life of Jesus in the people of our city.

name: Arnold Air Society
description:
The Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary service organization advocating the support of aerospace power. Our mission is to create outstanding Air Force officer candidates through the implementation of additional training, while working as a society to further the purposes, traditions, and concepts of the United States Air Force.

The George V. Holloman Squadron at North Carolina State University is a member squadron of Region 3, along with several other universities in the Southeast. Only AFROTC cadets who pass a rigorous candidate class are offered membership.

The Arnold Air Society Motto:

"The warrior who cultivates his mind polishes his arms." -Duc de Boufflers

The George V. Holloman Squadron Motto

"Tough times don't last, tough people do." -John Cooper
summary: The Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary and service organization advocating the support of aerospace power.

name: Art2Wear
description: Art2WearTM (A2W) is a year-long educational experience where students gain experience by coordinating an annual production. Young designers are challenged to imagine, create, and inspire by expressing their point of view through sartorial designs. Housed in the Department of Media Arts, Design and Technology and supported by the College of Design, this highly anticipated production fuses the talents of students throughout the university. Students are exposed to all aspects of productions and the broader field of fashion and wearable art.
summary: A student organized runway show that presents fashion, costume, and sartorial sculpture created by the students NC State University.

name: Asian Students Association
description: The Asian Students Association was established to serve as a cultural organization that promotes cultural awareness, social networking, community, and personal development. Our organization serves to provide the Asian American and Asian American allied communities with a space where they can feel a sense of belonging and form connections at any point in an individual's college career here at NC State.
summary: The Asian Students Association was established to serve as a cultural organization that promotes cultural awareness, social networking, community, and personal development.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics Student Chapter
description:
The purpose of the Association for Women in Mathematics is to encourage women & girls to study and to have active careers in the mathematical sciences, & to promote equal opportunity and the equal treatment of women and girls in the mathematical sciences.  

Our NCSU chapter is comprised of both men and women who seek to share their love of mathematics within our department and the surrounding community.  We host a number of informative events as well as social gatherings.  Each year we host Sonia Kovalevsky Day (SK Day), a day of workshops, talks, and problem solving with 7th and 8th grade girls in nearby middle schools.  We aim to encourage young women to continue their study of mathematics and embrace their inner nerds (while still being cool!)

AWM currently has more than 3000 members (women and men) representing a broad spectrum of the mathematical community — from the United States and around the world!
summary: The purpose of the Association for Women in Mathematics is to encourage women & girls to study and to have active careers in the mathematical sciences, & to promote equal opportunity and the equal treatment of women and girls in the mathematical sciences

name: Astronomy Club at NC State University
description:
The Astronomy Club at NC State aims to provide students and members with the resources necessary for observing celestial objects and events, and to encourage a strong appreciation for astronomy and the sciences in general.

NOTE: To request membership, sign in to GetInvolved and click the join button 

To better interact with out members we have created a Discord server!!! After joining the club here on Get Involved, please join the server by clicking the link in the discussion section below! 

(discussion section will only be visible if you're a club member)

 

 

 

 

 

 

https://youtu.be/4BZ3cNUdiWA

(2019)
summary: The Astronomy Club at NC State aims to provide students and members with the resources necessary for observing celestial objects and events, and to encourage a strong appreciation for astronomy and the sciences in general.

name: Baccalaureate Student Social Work Association
description: The BSSWA strives to complement the academic requirements of the undergraduate social work program at North Carolina State University. The association does this through workshops taught by social work professionals and structured interaction with other social work majors and social work practitioners. We give students in the association opportunities to implement the skills they've acquired in class into the community through service.
summary: This organization strives to enhance the professional development of undergraduate social work students through general body meetings, service events, workshops, and community involvement.

name: Bangladesh Student Association
description:
Bangladesh Student Association (BSA) at NC State University is a platform for students and faculty to come together, celebrate, and represent Bangladeshi culture through different cultural activities. BSA also plays a vital role by offering voluntary services and support to its members. We welcome new Bangladeshi students and try our best to assist them in coping with campus life and local culture. We aim to give our members the feeling of having an extended family and a dependable support system while living thousands of miles away from home. We also collaborate with friends from other cultural ethnicities on social and cultural grounds.

Find us on Facebook, Instagram and YouTube!
summary: Bangladesh Student Association (BSA) at NC State University is a platform for students and faculty to come together, celebrate, and represent Bangladeshi culture through different cultural activities.

name: Beta Alpha Psi - Iota Omega Chapter
description:
Founded in 1919, BAP has a long standing history of providing students with a valuable network while focusing on the core values of academic excellence, professional development, a commitment to serve, and shaping you into a confident leader. There are over 300 chapters on college and university campuses around the globe and over 300,000 members initiated. NC State's BAP provides students with connections to industry leaders from the Raleigh area and beyond all while fostering a sense of community and belonging. Not only will you be able to meet and connect with professionals, but you can join a group of over 150 of your own classmates from NC State. Throughout the year, we plan to hold professional meetings, community service events and social gatherings so come to a meeting and see what BAP is all about.  

  

For more information, go to our website!
summary: Beta Alpha Psi is an international honors organization for IT, Finance, Accounting students and professionals. Beta Alpha Psi provides students with opportunities for professional development, networking and community service.

name: Beta Upsilon Chi
description: We are Beta Upsilon Chi, Brothers Under Christ, or just BYX ("bux"). We are a Christian Fraternity whose purpose is to establish brotherhood and unity among college men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ.
summary: We are Beta Upsilon Chi, Brothers Under Christ, or just BYX ("bux"). We are a Christian fraternity whose purpose is to establish brotherhood and unity among college men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ.

name: Beta-Upsilon Chapter of Kappa Sigma
description:
Kappa Sigma is the largest college social fraternity in the world, with more than 200,000 living members, including over 17,000 undergraduates and more than 300 chapters and colonies located throughout the United States and Canada. Founded in 1869 at the University of Virginia, Kappa Sigma International Headquarters is based in Charlottesville, Virginia. 

Kappa Sigma is focused on the Four Pillars of Fellowship, Leadership, Scholarship, and Service. As a values-based men’s fraternity, Kappa Sigma strictly forbids hazing and fosters meaningful college experiences by offering progressive membership development and pledge education.
summary: The Kappa Sigma Fraternity – Beta-Upsilon Chapter at NC State is a social fraternity focused on leadership, academic achievement, community service, and personal growth. Originally founded in 1903 and recharted in the Spring of 2025.

name: Biochemistry Club at North Carolina State University
description:
The vision of the Biochemistry Club is to foster fellowship and networking, stimulate interest in biochemistry, and provide academic support and career advice to students in a casual setting. The club focuses on topics directly related to biochemistry majors, but all majors are welcome to join! We understand the value of network and community early in your undergraduate career and are focused on pioneering a supportive environment to all students. 

 

Meetings for Fall 2025 will occur on alternating Thursdays either from 6-7 pm or 7-8pm in Polk room 128 (the TOVE room), Start date and time will be finalized at the start of the semester. Be sure to follow us on Instagram @biochem.at.ncsu for updates!

 

Contact us at biochem.eboard@gmail.com for more information!
summary: The mission of the Biochemistry Club is to foster fellowship and networking, stimulate interest in biochemistry, and provide academic support and career advice to students.

name: Biological & Agricultural Engineering GSA
description: The purpose of the BAE GSA is to represent the graduate student body within the Department of Biological and Agricultural Engineering while promoting professional interest and fellowship among BAE graduate students.
summary: The purpose of the BAE GSA is to represent the graduate student body within the Department of Biological and Agricultural Engineering while promoting professional interest and fellowship among BAE graduate students.

name: Biomedical Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: The Biomedical Engineering Graduate Student Association (BME-GSA) is composed of graduate students in the Joint Department of Biomedical Engineering at UNC Chapel Hill and NC State University. We are a registered student organization recognized by the University Graduate Student Association at NC State and the Graduate and Professional Student Federation at UNC at Chapel Hill. The goal of our group is to plan social and community outreach events for our members and to foster community within the graduate students of our department.
summary: The Biomedical Engineering Graduate Student Association (BME-GSA) is composed of graduate students in the UNC Chapel Hill and NC State University Joint Department of Biomedical Engineering.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society Student Chapter at NC State
description: BMES aims to educate all students interested in the field of biomedical engineering in professional pathways to help them attain their goals. Our organization is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive community of aspiring biomedical engineers and anyone interested in the fascinating field of biomedical engineering. Whether you are a student majoring in biomedical engineering or simply curious about this multidisciplinary domain, our club offers a wealth of opportunities for personal and professional growth. We strive to introduce students to people in the industry, help students gain an understanding of the graduate school process, and network with students through the Joint Departmental Program. BMES facilitates interaction and shared educational experiences of biomedically focused students despite the Engineering department to enhance and grow the professional end educational network of all interested students.
summary: BMES aims to educate and inform students interested in the field of Biomedical Engineering with career options and educational pathways in a professional and friendly environment. Head to our website bit.ly/ncsubmesclub to sign up!

name: Black Business Students Association
description:
The purpose of the Black Business Students Association (BBSA) is to assist in the academic, personal, and professional development of our members. The BBSA supports its members in four ways:

  • Career and Professional Development: Career and Professional Development events such as interview preparation, panels, and professional development workshops allow members to develop connections with companies. These events also provide support to our members throughout the recruiting season.
  • Programming: Programming provides members a way to connect with each other and the greater Raleigh Community. 
  • Academic Success: Study guides and group tutorial sessions provide opportunities for BBSA members to excel in the rigorous academic environment of the Poole College of Management.
  • Alumni Outreach: Alumni outreach is encouraged through mentoring and networking events.
summary: The purpose of the Black Business Students Association (BBSA) is to assist in the academic, personal, and professional development of our members.

name: Broadcast Meteorology Club at NC State
description: The Broadcast Meteorology Club serves to further the education of meteorology students and others interested in pursuing broadcast meteorology as a career choice. The Club provides opportunities for members to learn and practice their skills distributing forecasts to a variety of media sources such and "The Technician", Facebook, YouTube, and others.
summary: The Broadcast Meteorology Club serves to further the education of meteorology students and others interested in pursuing broadcast meteorology as a career choice. The Club provides opportunities to learn about weather and build broadcast skills.

name: Alpha Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc.
description: Alpha Phi Gamma National Sorority, Inc. is an Asian Interest Sorority at NC State University, established on June 14, 2015. Our mission is to create a strong and life-long Sisterhood as well as raise awareness of the lifestyle of an Asian American Woman.  Our National Philanthropy is to raise awareness of violence towards women. We hope to strengthen the Asian community and help it to grow and prosper. Alpha Phi Gamma promotes Academic Excellence, Scholarship, Personal Development, and Leadership.  For more information, contact our President, Holly Ngo: hhngo@ncsu.edu and check us out on Facebook (NCSU APhiG) or Instagram (@aphig_ncsu) for upcoming events.
summary: Alpha Phi Gamma National Sorority, Inc. is an Asian Interest (but not Asian exclusive) sorority dedicated to Sisterhood, Academics and Scholarship, Personal Development, and Leadership. We raise awareness of the lifestyle of an Asian American Woman.

name: Campus Christian Fellowship
description: CCF is a non-denominational campus ministry that welcomes any college student to come check us out! Join us for worship services, small group Bible studies, camping trips, mission trips, game nights, prayer nights, retreats, discipleship, pizza parties, beach trips, service projects, outreach events on campus, and more experiences that relate to college-life.
summary: CCF is a campus ministry focused on making disciples on campus who impact their world.  We desire to love God and love others... and as we do this, we hope to change our campus and our world for God's glory!

name: Caribbean Student Association
description: The purpose of the Caribbean Student Association is to foster a community of Caribbean students on campus and to also promote and represent Caribbean culture on campus.
summary: The purpose of the Caribbean Student Association is to foster a community of Caribbean students and to also promote Caribbean culture on campus.

name: Catholic Campus Ministry at NC State
description:
Check out our website and Instagram for more information! We offer opportunities for students to grow in their faith and personal lives so that they leave college with a strong human and spiritual formation. We currently offer daily Mass and adoration, a Wolfpack Worship Night every Wednesday, social events, service events, bible studies, and men's and women's ministries! You are welcome here!

https://www.catholicpack.org/
summary: We are a family dedicated to having a rich sacramental life and a deep understanding of God. We offer opportunities for students to grow in a Catholic community to build a strong foundation of the faith. Sacraments, social and service events, and more!

name: Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: This group is the graduate student association for the Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering department. This group provides graduate students in the Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering an active voice in departmental matters and gives the graduate students an open forum to discuss concerns they have with the department. This group is responsible for organizing department-wide events to promote and foster unity within the graduate department including seminars, social gatherings, and other activities. This group is responsible for distributing information to the CBE graduate student body from the UGSA.
summary: We are composed of all the graduate students in the CBE department at NCSU. Our purpose is to give our members an active voice in departmental matters and to promote and foster unity within the graduate community through seminars and social gathering.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this association shall be to provide chemistry graduate students with a voice in the University Graduate Student Association and to function in conjunction with the leading graduate student scholastic organization within the department, the North Carolina State University Chapter of Phi Lambda Upsilon Honorary Chemistry Society, to promote seminars, graduate student orientation events, and social events for the Chemistry Department.
summary: The purpose of this association shall be to provide chemistry graduate students with a voice in the University Graduate Student Association and to function in conjunction with the leading graduate student scholastic organization within the department.

name: Chi Alpha Christian Fellowship
description:
Chi Alpha is not a fraternity or a sorority; however, if you're looking for brothers and sisters you've come to the right place! We're a group of students who are passionate about being devoted disciples of Jesus. There's always room for more and we'd love for you to join us on this journey. Our Greek name means "Christ's Ambassadors" and comes from 2 Corinthians 5:20 which states "We are therefore Christ's ambassadors, as though God were making his appeal through us. We implore you on Christ's behalf: Be reconciled to God." 

 

Our weekly worship service, XA Live, meets Tuesdays from 7:00-8:30pm. To find out what room we are in and about upcoming events, follow @raleighxa.ministry on social media or check out our website www.raleighxa.com. 

 

We also have Life Groups (our version of Small Groups) that meet weekly to not only study the Bible together, but to also live life together. For current life group times/locations, go to raleighxa.com. We would love to have you join us!
summary: We're a group of students who are passionate about following Jesus, building deep relationships, and serving our campus and community.

name: Chi Psi Fraternity
description: Chi Psi is a private fraternity of gentlemen devoted to the personal and collective needs of its members. A selective association of diverse individuals, Chi Psi is dedicated to the cultivation of a unique environment which instills a lifelong commitment to Brotherhood.
summary: Chi Psi is a private fraternity of gentlemen devoted to the personal and collective needs of its members.

name: Chinese Students & Scholars Friendship Association at NC State
description:
Chinese Students and Scholars Friendship Association (CSSFA) at North Carolina State University, formally is affiliated to the Office of International Services and Student Organization Resource Center at North Carolina State University, as well as the Office of Educational Affairs of the Embassy of P.R.China in USA . Besides, CSSFA has very good cooperation relationship with 15 Chinese Organizations in North Carolina.  Founded in 1981, we are a non-politics, non-religious, non-profit volunteer-run body whose primary members are Chinese students, scholars, faculties and staffs, their families, as well as some Chinese who work in the triangle area.

CSSFA aims at: (1) Assisting new comers to smoothly and successfully adapt to their lives in US; (2) Promote mutual understanding, assistance, and benefits among Chinese at North Carolina State University; (3) Facilitate cultural exchange among Chinese, American and international friends; (4) Enhance our network aiming for the welfare of the student community.

We are proud to say that CSSFA has been accumulating successful experience year after year on organizing events to revive memories from back home and get everyone together to know our fellow students better. CSSFA is now composed of seven Offices: 

  1. Office of Welcoming and Social. Facilitating the smooth transaction of newcomers to the US environment by providing airport picking-up, publishing new comer's guide, hosting information fair during orientation and organizing social events; 
  2. Office of Sports, plans basketball, football and badminton games throughout the year; 
  3. Office of Arts, hosts karaoke and gala shows to celebrate traditional festivals as well as enrich and diverse campus culture; 
  4. Office of Career Development, hosts seminars and series lectures on career related topics such as job hunting skills and resources, self evaluation, resume and cover letter writing, legal services, etc.
  5. Office of Alumni, builds up the network of North Carolina State University graduated Chinese to facilitate communication between current enrolled Chinese students and alumnus, provide chaces for career guidence and internal referals.   
  6. Office of Administration, works on administrative and public affairs of CSSFA, such as sponsor relationship, project management, and inter-office communication.
  7. Office of Media, Manages all the online/offline medias of CSSFA and the publication of CSSFA information by using the offical CSSFA website, Wechat public account, email list, and social medias.

We welcome each one of you on this vast land and invite you to live the CSSFA experience!
summary: CSSFA is the official Chinese organization on campus registered under the Chinese organization the Office of Education Affairs of the Embassy of P.R.China. We commit to the serving the Chinese community on campus and facilitate China-US communication.

name: Chinese Undergraduate Student Association
description: CUSA at NC State is a community of Chinese students and those interested in Chinese culture. Through organizing social, cultural, and educational events, our mission is to create a gathering place for students with similar backgrounds and interests to come together and celebrate the beauty of Chinese culture.
summary: CUSA at NC State is a community for Chinese students and those interested in Chinese culture. We organize events to create a gathering place for students with similar backgrounds and interests to come together and celebrate the beauty of Chinese culture.

name: Christians on Campus
description:
Christians on Campus is a registered student organization at North Carolina State University. We love the Lord Jesus and His Word and aspire to know the truths of the Bible and experience the riches of Christ. We desire to live a happy and purpose-filled Christian life through a personal relationship with Jesus Christ and share the gospel with those around us (Job 23:12;1 Cor. 2:9-10; Phil. 1:21; Gal. 2:20 ; Eph. 3:8).

 

Come grow with us! Our Christian faith is strengthened by regularly meeting together with other believers in Christ to build ourselves up in our most holy faith that we may experience the reality of the Body of Christ (Rom. 1:12; Heb. 10:25; Jude 20). Praise Jesus!

 

For more information, visit our website.
summary: Come grow with us! Our Christian faith is strengthened by regularly meeting together with other believers in Christ to build ourselves up in our most holy faith that we may experience the reality of the Body of Christ (Rom. 1:12; Heb. 10:25; Jude 20).

name: Comedic Improvisational Alliance at NC State
description:
Improv is a form of theater where there is no script, and scenes are made up on the spot by players. Improvised, if you will!

      The CIA has been around for twenty years as NC State's premiere and only Improv Comedy troupe. We put on three to four FREE shows each semester! Our practices are open to all, are non-audition and non-mandatory, from 6:00PM to 8:00PM Tuesdays and Thursdays. No experience? No worries, you're new to everything before you try it! Don't want to do Improv/don't have the time? Come watch our shows and tell your friends about us! Give us a follow on Instagram @NCSUCIA

and here is our linktree page! linktr.ee/ncsucia

     For more information about show dates and locations and any questions you may have, email us at theciawantsyou@gmail.com.
summary: The CIA is the first, best (and only) improv comedy troupe at NC State. Our club is open to anyone and everyone, and our practices our Tuesdays and Thursdays 6-8 pm! No experience needed.

name: Circle K International
description:
Vision Statement

The NC State Chapter of Circle K International will be a prominent, inclusive, and impactful student-led organization helping to improve the quality of life for the people of Triangle Area and NC State students through service, leadership and fellowship.

 

Mission Statement

NCSU Circle K will realize its vision by:

  • Publicizing, promoting, and practicing the values of service, leadership and fellowship throughout the Triangle Area.
  • Increasing the impact of our service by partnering with branches of the Kiwanis family, other NC State student organizations, and the community at large.
  • Distinguishing ourselves as an organization focusing on the well-being of the community and our campus.
  • Being a consistent, positive presence, and an interactive role model in the lives of future leaders.
summary: Circle K International is the collegiate level of Kiwanis International dedicated to Service, Leadership, and Fellowship.

name: Civil Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: Purpose of CEGSA is to represent the graduate students in the Department of Civil, Construction, and Environmental Engineering. Its purpose shall include, but not limited to, the development of projects, undertakings, studies, panels and other activities and programs in cooperation and coordination with the Civil, Construction and Environmental Engineering Department, North Carolina State University student associations and other organizations.
summary: Purpose of CEGSA is to represent the graduate students in the Department of Civil, Construction, and Environmental Engineering.

name: Club Baseball at NC State
description: The purpose of this club will be to provide the opportunity to play baseball at a collegiate level while not requiring the time commitment of varsity athletics. It is also the mission of this club to give back to the community through service projects and working with the City of Raleigh Parks, Recreation, Miracle League of the Triangle, and Cultural Resources Department.
summary: Club Baseball at NC State, competing in the Mid Atlantic South Conference as part of the National Club Baseball Association

name: Club Golf at North Carolina State University
description:
The Club Golf team at NC State is a proud member of the National Collegiate Club Golf Association (NCCGA) and provides opportunities for college students to continue playing at the competitive level! We are proud to say we are current National Champions

NCCGA: http://nccga.com
summary: The Club Golf team at NC State is a proud member of the National Collegiate Club Golf Association (NCCGA) and strives to provide opportunities for college students to continue playing golf at the competitive level!

name: Club Softball at NC State
description: NC State Club Softball offers students the opportunity to play softball competitively while balancing their passion for softball with academics and other interests. Our organization brings together athletes from diverse backgrounds while also strengthening teamwork and sportsmanship skills. Our team organizes practices, scrimmages, and tournaments throughout the year, fostering both skill development and fun competition. We compete against other club softball teams in our conference and around the United States. Each season, our team aims to qualify for the Club Softball Regional tournament and the Club Softball World Series.
summary: Club Softball at NC State is a women's fastpitch organization that competes in the NCSA. Each year we compete for a bid to regionals and the NCSA World Series.

name: Club Swim Team of NC State University
description:
Do you want to make some new friends, stay fit, and have fun? Join North Carolina State University's Club Swim Team! We are a tight-knit group of swimmers that exercise and hang out together. As a team, we practice and compete in meets both at home and at other universities about three times a semester. Most of the meets we attend are opportunities to qualify for Nationals, the biggest meet, and the last meet of the spring semester. We also host social and fundraising events and co-host events with other club sports at NC State. It doesn't matter if you are a year-round swimmer or are just learning how to swim, you have a spot on our team! We practice three times a week on Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday evenings, from 8-9PM. Come check us out at RecFest or the pool!

 

If you would like to register for our email list to get information on the 25-26 season please fill out this interest form: interest form

 

Please email us at swimclub.ncsu@gmail.com or text (704)-770-6433 with any questions you have about joining! 

Visit our Instagram or Swimtopia page to see more updates and events going on with the team!
summary: North Carolina State University's Club Swim Team provides a fun environment for competitive as well as laid back swimming. All levels of swimming are welcome.

name: Club Tennis at NC State
description:
OUR MISSION IS TO CREATE AND DEVELOP A NATIONALLY COMPETITIVE TEAM WHILST PROVIDING EQUAL OPPORTUNITY FOR ALL MEMBERS TO DEVELOP THEIR GAME IN A CONSTRUCTIVE MANNER. THE GOAL OF THE CLUB TENNIS TEAM FIRST AND FOREMOST IS TO DEVELOP A COMPETITIVE TEAM WHICH MAY REPRESENT THE UNIVERSITY AND OUR CLUB IN THE HIGHEST REGARD.

 

General Info:

All dates are for 2025

Recfest August 17th 6:30PM-8:30PM Miller Fields

Interest Meeting #1: Wednesday, August 20th 7:30PM-8:30PM Talley Student Union Room 4270

name: Club Water Polo
description:
To provide members of the NCSU community the opportunity to participate in recreational water polo. In addition, to give students a chance to travel, serve their community, and gain leadership experience, and get involved on campus.

We practice Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday typically from 8:00-10:00 PM at the Carmichael Pool. If interested in the team, please email: waterpoloclub.ncsu@gmail.com or show up to any of our practices. 

We look forward to having you! :)

GO PACK!!
summary: To provide members of the NCSU community the opportunity to participate in recreational water polo. In addition, to give students a chance to travel, serve their community, and gain leadership experience, and get involved on campus.

description:

Follow us on Instagram! @NCSUdems


Join our GroupMe or email ncsudemocrats@gmail.com for more information!

 

College Democrats at NC State is an organization that advocates democratic principles by promoting service and involvement in our community. We aim to provide a safe place for students to share and develop their diverse political beliefs. The NC State College Democrats organization was founded by Governor Jim Hunt. The College Democrats of NC State University are committed to advocating and promoting the Democratic Party at NC State University. Our close proximity to North Carolina Democratic Party Headquarters, along with Raleigh being the Capital of our great state, provide us with a unique advantage to be involved in local, state, and national politics as activists and professionals to bring about the changes this state and country need.

 

For our up-to-date calendar, check out our website, NCSUdems.org.
summary: The College Democrats at NC State promote Democratic values through voter registration, civic engagement, and student involvement. We aim to elect Democrats, inform voters, and provide a space for students to explore and develop their political beliefs.

name: College of Education Graduate Student Advisory Board
description:
The purpose of GSAB is to represent the College of Education as graduate student liaisons between the College of Education’s leadership team and the graduate student body within the College of Education. Made up of graduate student liaisons from each academic department and an executive committee, the Board aims to collaborate in building and maintaining an inclusive community that centers student voices and experiences in order to support graduate students holistically. 

GSAB board members serve the graduate student body of the College of Education by:

  • Advocating for our educational and wellness needs, 
  • Elevating our collective voices and experiences, 
  • Serving as a liaison between students, faculty, and administration,
  • Empowering graduate students to ownership as educational leaders.
summary: The purpose of GSAB is to represent the College as graduate student ambassadors from each academic department throughout the academic year and provide academic and social interactions for graduate students in the College.

name: Information Technology and Business Analytics Club
shortName: IT Club at NC State University
description:
Information Technology and Business Analytics Club provides students interested in information technology with opportunities to network with industry leaders, learn about IT/Business Analytics companies in the region, and develop members into future IT/Business Analytics professionals.

Our typical meetings include presentations, speaking with recruiters, AWS Cloud Practitioner Workshops, company visits, participating in member projects, and resume reviews. Members will have multiple chances to network with professionals from companies such as AWS, RedHat, SAS, ABB , Vaco, etc. 

Membership is free. Students with an interest in technology and/or business analytics are welcome to join, regardless of classification or major.

Mission: To provide students interested in information technology with opportunities to network with industry leaders, learn about IT/Business Analytics companies in the region, and develop members into future IT/Business Analytics professionals.

To view our upcoming events, join our Google Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/u/4?cid=OTliMzQ1YjI0YmZjZDUyYmUwZmVkY2NlNmZiYmU5YjNlZGIxZDhkMGE0MTQ3NDk4ZmU5MDI5OGVkMWRmNmU0NUBncm91cC5jYWxlbmRhci5nb29nbGUuY29t
