name: College Republicans
description:
The College Republicans Club at NC State work to foster and advance the ideals and aims of the Republican Party; furthermore, we work to do all things necessary and incidental to the building and strengthening of the GOP on campus.

 

 

 

https://groupme.com/join_group/99595709/3IOBuBeW

name: TableTop Gamers Club at NC State
description:
We provide a positive, relatively organized atmosphere for enthusiasts of: Pencil and Paper Role-Playing Games (PnP RPGs), War Games, various Collectible Card Games (CCGs), Strategic Board Games, and other Science Fiction/Fantasy games. We wish to bring these games to others in various ways. We also wish to utilize these games as tools to promote critical and creative thinking; problem solving; social interaction; math, reading, and logical skills; friendly competition; and as a social outlet.

 

You can email us at: tabletop-org@ncsu.edu

Feel free to ask about joining the mailing list to keep up to date on meetings.

You can join our discord at: https://discord.gg/YWPGfkWpjy
summary: We provide a positive, relatively organized atmosphere for enthusiasts of: Pencil and Paper Role-Playing Games (PnP RPGs), War Games, various Collectible Card Games (CCGs), Strategic Board Games, and other Science Fiction/Fantasy games.

name: Shepherd's College Ministry
description:
We are a Student Organization Ministry based from a church called The Shepherd's Church just 10 minutes from NC State. We would love to have you be a part of our large group on Sundays, small groups throughout the week, or any of our upcoming events.

Sunday Meetings: We meet every Sunday at 11am at 6200 Holly Springs Rd., Cary, in a house next door to The Shepherd's Church.  We'd love for you to join us for praise and worship followed by a message from God's Word!

C-Groups: Our Community Groups exist to connect each student with a small group of believers for encouragement, growth, and accountability. We focus on finding joy in Christ, giving our lives to serving others, and going into the world to make disciples. Each group meets on a different day and time during the week, depending on work/class schedules. If you would like to join a small group, or simply would like more information, please visit our website: shepherds.org/college/.

For up-to-date information on our events, please visit our Facebook page: "Shepherd's College Ministry," our Instagram: "@shepherdscollegeministry." or check out our Discord server https://discord.gg/UaRudkd7K2
summary: We are a Student Organization Ministry based out of The Shepherd's Church, just 10 minutes from NC State. We would love to have you be a part of our large group on Sundays, small groups throughout the week, or any of our upcoming events.

name: Communication, Rhetoric and Digital Media Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to promote the academic and professional development of graduate students in the CRDM doctoral program and to give graduate students a platform to meet, learn, and share information and knowledge with their peers.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote the academic and professional development of graduate students in the CRDM doctoral program and to give graduate students a platform to meet, learn, and share information and knowledge with their peers.

name: Companion Animal Club
description: The Companion Animal Club is composed of people from all majors and backgrounds who are interested in enhancing their knowledge of companion animals, spending time with people with similar interests, and celebrating the contributions made by pets to the emotional and physical well-being of their owners.
summary: The Companion Animal Club is composed of people from all majors and backgrounds who are interested in enhancing their knowledge of companion animals, spending time with people with similar interests, and celebrating the contributions made by pets.

name: Computer Science Graduate Student Association
description: The CSGSA represents and serves the Graduate Student body of the Computer Science Department at NC State University.
summary: The Computer Science Graduate Student Association represents and serves the Graduate Student body of the Computer Science Department at North Carolina State University.

name: Contra Club at NC State
description:
Contra dancing is a type of folk dance similar to, but in fact older than, square dancing. In a contra dance, couples interact with other pairs in long lines, and eventually, everyone ends up dancing a turn with almost everyone in the line. Because of its unique structure, contra dances are easy to learn, great exercise, and most importantly, really fun. Our organization aims to spread the joy of contra dancing to anyone who comes to our dances, so please come out, experience this wonderful style of dancing, and become a part of the fantastic contra community we have here in the Triangle! Please visit our website for an updated version of our dance schedule or connect with us via Facebook in order to receive information on our events. Hope to see you at one of our dances soon!

 

Website: https://contradanceswithwolves.weebly.com

Contact: ncsucontraclub@gmail.com
summary: Contra Club at NC State (Contra Dances With Wolves) holds monthly contra dances throughout the school year. Open to students and the public!

name: Cricket Club at NC State
description:
The Cricket Club at NC State is dedicated towards promoting the sport in the university as well as around the Triangle. It gives its members an opportunity to enjoy the sport they love by participating year-round in recreational and competitive activities, including the Premier League (40 overs, red leather ball) hosted by the Triangle Cricket League, the format in which our Pack partakes! The NC State Cricket Club has won the following awards in the past:

- Triangle T20 Champions 2011

- Ranked #1 college cricket team in 2010

- Mid- Atlantic Champions 2010

- Triangle T20 Champions 2010

- MACC Champions 2009.

The club plans to participate in the American College Cricket (ACC) tournament, a competitive national collegiate intercollege tournament, starting from next year. We hope to continue the same success in the coming years.

For more inquiries and information about future events, please contact us via email at ncstatecricketclub@gmail.com

We look forward to hearing from you and helping you get involved with cricket at NC State!
summary: We at the Cricket Club look to promote the sport in and around the Triangle.

name: Club Cross Country/Track and Field at NC State
description: Any NC State students interested in running competitively or recreationally are welcome to join the Club Cross Country/Track of NC State. All distances and skill levels are welcomed! Visit our website for more information www.ncsuclubrunning.com
summary: Any NC State students interested in running competitively or recreationally are welcome to join the Club Cross Country/Track of NC State. All distances and skill levels are welcomed! Visit our website for more information www.ncsuclubrunning.com

name: Cru
description:
Everyone is on a spiritual journey and wherever you are on that journey, you are welcomed and accepted. Cru is a safe place where you can ask questions, share doubts, and investigate the God of the Bible. We do not pretend to have all the answers, but we do believe that God created us to know Him personally and life was meant to be lived in relationship with Him.

Cru is also a place for those who have discovered this life-changing relationship and want to grow in spiritual maturity and make a difference for Christ on campus.

No one wants to journey alone. Cru has tons of ways for you to connect to other like-minded friends and experience authentic community. We offer weekly meetings, Bible studies, retreats, social events, intramural sports teams, summer mission opportunities and so many other fun things.
summary: A caring community passionate about connecting others to Jesus Christ.

name: Leadership and Civic Engagement
description: Mission Statement - To provide unique learning experiences that embody the value of leadership, service, responsible citizenship and ethics. Vision Statement - We envision leaders with wisdom, compassion and integrity who will promote a lasting commitment to the betterment of society.
summary: Mission Statement - To provide unique learning experiences that embody the value of leadership, service, responsible citizenship and ethics.

name: CSLEPS - Leadership In Action (LIA)
description: The Leadership In Action (LIA) program serves as the Center for Student Leadership, Ethics & Public Service premier leadership program. Fifty students are invited to participate in the program each year. During the freshman year, students participate in a weekly class to learn about the "Social Change Model of Leadership". Participants in LIA go on a yearly retreat, attend a monthly meeting and complete the Visionary Leadership Certificate and leadership capstone project as a senior. Additionally participants complete several service projects to benefit the Raleigh Community.
summary: The Leadership In Action (LIA) program serves as the Center for Student Leadership, Ethics & Public Service premier leadership program.

name: Cycling Club at NC State
description: The Cycling Club participates in the Atlantic Collegiate Cycling Conference and races in many disciplines including mountain, cyclocross, track, and road. Besides racing, the cycling club encourages cyclists from all backgrounds to participate in weekly organized group rides and skills sessions.
summary: The Cycling Club is a recreational and club race team that participates in the Atlantic Collegiate Cycling Conference and races mountain, cyclocross, track, and road bikes.

name: Dairy Science Club
description:
The purpose of the Dairy Science Club is to promote and stimulate interest in the dairy industry. The club provides educational activities for the students interested in agriculture, promotes scholarship and leadership skills among members, and encourages mutual interest and enthusiasm for the dairy industry among students and faculty.

Please see our full website for complete information
http://ncsudairyscienceclub.my-free.website/.
summary:
Please see our full website for complete information
http://ncsudairyscienceclub.my-free.website/.

name: Dance Marathon @ NC State
description: Dance Marathon @ NC State (RaleighThon) is a nationwide fundraising movement involving college and high school students at more than 300 schools across the country. NC State's RaleighThon benefits the Children's Miracle Network at Duke Children's Hospital. 100% of the funds raised throughout the year are donated directly to support various initiatives including crafts, music therapy, and research opportunities. Students involved in the organization spend a year raising funds and recruiting others for the cause. The year culminates with an 8-hour long event where dancers stay on their feet through dancing, games, and entertainment in honor of the kids who can't. Dancers are also able to interact personally with Miracle Kids at the event. The event ends with the year-long total being raised for everyone to see. Positions are available on our Board of Directors and Managers for students looking to increase their leadership capabilities and contribution to Duke Children's Hospital.
summary: Dance Marathon @ NC State (Raleigh Thon) is a fundraising organization that raises money for children at Duke Children's Hospital. Students will spend the year raising funds, forming connections, and making a significant impact in the community.

name: DanceLife
description:
About Us

DanceLife is a student organization dedicated to providing low-commitment dance classes and performance opportunities to fellow NC State students. Open classes are offered periodically during the fall and spring semesters which include styles such as ballet, tap, jazz, hip-hop, contemporary, and lyrical. Dance classes are taught at an intermediate level, therefore, prior dance experience is a plus, however, anyone is welcome. Students may participate in classes at any time during the semester. The class schedule and other meetings are posted on Instagram. 

 

Performances

We organize and participate in collaborative dance performances with other dance organizations at NC State. In the fall and spring we host the  DanceLife Showcase which gives individuals the opportunity to perform and showcase their choreography. Members of DanceLife have the choice to participate in any dance that fits their schedule. Also, members are encouraged to perform solos, duos, and trios. 

 

Community Outreach

DanceLife is passionate about giving back to the community. Each dance organization involved in Best Dance Crew selects a charity to which they wish to donate. A portion of the proceeds from ticket sales is donated to the charity of the "winner" of the competition. For the Annual DanceLife Showcase, participating DanceLife members choose a charity to which a portion of the proceeds is donated. The rest of the proceeds are used to secure and pay for the venue. 

In the past, we have donated to Stop Hunger Now, St. Judes, AFSP, and Mental Health America.
summary: DanceLife is a student organization dedicated to providing low-commitment dance performance opportunities to fellow students at NC State. Opportunities offered include styles such as ballet, tap, jazz, hip-hop, contemporary, lyrical, modern, etc.

name: Dancing With Wolves Ballroom Dance Club
description:
We provide an opportunity to NC State students, faculty, and staff to learn ballroom dancing. We also travel to competitions along the East Coast and perform in shows throughout the Triangle, as well as provide opportunities for you to dance socially in the Triangle area!

  • Beginner lessons: Wednesday nights 7:00-9:00 pm in the Dance Studio 1206 in Carmichael Gym.  No partner or experience is needed!  Just wear comfortable clothes and socks to dance in.
  • Advanced lessons: Thursdays at 7:00-9:00 pm -  in the downstairs Dance Studio Room 1206.
  • Open Practice Hours: Sundays 2:00-6:00 pm. Downstairs Dance Studio Room 1206.
summary: We provide an opportunity to NC State students, faculty, and staff to learn ballroom dancing. We also participate in competitions, perform locally, and provide opportunities for members to dance socially around the Triangle area!

name: Delta Gamma
description:
Delta Gamma was charted at NC State in 2002, and has grown to become a strong chapter with excellent sisterhood. The members of Delta Gamma build their personal and professional character by involving themselves in our foundation, Service for Sight, and achieving academic excellence. Delta Gamma is sure to provide you with an enriching experience and is a great way to become more involved on NC State's campus. We create strong and empowered women that leave our chapter to make changes in the world!

2024 Recruitment Video
summary: Delta Gamma was founded in 1873 at the Lewis School for Girls in Mississippi. Our Fraternity is based on values, sisterhood, scholarship, philanthropy and a social responsibility to the community. Our philanthropy is Service for Sight.

name: Delta Kappa Epsilon
description: Fraternity with NC State.
summary:
Delta Kappa Epsilon- Delta Rho
Delta Rho was founded in 2016 and has since been involved in all aspects of campus life including sporting events, philanthropy events both on and off campus

name: Delta Sigma Theta
description: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated was founded by twenty-two dynamic women on the campus of Howard University on January 13, 1913. They had a mission to establish an organization that would promote academic achievement and provide the community with assistance to those in need. Delta was founded on three basic principles; scholarship, public service, and sisterhood. The Mu Omicron Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. was the first African American Sorority on NC State's Campus. Since 1975 our chapter has been providing with educational programs, service projects, and other prominent events.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated was founded by twenty-two dynamic women on the campus of Howard University on January 13, 1913.

name: Delta Zeta
description:
We are the Omicron Lambda Chapter of Delta Zeta at North Carolina State University. In Delta Zeta, our purpose is to enrich our members' college experiences and to make our campus and community a better place. Delta Zeta is not a club, but a commitment to live our lives with the highest of values. Delta Zeta membership endures for a lifetime and involves many privileges and obligations. We strive to excel in the areas of academic achievement, campus involvement, community service, leadership development, philanthropy, spiritual growth, and social success.

   Our chapter takes pride in academics, community service, and our philanthropy- Starkey Hearing Foundation. We hold many events to benefit the Starkey Hearing Foundation such as percentage nights at local restaurants and boutiques, Hot Dogs for Hearing, and Hoops for Hearing, our annual basketball tournament.
summary: The women of Delta Zeta Sorority share a common purpose: to make life fuller, more meaningful, and more rewarding.

name: Esports Club at NC State
description:
Esports Club at North Carolina State University will provide an environment for casual and competitive gamers to collaborate and compete against one another and other universities.  We also work with charitable organizations to host informative and fun events during the year.

Make sure to join our official Discord server at https://discord.gg/e6UUpuzSWw 

(If the invite link has any issues, please email an officer and include your Discord username so we can add you, and invite you to the server through Discord itself. Thank you!)
summary: Esports Club at North Carolina State University will provide an environment for casual and competitive gamers to collaborate and compete against one another.  We also work with charitable organizations to host informative and fun events during the year.

name: Earthquake Engineering Research Institute Student Chapter at North Carolina State University
description: The objective of EERI at NC State is to promote the study and practice of earthquake engineering in the university and the community, to strive to solve global earthquake engineering problems in order to protect people and property from the effects of earthquakes, to further the professional development of the students, and to improve inter‐disciplinary communication.
summary: The goal of the organization is to study and practice earthquake engineering in the university and community in order to protect people and property from the effects of earthquakes.

name: Alpha Omega Campus Ministry
description:
We are a diverse group of people who love Jesus and want to build community with those who strive to be more like Him every day. We have weekly Bible discussions, scripture studies, fellowship, and fun activities!

Our Linktree Link! https://linktr.ee/AOtriangle
summary: Love God, Love People, Change the World

name: EKTAA (South Asian Student Association)
description:
EKTAA, which means "UNITY" in Hindi / Urdu, is the South Asian Student Organization at North Carolina State University. The organization was founded in 1970 and since then, EKTAA's membership has grown to over 150 and is still growing. Our motto is "unity within diversity". EKTAA strives to spread culture and heritage of South Asia all over the campus as well as our local community. We work in collaboration with many organizations on and off campus as well as South Asian organizations from area universities.

For more information about events and all please follow our Facebook page and join our mailing list.
summary: EKTAA, which means "UNITY" in Hindi / Urdu, is the South Asian Student Organization at North Carolina State University.

name: Engineers' Council
description:
Who We Are:

  • Operate as the student council for undergraduate students in the College of Engineering
  • Serve as the umbrella organization for all other engineering student organizations by providing mentorship and funding.
  • Represent our peers in the College of Engineering, bridging the gap between students, faculty, and staff.
  • Strengthen both individuals and organizations to succeed during and after their college careers.
  • Enable students to prosper educationally, professionally, and socially.

We are a committee-based organization comprised of 5 central committees and multiple leadership positions:

Academic & Professional Development

Serves as the bridge between NC State faculty, administration, and students. Events include Dinner with the Dean, Faculty Lecture Series, Engineering Town Hall, and the Industry Speaker series. In addition, this committee is responsible for reaching out to engineering students across campus to plan events that help prepare them for their professional and academic careers. Past events include Major Mixer, E-Day Mock Interview events, resume builders, and alumni speakers/networking events.

Engineering Relations (E-Relations)

Responsible for connecting with other engineering organization leaders and advocating for their interests and concerns. Host Engineers’ Week (E-Week) every spring along with facilitating the planning of Welcome Back Bash. Members also create our monthly newsletter, "EngiNews".

Community Engagement

Dedicated to fostering service, leadership, and education within the NC State community. This committee spearheads a diverse range of initiatives, including various fundraisers, charity events, and volunteer opportunities, all aimed at making a positive impact. Beyond these efforts, the committee also plays a pivotal role in spotlighting other campus organizations while simultaneously nurturing the growth of the STEM community. Past events include high school STEM outreach events and engaging in Shack-a-thon. 

Internal Committee

Dedicated to enhancing camaraderie among E-Council members, the Internal Committee orchestrates social gatherings within and beyond our standard meetings. Some of their tasks include working on planning icebreaker activities, social events, and group outings, all designed to foster enrichment and cultivate a strong sense of community.

Publicity Committee

The outward face of the E-Council that helps improve our visibility on campus. Responsible for facilitating social media platforms, websites, recruitment events, advertisement, and public relations.

POP Back Task Force

Plans one of the largest engineering events to help welcome students back to campus in August on the Oval near EBII.
summary: Engineers' Council is the student council for the College of Engineering. As the umbrella organization for undergraduate engineering groups, we plan and execute college events, appropriate funds, and bridge the gap between faculty and students.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description:
The NC State chapter of Engineers Without Borders is a student organization dedicated to building a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs.

Within Engineers Without Borders, we have four different project teams across multiple disciplines. Our project teams are Guatemala Water & Sanitation Systems, Sierra Leone Renewable Energy, Sierra Leone Water, and North Carolina Projects.

For up-to-date information about our chapter, please visit our website: www.ewbncsu.org

If you would like to be added to our email list, please send an email to our President: president@ewbncsu.org
summary: EWB-NCSU is a student organization dedicated to building a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs.

name: English Club
description:
We are NC State University students interested in English (literature, poetry, performative arts, etc.) looking for a connection with students of similar interests. One of the main focuses of this club is to encourage creativity through writing, reading, and learning. We currently hold workshops in which students can submit their stories, poems, etc. for critique by other members. They can learn what works, what doesn't, and how their writing can grow from this experience.

 

If you are interested in learning more or joining English Club, please reach out to Milind Nanda at mnanda2@ncsu.edu! He can answer any questions you may have.
summary: The English Club meets weekly on Tuesdays at 6 p.m. Meetings function as a creative writing workshop for members to take part in and read their peers' stories and have their own read and critiqued. We also attend CHASS events, hold other workshops, etc

name: Entomology Graduate Student Association
description: EGSA is an organization of arthropod enthusiasts and entomologists. We are known for our extensive outreach, education efforts, fundraising items, and passion. All are welcome to join.
summary: EGSA is an organization of arthropod enthusiasts and entomologists. We are known for our extensive outreach, education efforts, fundraising items, and passion. All are welcome to join.

name: Equestrian Dressage Club at NC State
description: The Equestrian Dressage Club is a club committed to the promotion of Dressage and other horse sports. The team competes in Region I of the Intercollegiate Dressage Association (IDA). For more information visit our website, our Facebook page, or contact one of the officers! More information about IDA can be found on their website, www.teamdressage.com
summary: The Equestrian Dressage Club is a club committed to the promotion of Dressage and other horse sports.

name: Equestrian Western Club at NC State
description: The Equestrian-Western Club at NC State University was founded in the Fall of 2008. The team competes through the nation-wide organization, the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association (or IHSA). It is a very strong group of committed riders with a lot of talent who compete in Horsemanship and Reining. We hold tryouts during the fall semester.
summary: The team competes through the nation-wide organization, the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association (or IHSA). Our team is split into 6 divisions based on the members' riding experience.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
description: Eta Kappa Nu is an honor society that recognizes and promotes excellence in electrical and computer engineering. Founded at the University of Illinois at Urbana in 1904, the society includes students, alumni, and professionals who have shown significant academic and professional achievements. The Beta Eta chapter, established in 1938 at North Carolina State University, commits to fostering educational excellence and supporting students. This chapter upholds the society's values of scholarship, leadership, and character, serving both its members and the wider community.
summary: Eta Kappa Nu is a unique membership organization dedicated to encouraging and recognizing excellence in the electrical and computer engineering field.

name: Every Nation Campus Ministry
description:
Every Nation Campus Ministry exists for those who want to know Jesus and grow in lifelong friendships.

Check out our instagram @enc_raleigh to see what's going on this semester!

Here is our GroupMe link: https://groupme.com/join_group/109117295/3OjL87Pj

 

What's Every Nation up to @ NCState!?

Mondays:

Life Group: Talley starting at 8PM every Monday evening!

Contact us via instagram @enc_raleigh or email tyler.osborne@everynation.org if you have any questions about Life Groups!

Wednesdays:

Social and sports events are happening regularly! Check out our instagram @enc_raleigh

Thursdays:

Large Group Weekly Worship Service @ 7:30pm: we meet for worship every Thursday in Talley. Look for our large banner that says "EVERY NATION" as we do move around rooms a bit. Check out our instagram @enc_raleigh

Friday:

We got social events happening in Talley on Fridays. Check our instagram @enc_raleigh to stay up to date!

 

SUNDAY SERVICE

Rides leave from the loop behind Talley (where the spire and outdoor elevator is) @ 9:400 am Sunday mornings. You can check out our church website at www.kingsparkraleigh.com

King's Park Raleigh @ 10:00 am | 201 Tryon Road Raleigh, NC : about a 10 min drive from campus!
summary: Every Nation Campus Ministry is a community of Christian college students in Raleigh who are being loved by Jesus. Through Jesus’ great love for us we are committed to loving God and others through providing a lively community.

name: FarmHouse Fraternity
description:
FarmHouse Fraternity is an international men’s social fraternity founded on April 15, 1905, by seven agricultural students at the University of Missouri. Rooted in agriculture, the fraternity has grown to include 32 active chapters across North America. The name “FarmHouse” serves as an acronym representing the fraternity’s core values: Faith, Ambition, Reverence, Morality, Honesty, Obedience, Unity, Service, and Excellence.

The North Carolina State Chapter was chartered on May 15, 1954, and stands today as one of the longest continuously operating fraternities on campus. Guided by the motto “Builder of Men,” our chapter strives to embody this principle through excellence in academics, leadership, service, philanthropy, and campus involvement.

While our roots are in agriculture, FarmHouse welcomes men from all academic disciplines who exemplify strong character and a commitment to personal growth. We participate in both Fall and Spring Rush through the Interfraternity Council (IFC), and we’re always happy to connect with students interested in learning more about what it means to be a FarmHouse man.
summary: FarmHouse Fraternity, founded in 1905, builds men through leadership, scholarship, and service. The NC State chapter, chartered in 1954, welcomes members from all majors who exemplify strong character and strive for excellence.

name: Food Science Club
description:
The Food Science Club at NC State is a student-based organization with over 30 active members. Meetings will be held on Tuesday nights throughout the year from August to May with occasional and optional in-person activities. These meetings consist of presentations by advisors or food industry representatives followed by a discussion about club business and activities. All students are welcome to attend regardless of their major. The club consists of 10 committees. Members are required to be active on a committee and participate at least once virtually or in person through our outreach or public service committees to be considered an active member. There is a fundraising event involving the sale of our Howling Cow ice cream that will be determined; participation will be virtual or in-person and required for active membership. The proceeds from our fundraiser allow the club to fund professional development opportunities for our members, including scholarships and attendance to meetings such as the Institute of Food Technologists annual meeting. 

The main objectives of the Food Science Club at NC State are to encourage leadership, to allow students to gain a fuller understanding of the scope of food science and technology, to expand awareness of the field of food science within the university and throughout our community, and to develop and maintain a close relationship between the students in the club and the faculty and staff of the NC State Department of Food, Bioprocessing and Nutrition Sciences.
summary: The main objectives of the Food Science Club at NC State are to encourage leadership, to provide members with a fuller understanding of the scope of food science and technology, and to expand awareness of the field of food science within the university.

name: Friends Campus Ministry-Raleigh
description: Friends Campus Ministry exists to strengthen and support the spiritual life of all individuals involved and help build community among fellow Christian Quakers as we worship Jesus Christ our Lord on campus and in the greater Raleigh area.
summary: Friends Campus Ministry exists to strengthen and support the spiritual life of all individuals involved and help build community among fellow Christian Quakers as we worship Jesus Christ our Lord on campus and in the greater Raleigh area.

name: Fulbright Student Association
description: The Fulbright Student Association at NC State is dedicated to representing the goals of the Fulbright Exchange Program and promoting a sense of belonging among fellows and scholars at North Carolina State University. Our mission is to create a supportive network for current and incoming Fulbright scholars, providing a welcoming community while advancing the program’s global objectives. We aim to promote educational exchange, strengthen higher education and research collaborations, and empower members to address local and global challenges through cooperation, mutual understanding, and a shared sense of belonging.
summary: The Fulbright Student Association at NC State is meant to best represent the goals of the Fulbright program. Our mission is to provide a peer support network for current and upcoming Fulbright Students and Scholars in North Carolina.

name: Fusion Dance Crew
description:
Fusion Dance Crew is a multicultural dance crew at NC State University. We perform at university events to uplift and promote other dance teams, styles, and cultures through other on-campus organizations. In addition, we travel and perform off-campus to support other teams and grow our connections within the dance community in and beyond North Carolina. Our team’s individuals are diverse in background, but we all come together to create sets that show our love for dance and each other.

Accomplishments:

* 2023-2024 Club Sport of the Year

* 2024 Silver Well Wolfpack Certified

* 2022-2023 Most Improved Club Sport
summary: We are a performance-based multicultural dance crew at NC State University!

name: Genetics Club
description: The Genetics Club is for anyone who wants to learn more about genetics, research, opportunities on campus, and professionals around the Triangle. All majors are welcome. The Genetics Club meets monthly with meetings including speakers from on and off campus, service projects, lab tours, museum visits, and many other opportunities to explore genetics. Any meeting topics and suggestions are welcome.
summary: The Genetics Club is for anyone who wants to learn more about genetics, new research, opportunities on campus, and professionals around the Triangle. All majors are welcome.

name: Genetics and Genomics Graduate Student Association
description: The Genetics and Genomics Graduate Student Association (G3SA) is the Genetics and Genomics Chapter of the UGSA. The purpose of the G3SA is (1) To disseminate information of interest or importance to the members; (2) To provide a forum for discussion of matters important to the members; and (3) To represent the graduate students of the Genetics and Genomics Program in any and all matters which may affect the welfare of the students. All Genetics and Genomics graduate students are members of the G3SA and serve in a variety of capacities within the organization. The executive offices are the Co-Presidents, Vice President, Secretary, Webmaster, and Treasurer, and the standing committees are the Social Committee, the Student Symposium & Recruitment Committee, the Outreach Committee, the Industry Committee, the Sports Committee, and the T-shirt Committee. The G3SA has representatives that serve as liaisons to the program Academic Committee and Faculty Committee meetings. It also oversees awarding travel funds that support graduate students to travel to conferences. The G3SA also identifies and invites guest speakers to give presentations twice a year. The largest annual event for the G3SA is the Genetic and Genomic Graduate Student Symposium. This is a day-long event where all of the graduate students in the program give a talk about their research.
summary: The purpose of the G3SA is to disseminate information of interest or importance to the members and much more.

name: Geo-Institute Graduate Student Organization at the North Carolina State University
description: The mission of the Student Organization of the Geo-Institute is to be a student-led body that functions to enhance the educational and personal experience of students in the geo-engineering community at the North Carolina State University. The organization will be committed to increasing student awareness of the geotechnical engineering profession as well as the North Carolina State University’s reputation as a leader in graduate geotechnical engineering education. The graduate student organization will work to engage the undergraduate civil engineering population at the North Carolina State University by promoting participation in student activities organized by the Geo-Institute.
summary: The mission of Student Organization of the Geo-Institute is to be a student- led body that functions to enhance the educational and personal experience of students in the geo-engineering community at North Carolina State University.

name: Geology Club @ NC State
description: The mission of our club is to increase knowledge of the Geological sciences, to benefit students in their careers and endeavors in geology and to educate and support the community.
summary: The mission of our club is to promote an increasing knowledge of the Geological sciences, to both benefit students in their careers and endeavors in geology and to educate and support the community.

name: German Honor Society
description:
The chapter of the German Honor Society at NC State is a group of students that have achieved academic excellence in the realm of German Studies. We host several German-themed events, some only for members and others for the public. We also gather bi-weekly for Kaffeeklatsch, an informal German conversation hour. Members of our organization provide free German tutoring for NC State students. 

https://sites.google.com/ncsu.edu/delta-phi-alpha-ncsu/home
summary: We are the local chapter of the national German Honor Society Delta Phi Alpha at NC State University. The society seeks to promote the German language and culture.

name: Grace Christian Life
description: Welcome to Grace, a non-denominational church that meets on campus at NC State. We know that there is a gap between people, God, and one-another. Jesus Christ is the one who can bridge that gap. We are a diverse gathering of college students and young adults who seek to give an accurate picture of Jesus and His church to all people everywhere. We refuse to fall into the trap of complaining about the inconsistencies in the Church. Rather than complain we believe that the model Jesus gave us for Church is attainable and can make the difference in our world. Because of Jesus Christ anyone can be given new life. We want to learn what it means to follow Jesus Christ and see others come alive to His wonder.
summary: We are a non-denominational church that meets on campus at NC State. We facilitate Bible Studies and social events to allow Christian students to grow together and in their relationship with God.

name: Graduate Association for Students in Psychology (GRASP)
description:
Visit our website at: www.ncsugrasp.org

The mission and purpose of this organization is to provide both academic and social support to graduate students in the Department of Psychology at North Carolina State University (henceforth referred to as the Department). Support may take the form of: (a) organizing professional development workshops and seminars, (b) organizing social events for students in the Department, (c) fundraising and/or lobbying for funds to assist students' attendance at professional/career development events, (d) facilitating communication between faculty, staff, and students, and (e) responding to the needs of students in the Department as they occur over time.
summary: The mission and purpose of this organization is to provide both academic and social support to graduate students in the Department of Psychology at North Carolina State University.

name: Graduate Association of Public Administration
description:
The Graduate Association of Public Administration (GAPA) sponsors professional, social, and community service activities during the academic year. GAPA offers students opportunities to discuss issues surrounding public administration and provide faculty with input about the PhD and MPA programs.

GAPA also encourages networking between public administration practitioners, faculty, alumni, and other students through various events. Every student in the PA program is automatically a member of GAPA and our meetings are open to all members. If you have any questions or concerns about GAPA or PA-related issues, please do not hesitate to send an e-mail to any of the GAPA officers.
summary: The Graduate Association of Public Administration (GAPA) sponsors professional, social, and community service activities during the academic year. GAPA offers students networking opportunities to discuss issues surrounding public administration.

name: Grains of Time
description:
The Grains of Time are NC State’s premier all-male a cappella group on campus. Founded in 1968, they are also proud to be the oldest all-male collegiate a cappella group in North Carolina. The Grains love to be a part of upholding the rich history and longstanding traditions of the group, while also pushing new creative boundaries as a student-led organization with their song choices, arrangements, and choreography.

The Grains of Time often get invited to showcase their talent and perform at university events, gigs in the greater Raleigh area, as well as with other collegiate a cappella groups across the state. The Grains also put together their most impressive arrangements and choreography to compete at the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella, recently advancing to the ICCA South region Semifinal in 2023. The group has even taken their talents abroad, performing a European tour and competing in Japan. 

The Grains also put on a fall and spring concert each year at Stewart Theatre in Talley Student Union. These guys are incredibly tight-knit, and pride themselves on their connection outside of rehearsal and performances, always finding time to hang out throughout the week, as well as travelling together on their annual fall and spring retreats.

The Grains also love to record and release music – check us out on all streaming platforms and social media! 

If you have any interest in joining The Grains of Time, make sure to drop into a cappella auditions in September – more information on our social media.
summary: Grains of Time is NC State's premier male a cappella group. The purpose of this organization is to provide a cappella singing for university and community functions.

name: Greater Good Textile Group
description: Greater Good Textile Group (GGTG) is a campus organization dedicated to spreading the awareness of sustainability in the textile industry while also being a supporting advocate for the ethical and humane treatment of industry workers. We aim to do this through the management of several service, educational, and campus-wide projects.
summary: Student advocates for ethical fashion through fair labor practices, sustainability, and community outreach projects.

name: Club Gymnastics
description: Club Gymnastics at NC State is both a competitive and non-competitive club open to all skill levels. The competitive season is during the Spring semester. Competing is not mandatory, although all members are welcome to compete. We practice three times a week on Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Sundays; attendance is not mandatory. Practice is a time to work on old skills, new skills, or socialize with others in a fun, stress-free environment. Our club motto, taken from the NAIGC, is "For the love of the sport." Club Gymnastics is all about having fun and staying healthy within a judgement-free zone.
summary: Club Gymnastics at NC State is both a competitive and non-competitive club open to all skill levels. Competition season is during Spring semester. We practice three times a week on Sundays, Tuesdays, and Wednesdays; attendance is not mandatory.

name: Herpetology Club at NC State
description: NC State students united with common interests in reptile and amphibian research and educating the public on their environmental significance.
summary: The Herpetology Club at North Carolina State University is a group for those who have a common interest in reptiles and amphibians and their research.

name: Higher Education Masters Association
description: HEMA will serve as a catalyst for the development of scholarly leaders within the higher education community by providing communication, outreach, and programming. HEMA will also serve as the voice of students in the Higher Education Master's program at North Carolina State University.
summary: The purpose of the association shall be to enhance the development of the HEMA student body by facilitating communication among the students, departmental faculty, and staff, and North Carolina State University.

name: Hillel @ NC State
description:
Are you Jewish or interested in Jewish culture?  Come join us at Hillel @ NC State!  Located on Hillsborough Street, our Hillel Center is the home of lots of great cultural, religious/spiritual, and social events. 

Hillel: The Foundation for Jewish Campus Life is the largest Jewish campus organization in the world. The organization serves students at more than 550 colleges and communities throughout North America and globally. Hillel helps students expand Jewish knowledge, hone leadership skills, volunteer in social justice work, and bolster ties to Israel. Hillel seeks to engage every Jewish student on campus, from those with strong Jewish backgrounds to those with none at all. Hillel today encourages students to take ownership of their Jewish experience and define Judaism in their own way. The destination of their Jewish journey is up to the student, and Hillel provides the resources.
summary: Hillel: The Foundation for Jewish Campus Life is the largest Jewish campus organization in the world.

name: Hindi-Urdu Club at North Carolina State University
description: We are a club open to all who are interested in the unique language of Hindi and/or Urdu. Whether you’re a new learner, a native speaker, or a curious newcomer- join us to celebrate and explore the beauty of Hindi-Urdu and its surrounding culture. Events include Bollywood movie nights, trivia, karaoke, and more!
summary: The purpose of this club will be to create a Hindi-Urdu atmosphere on campus through South Asian gatherings, meetings, and cultural events and to create a bond between the diverse students by giving them a flavor of South Asian culture .

name: Horticultural Science Graduate Student Association
description:
Organization to support the graduate students of Horticultural Science.

The objectives of this organization shall be to:

  1.  Provide members an opportunity to organize meetings, seminars, field trips, and other functions to broaden and stimulate professional interests of the members.
  1.  To represent the members’ interests: a) in Departmental affairs, b) in University affairs through representation in the Graduate Student Association, hereafter referred to as GSA.

 

Mason Pharr Seminar

Annual interdisciplinary seminar hosted by HSGSA and another graduate department association (changes each year).

The Horticultural Science Graduate Student Association ("Association") and Department of Horticultural Science (''Department") hereby establish a permanent endowment in the North Carolina Agricultural Foundation, Inc., to honor and recognize the professional research and teaching accomplishments of Dr. David Mason Pharr, William Neal Reynolds Distinguished Professor.  Dr. Pharr held a faculty position at NC State University for 35 years.  His work was in the area of graduate education and research.

The primary focus of his research program was the area of plant carbohydrate metabolism with emphasis on the roles of simple carbohydrates in quality and stress tolerance in plants. Dr. Pharr's work involved collaborations in the United States, as well as in the countries of lsrael, Switzerland, Japan, Finland, and Italy.

Dr. Pharr taught graduate level courses throughout his career, and in 1995 he was appointed a Distinguished Graduate Professor by the NC State Alumni Society and was named a W. N. Reynolds Professor of Horticultural Science in 2000.  He was also recognized by the American Society for Horticultural Science with the Outstanding Researcher Award and the Graduate Educator Award.  He was the only member of the society (at that time) to receive both career awards.

The Mason Pharr Seminar was established by the Horticultural Science Graduate Student Association in December 2005, and this endowment will continue and honor Dr. Pharr's legacy in Graduate Science studies.
summary: This organization aids in the professional development, support, as well as intellectual growth and development of horticultural science graduate students.

name: Human Factors & Ergonomics Student Chapter at NC State
description:
The HFES NCSU Student Chapter emphasizes close contact between faculty and students, with an emphasis on research training gained via apprenticeship relationships. There are usually about 20 students in the chapter. Many affiliated faculty members are drawn from various disciplinary areas and other departmental programs and participate in HFES events such as brown bags, job talks, and social events to discuss research. 

In the Human Factors and Applied Cognition Program, there is an emphasis on cognitive engineering/HF in systems design, industrial ergonomics/biomechanics, and occupational safety and health. Students gain breadth of knowledge of physical ergonomics, understanding of approaches to usability evaluation and interface design for human-machine systems, and methods for recognition, evaluation and control of safety hazards in complex task environments. Goal is broad preparation. Courses emphasize contemporary theory, concepts and principles; observational methodologies and research techniques; and systems approach to analysis, design and modeling. Additional courses may be taken in variety of fields, including biomedical engineering, computer science, epidemiology, industrial hygiene, psychology, and public health. (Several courses through ISE program are cross-listed with Psychology.) For superior students, direct-track PhD program exists (MS degree must be completed along the way). Being a member of the HFES organization increases knowledge of these skills, provides opportunities for connections, and enhances the overall research experience. 

NCSU is recognized as one of the schools in the United States to have a Human Factors graduate program and providing these students with an organization to explore opportunities and connections is something that HFES strives to achieve. 

 

We are NCSU HFES!
summary: The mission of the  Human Factors and Ergonomics Student Chapter is to promote the study and application of human factors and ergonomics at NC State and in the surrounding community through educational and networking activities.

name: Hunt Seat Equestrian Club at North Carolina State University
shortName: Hunt Seat Equestrian Club at NC State
description: The Hunt Seat Equestrian Club at NC State is a competitive team that competes locally and nationally at IHSA sanctioned horse shows. Our team is made up of riders from all skill levels. You will learn from riding with our team that the walk/trot rider is just as important as the rider who has done it all. Apart from the horse shows, team and club members take part in weekly lessons, fundraising events, community service, and club socials. Team members are required to take weekly lessons from our coach in preparation for the horse shows through the year.
summary: The Hunt Seat Equestrian Club at NC State is a competitive team that competes locally and nationally at IHSA sanctioned horse shows.

name: Impact Leadership Village
description: The IMPACT Leadership Village develops emerging leaders by providing students with the opportunity to learn about leadership models, organizational development, ethics in leadership, and global leadership. The Village helps students identify and develop their leadership strengths and provides participants with the opportunity to engage in practical application of leadership to real world issues. There have been none before us, we are IMPACT! An impacting body of determined students who are passionate about leadership, service, and creating positive change.
summary: The IMPACT Leadership Village develops emerging leaders by providing students with the opportunity to learn about leadership models, organizational development, ethics in leadership, and global leadership.

name: ISE Graduate Student Association at NC State
description:
ISE GSA represents the graduate student body of the Edward P. Fitts Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering and serves as an intermediary between the department and its graduate students for any and all concerns. Our mission includes:

1. To represent the graduate student body of the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering.

2. To give the graduate students an open forum to discuss their concerns with the department.

3. To disseminate information of interest or importance to its member students. 

4. To promote and foster unity among the graduate students through seminars, social gatherings, and other activities.

 

The membership of the ISE GSA consists of all graduate students enrolled in the Industrial and Systems Engineering department at North Carolina State University.
summary: The ISE GSA is for all graduate students in the Edward P. Fitts Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering. The organization supports the success of each student by enhancing community through social events and as a liaison to the dept. faculty.

name: HackPack
description:
Our goal is to cultivate a friendly environment where students can learn and practice security concepts judgement-free. We provide learning resources, host weekly practice challenges, and compete in monthly security Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions.

Discord

We use Discord to coordinate and socialize!

You can find a link to our Discord at: https://hackpack.club/
summary: Our goal is to cultivate a friendly environment where students can learn and practice security concepts judgement-free. We provide learning resources, host weekly practice challenges, and compete in monthly security Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
description:
IEEE at NC State Student Branch

Official Chapter Page: IEEE at NC State - Dive into the official NC State student chapter of IEEE and join of our vibrant community. EST. 1947

Social Media & Platforms:

  • Instagram: NC State IEEE Student Branch (@ieeencstate) - Browse through our event snapshots and stay updated with upcoming promotions.
  • Discord: Join Our Server - Network, collaborate, and chat with fellow IEEE student members.
  • Linktree: ieeatncstate on Linktree - Discover all our platforms and stay connected.

Our Mission: IEEE stands as the globe's premier professional and academic organization dedicated to all students within the College of Engineering. Our mission is to propel technology forward for the betterment of humanity. As the NC State student chapter of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), we are committed to:

  • Engaging students and building lasting relationships with industry leaders.
  • Offering students avenues to the latest industry insights through workshops, technical discussions, robotics and diverse events.
  • Bridging the gap between the student community and industry experts, fostering innovation that will ultimately enrich society.

Join us in our journey to make a difference!
summary: Student Chapter of the Institute of Electrical and Electronic Engineers ( IEEE ) at NC State University.

name: Institute of Industrial & Systems Engineers
description:
We are a student organization for prospective or current students in Industrial & Systems Engineering; any other majors are always welcome. We typically meet 1-2 times per month and meetings may consist of company visits, plant tours, social events, or professional development.

The objectives of the Chapter shall be to promote the profession of Industrial & Systems Engineering through organized effort in study, research and discussion of the fields of Industrial & Systems Engineering and the dissemination of knowledge thereby gained. The Chapter shall promote membership in IISE by acquainting the student body with the ideas, purposes and objectives of IISE.

GET ON OUR EMAIL LIST WITH THIS LINK: https://forms.gle/MAywR9eymTpNnGpy5

ACCESS ALL OUR RESOURCES: https://linktr.ee/iise_ncsu
summary: We are a student organization for prospective or current students in Industrial & Systems Engineering. The Chapter shall promote membership in IISE by acquainting the student body with the ideas, purposes and objectives of IISE.

name: Institute of Management Accountants
description: The student version of the professional Institute of Managerial Accountants, comprised of about 60,000 professionals, striving to provide an opportunity for students to learn from managerial accountants and other professionals in corresponding fields. We also encourage our members to join the professional chapter and attend its meetings to further their develop skills and network. The professional IMA provides a dynamic forum for management accounting and financial professionals to develop and advance their careers through its Certified Management Accountant (CMA) program, cutting-edge professional research and practice development education, networking, and the advocacy of the highest ethical and professional practices.
summary: The student version of the professional Institute of Managerial Accountants, comprised of about 60,000 professionals, striving to provide an opportunity for students to learn from managerial accountants and other professionals in corresponding fields.

name: Institute of Transportation Engineers,  American Society of Highway Engineers, and American Railway Engineering and Maintenance-of-Way Association at NC State
description: ITE/ASHE/AREMA was created to help develop the potential of young transportation professionals and show how transportation engineers function in the real world. There are monthly meetings of the student chapter to expose students to a variety of topics in the transportation field. ITE is a good foundation upon which to build a source of contacts that will be of immeasurable value after graduation. Student members attend state and national meetings and meet a good number of nationally known transportation engineers. In addition, the student chapter runs one of the statewide ITE meetings. Students also have many opportunities to connect through social and volunteer events throughout the year.
summary: Created to help develop the potential of young transportation professionals and show how transportation engineers function in the real world.

name: Inter-Residence Council
description: The Inter-Residence Council is comprised of the 12 Residential Councils that collectively represent the over 10,000 residential students at NC State University, with the purpose of improving student life on campus and creating a sense of community.
summary: The Inter-Residence Council is comprised of the 12 Hall and Area Councils that collectively represent the 10,000 residential students at NC State University, with the purpose of improving student life on campus and creating a sense of community.

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide peer governance and leadership to 21 men's fraternities on our campus, who have applied and been admitted to the IFC. Further, the IFC serves to foster and promote values of scholarship, service, philanthropy, and leadership within not only our community, but beyond. The IFC serves as a body to hold members accountable, and to help guide the fraternal experience on our campus.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a vital and articulate instrument for member Fraternities in order to secure harmony in Inter-Fraternity relationships, to promote the welfare of students within the individual fraternities.

name: International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering
description:
ISPE is an active and thriving society of students and professionals alike who are interested in the biotechnology and pharmaceutical industry. The name has traditionally stood for International Society of Pharmaceutical Engineers, but that has changed just as much as the industry of people who represent it has! This is because ISPE is made up of all backgrounds of people with high levels of involvement from majors such as Chemical, Biomedical, Industrial, or Biological Engineering, Biochemistry, Bioprocessing Science, Biology, Microbiology, Business Administration and more! The NCSU Chapter is one of the largest student chapters in the country and by far the largest in the south. Our main goal is to build your network, introduce you to the leading professionals in the area, and land you an internship, co-op, or full time position in the thriving biotechnology industry while providing a fun and inviting club atmosphere. ISPE offers around 2-3 general meetings a month in addition to 1-2 workshops each semester, company tours, service events and social events both within the club and with other student organizations on campus. We bring in professionals from companies around the area to speak and network with YOU about what they do. Free food at all meetings doesn't hurt either, right? If you are looking to get some leadership experience, we are the people to come to. We have a diverse executive board and are always welcoming of more positions and ways to get involved and contribute.

This past 2020-2021 school year, ISPE held meetings with well known biotech/pharmaceutical organizations and organizations associated with biotechnology and biopharmaceutical companies including Pfizer, Novartis Gene Therapies, and Seqirus as well as representatives from IPS, KBI, and CAI. In addition to these great company events, ISPE typically participates in community service projects throughout the year, which in the past have included book drives, food drives, and serving ice cream with the NCSU Food Science Club Dairy Bar at the State Fair. Past social events include game nights, movie watch socials, and an End-of-the-Year Field Day with the American Institute for Chemical Engineers. For professional development, we sometimes host workshop meetings that focus on things like resumes and interview tips. Finally, ISPE members are encouraged to attend the BTEC career fair featuring many local companies such as Biogen, Seqirus (formerly Novartis), Grifols, CAI, NNE PharmaPlan, IPS, Novozymes, and Novo Nordisk.
summary: ISPE is a professional organization that aims to bring students with an interest or desire in the pharmaceutical industry with professionals in the industry through guest speaker meetings, Q&A panels, career events, resume workshops, and social events.

name: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship at NC State
description:
InterVarsity Christian Fellowship at NC State's mission is to help students grow in their love for God, love for God's word, love for people of every ethnicity and culture, and love for God's purposes in the world.

Come meet us in Talley every Thursday night at 7:00 for our weekly community night to study God's word, meet and engage with other students, and connect with one of our small groups. You can also connect with us through our Instagram @ncstate_iv
summary: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship at NC State seeks to be a diverse witnessing community of Christians and non-Christians exploring Jesus' relevancy in our lives and on campus.

name: Iranian Students Association
description: ISA is a volunteer student organization which sponsors Iranian social and cultural activities and events at NC State University. ISA aims to promote an understanding of Iranian culture, to help foster friendship among different cultural groups. It tries to provide a source of union and support for the Iranian community at NC State University and to involve the Iranian student body in organization events, emphasizing celebrations and advancements for the culture and society of the Iranian people.
summary: ISA is a volunteer student organization which sponsors Iranian social and cultural activities and events at NC State University.

name: Japan Club
description:
The Japan Club at NCSU commits itself to making information on all aspects of Japanese Culture available to the general student body, faculty and staff of NCSU. The Japan Club also exists to:

  • Promote knowledge of Japanese language, culture, and customs.
  • Work with the North Carolina Japan Center to provide information about study abroad opportunities.
  • Make students aware of resources and opportunities related to Japanese culture/language both on and off campus.

Event announcements posted regularly on:
Discord & Instagram

Click this text to join our discord!
summary:
The Japan Club at NCSU commits itself to making information on all aspects of Japanese Culture available to the general student body, faculty and staff of NCSU. 
Learn about Japanese holidays, food, or games! Sharpen your Japanese! Or make new friends!!

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description:
The members of Kappa Alpha Order pride themselves on a tradition of chivalry and of the values of the gentleman. This is reminiscent of a romanticized version of the Knights of the Crusades. This all culminates into a modern college fraternal experience. A member of Kappa Alpha Order strives to offer reverence to both God and women, as described in the motto. Further, the idea that "Excellence is our Aim" further emboldens members to always apply themselves to self-improvement, realizing that you should always continue to strive for excellence. Finally, being that the organization is an Order, members typically have an idea of the above values before joining. Kappa Alpha does not intend to change anyone, rather bond together those men who have these similar values so that they may experience brotherhood, and pursue improvement, with one another.

 

Status: Disciplinary Probation through August 01, 2025. Violations: FSL Social Events Policy
summary: The members of Kappa Alpha Order pride themselves on a tradition of chivalry and of the values of the gentleman.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc.
description: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. was founded on the campus of Indiana University on January 5, 1911. The Fraternity's fundamental purpose is achievement. Fashioning achievement as it's purpose, Kappa Alpha Psi began uniting college men of culture, patriotism and honor in a bond of fraternity.
summary: Striving For Achievement In Every Field of Human Endeavor.

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description:
Kappa Alpha Theta, the first Greek-letter fraternity known among women, was founded in 1870 at DePauw University in Indiana by Bettie Locke Hamilton, Alice Allen Brant, Bettie Tipton Lindsey, and Hannah Fitch Shaw. Theta is now over 170 chapters strong with houses all across the United States, as well as in Canada. Over 170,000 women have been initiated into Kappa Alpha Theta. Notable alumnae include Laura Bush, Sheryl Crow, Tory Burch, Amy Grant, and more.

Membership in Kappa Alpha Theta leads women into a life of friends, experiences, and goals that they might not have had otherwise. Being a Theta leads a woman to personal excellence, offering her ways to become her best self. Our ritual, in fact, commands us to be nobler women, to lead ourselves to personal excellence and to lead others there as well. It commands us to set the bar high. And when Thetas model personal excellence, we not only have the chance to lead on our campuses, in our communities, and in our professions, but we also have the chance to effect real change - just as our founders Bettie, Hannah, Alice, and Bettie did.
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta was founded in 1870 at DePauw University, and is the first Greek-letter fraternity known among women. Membership in Kappa Alpha Theta offers lifelong opportunities for social, intellectual, and moral growth.

name: Kappa Delta Sorority (Eta Upsilon Chapter)
description:
Welcome to Kappa Delta! Our chapter is part of a national sisterhood with more than 230,000 members and chapters across the country. Kappa Delta inspires its members to reach their potential by offering opportunities for leadership, personal development, academic success and community service, all in the context of true friendship. Eta Upsilon chapter of Kappa Delta is home to 150 wonderful women who have helped us develop into a chapter that thrives on sisterhood, since our colonization in 2010.

"Let us strive for that which is honorable, beautiful and highest."
-Kappa Delta's Open Motto 

As Kappa Deltas, we strive each day to live up to the words of our open motto through academic excellence, leadership, philanthropic involvement, social success and our membership to the sorority.  On NC State’s campus, our chapter participates in Student government, Greek Life philanthropic events, and numerous other campus organizations. We take pride in academics, leadership, community service, and our own philanthropic events.  We host an annual Shamrock event which raises money for Prevent Child Abuse America. In addition, we host monthly Girl Scout events in which we inspire confidence and serve as great mentors and leaders to the future generation of women.
summary: The Eta Upsilon chapter of Kappa Delta joined Panhellenic's community in November 2010. We are committed to building confidence and inspiring action, as well as upholding our open motto, "Let us strive for that which is honorable, beautiful and highest."

name: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc
description: On November 10th, 2012, the Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority Incorporated at North Carolina State University was successfully chartered by eleven hardworking women: Quynh Tran, Crystal Tsang, Nancy Xiong, Janet Nguyen, Rosie Dang, Diana Do, Wendy Yan, Christina Norton, Dorothy Dai, Jennifer Jung, and Denise Suter. Bonded by a common goal of establishing an organization that brings together cultural awareness with the potential for nurturing and developing successful leaders, these eleven women brought to their school a Sisterhood that focuses on service, cultural diversity, and academic excellence, making them the first and largest Asian-American interest Sorority on campus.
summary: This Asian-American Interest Sorority brings together cultural awareness with the potential for nurturing and developing successful leaders. This is a Sisterhood that focuses on service, cultural diversity, and academic excellence.

name: Korean Student Association
description:
Over our 40-year history, the NCSU KSA (Korean Student Association) remained to the founding mission of serving as point-of-contact for hundreds of incoming and current NCSU graduate students, faculty members, post doctorates, alumni, etc. We strive to promote stronger relationships with neighboring institutes (UNC Chapel Hills, Duke, etc.) as well as to create professional network relationships with local/foreign industries, academia, and many other opportunities. Our door is always open towards any Korean/Korean-related people who are seeking for any help and opportunities. We, NCSU KSA, will do our best to help and assist our community.

 

KSA Mail Subscribe

OpenKakaoTalk
summary: The KSA is always trying to reach out to all the people that have to do with NCSU and/or Korea.

name: Lacrosse Club at North Carolina State University
description: A student led organization that participates in the Atlantic Lacrosse Conference as a part of the MCLA.
summary: A student led organization that participates in the Atlantic Lacrosse Conference as a part of the MCLA.

name: Ladies in Red
description:
The Ladies in Red are NC State's premier all-women and non-binary A Cappella group, established in 1993. The Ladies are a close-knit group of friends, made up of diverse women with different majors and musical backgrounds, that share a strong passion for singing. The group is entirely student-led with support from the NC State Performing Arts and Technology Department. The Ladies arrange and sing a variety of genres such as pop, rock, indie, alternative, country, soul, R&B, etc.

 

They work hard and have fun showcasing their talents for NC State events, the Triangle area, and beyond! Some favorite performance opportunities include invitationals at other universities, the ICCA competition, holiday gigs, the NC State commencement ceremony, and so much more. The Ladies also collaborate with a local professional a cappella recording studio to put out singles and albums on streaming services for all to enjoy! Ladies in Red put on a fall and spring concert in Stewart Theatre each year (as well as go on a fall and spring retreat).

 

If interested in joining, drop-in anytime 10am-4pm September 2nd for a cappella auditions (Price Music Center, room 120)! Prepare a verse and chorus of a song that showcases you and your voice best, do some one-on-one pitch matching and range testing, and look for an email that night about callback times the following day (September 3rd). Hope to see you there!

 

Check out a performance from the Ladies in Red Fall '22 concert "Lady Like" :) here
summary: The Ladies in Red are NC State's premier all-women A Cappella group, established in 1993. The Ladies work to empower and inspire others through song. The group is student-led with support from the NC State Performing Arts and Technology Department.

name: Lambda Chi Alpha Fraternity
description:
A values-based fraternity whose mission is to improve the lives of its members and the community that surrounds them.

https://www.ncsulxa.com/

 

Status: Disciplinary Probation through December 12, 2025. Violations: Hazing & Failure to Comply
summary: A values-based fraternity whose mission is to improve the lives of its members and the community that surrounds them.

name: Lambda Pi Eta
description: Lambda Pi Eta is the academic honor society for undergraduate students in the Department of Communication, sponsored by the National Communication Association. Our focus is on academic excellence and scholarship within the field of communication. Founded in 1985 at the University of Arkansas, the Greek letters Lambda, Pi, and Eta were chosen for the organization's name to represent the three points of Aristotle's rhetorical triangle: logos, pathos, and ethos. The NC State chapter of LPH is the Beta Chapter, or the second to be founded. There are now more than 300 chapters at universities across the United States.
summary: Lambda Pi Eta is the academic honor society for undergraduate students in the Department of Communication, sponsored by the National Communication Association.

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc
description: The year was 1975. 17 lovely ladies envisioned an organization that would one day empower all women.  A vision that began with the simple idea of unifying Latinas on their campus.  An idea that would cater to the needs of these women as they are making their way through seemly endless labyrinth we know be college. This vision manifested itself as Lambda Theta Alpha, the 1st Latin Sorority in the Nation. Over 40 years later, Lambda Theta Alpha continues to thrive as one of the largest traditionally Latin based Sorority in the nation. Not catering only to Latinas, but to all women regardless of their race, creed or sexual orientation. LTA is Latin by Tradition, Not Definition. We are scholars who take pride in giving back to our communities as well as promoting unity through charitable and educational programs. We are campus leaders who provide and participate in political, social, and cultural activities. Most importantly, we are sisters of an organization that changes lives. On behalf of the Epsilon Nu Chapter of Lambda Theta Alpha at NC State, we would like to thank you for taking the time to learn a little more about our remarkable organization.
summary: Lambda Theta Alpha is a sisterhood based on Unity, Love, and Respect. An organization with a mission to develop strong leaders who will provide and practice political, social, and cultural activities, while maintaining a higher standard of learning.

name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc.
description: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. is a national fraternity with chapters across the United States promoting education, leadership, community service and history. Lambda Theta Phi was the First Latino Greek Letter Organization in the US, founded in 1975 with the intent of establishing brotherhood and unity among the Latinx community. The Gamma Sigma chapter of Lambda Theta Phi at NC State seeks to continue the organization's missions and objectives and further expand Latinx cultural awareness in the Raleigh/North Carolina region. "Progress and empowerment of our people" is the ultimate goal of Lambda Theta Phi and the Gamma Sigma chapter works towards this goal. <https://youtu.be/7U_0MUMVF78>.
summary: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. is a national fraternity with chapters across the US promoting education, leadership, community service and history.

name: Order of Omega - Lambda Upsilon Chapter
description: Order of Omega is a leadership honor society for members of Greek organizations. Order of Omega recognizes juniors and seniors who have exemplified high standards in the areas of scholarship, leadership, involvement within their respective organization and within the Greek, campus, and local community. Members are selected from the top 3% of students at each institution. To date, over 500 chapters have been chartered throughout North America, each sharing the common goal of recognizing the many outstanding student leaders at their institution. The Lambda Upsilon Chapter was of the Order of Omega was chartered at NC State University in 1991. The purpose of The Order of Omega shall be threefold: First, to recognize fraternity men and women who have attained a high standard of leadership in inter-Greek activities, to encourage them to continue along this line, and to inspire others to strive for similar conspicuous attainment. Second, to bring together the most representative fraternity and sorority members, and to create an organization which will help to mold the sentiment of the institution on questions of local and intercollegiate affairs. Third, to bring together members of the faculty, alumni, and student members of the institution's fraternities and sororities on a basis of mutual interest, understanding, and helpfulness.
summary: Order of Omega recognizes juniors and seniors who have exemplified high standards in the areas of scholarship, leadership, involvement within their respective organization and within the Greek, campus, and local community.

name: Latin American Student Association
