description: The Latin American Student Association is a student body organized within the rules and regulations of North Carolina State University. The Association promotes the professional development for NCSU students and the interaction and coexistence of people from the five continents who share an interest for Latin America. LASA organizes academic and social events in order to disseminate aspects of Latin American life, culture, tradition and values while enhancing the interaction between the student community members. Attendance to LASA events is open to all persons and not restricted to members of the community.
summary: The Association promotes the professional development for NCSU students and the interaction and coexistence of people from the five continents who share an interest for Latin America.

name: Latinas Promoviendo Comunidad/Lambda Pi Chi Sorority, Inc.
description:
Las Hermanas of Rho Chapter at North Carolina State University would like to extend a warm welcome to you and thank you for visiting our chapter page. We are Latine based, but not exclusive to any background in our organization. Latinas Promoviendo Comunidad/Lambda Pi Chi Sorority, Inc. strives to be a vehicle for the promotion of the Latine community and culture. Through our website, we hope that you will gain a sense of how we have accomplished this on our campus and how the Hermandad we share furthers our motivation to uplift our community, as well as advance ourselves personally and professionally.

You may see more information about us and what we stand for on our Chapter website, https://rho0chapter3.my.canva.site/rho-chapter-website
summary: Our mission is to empower womxn by providing a supportive network dedicated to their personal and professional advancement. Our Hermandad is further advanced by our shared dedication and promotion of public service and cultural awareness.

name: Le Cercle Français
description:
Le Cercle Français is a student-run organization seeking to expose students to French culture worldwide through various interactive ways, including movies, games, culture, cuisine, art, history, francophone countries and many more! In the past, we have had cheese tastings, movie days, and other fun meetings, so come join and we promise you won't be disappointed!

lcfncsu@gmail.com
summary: The French Club at NCSU aims to further acquaint students with the French language and culture and to grow the francophone community on campus.

name: League of Legends Collegiate Club
description:
The League of Legends Collegiate Club is a place for fans of the most popular online game in the world to play and discuss the game, League of Legends, with fellow NCSU student fans. Having a player base of over 40 million players, there will be plenty of people to meet and play with on campus. There will be casual and competitive events held for every type of player to enjoy themselves and meet other players with a focus on building an ever-expanding inclusive community of players and friends.

Please contact us through getinvolved and for events and club updates, please visit our Instagram page linked below and join our discord server.

 

Discord: https://discord.gg/guUg57D

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/lolpackncsu

Email: lolpackncsu@gmail.com
summary: The League of Legends Collegiate Club is an inclusive place for fans of the most popular MOBA (multiplayer online battle arena) in the world to play League of Legends with fellow NCSU student fans.

name: Leopold Wildlife Club
description: The Leopold Wildlife Club is the student chapter of The Wildlife Society at North Carolina State University.  We are dedicated to providing opportunities for professional development, networking, extracurricular training, and social events for students interested looking for careers in the wildlife field.
summary: Dedicated to wildlife conservation and exposing students to wildlife professionals as well as career development opportunities.

name: Linux Users Group (LUG)
description: [ We don't use GetInvolved- see us at https://lug.ncsu.edu ] We are the Linux Users Group at North Carolina State University, created for the purpose of promoting and supporting the use of Linux and related Free and Open Source programs within the NC State campus community. This includes activities such as informational talks, installfests, and open forums.
summary: We are the Linux Users Group at North Carolina State University, created for the purpose of promoting and supporting the use of Linux and related Free and Open Source programs within the NC State campus community.

name: MAITRI - Indian Graduate Student Association
description:
MAITRI is the official Indian Graduate Student Association at NC State University. Our goal is to provide a sense of community for the Indian graduate students who fly to NC State, and share our culture and traditions with everyone else interested in experiencing a bit of India!

We host events throughout the year - including for festivals like Diwali: the festival of lights and Holi: the festival of colors. In Spring 2023, we had over 700 people attend our Holi event held in collaboration with EKTAA (South Asian Student Association) and the GSA (Graduate Student Association), and we hope to carry on the spirit of collaboration. We want to make sure you aren't missing home during these yearly milestones. All of our cultural events are also open for anyone to attend - we would love if you'd bring your friends too! We also conduct casual events frequently, such as welcome parties for incoming students, meet 'n' greets, mehfil nights, board game nights, and more.

Apart from on-campus events, MAITRI also provides all-round support for all incoming Indian graduate students. We host information webinars for incoming international students, and help them understand life in the USA, settling into Raleigh, getting their rent/utilities set up, understanding how the university functions and what resources are availabe, etc. With the help of OIS (the Office of International Students), we also provide airport pickup services for when you first land in RDU. We want to make sure you're not lost, and know that we have your back. You're not alone, there's a whole community of students looking out for each other.

MAITRI has been working with many organizations across NC State to keep up the Indian spirit high far away from home. We are recognized as a student association by both the Student Government and NC State administrative affairs. Over our long presence here at NC State, we have built an extensive network of alumni and current students - and we hope we can be good resources for your learning and career as well.
summary: Indian Graduate Student Association

name: Marine, Earth, and Atmospheric Sciences GSA
description: The goal of this organization is to provide resources for graduate students in the Marine, Earth, and Atmospheric Sciences Department, and to initiate opportunities for social interactions.
summary: The goal of this organization is to provide resources for graduate students in the Marine, Earth, and Atmospheric Sciences Department, and to initiate opportunities for social interactions.

name: Silver Wings: Martha E Metz Chapter
description:
What is Silver Wings?

The Mission of Silver Wings:

Silver Wings' predecessor organization, Angel Flight, had a specific objective of supporting the mission of Arnold Air Society. While this is no longer explicitly stated, Silver Wings remains committed to developing strong civilian leaders with an understanding of aerospace power–and obviously, the continuing partnership between AAS and SW is critical to building this understanding.

The Silver Wings National Constitution specifies that Silver Wings (SW) is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.

This mission includes the following interrelated objectives:

Personal Development of Individual Members:

Local chapter activities and projects provide character growth through service to the community, enabling the individual member to develop a sense of purpose and responsibility to the local community.

Professional Development of Individual Members:

Local chapter activities and the national organizational structure provide members with leadership opportunities and develop professional skills. These include but are not limited to oral/written communication, public speaking, and project planning and execution.

Civic Awareness:

Exposure to Air Force issues and personnel enables members to develop a personal awareness of aerospace power and its role in the national defense. The leadership and skills development objectives are enhanced by opportunities for members to actively promote aerospace issues in the community through educational programs, seminars, public relations activities, and voter awareness projects.

While Arnold Air Society and Silver Wings are separate organizations, our strong partnership is based on highly complementary missions. The focus of the Arnold Air Society's mission is to build solid officers for the United States Air Force. Silver Wings also focuses on leadership development, and both organizations work together to further each others' goals.
summary: A professional organization comprised of civilians and Air Force ROTC cadets which emphasizes personal and professional growth as well as encouraging civic awareness in the community.

name: Master of Business Administration Student Association
description: The MBASA was formed for the purpose of building a network among MBA students. The MBASA provides a chance for students to interact with fellow students, faculty, business leaders, and the surrounding community. The organization offers a series of activities to address professional and business issues that are not generally included in the formal program but are critical to the overall MBA experience.
summary: The MBASA serves as a bridge to provide students a chance to interact with fellow students, faculty, business leaders, and the surrounding community while developing skills personally and professionally.

name: Master of International Studies Graduate Student Association
description: This association represents the graduate student body in the Master of International Studies program, provides a forum for and voices graduate student opinion in all matters of mutual interest to graduate faculty and students, and promotes professional interest and fellowship among graduate students.
summary: This association represents the graduate student body in the Master of International Studies program.

name: Materials Science & Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: The department of materials science & engineering chapter of the Graduate Student Association (GSA).
summary: The department of materials science & engineering chapter of the Graduate Student Association (GSA).

name: Mathematics Graduate Student Association
description:
The purpose of MGSA is:

-- To represent the graduate student body of the Mathematics Department.

-- To provide a forum for graduate student opinion.

-- To voice graduate student opinion in all matters of mutual interest to graduate faculty and students.

-- To disseminate information of interest or importance to its member students.

-- To promote professional interest and fellowship among graduate students.
summary: The Mathematics Graduate Student Association represents the graduate student body of the Mathematics Department at NC State.

name: Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering GSA
description:
The MAE Graduate Student Association is the primary graduate student advocacy group within the department. The association serves as an intermediary between the department and its graduate students for any and all concerns. We have also launched a "Graduate School Survival Guide" - a new student-led resource for newer, older and incoming grad students, to read and submit anonymous advice/stories on navigating the ups and downs of grad school. This guide contains tips about academia, work-life balance, media resources, and links to campus and mental health resources. You can also submit a piece of advice on our anonymous submission form.

If you're a graduate student in the MAE department, you can join our organization on Get Involved, look for flyers around the hallways, follow our Instagram page,  and check your NCSU email regularly to stay in the know on our events! In addition to resolving graduate student issues, we host regular coffee & donut sessions, social nights and other events throughout the year, giving you a chance to mingle with other graduate students as well as take a break from your deadlines!

Add our event calendar: MAE GSA Event Calendar

For more info, email mech-aero-gsa@ncsu.edu.
summary: The MAE Graduate Student Association is the primary graduate student advocacy group within the department. The association serves as an intermediary between the department and its graduate students for any and all concerns.

name: Men in Social Work
description:
The NCSU Men in Social Work student organization is not a group exclusive to men, but rather an opportunity for those who work alongside men, for men, and on behalf of men to discuss and contribute to how men occupy space in the profession of social work.

This means everyone has a seat at our table and the quality of our dialogue on gender equity in the field of social work is dependent upon a multitude of voices from diverse identities. We have built a community that provides support and guidance for our members through open dialogue and lectures that examine gender through an intersectional lens in the context of social work practice.

We strive to maintain a space where NCSU BSW and MSW students can collectively engage in community building, and the promotion of social justice and transition their knowledge from the classroom to practice. 

Men in Social Work embodies the idea and practice of men navigating the spaces of the social work field while also creating space in the communities and places in which they serve. Part of this mission includes serving as volunteers and participants in other initiatives in a way that is meaningful and has a positive impact.

It is the intention of Men in Social Work to support others in their work, as well as invite that work into the Men in Social Work meetings/space to foster and develop partnerships within the communities served.
summary: Our purpose is to examine gender in the context of Social Work, and specifically the role of men in promoting gender equity in both the profession and broader society in the NCSU Department of Social Work.

name: Men's Club Basketball at NC State University
description:
Men's Club Basketball provides a competitive, fun, and inclusive environment for university students to play basketball. The club is a not-for profit organization that receives funding from member dues and fundraisers while making its own donation efforts.

In our most recent seasons the club advanced to the NIRSA Nationals Final Four at the University of Wisconsin-Madison(Spring 2024), and the next year won 1st place at the Georgia Tech Ramblin’ Wreck Regional tournament in Atlanta Georgia! We finished both semesters strong and look forward to making another push for a national championship in the future. Excited for this next semester of hoops!
summary: Men's Club Basketball provides a competitive, fun, and inclusive environment for university  students to play basketball. The club is a not-for-profit organization that receives funding from member dues and fundraisers, making its own donation efforts.

name: Men's Club Soccer
description:
Men's Club Soccer at NC State is a highly competitive organization that has the opportunity to compete in a very renowned area for soccer throughout the South East. The team travels to several tournaments all across the south east and consistently competes at the regional championship at the end of the fall season. With nationals always in consideration, our intensity and ability to assemble a roster that has the talent and experience to play at the highest level has become a tradition within the club with some of the best players in the region coming to NC State for a place to play.

For more information about our club and tryout dates, please visit www.ncsusoccer.com
summary: Men's Club Soccer at NC State is a highly competitive organization that has the opportunity to compete in a very renowned area for soccer throughout the South East.

name: Men's Rugby Club
description: The Rugby Club at NC State is dedicated to providing students with the opportunity to play rugby at a competitive level. Everyone is welcome regardless of their knowledge of the sport. Practice is held every Tuesday and Thursday from 5:30 to 7:30 on the Method Rd Fields. If the fields are closed we usually practice on Miller or Lee fields. Any further questions you may have, please email Charlie Howard @clhowar5@ncsu.edu
summary: The Rugby Club at NC State is dedicated to providing students with the opportunity to play rugby at a competitive and social level. Everyone is welcome regardless of their knowledge of the sport.

name: Men's Ultimate Club
description: We are the Men's Ultimate Club. We invite any student to tryout and join one of our teams, with no experience required! We love representing our school, we love each other, and we love to play ultimate frisbee. For more information as well as ways to reach out to us, please see our email listed below or visit our twitter account for other updates.
summary: We are the Men's Ultimate Club. We host tryouts the first week of the Fall semester. We invite any student to tryout and join one of our teams, with no experience required!

name: Mi Familia at NC State
description: The purpose of Mi Familia is to provide a social, cultural, and political forum for students at NC State and members of the surrounding community interested in experiencing, learning, and promoting the richness of the Latinx community and culture. We welcome all students, graduate students, and faculty/staff.
summary: Mi Familia aims to be a social, cultural, and political forum for students and the surrounding community interested in experiencing and promoting the richness of the international Latinx & Hispanic community/culture.

name: Microbiology Undergraduate Club at NC State
description:
The Microbiology Club for Undergraduate at NC State University.

Our purpose is to bring together individuals who share a common interest in the field of microbiology. We hope to explore its many contributions to society as well as its exciting new endeavors. Our activities include tours, service trips, guest speakers, and social functions. Our membership is based on participation. We explore career interests including industry, academia, and healthcare. You can contact an officer or faculty advisor listed on this page for more information about the club!
summary: Open to all majors, the club aims to  engage students interested in microbiology and its applications.

name: Microbiology Graduate Student Association
description: Graduate student association for the microbiology program at North Carolina State University.
summary: Graduate student association for professional development and social networking of graduate students studying or researching microbiology or related science fields.

name: MANRRS
description: The National Society of MANRRS strives to nurture potential, support unique journeys, and connect members to opportunities that inspire leadership and innovation. The NC State University chapter of MANRRS consists of students (undergraduate and graduate) across campus whose academic interests are focused in agriculture, environmental sciences, natural resources, and related disciplines. The majority of the students may be found within the College of Agriculture and Life Sciences, the College of Sciences, and the College of Natural Resources. However, membership is open to all interested students.
summary: MANRRS strives to develop and empower individuals in the fields of agriculture, environmental sciences, natural resources, and related sciences through professional development, service, and social events.

name: Alpha Chapter of Mu Beta Psi
description:
Mu Beta Psi is a gender-inclusive National Honorary Musical Fraternity founded at NC State in 1925. We provide service to the NC State Music Department and fundraise for non-profit music organizations like VH1's Save the Music. Through our four purposes, we strive to be of service to the Music Department and the musical community. The members of Mu Beta Psi are united by a common love for music and a desire to promote it to the surrounding community. Brotherhood, comprised of men and women, is the most important aspect of our fraternity life. It is the relationship between our Brothers that is the most fulfilling and rewarding aspect of joining Mu Beta Psi National Honorary Musical Fraternity.

We welcome everyone who has a love of music- you are not required to be enrolled in a music class at NCSU.
summary: One of the nation's oldest music Fraternities, Mu Beta Psi has been dedicated to music service, excellence, and fellowship for 90 years.

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description: The purpose of the Multicultural Greek Council (MGC) is to provide a council for Greek organizations that contribute specifically to diversity on NC States campus. The MGC serves as the governing body and network for culturally-based Greek organizations to create and maintain high standards in the life of Fraternities and Sororities by addressing, coordinating, and developing strategic action plan to unify affiliations, promote higher education, provide community service, enhance leadership and serve as a liaison between the MGC and the University student body, and administration; thereby improving the campus climate.
summary: The Multicultural Greek Council consists of 8 active Greek-letter organizations, all with culturally-based missions and backgrounds.

name: Muslim Student Association at NC State
description: The Muslim Student Association is an organization that strives to build unity amongst Muslim students at NC State University and the greater Triangle area. MSA is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive community that embraces diversity, empowers personal and academic growth, and promotes a deep understanding and appreciation of Islamic principles and values. Our mission is to provide a supportive and welcoming environment for Muslim students, as well as those interested in learning about Islam, irrespective of their cultural, ethnic, or religious backgrounds. Through a commitment to education, dialogue, and community service, we aim to bridge gaps, cultivate mutual understanding, and contribute to religious exchange within our university.
summary: The Muslim Student Association is an organization that strives to build unity and community amongst Muslim students at NC State University and the greater Triangle area.

name: Nazaare at NC State
description: Nazaare is a competitive dance team that combines various South Asian dance forms, such as Bhangra, Bharatanatyam, and Bollywood, with Western styles like Hip-Hop and Contemporary. We compile all of these dance forms to create an energetic, entertaining, and intricate routine for both local and national performances.
summary: NC State's premiere Bollywood-Fusion dance team.

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) On Campus
description: NAMI On Campus is a student-run, student-led organization that provides mental health support, education and advocacy on the campus of North Carolina State University. Our mission is to improve the lives of students who are directly or indirectly affected by mental illness, increase the awareness of mental health services on campus, and to eliminate the stigma students with mental illness face.
summary: NAMI On Campus is a student-run, student-led organization that provides mental health support, education, and advocacy on the campus of North Carolina State University.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is a collaborative organization of nine historically Black International Greek lettered fraternities and sororities. The nine NPHC organizations are also colloquially referred to as the "Divine Nine." The member organizations have not formally adopted nor recommended the use of this term to describe their collaborative grouping. The NPHC was formed as a permanent organization on May 10, 1930 on the campus of Howard University, in Washington, D.C. NPHC was incorporated under the laws of the State of Illinois in 1937. The council promotes interaction through forums, meetings and other mediums for the exchange of information and engages in cooperative programming and initiatives through various activities and functions. Each constituent member organization determines its own strategic direction and program agenda.

Today, the primary purpose and focus of member organizations remains camaraderie and academic excellence for its members and service to the communities they serve. Each promotes community awareness and action through educational, economic, and cultural service activities.

For any further information email us at: nphc_president@ncsu.edu
summary: The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is a collaborative organization of nine historically Black International Greek lettered fraternities and sororities. Each promotes community awareness and action.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description: Our meetings are held every other Thursday in Riddick Hall. For specific dates, upcoming calendar events, and membership sign up form, visit our linktree or join our groupme.
summary: Our mission is "To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."

name: National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The NSLS is the largest leadership honor society in the United States. Our NSLS chapter at North Carolina State University was started in 2012. Our local chapter is part of the national organization with 700+ chapters and over 1.4 million members. 

Students are selected based on exemplary academic achievement. As of 2023 we have 3,353 members who have access to:

  • More than $400,000 in scholarships.

  • An exclusive NSLS job board.

  • A personalized letter of recommendation. 

The NSLS leadership program teaches the interpersonal skills that employers seek to give you an edge in the job market. Hear directly from current members as they share how their experience has changed their life.

If you become a member, some of the other benefits are:

  • Learn from exceptional leaders like former President Barack Obama during exclusive Speaker Broadcasts.

  • Meet new friends and make lifelong connections (on-campus and virtually).

  • Overcome challenges with success coaches shortcutting your way to success.

  • Access to private networking events.

Visit the NSLS website to learn more about and accept your nomination. 

Frequently Asked Questions:

Some of the common questions we receive are listed below. If you have any other questions, visit nsls.org to chat with a team member or email help@nsls.org.

What do I have to do to become inducted?

Our five-step program takes approximately 12 hours over the course of the semester at your pace. 

Is this recognized on campus?

We are a Registered Student Organization (RSO). As of 2023, we had 3,353 in our chapter and there are more than 1.4 million members nationwide.
summary: We are the official North Carolina State University Chapter of the National Society of Leadership and Success (NSLS). Our Chapter's official organization title is North Carolina State University National Society of Leadership and Success (NCSU NSLS).

name: Native American Student Association
description: This organization was formed to create a community among the Native American Students here at NC State. Our aim is to focus on the cultures of our ancestors and to enrich the awareness of others on the campus and the surrounding community. NASA is open to all people of all races who want to learn more about the indigenous people of this land and we welcome you to join us.
summary: Our organization was formed to create a community among the Native American Students here at NC State with an aim to focus on the cultures of our ancestors and to enrich awareness of others on the campus and the surrounding community.

name: Kappa Student Chapter of the North Carolina Council for Teachers of Mathematics at North Carolina State University
description:
If you like...

- Math

- Education

- Some combination of math and education

- Food

- Making new friends

- Networking with students, faculty, and professionals in your major

- Volunteering

- Getting plugged in on campus and local schools

- General silliness

... then this club is for you!

Join the email list by filling out this google form with your NCSU email
summary: If you like math, education, some combination of math and education, making new friends, and networking, then this club is for you!

name: Nubian Message
description: Nubian Message is a biweekly student newspaper that serves as the media representative of the Black and underrepresented students at North Carolina State University. First published in November 1992, the paper was created in response to misrepresentation and underrepresentation of Black students in existing media. Nubian Message is a publication where students can learn about different aspects of Black culture as well as other cultures, with a renewed emphasis on intersectional ideals and serving as a platform for many underrepresented groups. Along with the rest of Student Media, Nubian Message serves as another way for students to let their voices be heard. New print editions are on stands every other Thursday during the academic year.
summary: Nubian Message is the Black student publication at NC State. As stated in its original mission, Nubian Message emphasizes coverage of the African-American community, and also focuses on issues relevant to any underrepresented community at NC State.

name: Omega Phi Alpha
description:
Omega Phi Alpha is a national service sorority founded in Bowling Green, Ohio in 1967 to promote the ideals of friendship, leadership and service. The Alpha Lambda chapter of Omega Phi Alpha was chartered here at NC State in 2010, making OPA one of the newest additions to the community service family of NC State. Omega Phi Alpha sisters are committed to four prime areas of service: the university community, the community at large, the members of the sorority, and to the nations of the world, and seek to develop projects that address these areas, as well as the national permanent project: mental health. Omega Phi Alpha sisters are multi-faceted in the field of service, and participate in campus events such as Miracle League, the Krispy Kreme Challenge, Shack-a-Thon, and the annual Outrace the Stigma Mental Health 5K. Sisters also create their own events to act as fundraisers, bring awareness to important social issues, or support a local cause.

Today's Friends, Tomorrow's Leaders, Forever in Service!
summary: Omega Phi Alpha is the most respected provider of lifelong sisterhood-based community service and leadership development.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc.
description: Founded on November 17, 1911 the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization to be founded on the campus of a historically black college.
summary: The Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization to be founded on the campus of a historically black college.

name: Panhellenic Association
description:
Lead. Empower. Belong. Pursue your potential with the Panhellenic Association! Panhellenic is the governing body over the 11 National Panhellenic Conference Sororities and 2 Associate Member Chapter at North Carolina State University. Our chapters are: Alpha Omega Epsilon, Alpha Delta Pi, Alpha Xi Delta, Chi Omega, Delta Gamma, Delta Zeta, Kappa Alpha Theta, Kappa Delta, Kappa Kappa Gamma, Pi Beta Phi, Phi Sigma Rho, Sigma Kappa, and Zeta Tau Alpha.

Being a part of a sorority is a transformational experience! Learn how to be a part of this powerful experience by looking at our website, or signing up for recruitment! Fall Formal Recruitment takes place over Labor Day weekend, and allows you to visit each of our NPC chapters, and see where you're best fit lies. Even if you're not sure if sorority is for you, recruitment is a great way to get to meet other students and learn something new about yourself as well! 

Recruitment Registration Link: https://enroll.icsrecruiter.com/pan/ncstun#!/enroll/enrollment-disclaimer

Panhellenic Website: http://www.ncsupanhellenic.com
summary: Governing body over the Panhellenic Sororities at North Carolina State University.

name: Parks, Recreation, and Tourism Management Graduate Student Association
description: This Graduate Student Association is for all graduate students who are pursuing a degree administered through the Department of Parks, Recreation and Tourism Management, or those who are under the advisement of PRTM faculty.
summary: This Graduate Student Association is for all graduate students who are pursuing a degree administered through the Department of Parks, Recreation and Tourism Management, or those who are under the advisement of PRTM faculty.

name: Finance Club
description:
The Finance Club hosts meetings with alumni representatives from leading financial services firms. We host meetings with firms such as Goldman Sachs, Wells Fargo, Regions, Truist, Citizens Bank, PNC, PIMCO, CAPTRUST, BlackArch Partners, CISCO, IHS Markit, Merrill Lynch, and more. 

 

We also run what we call our Mentorship Program. Accepted students will be paired with an alumni who is currently working in financial services for one semester. Over the course of the semester, student mentees will have two phone calls and an optional mock interview with their alumni mentor. The program is not required for membership, and applications will be emailed out 1-2 months before the start of each semester. 

 

The organization is is open to students from all disciplines and grade levels. No experience in finance is required to join.
summary: The Finance Club at NC State provides networking and career development opportunities to undergraduate students. Email ncstatefinance@gmail.com to join the club.

name: PGA Golf Management Club at NC State
description: The mission of the PGA Golf Management Club at NC State is to promote the general welfare of PGA Golf Management students by providing opportunities for career enhancement, academic assistance, social programs, tournament opportunities, as well as promoting the PGA Golf Management program at NC State.
summary: The mission of the PGA Golf Management Club at NC State is to promote the general welfare of PGA Golf Management students.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. - Xi Zeta Chapter
description:
Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. is a black greek letter organization in the Divine Nine that views itself as a part of the general community and seeks to uplift our community through this perspective. We work to strengthen the community in which we serve by organizing events that enhance the excellence and overall experience of the black student population at NC State. We live by the principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Service, and we emphasize our motto "Culture for Service, Service for Humanity" in our everyday lives.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5cXsWkauA8Y
summary: We work to make the community in which we serve a better environment than before. We live by the principles of brotherhood, scholarship, and service.

name: Phi Psi National Textile Fraternity - Eta Chapter
description: Phi Psi Textile Fraternity is the only professional co-ed textile fraternity in the United States. The fraternity encourages high standards of work, fellowship and brotherhood, as well as strong character and integrity. Eta Chapter located at N.C. State University, is the largest of two current national chapters. Any full-time Wilson College of Textiles student who meets the GPA and class standing requirements (at least one semester in the WCOT) is eligible to pledge. In addition to regular meetings and activities, the fraternity engages in community service, professional events and occasional fundraisers.
summary:
Phi Psi is the only professional co-ed textile fraternity in the United States. The fraternity encourages high standards of work, fellowship and brotherhood, as well as strong character and integrity. 

Current active chapters: Alpha & Eta

name: Phi Sigma Nu
description: Phi Sigma Nu American Indian Fraternity, Inc. is a brotherhood composed of undergraduate, graduate and professional men nationally, established on February 13, 1996 at the University of North Carolina at Pembroke. Phi Sigma Nu is the first national American Indian Fraternity established and recognized by an institution of higher learning. We devote ourselves towards the empowerment of our American Indian community by aiding in recruiting and retention efforts of American Indians in higher education and providing cultural and social programming and activities geared toward the appreciation, promotion and preservation of the American Indian Culture. Phi Sigma Nu strongly encourages education through the pursuit of undergraduate degrees, advanced degrees, and the attainment of professional credentials that strengthen our members so we may contribute to the greater good of the American Indian community. We challenge our Brothers to surpass the existing tribal boundaries that have separated the American Indian community for centuries. We understand that our members have their own unique tribal identity but we pride ourselves in Pan-Indianism and support and promote actions leading to the unification of all American Indian tribes.
summary: Phi Sigma Nu American Indian Fraternity, Inc. is a brotherhood composed of undergraduate, graduate, and professional men nationally, established on February 13, 1996, at the University of North Carolina at Pembroke.

name: Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity
description: Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity is a gender-inclusive organization based on a tripod of three ideals- scholarship, leadership, and fellowship. We plan and execute multiple events over each semester that apply to our tripod. We currently have approximately 50 active brothers. To be eligible for the initiation process you must have completed at least 12 credit hours or have transfer/AP credit, have a minimum GPA of 3.0, and have at least two semesters left. We hold the initiate process at the beginning of the fall and spring semesters.
summary: Our organization is based on a tripod of three ideals- scholarship, leadership, and fellowship. We plan and execute multiple events over each semester that apply to our tripod.

name: Pi Alpha Xi
description: Though an honor society, Pi Alpha Xi is more than a society. The organization was originally established to unify students, educators, and professional horticulturists. The society promotes high scholarship, fellowship, professional leadership, and the enrichment of human life through plants. NC State is the Iota Chapter of Pi Alpha Xi, founded in 1957.
summary: Though an honor society, Pi Alpha Xi is more than a society. The organization was originally established to unify students, educators, and professional horticulturists.

name: Pi Beta Phi Fraternity
description: The mission of Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service. Pi Beta Phi was founded on April 28, 1867 in Monmouth, IL as the very first organization for women. Pi Beta Phi is also recognized as the first organization to organize a national philanthropic project. The fraternity's colors are wine and silver blue and the symbol is the arrow. Pi Beta Phi is one of the only social organizations that directly interacts with their philanthropic efforts by participating in the CAR program. Champions Are Readers is a program where Pi Beta Phi members go to local schools multiple times per week to read to, interact with and teach young children that are in need of additional education. NC State's Pi Beta Phi chapter, NC Delta, holds many events throughout the school year including date functions, sisterhood events, parent's cocktails and tailgates and many other events for members. See website for additional information.
summary: The mission of Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description:
Pi Kappa Alpha is dedicated to developing men of integrity, intellect, and high moral character and to fostering a truly lifelong fraternal experience. To fulfill this mission, Pi Kappa Alpha will:

  • Encourage all our members to live the values taught in our ritual.
  • Provide innovative services and programs designed to enrich the lives of our undergraduates, alumni, and communities.
  • Create opportunities for our undergraduate and alumni members to participate in the life of Pi Kappa Alpha on a daily basis.
  • Promote and foster a spirit of pride, loyalty, inclusion and respect for Pi Kappa Alpha and its rich history.
  • Empower our undergraduate and alumni members to help shape Pi Kappa Alpha's future by being informed ambassadors and involved advocates.
  • Encourage our undergraduate and alumni members to take an active role in advancing Pi Kappa Alpha's interests by volunteering their time and by contributing to the fraternity's annual giving campaign.
  • Communicate effectively and efficiently with our alumni, students and host institutions through various channels on a continuous basis.
  • Value academic achievement and practice academic integrity.
  • Establish positive and lasting relationships with our host institutions, alumni and communities.
  • Encourage our members to be of strong mind and body.
summary: Pi Kappa Alpha will set the standard of integrity, intellect, and achievement for our members, host institutions, and the communities in which we live.

name: Pi Tau Sigma
description: Pi Tau Sigma is the Mechanical Engineering Honor Society. "The object of this organization shall be to foster the high ideals of the engineering profession, to stimulate interest in and coordinate departmental activities, to promote the mutual professional welfare of its members, and to develop in students of mechanical engineering the attributes necessary for effective leadership and the assumption of the responsibilities of a citizen in a democracy."
summary: Mechanical Engineering Honor Society

name: Botany Club
description: The Botany Club is an academic club at NC State. The club was formed to promote and encourage scholarly interactions among students at NC State with an interest in the fundamental study of plants. The club is open to all NC State students with most members having Plant Biology or another plant science or life science discipline as their academic major or academic minor.
summary: We are a club dedicated to plants in all their wonderful forms.

name: Plant Biology Graduate Student Association
description: We are the official group for Plant Biology Graduate Students here at NC State!
summary: We are the official group for Plant Biology Graduate Students here at NC State

name: Plant Breeding Club
description: The purpose of the plant breeding club is to promote awareness and participation in plant breeding research at NC State. The plant breeding program at NC State is an extensive interdepartmental program encompassing students in four departments: Crop Science, Horticultural Science, Forestry, and Plant Pathology. These departments are based on traditional commodity groupings, and as such do not encompass all the educational or professional needs of their plant breeding students. The plant breeding club shall provide for the professional needs of plant breeding students throughout NC State as requested by its members and as possible with available resources. The plant breeding club will particularly aid in the development of networking opportunities and professional skills required by commercial and academic institutions generally not obtained in classroom settings. Activities may be suggested by any club member and shall be approved by the officers committee.
summary: The purpose of the plant breeding club is to promote awareness and participation in plant breeding research at NC State .

name: Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association
description: The Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association acts as a liaison between the graduate students and the faculty of the Department of Plant Pathology, furthers the personal and professional interests of the graduate students in the Department, and assists in departmental functions.
summary: The Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association acts as a liaison between the graduate students and the faculty of the Department of Plant Pathology.

name: Political Science Club at North Carolina State University
description: The Political Science Club is a non-partisan, student-led organization that serves as a connector between peers, public servants, and faculty. We host discussions on current affairs, panel events with speakers, open floor debates, career development opportunities, and more! We encourage people of all majors and ideologies to come. To get involved, send an email to Pierce Bruns at pubruns@ncsu.edu
summary: The Political Science Club is a non-partisan, student-led organization that hosts discussions on current affairs, panel events with speakers, open floor debate, career development opportunities, and more!

name: Poole College of Management Peer Leaders
description:
The Peer Leaders organization seeks to foster a sense of community within the Poole College of Management. This is achieved through welcoming new students, providing one-on-one mentoring to First-Year and transfer students, and providing academic support for Poole courses. Peer Leaders serve as student leaders in the M100 Courses. They build community through facilitating thoughtful discussions and creating an inclusive environment. Additionally, Peer Leaders organize academic programming for students, as well as social and professional development opportunities for members.  The organization assists the Poole College of Management with events such as new student orientation and wolfpack welcome week. 

If you are a Poole College of Management student who is interested in learning more about Peer Leaders visit our website. (will open in new window): https://yourpeerleader.wixsite.com/ncsupoolepeerleaders
summary: The Poole College of Management Peer Leaders is an organization that seeks to foster a sense of community within the college by welcoming new students as well as providing one-on-one mentoring to First-Year & transfer students.

name: Poole College of Management Student Ambassadors
description:
Our Mission: The members of this organization serve as the face of the Poole College of Management through a variety of events including prospective student recruitment, campus/community service, and career development. 

What Do We Do?

  • Share stories of our experiences with prospective students and families 
  • Answer questions of prospective students and families, while building meaningful connections with them
  • Use our backgrounds and experiences  to represent the environment and relationships within the college

Help with Prospective and Admitted Student Events such as: 

    • University Open House
    • Poole Preview
    • Experience NC State 
    • Friday Information Sessions

Eligibility: Potential members must be Poole College of Management majors. Members are not only involved in Poole, but also the NC State Community. 

Applications open every Spring semester. For more information: Poole Leadership Organizations
summary: Our mission is to serve the Poole College of Management community - current and prospective students, faculty, alumni and business professionals - to foster a strong sense of community within the college and to develop leadership and networking skills.

name: Pre-Health Club
description: The Pre-Health Club at NCSU (or PHC) is a student-led organization on NC State's campus that was founded with the purpose of providing its members with the skills and opportunities to enhance their academic, networking and clinical skills in pursuit of a health-related profession. The Pre-Health Club consists of one large club supporting all pre-health interests as well as several Specialty Groups that include: Medical/Podiatry, Dental, Optometry, Pharmacy, PA/Nursing, PT/OT, HOSA, and two honor societies DDS (pre-dental), and AED (pre-medical and any other pre-health careers) Honor Societies. Students are will gain access to numerous resources that will help guide them their pre-health journey. If you are interested in any pre-health profession and you love helping others through service and leadership then this club is for you!
summary: The Pre-Health Club at NCSU was founded with the purpose of providing its members with the skills and opportunities to enhance their academic, networking and clinical skills in pursuit of a health-related profession.

name: Pre-Pharmacy Club
description: Pre-Health Club subgroup at NC State that exposes students to the wide variety of career opportunities available in the field of pharmacy. Engages students in pharmacy speaker series, PCAT prep, service projects, and professional skills workshops. Introduces interaction within pharmacy and other health care fields through service experiences and various perspectives within pharmacy.
summary: Pre-Health Club subgroup at NC State that exposes students to the wide variety of career opportunities available in the field of pharmacy, ranging from retail, hospital, veterinary, and industry.

name: Pre-Veterinary Medical Association at North Carolina State University
description: NCSU Pre-Veterinary Medical Association exists to empower and educate pre-veterinary students through social, service, and fundraising projects to promote camaraderie and networking so that students can grow as leaders in the veterinary community.
summary: The objective of this association is to promote and stimulate interest in the field of veterinary medicine.

name: Presbyterian Campus Ministry
description:
Presbyterian Campus Ministry (PCM) of Raleigh is a Presbyterian Church (USA) More Light Campus Ministry for college-age students in Raleigh, NC. All are welcome. All are loved. 

As an extension of the ministry of the Presbyterian Church (USA), PCM of Raleigh seeks be a unique voice of God’s grace on the college campuses in our community. Our Mission is:

  • To be a place where faith exploration is biblical, theological, and socially honest.
  • To be a place where students are able to explore their faith in an open and loving environment where, through God’s grace, disciples are formed.
  • To be a community of inclusion for all seeking understanding of whom they are in relationship to God as creator, redeemer and sustainer.
  • To be a place where lasting and healthy friendships are built.
  • To continually seek a deeper understanding of faith while actively engaging the world and academics from a Christian perspective.
  • To be a ministry that seeks to have a positive Christian impact on our university campuses.
  • To help young adults discover their calling as active followers of Christ seeking justice, loving kindness, and walking humbly with their God.
  • To help connect young adults to a more meaningful and sustaining relationship to the larger church community.
  • To challenge the church to consider the unique needs and involvement of young adults in their ministry.
summary: Presbyterian Campus Ministry (PCM) of Raleigh is a Presbyterian Church (USA) More Light Campus Ministry for college-age students in Raleigh, NC. All are welcome. All are loved.

name: Providence College Ministry
description: Providence College Ministry exists to glorify God by introducing all college-aged individuals to Jesus Christ and growing them up to love and worship Him. They meet weekly for corporate worship on Sunday mornings, and throughout the week in small group communities and events. On this page, you’ll find everything happening in PCM and how you can take your next step to get connected to gospel-centered community.
summary: We are the college ministry of Providence Church in Raleigh, NC. We seek to reach, equip, and send college students and grow in community!

name: Public Relations Student Society of America at North Carolina State University
description:
The PRSSA Chapter at NC State strives to provide a place for students interested in the field of Public Relations to come together to enjoy guest speakers, workplace tours, professional development, PRSSA at NC State's PR Day and other beneficial events. PRSSA is not exclusive to public relations students. Anybody with an interest is welcome to join! 

PRSSA at NC State, as an organization, presents its members with great opportunities to explore the field of public relations further through a variety of events while connecting members with the greater Raleigh-Durham community of communications professionals.
summary: This pre-professional organization exists to assist students by enhancing their education, broadening their network, and launching their career. This organization is not exclusive to public relations students. All academic disciplines are welcome!

name: Quiz Bowl at NC State
description:
If you are interested in history, literature, science, philosophy, religion, mythology, or any and all academic subjects, come to Quiz Bowl! Quiz Bowl is a team-based trivia game (think Jeopardy!) in which students can learn about a wide variety of academic topics and apply their knowledge in a fun and competitive setting. Quiz Bowl is great for trivia-lovers and academia-lovers alike. If you are interested, reach out to John Gamble (jpgambl2@ncsu.edu) or come out to any of our meetings!

Current meeting details (Fall 2025): Talley Student Union Room 5101. Weekly on Fridays from 5:30-7:30PM.
summary: Quiz Bowl at NC State is a fun trivia organization in which members practice and compete in regional and national Quiz Bowl competitions. All skill levels are welcome!!

name: Racquetball Club at NC State
description: The racquetball club is a place for all people who enjoy racquetball from beginners to the experienced players. We practice twice a week and attend several collegiate tournaments throughout the year.
summary: The racquetball club is a place for all people who enjoy racquetball from beginners to the experienced players. We practice twice a week and attend several collegiate tournaments throughout the year.

name: Raleigh Chinese Christian Church Student Fellowship
description:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1-1jiMzSfOs

We are a group of Chinese Christians and friends who are interested in Christianity. We meet at 6:30 pm every Friday to share the delicious dinners, worship together, and have discussions with each other. 
我们的聚会地点（address）：2700 Avent Ferry Rd, Raleigh, 27606 

团契活动时间表(Fellowship Schedule)：

6:30 - 7:00pm: 晚餐（Dinner）

7:00 - 7:30pm: 赞美敬拜 （Worship & Prayer）

7:30 - 7:40pm: 欢迎新朋友 (Welcome)

7:40 - 8:30pm: 查经与信仰讨论（Bible study and group discussions）

8:30 - 9:00pm: “甜美”交流 （dessert and fellowship）
summary:
RCCCSF is affiliated with Raleigh Chinese Christian Church. It serves as an on-campus fellowship group of Raleigh Chinese Christian Church.
Starting in 2022(post-pandemic), we are also dedicated to providing more services to the local community.

name: Raleigh Wesley Foundation
description: The United Methodist Campus Ministry for the Raleigh Area. We exist to nurture the Christian faith through fellowship, worship, study, and service.
summary: The United Methodist Campus Ministry for the Raleigh Area

name: Ratio Christi
description:
Ratio Christi seeks to encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students at public and private institutions across the country, while sharing Christ's message and love to those who have not yet accepted Him. By situating apologetic clubs at universities across the nation, we hope to take part in the battle for the mind and begin to reverse the prevalent humanism in the university by providing and equipping students with the answers, or means of getting the answers, to the pressing questions of life.

 

VIEW MEETING NOTES/VIDEOS HERE
summary: Ratio Christi seeks to encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students at public and private institutions across the country, while sharing Christ's message and love to those who have not yet accepted Him.

name: Red Cross Club
description: The Red Cross Club will learn, practice, and exemplify humanitarian values through mission-related service projects.
summary: The Red Cross Club at NC State works with the North Carolina regional chapter of the American Red Cross. We help out in 5 key areas: Disaster Relief, Blood Services, Training & Certification, Service to the Armed Forces, and International Services.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description:
Reformed University Fellowship (RUF) is the campus ministry of the Presbyterian Church in America. Although we are sent to the campus by the PCA, we welcome students from any and all walks of life. Students from a wide variety of church and religious backgrounds are involved here at North Carolina State University. We are here for the "convinced" and the "unconvinced." We believe that you are never so good that you are beyond the need for God's grace and you are never so bad that you are beyond the reach of God's grace. 

 

We hold large group meetings on Tuesday nights from 7:30-8:30. More information can be found on social media.
summary: Reformed University Fellowship (RUF) is the campus ministry of the Presbyterian Church in America.

name: Rowing Club
description:
Since 1988, the Rowing Club at NC State has competed in collegiate events at regattas across the Eastern Seaboard. The team practices at Lake Wheeler in the fall and spring and at an indoor rowing facility in the winter. Lake Wheeler is a 15 minute drive south from Main Campus.

Rowing is a team sport. Each rower pushes themself at practice not only for personal improvement, but also for the knowledge that every person in the boat is giving their best effort to move the crew through the water. Club members regularly exceed their personal expectations for the betterment of their friends in the boat with them.
summary: Since 1988, the Rowing Club at NC State has competed in collegiate events at regattas across the Eastern Seaboard. The team practices at Lake Wheeler in the fall and spring and at an indoor rowing facility in the winter.

name: Sailing Club at NC State
description: The purpose of the Sailing Club at North Carolina State University shall be to provide its members with the opportunity to participate in recreational and competitive sailing; to educate its members in the sport; to enable certified members to check out and assume responsibility for university sailboats; thereby encouraging, advancing and improving the sport of sailing.
summary: The purpose of the Sailing Club at NC State is to provide members with the opportunity to participate in recreational and competitive sailing, learn all they wish about sailing, and build a vibrant sailing community at NC State.

name: SG Appropriations
description: Appropriations is the process through which registered student organizations at NC State University receive funding for their organizational needs and to serve the student community. This portal provides online Appropriation request forms for the student groups to fill their request and many other information.
summary: Appropriations is the process through which registered student organizations at NC State University receive funding for their organizational needs and to serve the student community.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description: NC Alpha of SAE was founded in 1946 by a group of 12 WWII veterans. These men laid the foundation for this brotherhood for many years to come.  We pride ourselves in our tight bond as brothers as well as striving to be "The True Gentleman" through academics, service, and philanthropy.
summary: The True Gentleman

name: Sigma Alpha Omega
description:
Sigma Alpha Omega® was formed as a Sister Sorority to Chi Alpha Omega fraternity. Chi Alpha Omega was founded as a co-ed Christian fraternity in the spring of 1987 at East Carolina University in Greenville, North Carolina. The purpose of Chi Alpha Omega was to assemble Christian students in a Christ-centered brotherhood in order to enjoy Christian fellowship, service, accountability, and social activities.

 

In January 1998, the active brothers (both males and females) of the Beta Chapter of Chi Alpha Omega  at North Carolina State University voted to form Sigma Alpha Omega® in hopes of benefiting both the fraternity and the sorority. Both groups committed to living according to the ideals of Christ and His Word, uplifting each other through fellowship and service, and bringing the love of Christ to North Carolina State University. 

 

Sigma Alpha Omega® Christian Sorority, Inc. was officially recognized as a national non-profit organization under the laws of North Carolina in June 2004. Since the founding of the sorority, chapters of Sigma Alpha Omega® have been established at many colleges and universities across the nation.

 

Ultimately, we are a Christ-centered social sorority founded on the belief of being "One in Christ through Unity in Sisterhood" ®. We welcome all women in all walks of faith.

 

Interested in joining us? Contact our president, Emily Peedin, at enpeedin@ncsu.edu!
summary: NC State's Christian Sorority

name: Sigma Alpha Professional Agricultural Sorority
description: Sigma Alpha is a professional agricultural sorority that promotes scholarship, leadership, service and fellowship among its members. The sorority was founded in 1978 at The Ohio State University by five women who wanted an alternative to the social Greek sorority system. Since that time, Sigma Alpha has become a national organization consisting of more than 70 chapters, comprised of undergraduate and alumnae members. Today, more than 19,000 members have been initiated into Sigma Alpha Sorority. Please visit ncsusigmaalpha.wordpress.com for more information regarding NC State's chapter.
summary: Sigma Alpha is a professional agricultural sorority that promotes scholarship, leadership, service and fellowship among its members.

name: Sigma Chi Fraternity - Delta Epsilon
description:
The fundamental purpose of the Sigma Chi Fraternity is the cultivation, maintenance, and promotion of the core values of friendship, justice and learning. In the pursuit of these high ideals, the Sigma Chi Fraternity is able to offer tremendous value in augmenting the collegiate experience and supporting the life journey of each of our members. We hope to assist brothers to become men of character, caring husbands, compassionate fathers, and community leaders.

Status: Organizational Probation through October 12, 2025.

Violations: Violation of FSL social event policies
summary: The fundamental purpose of the Sigma Chi Fraternity is the cultivation, maintenance, and promotion of the core values of friendship, justice and learning.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description:
Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. was founded on November 12th, 1922 on the campus of Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana. The Kappa Omega Chapter was founded on the NC State Campus in 1987. Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority's aim is to enhance the quality of life within the community through public service, leadership development, and education of youth.

 

Chapter Website: https://kappaomegancsu.wixsite.com/1922
summary: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority's aim is to enhance the quality of life within the community through public service, leadership development, and education of youth.

name: Sigma Kappa
description: Sigma Kappa is very proud to have been the first sorority chartered on campus. Sigma Kappa women have found friendships that will last a lifetime and have learned skills that make them revolutionary leaders of today and tomorrow. Since the chapter's establishment in 1960, Sigma Kappa has been continually dedicated to serving and supporting the community through campus leadership, scholarship, philanthropic projects and school spirit.
summary: Sigma Kappa was the first sorority at NC State. The purpose of Sigma Kappa Sorority is to provide women lifelong opportunities and support for social, intellectual and spiritual development by bringing women together to positively impact our communities.

name: Sigma Nu Fraternity
summary: Founded in 1895 at NC State, the Sigma Nu Beta Tau chapter is committed to developing ethical leaders via the principles of Love, Honor, and Truth.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon Fraternity
description:
Sigma Phi Epsilon is a social fraternity based on the Cardinal Principles of Virtue, Diligence, and Brotherly Love. Founded at NC State on March 4th, 1905, the NC Beta chapter strives to maximize the college experience of its members. While brothers maintain high standards of academic and personal development, the chapter also succeeds by continuing a strong social presence on campus.

Through the use of the award-winning Balanced Man Development Program, we look to continuously develop our members into the world's next leaders through a 4-year process. This program allows for members to stay involved and continue their personal development that is of vital importance throughout their time at NC State.
summary: Sigma Phi Epsilon was founded at the University of Richmond on November 1, 1901, on the Cardinal Principles: Virtue, Diligence, and Brotherly Love.

name: Ski & Snowboard Club Team
description: The Ski & Snowboard Club Team at NC State University is an active club that races with other universities in the Southeast. We will have a few events and complete registration during the fall semester. During the spring semester, we go on 5 race trips followed by regionals and nationals. Dues include lodging, lift tickets, entrance fees, and dinner. If you have little or no experience, you should still come check us out!
summary: The Ski & Snowboard Club at NC State University is an active club that races with other universities in the southeast. A handful of athletes also typically compete in the USCSA Nationals Competition.

name: Society for Biomaterials Student Chapter at North Carolina State University
description: This group has been created to socialize and communicate with various disciplines at NC State who have the same burning desire of learning about bio materials, its use and their awesome cool researches.
summary: This group has been created to socialize and communicate with various disciplines at NC State who have the same burning desire of learning about bio materials...

name: Society for Human Resources Management at NC State
description:
Membership Plans:

  • Fall Semester Plan: $15.00
  • Year-long Plan: $20.00
  • All dues are to be venmoed to @NCSU-SHRM
  • Spring Semester Dues are Open!

Join our email list!

https://forms.gle/76SBErfvzdtFRwjV8

What is SHRM?

NCSU SHRM provides NC State students with the opportunity to gain knowledge and insight into the effective management of human capital in the field of Human Resource Management and to act as an important vehicle for promotion. We encourage the acquisition, growth and continuation of our respective careers in human resource management through expanded knowledge, respect and mutual support among student chapter members and through our affiliation with SHRM and our sponsoring chapter. 

 

Why should you join NCSU SHRM?

Networking:

  • Exposure to established HR professionals, NC State Alumni, and the Raleigh Metro SHRM professional group
  • Guest speakers from a variety of backgrounds
  • Internship and job opportunities

Leadership Experience:

  • If you are Interested in gaining leadership experience there are many opportunities to take an active role on our SHRM board
  • We will be recruiting for the 2025 Spring Semester and beyond! 

HR Exposure:

  • Learning and development in various areas of HR that go beyond the classroom
  • Development workshops 
  • Resume and LinkedIn building
summary: NCSU SHRM helps NC State students gain HR management skills and advance their careers. We support growth and professional development through knowledge sharing, respect, and our affiliation with SHRM and our sponsoring chapter.

name: Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics Student Chapter
description: The goals of SIAM at the national/international level are to: -- Advance the application of mathematics and computational science to engineering, industry, science, and society; -- Promote research that will lead to effective new mathematical and computational methods and techniques for science, engineering, industry, and society; -- Provide media for the exchange of information and ideas among mathematicians, engineers, and scientists. The NCSU student chapter of SIAM provides a forum in which students at NCSU can pursue math research focused on applications in science and industry. Additionally, we facilitate interactions between students, faculty, and SIAM through official SIAM activities of the chapter.
summary: The NCSU student chapter of SIAM provides a forum in which students at NCSU can pursue math research focused on applications in science and industry.

name: Society for Undergraduate Mathematics
description: SUM Club puts on academic and social programs for undergraduate students in mathematics and any students interested in math. Our goal is to have the math students network and learn about opportunities to further their educational experience. Meetings are currently scheduled for generally biweekly on Friday from 5:30-6:30pm in SAS 2235. Please reach out if you would like to join our email list for more information about upcoming meetings and events.
summary: SUM Club is dedicated to bringing together the undergraduate math community through social, service, and academic programs. Our mission is to create a strong network between students, the Math Department, the College of Sciences, and the real world.

name: Society of American Foresters Chapter at NC State
description:
The Society of American Foresters (SAF) is the largest professional society representing foresters and natural resource professionals. SAF for students is a means of connecting with professionals to find internships or jobs, but more generally it is a resource for students to develop relationships in the professional realm. Another function of SAF is to accredit universities and colleges' curricula in forestry. Each university with an accredited degree has the opportunity to establish a student chapter. NC State's SAF student chapter is a group accessible to both undergraduate and graduate students. 
 
The NC State student chapter brings together students from all stages of the forestry program, as well as students in other natural resource fields. We provide a unique networking and field experience to students to better prepare them for their future career. Student members also engage in coordinated meetings and conferences at the national and local levels, associated with Appalachian SAF (APSAF) and the Triangle SAF chapter. Each year, students have the opportunity to attend a backpacking trip prior to the national SAF conference. This serves as an opportunity to develop friendships with peers followed by the conference providing numerous opportunities for jobs, internships, research, and technology.
 
The club also offers events to benefit its members such as professional speaker engagements and technical workshops both of which are held throughout the year. Finally, each year the club hosts a  professionals and students social in April for students to network and enjoy fellowship.
summary: We are a student chapter of the Society of American Foresters, a national professional organization connecting with forestry professionals. Our goal is to support students entering the field by providing networking and development opportunities.

name: SME Student Chapter at NC State
description:
The Society of Manufacturing Engineers (SME) is an international professional organization dedicated to serving the manufacturing community by promoting advanced manufacturing technology and developing a skilled workforce. The objectives of the Chapter shall be to promote manufacturing through organized effort in study, research, and discussion of the profession and the dissemination of the knowledge thereby gained. The Chapter shall promote membership in the SME by acquainting the student body with the ideas, purposes, and objectives of the SME.

If you are not currently on our email list, please email ncstate-sme@ncsu.edu to be added to our email list.

Link to join SME: https://www.sme.org/sme-membership/join-sme/
summary: The Society of Manufacturing Engineers (SME) is an international professional organization dedicated to serving the manufacturing community by promoting advanced manufacturing technology and developing a skilled workforce.

name: Society for Multicultural Scientists
description: Our primary goal is to increase the recruitment, retention and graduation rate of students from groups underrepresented in the College of Sciences. We work to create community among diverse groups of students in the Sciences by the implementation of social, enrichment and service events that invaluably benefit students interested in the areas of biological sciences, chemistry, genetics, geology, marine sciences, mathematics, meteorology, microbiology, physics, statistics, and zoology. We are open to all students interested in cultural diversity, science outreach, and service. Recruit. Retain. Graduate.
summary: Our primary goal is to increase the recruitment, retention and graduation rates of students from groups underrepresented in areas of study in the College of Sciences.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: SPS is an organization dedicated to the advancement and diffusion of knowledge of physics, the encouragement of interest in physics throughout the academic and local communities, and the introduction of students to the professional community. SPS is also dedicated to the development of community, both social and professional, in physics.
summary: The Society of Physics Students (SPS) is an organization for students interested in any field of physics, looking for collaboration and partnership in the form of guest speakers, service trips, research presentations,  networking, and more.

name: Sociology and Anthropology Graduate Student Association
description:
On behalf of the Sociology and Anthropology Graduate Student Association (SAGSA), I would like to welcome you to North Carolina State University! As a student run organization, the SAGSA’s primary goal is to ensure that all of our graduate students succeed as they pursue their PhD and MA at NC State. Our constitution states that the purpose of the SAGSA is to promote the academic and professional development of all graduate students in sociology and anthropology. In order to do this, we prioritize fostering an environment that is collaborative rather than competitive. By creating strong relationships with fellow students and faculty, the skills necessary for success can be cultivated. This includes enhanced teaching, publishing, networking, presentational skills, and more

 

The SAGSA meets every month to discuss departmental developments.

 

Above all else the SAGSA is here to provide our students with a unified voice. If you have already been accepted as an incoming student, congratulations, we look forward to meeting you! If you are currently exploring graduate programs, we hope you will consider us as a competitive match to other universities.
summary: The Sociology and Anthropology. Graduate Student Association serves to promote the academic, social and professional development of graduate students in sociology and anthropology. Membership is open to all graduate students in the department.

name: Spanish Club at North Carolina State University
description: The purpose of the club is to provide the opportunity for members to speak Spanish and increase their knowledge and awareness of the Spanish-speaking world through language, film, music, cuisine and history. Meetings are held weekly on Tuesdays in Withers Hall, room 331, from 6:30-7:30 PM. Although rare, the specific location can change depending on event, so check your email for details as we will send weekly updates about what we're doing for each meeting!
summary: The purpose of the club is to provide the opportunity for members to speak Spanish and to increase their knowledge and awareness of the Spanish-speaking world.

name: STARS Computing Corps
description:
The STARS@NCSU (Students in Technology, Academia, Research and Service) Computing Corps is a student organization that provides students with the opportunity to learn more about computer science careers, participate in service and outreach programs to local schools, engage in research, and meet with leaders in the computer field! The STARS@NCSU is part of the larger STARS Alliance, a system of national partnerships among colleges and universities, industry, primary schools and the community to collaboratively implement programs for student recruitment and success in computing.

Check out go.ncsu.edu/stars_info for more information about upcoming events. 

To join, please contact Ally Limke (anlimke@ncsu.edu) to be added to the mailing list.
summary: The STARS@NCSU (Students & Technology in Academia, Research and Service) Student Computing Corps is a student organization that provides students with the opportunity to learn more about computer science careers...

name: Statistics Graduate Student Organization
description: Statistics chapter of the Graduate Student Association. Membership is automatic when enrolled as a graduate student in the statistics department.
summary: Statistics Graduate Student Association

name: Stats Club at NC State
description:
Stats Club at NC State is for those interested in statistics and related professions or for those just looking to meet and socialize with other stats majors. The purpose of the club is to expose undergraduates to the endless applications of statistics and what a career in stats really looks like, along with providing opportunities for members to network. We have a variety of speakers who talk about their research/careers/education which range from working for famous sports teams to working at the FBI. We also hold workshops that can help you hone your programming skills.

Please email us at statistics-club@ncsu.edu with any questions, suggestions, or to be added to our email list!
summary: Stats Club is for those interested in statistics and related professions.

name: Student Alumni Association
