description:
The Student Alumni Association provides personal and professional mentorship benefits, including access to the NC Stace alumni network, opportunities for service and leadership, and numerous events throughout the year. The Student Alumni Association is your pass to an unforgettable NC State experience and a foundation for your future!
By joining SAA, you’ll dive into the heart of Wolfpack traditions, like the excitement of Homecoming, the fun of Ram Roast and the pride of the Celebration of Pack Traditions ceremony. SAA gives you the opportunity to meet passionate alumni who’ve been in your shoes and are ready to share their stories, advice and connections to help you thrive academically and get a start on your career.
summary: The Student Alumni Association provides personal and professional mentorship benefits, including access to the NC Stace alumni network, opportunities for service and leadership, and numerous exciting events throughout the year!

name: Student Chapter of the American Association of Bovine Practitioners
description: SCAABP is a student chapter of the American Association of Bovine Practitioners organization. The club is  for veterinary students at NC State interested in learning about bovine production, medicine, and veterinary practice. Additionally, it allows students the opportunity to participate in Quiz Bowl, Palpation Team, and attend the annual AABP meeting hosted in different locations around the country.
summary: SCAABP is a student chapter of the AABP, allowing for veterinary students at NC State to grow their interests and experience in the field of bovine production and medicine.

name: Student Chapter of the American Association of Equine Practitioners
description: SCAAEP, Student Chapter of the American Association of Equine Practitioners, is a student run organization that provides numerous educational opportunities for students in veterinary school and those wishing to attend, veterinarians around the country, and people within the community that hold an interest in equine medicine. SCAAEP organizes a variety of labs that provide hands on experience to veterinary students in areas such as diagnostic procedures, colic work ups, farriery, dentistry, safe horse handling, ophthalmic exams, joint injections, nerve blocks, and much more! SCAAEP also plans continuing education events for veterinarians such as Equine Health Symposium in February of each year as well as various short courses and lunch meetings throughout the year. Giving back to the community is important to SCAAEP. SCAAEP plans a Horseman's Youth Day in the Fall of each year to educate our future generation about equine medicine and husbandry. SCAAEP also provides free castration clinics bi-annually to help alleviate overpopulation and behavioral issues for people who cannot afford it otherwise. We provide assistance at horse shows all throughout the state such as helping in the D-box at horse trials. As a club, SCAAEP members also attend various AAEP events to supplement their veterinary education, such as FOCUS on Students and the national AAEP convention.
summary: We provide additional learning opportunities for equine-interested students, continuing education in equine medicine, as well as service projects for the community.

name: Student Chapter of the Feline Veterinary Medical Association
description: The Student Chapter of the Feline Veterinary Medical Association (SCFVMA, formerly known as SCAAFP) is a veterinary student organization dedicated to promoting interest in feline medicine and surgery. Through rounds discussions, lectures, wet labs, and community service activities, we hope to increase awareness of all feline-related issues.
summary: The Student Chapter of the Feline Veterinary Medical Association (SCFVMA, formerly known as SCAAFP) is a veterinary student organization dedicated to promoting interest in feline medicine and surgery.

name: Student Chapter of the American Association of Small Ruminant Practitioners
description: As a student-governed organization, we will strive to provide opportunities for all members to enhance their veterinary education regarding sheep, goat, camelid, and all other small ruminants by organizing and facilitating events that promote engagement and interaction with topics encompassed by this focus area.
summary: A student group interested in medicine related to sheep, goat, camelids, and all other small ruminants, organizing and facilitating events that promote engagement and interaction with the local small ruminant community.

name: Student Chapter of the American College of Veterinary Internal Medicine
description: As a student chapter, we believe that SCACVIM can provide many benefits to the students of NCSU CVM. For both small animal focus and large animal focus students, the SCACVIM will offer a minimum of two wetlabs each semester. These wet lab experiences will allow students the opportunity to get more hands on experience with techniques and contact time with our faculty. A lecture series including case discussions on various topics in veterinary medicine is being organized to supplement material presented in our current curriculum. Another unique feature of the SCACVIM is our monthly Clinician Meet and Greet, which allow students to interact with internal medicine clinicians and house officers in a relaxed environment to discuss various topics pertaining to their career choices, training experiences, and their life outside of the CVM.
summary: The Student Chapter of the American College of Veterinary Internal Medicine aims to provide veterinary students with opportunities for additional learning within the veterinary internal medicine specialty beyond what is provided in the classroom.

name: Student Chapter of the American Holistic Veterinary Medical Association
description:
(Official, please ignore the old account with the darker blue logo as of 2025):

The Holistic Medicine Club is an organization that provides educational opportunities in holistic veterinary medicine for veterinary students. Holistic medicine is a rapidly growing field that provides alternatives to traditional methods of healing. This club sponsors lectures on a variety of topics including acupuncture, chiropractics, herbs, and homeopathy.
summary: The Holistic Medicine Club is an organization that provides educational opportunities in holistic veterinary medicine for veterinary students.

name: Student Chapter of the Veterinary Business Management Association
description: The Veterinary Business Management Association is a student-driven organization dedicated to advancing the profession through increasing business knowledge, creating networking opportunities, and empowering veterinary students to achieve their personal and professional goals.
summary: Empowering future veterinarians to reach their business goals and advance the veterinary profession.

name: Student Fisheries Society
description: We are an active group of undergraduate and graduate students intrigued by the ecology and conservation of fisheries. We meet monthly to socialize, organize upcoming activities, and listen to fisheries related guest lecturers. The SFS prides itself on its involvement within the community. We encourage our member to assist with children's fishing events, stream clean-ups, freshwater mussel salvage, and aquatic research. The SFS holds numerous social activities throughout the year and sponsors selected members to travel to fisheries related conferences via travel scholarships.
summary: We are an active group of undergraduate and graduate students interested in fisheries. We meet monthly to discuss activities and learn from a speaker.

name: Student Government
description: Founded in 1921, Student Government is a student-run organization that serves as the official voice of the student body to better the student experience at NC State University. Both a governing body and an advocacy group, Student Government is involved in policy-making, adjudication, lobbying, community service, and countless other activities. The organization is a clearinghouse for concerns from students and answers from administrators. Student Government is an organization comprised of four main Bodies: the Executive Branch, the Student Senate, the Treasury Branch, and the Board of Elections. The Executive Branch is responsible for initiatives and implementing policies passed by Student Senate, Student Senate is responsible for making policy, the Treasury Branch oversees the internal budget and student organization appropriations, and the Board of Elections manages all elections for the organization. For more information, check out our website!
summary: Founded in 1921, Student Government is a student-run, student-funded organization acting as a representative, advocacy, and governing entity for the student body.

name: Student North Carolina Association of Educators at NC State
description: The SNCAE (Student North Carolina Association of Educators) is the largest organization of college students preparing to be classroom teachers in North Carolina, and we are NC State's chapter! If you believe in public education, we'd love for you to join. Not only do members receive $1,000,000 in liability insurance protection, but also have access to NCAE and NEA workshops and conferences tailored to fit the needs of future teachers. Most importantly, members have a unique opportunity to make valuable relationships, learn invaluable skills, and form a network of people with whom you share the same passion. Visit the SNCAE website to sign up and become a member.
summary: The SNCAE (Student North Carolina Association of Educators) is the largest organization of college students preparing to be classroom teachers in North Carolina, and we are NC State's chapter! If you believe in public education, we'd love for you to join

name: SEVA Bhakti Yoga Club
description: BYC facilitates the study and practice of bhakti-yoga, which the timeless Bhagavad-gita extols as the summit of spiritual yoga. Through philosophy, music, dance, and fellowship, we open our hearts and minds, while working to transform the world. We serve the NCSU community through compassionate, informed, spiritual activism; addressing vital and interwoven issues such as social and economic justice, environmental concerns and individual enlightenment. Through personal and interpersonal growth, BYC students learn to live peacefully in a stressful world. Further, they develop the skills necessary to play leading roles in Earth's critical future.
summary: SEVA Bhakti Yoga facilitates the study and practice of bhakti-yoga, which the timeless Bhagavad-gita extols as the summit of spiritual yoga.

name: Sube Ritmo Latin Dance Team
description: As NC State's first Latin dance team, Sube Ritmo offers members the opportunity to educate NC State students and the surrounding community about various Latin American dance styles. Formed over 20 years ago as La Raza de Mil Colores, our mission has been to unify and engage communities in celebrating Latin culture through dance. To join the team, tryouts are held at the beginning of each semester. If you can't join the team but want to get involved, we host many workshops, dance events, and collaborations with other student orgs throughout the year.
summary: Sube Ritmo is NC State's first Latin dance team, providing members with the opportunity to educate NC State students and the surrounding community about various styles of dancing from Latin America.

name: Surgery Club at NC State CVM
description:
An organization dedicated:

1. To promote interest in veterinary surgery and provide exposure to aspects of veterinary surgery outside of classroom experience.

2. To promote communication between surgical faculty and students interested in pursuing surgical internships and residencies, including connecting students with faculty mentors.

3. To provide students with opportunities to learn about new advancements in the field of veterinary surgery.
summary: An organization dedicated to providing veterinary students with additional knowledge and skills in both small and large animal veterinary surgery

name: Table Tennis Club at NC State University
description:
This club provides an organized structure for the participation and development of the sport of table tennis for the NC State University community. Whether you are looking for recreational play or competitive play and are a beginner or professional player, we welcome all to come and join us. Check our website for more information (https://tabletennisclub.wordpress.ncsu.edu), email us (ncsutabletennis@gmail.com), or follow our Instagram page (ncsutabletennis) if you have any questions. You can also find our discord server listed on the NC State discord server hub.

 

We will be holding practices and tournaments throughout the 2025-2026 academic year!
summary: Table Tennis Club is great for meeting new people and growing in the sport of table tennis. We welcome both competitive and casual players, with practices twice a week. We also bear the collegiate table tennis team for NCSU.

name: Taiwanese Student Association at North Carolina State University
description:
Welcome to the Taiwanese Student Association at NC State!
We’re a group of passionate students from Taiwan who love sharing our culture, traditions, and delicious food with the Wolfpack community. Whether you're from Taiwan, interested in the culture, or just want to make new friends, you’re warmly invited to join us! We’re here to support new students from Taiwan and create a fun, inclusive space where everyone can connect, learn, and enjoy. Come explore Taiwanese culture with us — everyone is welcome!
summary: We make efforts to help and assist the new students from Taiwan, introduce Taiwanese culture and food to every Wolfpack.

name: Tau Beta Pi - NC Alpha Chapter
description: Tau Beta Pi was founded in 1885 to recognize outstanding students across all fields of engineering who display both distinguished scholarship in technical fields and exemplary character. Tau Beta Pi is highly regarded within industry and academia, and has grown to include 255 collegiate chapters and more than 633,000 members (as of 2023). The North Carolina Alpha Chapter at North Carolina State University, established in 1925, is proud to support members from across the College of Engineering.
summary: Tau Beta Pi was founded in 1885 to recognize outstanding students across all fields of engineering who display both distinguished scholarship in technical fields and exemplary character.

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
description:
Vision
Tau Kappa Epsilon creates lifelong relationships that enhance educational, interpersonal, community and professional success.

Mission
Our mission is to aid men in their mental, moral, and social development for life.

Purpose
TKE contributes to the advancement of society through the personal growth of our members, and service to others.

Status: Organizational Probation through September 30, 2026.

Violations: Alcohol (b), FSL Social Events Policy, Failure To Comply (c)
summary: Tau Kappa Epsilon Fraternity

name: Technical Association of the Pulp & Paper Industry
summary: The Student Chapter of TAPPI at NC State provides a link between the professional, business, and industrial areas of the Paper and Pulp Industry and students in NC State's Paper Science & Engineering Department!

name: Technical Communication Association
description:
Technical Communication Association (TCA) represents students of technical communication at NC State. We aim to promote awareness about latest trends in the field of technical communication and achieve active collaboration between students and local technical communication industry. The student organization works closely with the English Department's Master of Science in Technical Communication (TCA) program. TCA is a registered non-profit organization under Federal Law.

We welcome graduate and undergraduate students as well as everyone interested in technical communication. Our members come from diverse backgrounds. They bring a variety of skill sets, international exposure, intercultural experience, and diverse research interests to the organization. We have a strong network of alumni, who are making their mark in the technical communication industry across the USA and beyond.

In addition to other events, TCA hosts its flagship conference Speedcon that provides budding technical writers a stage to present their ideas to industry professionals. Attended by local decision-makers and field experts, Speedcon offers a powerful networking opportunity to its members.
summary: Technical Communication Association (TCA) represents students of technical communication at NC State.

name: Technician
description: Technician, North Carolina State University's student-run newspaper, has been serving the campus community since 1920. It employs more than 100 students throughout the year and is instrumental in providing students, faculty, staff and alumni with campus news, sports, opinion, and arts and entertainment coverage that pertains to the NC State community. Technician is published in print every Thursday during the academic year and maintains continues news updates online at www.technicianonline.com throughout the calendar year. All final decisions about editorial content and advertising rest with the Editors-in-Chief, who is selected by the NC State Student Media Board of Directors.
summary: Technician, North Carolina State University's student-run newspaper, has been serving the campus community since 1920.

name: Technology and Engineering Education Collegiate Association
description:
TEECA is a student organization designed to promote professional development for future Technology and Engineering Educators. 


Join us in making and doing!

 

College of Education Recognized as a STEM School of Excellence by the International Technology and Engineering Educators Association
summary: One goal of technology education is to generate a general appreciation and understanding of modern technology. The program focuses on many of the technology skills required by our society as it faces increasing competition in a global economy.

name: Textile Technology and Engineering Society at NC State
description: Textile Engineering Society (TTES) is an organization open to all students who are curious or passionate about Textile Engineering and Textile Technology. We provide opportunities to learn more about the textile industry, bond with peers, and develop professionally.
summary: The goal of TTES is to build a network of motivated Textile Engineering and Textile Technology students, faculty, and alumni.

name: Horticulture Club at NC State
description:
A club that celebrates the diverse world of horticulture from your home to the professional field! We are open to ALL students, not just students studying horticulture!

Meetings: We have meetings every other Monday evening at 5:30pm in Kilgore Hall 159, room subject to change. We have activities and/or guest speakers and provide food at our meeting! Examples of activities include plant swaps, pumpkin painting, etc. 

Dues: $10, a great bargain for food at every meeting!!!

Opportunities: Typically, we offer a regional fall trip during fall break and a more local spring trip to significant horticultural sites and businesses. Additionally, we have a plant sale every semester from plants grown by us, now with our own greenhouse! We also offer occasional local garden visits and volunteer opportunities with PAX Honors Horticultural Fraternity and other local horticulture organizations. Lastly, you can compete alongside other high achieving horticulture students in HortPack at the National Collegiate Landscaping Competition (NCLC)!

Our Google Calendar 

EMAIL LIST/INTEREST FORM

Add it to your calendar by clicking the + 

Contact Us! Please email the sitting president and/or visit our Instagram to send us a message!
summary: A club that enjoys horticulture. We have meetings every other Monday evening at 5:30pm. We always have some sort of activity or guest speaker, as well as food!

name: The Investors' Association at NC State
description:
The Investors' Association (IA) is dedicated to expanding members' financial knowledge, building a strong network with industry professionals, and enhancing technical skills. We explore various investment vehicles, including stocks, mutual funds, ETFs, bonds, forex, futures, commodities, options, and real estate.

Additionally, we host guest speakers specializing in asset and wealth management careers and help prepare students for careers in investment banking (IB).

If you're interested in learning about investing, developing technical skills, and pursuing a finance career, IA is the perfect organization for you!
summary: An investment club dedicated to insightful discussions on stocks, mutual funds, ETFs, bonds, forex, futures, commodities, options, and real estate. We empower members with financial knowledge, strategic analysis, and a collaborative learning environment.

name: The Krispy Kreme Challenge
description:
The Krispy Kreme Challenge is an annual, student-run race benefiting the UNC Children's Hospital. Beginning in 2004 with a mere 12 participants, the race has grown exponentially and has rapidly become one of NC State's most cherished traditions. Over the past 20 years, we have donated over $2.1 million to the UNC Children's Hospital. Beginning at the NC State Belltower, each runner runs 2.5 miles through Downtown Raleigh to the Krispy Kreme store located at the intersection of Peace St. and Person St. After attempting to eat a dozen of the famous original glazed Krispy Kreme doughnuts, participants run the two and a half miles back. This is the Krispy Kreme Challenge.

 

To join the Krispy Kreme Challenge committee, please submit the application found here: go.ncsu.edu/k2c23
summary: 2400 calories, 12 doughnuts, 5 miles, 1 hour.  The mantra of the Krispy Kreme Challenge epitomizes the test of physical fitness and gastrointestinal fortitude for a good cause - to support the UNC Children's Hospital.

name: Philosophy Club at NC State
description:
The Philosophy Club aims to foster a more intelligent discussion on issues that face all members of our society. Our meetings, activities, special events, and research initiatives all focus on bringing the benefits of a philosophical way of thinking out to the community at large. We encourage participation by students, faculty, and community members. All Club functions, unless otherwise noted, are open to the public.

NCSU Instagram: https://instagram.com/philosophy.club.ncsu?igshid=MWZjMTM2ODFkZg==

Club GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/102735461/1GUuMvJD

NOTE: To request membership, sign in to Get Involved and click the join button
summary: The Philosophy Club aims to foster a more intelligent discussion on issues that face all members of our society.

name: Poole College of Management Council
description:
Who we are:
Leadership Council is 1 of 7 Leadership Organizations within the Poole College of Management. We serve as the bridge between the Poole student body and the Poole student organizations and administration. Being a part of this organization means having a voice and the ability to create meaningful change within the Poole College of Management.

What we do:
We host events for Poole College, such as Poole Party, Wellness Week, the Poole Student Org Kickball Tournament, Presidential Roundtable (PRT) meetings, philanthropic & charity events, and social gatherings for Poole Council and PRT. 

Why you should join:
Leadership Council is an incredible way to grow both personally and professionally in an informal setting. You will have the opportunity to bring students together, create change within the Poole College of Management, and expand your network with fellow Poole students. Our organization is growing, and we are looking for passionate and dedicated students to join us!

Applications will open in the spring 2026 semester.

Requirements: You need to be an NC State Freshman or Sophomore and have a Major and or Minor in the Poole College of Management. 

Time Commitment: Leadership Council meets biweekly for one hour, and members are required to attend committee meetings when necessary throughout the semester; as well as, volunteer at our events throughout the semester.
summary: Specifically, The Leadership Council hall act as a liaison between the college and NC State University Student Government.

name: The Society of Women Engineers
description:
WHO ARE WE?

The Society of Women Engineers (SWE), founded in 1950, is a not-for-profit educational and service organization. SWE is the driving force that establishes engineering as a highly desirable career aspiration for women. SWE empowers women to succeed and advance in those aspirations and be recognized for their life-changing contributions and achievements as engineers and leaders. SWE's motive is to stimulate women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expand the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity.

 

Our Mission:

  • Stimulate women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders
  • Expand the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life
  • Demonstrate the value of diversity
  • To encourage and support other women as they pursue their careers
  • To be a place for women engineers to network

What We Do:

  • Networking and career related events with company sponsors
  • Regional conference to interact with employers and SWE members from all over the country
  • Provide support with local collegiate network for other female students in engineering

Benefits of Membership:

  • Build skills useful for your career
  • Networking opportunities with both companies and classmates
  • Role models and advice from successful women in SWE
  • Information about companies, graduate schools, and careers
  • Fun!

We highly encourage you to become part of national SWE, which will allow you to attend the SWE National Conference, get the SWE magazine, and network with like-minded individuals across the world. A student membership is $20/year or $50 for your entire college career. See swe.org for more information
summary: To stimulate women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders. The world's largest advocate for women in engineering and technology.

name: Sports Analytics Club at NC State
description:
The Sports Analytics Club at NC State:
- A student-run organization
- Committed to the quantitative analysis of sports strategy and management
- Open to all students and faculty members of NCSU
- Enables students to share ideas and sports research projects
- Weekly meetings on Monday from 6:00-7:00pm at SAS 1108
- Please fill out our Interest Form
summary: The Sports Analytics Club is a student-run organization committed to the quantitative analysis of sports strategy and management. Open to all majors and experience.

name: Theta Tau Rho
description: Theta Tau is the Professional Gender Inclusive Engineering Fraternity at NC State. We have a wide variety of members covering almost all kinds of engineering. We take pride in maintaining a high standard of academic integrity while developing a strong fraternal fellowship among brothers. We give our members the tools to make an impact while on campus, as well as after graduation.
summary: Theta Tau is the Professional Gender Inclusive Engineering Fraternity at NC State. Our main focuses are Professional Development, Brotherhood, and Service.

name: The Thomas Jefferson Scholars at NC State
description: The aim of the Thomas Jefferson Scholars is to provide an environment of fellowship and support.  We combine intellectual and social fun, among students who are interested in both science and the humanistic disciplines.  We meet biweekly for programs, go on trips, and do service projects.  Scholars major in one of the biological or agricultural sciences and in one of the humanities or social sciences. We have a special course just for the Jeffersons each year.  Our Scholars are also members of other enrichment programs, including the the University Scholars and the Honors programs, and the Caldwell and Park fellowships. Successful graduates of the Jefferson Scholars Program receive a Bachelor's degree from each College.
summary: The Thomas Jefferson Scholars is a dual-degree program, the students of which are enrolled in degrees offered by the College of Agriculture and Life Sciences as well as the College of Humanities and Social Sciences.

name: Tompkins Textile Student Council
description:
TTSC is the main governing body over all Wilson College Student Organizations We meet once a month and all are welcome to attend. TTSC plans and/or promotes student-related activities and events within the College, such as the career fair, new student cookouts, blood drives, and an annual t-shirt design contest. TTSC members also serve on all of the standing Dean's committees within the College in order to represent the Undergraduate student body. The council consists of class representatives, organization representatives, and an executive council.

All Wilson College students are invited and welcome to attend meetings. Come to a meeting to find out what going on in textiles!
summary: TTSC is the main governing body over all Textile Student Organizations. We host events to promote community building and work with faculty and students for the betterment of the college. All are welcome to attend our meetings!

name: Toxicology Graduate Student Association
description: The Toxicology Graduate Student Association (TGSA) represents students enrolled in or affiliated with the Toxicology Program in the Department of Biological Sciences. Our curriculum and research goals focus primarily on mechanisms and outcomes of toxicity, and how exposure to various toxicants may adversely impact human and ecosystem health. As an organization, the TGSA works to enrich the graduate experience for current students by coordinating networking events with Toxicology alumni, bringing in speakers for seminar talks, as well as orientation programming for new incoming students. If you would like more information, please check out our website where you can also find contact information for the current TGSA officers.
summary: The Toxicology Graduate Student Association (TGSA) represents students enrolled in or affiliated with the Toxicology Program in the Department of Biological Sciences.

name: Triathlon Club at NC State
description: The NC State Triathlon Club, which is affiliated with USA Triathlon and the Mid-Atlantic Collegiate Triathlon Conference (MACTC), aims to promote the sport of triathlon with NC State students while providing a supporting network of people for training and racing triathlons. The club provides resources and coaching for a wide variety of athletes. Many members join before they have ever completed a triathlon and others are seasoned veterans that have completed ironman distance races. The main goal of the club is to have fun while promoting a healthy lifestyle. Club practices are held throughout the week in each of the three sports. The club competes in individual races in North Carolina and numerous out of state triathlons including Collegiate Nationals. twitter.com/NCSUTri facebook.com/NCSUTri
summary: Affiliated with USA Triathlon and the Mid-Atlantic Collegiate Triathlon Conference (MACTC), aims to promote the sport of triathlon with NC State students while providing a supporting network of people for training and racing triathlons.

name: Turkish Student Association
description: Turkish Student Association (TSA) seeks to represent Turkish students at NC State University. Its mission is to introduce Turkish culture to NC State community as well as the public.To advance social and cultural interaction among Turkish students as well as American and all international students. To introduce Turkish culture, art, cuisine, folklore, and music in the best possible way. To organize events such as picnics, cultural shows, concerts, trips and celebrations for its members and fellow students. To help new coming Turkish students to familiarize themselves with the NCSU community.
summary: TSA seeks to represent Turkish students at NC State University. Its mission is to introduce Turkish culture to NC State community as well as the public.

name: UAB Leadership Application Reviewers
summary: Edit your profile to add a summary!

name: AquaPack Robotics
description:
The AquaPack Robotics at NC State, founded in 2004, is a student-run organization that designs and builds autonomous submersible robots to participate in the annual RoboSub Competition. The event tasks teams from around the world to develop robots to complete complex tasks underwater.

Together the team works outside of the classroom to improve each other's technical skills and to spread the passion of robotics to the community. The club takes our robot, SeaWolf, to outreach events such as teaching elementary school students how they can get involved in robotics. AquaPack Robotics is student-run and always welcomes new members from any major and level of experience.

For more information and to see what we're up to, check out our website here!
summary: Like building robots? We are a club, open to any major, that designs, builds and programs an autonomous underwater robot for the annual RoboSub competition. We have four sub-teams that you can join- Mechanical, Electrical, Software, & Business.

name: Uninhibited Praise Gospel Choir
description:
The Uninhibited Praise Gospel Choir, also known as "UPGC", is a student organization on the campus of North Carolina State University. The Uninhibited Praise Gospel Choir, through its soulful interpretation of gospel music, moves audiences spiritually and creates community across racial, cultural, and economic boundaries. The purpose of this organization shall be to minister through song, encourage vocal growth, and create a positive social environment while winning souls to Christ. We strive to perform to the highest musical standards, hosting auditions every semester. We celebrate a love of gospel music, and seek to model community and reconciliation. We as an organization strive to promote vocal and spiritual growth by ministering to a diverse group of people. Being a student organization, we do advocate for the academic growth of the students involved in our organization as well as those who support us. We are an encouraging, engaging, upbeat inspirational ensemble, composed of persons from various backgrounds, races, and creeds. Our repertoire consists of spirituals and anthems along with varying forms of gospel/inspirational music. The Uninhibited Praise Gospel Choir also provides a forum in which gospel singers and musicians are able to transcend cultural differences. Our Gospel Choir is a vehicle for ministry, which educates and supports diversity outreach and minority recruiting efforts.

 

Please reach out to contact upgc-ncsu-org@ncsu.com for more information.
summary: The Uninhibited Praise Gospel Choir ministers through song, encourages vocal growth, and creates a positive social environment while winning souls to Christ. We strive to perform to the highest musical standards to enrich lives.

name: University Activities Board
description:
We are a student-led organization that plans both original and traditional events to attract students from all backgrounds. Comprised of four unique committees, the University Activities Board puts on programs with endless and ever-changing topics. We strive to enhance the student experience by providing fun, yet informative, campus programs to give students a break from academics.

Mission: The University Activities Board innovates programs, for students by students, that enhance the NC State experience.
summary: By students, for student. Bringing you the best activities for over 30 years. UAB is your place for Student Activities. Speakers, Concerts, Films and everything in between.

name: Graduate Student Association
description: Welcome to the SORC / OrgSync page for the GSA. Please feel free to visit our website - gsa.ncsu.edu - for more information about the GSA. Thanks!
summary: GSA represents all NC State graduate students. We are currently only utilizing this system for the Exec board and OIT. Please feel free to visit our website - gsa.ncsu.edu - for more information about the NC State GSA. Thanks!

name: Veterinary Educational Textbooks and Supplies
description: V.E.T.S. is a student-run, non-profit organization dedicated to making the acquisition of veterinary textbooks and supplies easier and more affordable. We give back to the CVM community through donations to various events and organizations.
summary: V.E.T.S. is a student-run, non-profit organization dedicated to making the acquisition of veterinary textbooks and supplies easier and more affordable.

name: Video Game Development Club at North Carolina State University
description:
FOR UPDATED INFORMATION, VISIT OUR WEBSITE AT https://www.vgdc.org/  OR OUR CLUB DISCORD https://discord.com/invite/4KFqxy2 

Check us out on other socials: Linktree

Typical meetings are on Wednesday at 7:00 pm in Lampe Hall, Room 407. Location changes depending on certain events.
Please join our mailing list, check the website calendar, or join the Discord for info on upcoming meetings and events!

Time: Wednesdays 7 - 9 pm

    Want to make video games for a living? Want to at least make them for fun?

    The Video Game Development Club (VGDC) is a student-run organization with a mission: to provide an environment in which students can practice and grow in disciplines related to the development of video games. The club acts as a mentorship resource for those interested in video game development and the video game industry, a networking outlet for aspiring video game professionals, and as a functioning video game studio in the pursuit of hands-on experience through long-term projects.

    We welcome people from all disciplines, including art, engineering, the sciences, the humanities, and so on, who wish to create and build games--from serious simulation experiences to mobile games.

    Don't have a technical or creative skillset? No problem. We can help direct you to the resources you need to get started, and we'll help guide you as you learn.   At the end of the day, it's all about the game. See you there!
summary: The mission of The Video Game Development Club at North Carolina State University (VGDC at NCSU) is to provide an environment and community in which students can practice and grow in disciplines related to the development of video games.

name: Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) at North Carolina State University
description:
The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization composed of members who are interested in Vietnamese culture. Our mission is to strengthen the awareness of Vietnamese culture within NC State through our meetings, excursions, and events. We also outreach to the surrounding Vietnamese community and are a member of the Mid-Atlantic Union of Vietnamese Student Associations (MAUVSA). VSA hosts an annual Viet Night to promote Vietnamese culture with the help of Alumni, Vietnamese communities, and other VSA organizations in the surrounding area.

We look forward to meeting you! Please like us on Facebook and email us to get started!
summary: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) was founded to promote the awareness of the art, beauty, culture, diversity, and history of Vietnam to NC State and the greater Triangle community.

name: Wildlife, Avian, Aquatic, and Zoological Medicine
description: WAAZM is an active student organization dedicated to promoting interest, experience, and education in wildlife, avian, aquatic, and zoological medicine. WAAZM's mission is to benefit veterinary students interested in various career paths including: exotic animal private practice, wildlife rehabilitation/management, aquatic animal medicine, and zoo practice. The club hosts a variety of programs, wetlabs, events, trips, and speakers that complement the traditional small animal and large animal focused veterinary curriculum. Club membership is limited to current veterinary students.
summary: WAAZM is an active student organization dedicated to promoting interest, experience, and education in wildlife, avian, aquatic, and zoological medicine.

name: WKNC 88.1 FM HD-1/HD-2
description:
Instead of clicking the "Join" button here, we ask that you check out our Interest page instead to get involved with WKNC. We do not accept members through this website. 

https://youtu.be/-eVwyQJEnmM?si=8wYjx9Igf2QZWoVK

 

WKNC 88.1 FM HD-1/HD-2 is student-run non-commercial radio. We pride ourselves on our alternative programming of indie rock, electronic, underground hip-hop and metal and have been consistently named among the best in the Triangle by Independent Weekly. Boasting 25,000 watts, WKNC can be heard throughout the Triangle and far beyond via our webcast. Our aim is to provide NC State students with the knowledge needed for a career in the broadcast industry and listeners with music that doesn't suck.
summary: WKNC 88.1 FM HD-1/HD-2 is student-run non-commercial college radio, with students serving as on-air DJs, bloggers, videographers, podcasters, interviewers and reporters.

name: Wolfgang A Cappella
description: Wolfgang A Cappella (Est. 1997) is an all-gender a cappella group at NC State University created to build exceptional musical experiences. We sing a variety of pieces that aim to foster creativity and feel powerful, inspiring, and meaningful. We strive to promote personal growth, incite originality, and bring together a diverse group of individuals with a common love for music. Email us with any questions about auditioning or booking us for your events!
summary: Wolfgang is an all-gender a cappella group at NC State University created to build exceptional musical experiences.

name: Pack Clogging Club
description: Club Clogging at NC State, founded in 1989, is an award-winning, competitive clogging team. The team performs multiple styles including contemporary and traditional, across North Carolina and the east coast, including NC State's campus! Dancers meet twice a week to learn choreography, work on technique, and build community.
summary: Club Clogging at NC State is a competition and performance-based club team that travels around North Carolina and the east coast to dance and spread the art of clogging.

name: Environmental Student Association at NC State
description:
The Environmental Student Association at NC State (ESA) is a student club and nonprofit established and run by NC State students. The purpose of ESA is to promote environmental awareness and protection on North Carolina State University’s campus and within its colleges. ESA strives to connect with other universities, students, and Wake County residents interested in preserving natural resources in order to stimulate environmental support domestically and internationally. Although environmental advocacy is strongly encouraged, ESA, as a 501c3 nonprofit, cannot significantly influence legislation as part of its activities and/or participate in campaign activity for or against political candidates or else its nonprofit status will be revoked.

Additionally, members of this organization plan and lead environmental initiatives at NC State University, Wake County, and beyond. In addition to planning and executing environmental projects, ESA members also organize professional development activities such as job fairs and professional workshops.

If you are interested in joining ESA, please use this page to sign into your NC State account and request to be a member or follow us on the linked social media accounts for more information!
summary: ESA promotes environmental awareness at North Carolina State University, completes student-led environmental initiatives, and connects with other universities, students, and local citizens to stimulate environmental advocacy in Wake County and beyond.

name: Pack Twirlers
description:
The Wolfpack Majorettes has a long history going back to the start of the NC State Marching Band. Since the beginning, our program has grown to represent peak athletic ability in the twirling world with our members holding regional, national, and world championship titles while still representing the Wolfpack exuberance at NC State athletic events and pep rallies.

Our initiative is to spread school spirit through baton twirling in Raleigh-area and represent Wolfpack spirit whenever we compete or perform. We also aim to educate people about proper technique and history of the sport of baton twirling. Our final aim is to have our members grow as people by encouraging self-esteem, confidence, sportsmanship, and teamwork.
summary: The Wolfpack Twirlers promote baton twirling in the Raleigh area and represent the Wolfpack spirit at national and international baton twirling events.

name: Pack Motorsports Baja SAE
description:
Welcome to Pack Motorsports Baja SAE Team. We are a student-run organization at North Carolina State University in Raleigh, North Carolina. We design, build, and compete an off-road UTV style of racecar in annual collegiate competitions sponsored by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE). Our program is housed under the College of Engineering at N.C. State, but any member of the student body, graduate or undergraduate, can be a member regardless of your major. The unique opportunity to design and build a new racecar from scratch every year gives students a wealth of hands on engineering experience working on something they enjoy. Students studying engineering are given a chance to apply what they learn in the classroom and develop important skills that are applicable in the automotive engineering field.
We host recruiting events every fall but welcome anyone to join at any time. Reach out to us at ncstatebaja.com to learn how to join.

Please do not request to join via this page, as we will not accept the request.
summary: We are a student-run organization at North Carolina State University in Raleigh, North Carolina. We design, build, and compete with an off-road UTV style racecar in annual collegiate competitions sponsored by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE).

name: Club Quadball at NC State
description: Club Quadball at NC State is here to bring the growing sport of Muggle Quidditch -- now also known as 'Quadball'--  to the NCSU campus. We promote the sport of Quidditch and bridge the gap between fantasy enthusiasts and sports enthusiasts. The club competes against other college and community teams in the Mid-Atlantic region and aims to build community within the broader Quadball community through scrimmages, tournaments, and social events. We are an all-gender inclusive sport and require no prior experience to participate!
summary: Club Quadball (formerly quidditch)! An all-gender inclusive club sport mixing rugby, dodgeball, and flag football competing at the regional and national levels with a focus on building community, teamwork, and athletic skills.

name: Women in Mechanical and Aerospace at NC State University
description: Women in Mechanical and Aerospace is an organization within the Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering Department on Centennial Campus. WIMA empowers women in mechanical and aerospace disciplines to succeed and advance in their fields. We focus on providing our members with job, internship, scholarship/fellowship opportunities, academic support, professional development opportunities, and outreach events. Additionally, we seek to build personal and professional relationships between undergraduate students, graduate students, and professors within the organization. We encourage everyone with an interest in mechanical or aerospace to join regardless of major!
summary: We focus on empowering women in mechanical and aerospace disciplines to succeed and advance in their field.

name: Women in Computer Science
description:
The mission of the Women in Computer Science (WiCS) program is to support, promote, and retain women in computer science by providing opportunities for personal and professional development, social interaction, and outreach.

Visit our website: https://www.wicsatncsu.org/

Become a member: https://www.wicsatncsu.org/get-involved/
summary: The mission of the Women in Computer Science (WiCS) program is to support, promote, and retain women in computer science by providing opportunities for personal and professional development, social interaction, and outreach.

name: Women in Nuclear at NC State
description: Women in Nuclear at NC State is an organization where students in nuclear engineering or related fields engage young women to explore and possibly join the nuclear community. The organization is open to all genders.
summary: Women in Nuclear is an organization for students in nuclear engineering and related fields to contribute to the building of a nuclear community. For example, the organization engages with youth, especially young females to explore nuclear technology.

name: Women's Club Basketball
description:
We are a dedicated and motivated group of women who love the game of basketball. Through leadership, commitment, and sportsmanship, we strive to provide a competitive atmosphere for the game.

We practice 2 nights a week (8-10 pm), Tuesdays and Thursdays, and attend tournaments of all sizes on the weekends.

Follow us on Instagram to keep updated! (@ncsuwclubball)
summary: We are a dedicated and motivated group of women who love the game of basketball. Through leadership, commitment, and sportsmanship, we strive to provide a competitive atmosphere for the game. Our goal is to have a closely-knit team & create a community.

name: Women's Club Lacrosse at NC State
description:
We are a club sport open to all female NCSU students of all experience levels. We practice Mondays and Wednesdays from 5:00 to 7:00pm on the Lower Method Fields. For more information please email womensclublacrosse.ncsu@gmail.com!

Updated team site: https://sites.google.com/view/womens-club-lacrosse-ncsu/about
summary: We are a competitive club sport organized to promote the development of lacrosse skills and provide a safe, supportive environment for girls at NC State.

name: Women's Club Rugby
description: Women's Club Rugby at NC State is both a competitive and social team. We accept people of all shapes, sizes, and experience levels. Our team travels year-round throughout the southeastern US to play many different colleges and make new friends along the way. Not only do we host home games and travel to away games throughout our season, but we also frequent several tournaments during the course of the year. We do not hold tryouts and will teach new members everything they need to know about rugby. We practice on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 5 to 7 pm on the Upper Method Field of NC State's Campus. Please feel free to contact us or show up to practice if you are interested in seeing what this amazing sport is all about!
summary: We are a competitive and social club sports team at NC State University. Practices are 5-7 pm on Tuesdays and Thursdays at Method Rd. Fields. Come out and give it a try!

name: Women's Club Volleyball at NC State
description:
Our most up to date information can be found on our Facebook page (search NC State Women's Club Volleyball) or our organization website (see link below)! We will be frequently updating our new website with the following link. Please visit there for the best updated information.

https://sites.google.com/ncsu.edu/ncsuclubvolleyball/home

 

We are a competitive volleyball team that represents NC State at weekend tournaments against other universities. Our main goal is to prepare our team during the season in order to be competitive for Nationals. The club is a great experience and a great way to keep volleyball in your life and meet other people. Through Club Volleyball at NC State we work towards enhancing the collegiate experience through leadership, teamwork, sportsmanship, ethical and responsible conduct, and a healthy active lifestyle through the competitive nature of volleyball. We work towards creating a community of girls that work hard to compete as well as have fun.
summary: NCSU Women's Club Volleyball is a Club Sports Organization that participates in high level competitions throughout the semester. We practice twice a week, and represent NCSU as we compete against different universities at regional/national tournaments.

name: Women's Soccer Club At NC State
description: We aim to provide a competitive atmosphere for all those interested in joining the women's club soccer team. We are committed to providing a fun and safe environment for our players and encourage those who have a love and appreciation for the beautiful game to come to our tryouts in either the fall or the spring. We enjoy hosting our annual tournament in the spring and competing in the Southeast Collegiate Soccer Alliance league in the fall. Our club is a great way to make new friends and continue playing soccer at the club level!
summary: We aim to provide a competitive atmosphere for all those interested in joining the Women's Club Soccer team. We are committed to providing a fun and safe environment for our players.

name: Women's Ultimate Club
description:
Women's Club Ultimate Frisbee is part of the USA Ultimate Women’s Division and offers a chance for athletes of marginalized genders—including cis women, trans women, non-binary, and gender non-conforming people—to play a competitive sport in a friendly and fun atmosphere. You’ll join a welcoming community, make awesome new friends, and travel to tournaments throughout the Southeast region.

 

OUR PRACTICE SCHEDULE- Fall 2025 through Spring 2026

Starting after Labor Day, our regular practice times will be:

  • Monday & Tuesday: 6:30pm to 8:30pm

  • Thursday: 7:00pm to 9:00pm

  • Location: Method Fields (just up the street from main campus)

No worries about transportation—we organize carpools to help everyone get to practice on time!

We attend a few tournaments in the Fall, but our main competitive season starts in the Spring. So Fall is a perfect time to come out, meet the team, and give Ultimate a try!

Never played before? No problem! Join us at our first practices and we’ll teach you everything you need to know.

Learn more at this link… https://usaultimate.org/college/ 

 

Questions? Concerns? We'd love to talk with you. Send us an email at womensclubultimate.ncsu@gmail.com


Or Instagram @ncsujagamonsta
summary: Women's Club Ultimate Frisbee offers a chance for athletic women to play a competitive sport in a friendly and fun atmosphere.

name: Young Life College
description: A non-denominational Christian ministry that reaches out to adolescents through volunteers, staff, club meetings, and camps by building meaningful relationships.
summary: A non-denominational Christian ministry that reaches out to adolescents through volunteers, staff, club meetings, and camps by building meaningful relationships.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. the Mu Xi Chapter
description: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was founded on January 16, 1920 by five illustrious women whom the organization affectionately calls the Five Pearls. To our founders, Zeta was more than an organization— it was a movement, a belief system that reflected, at its core, the desire to embrace Scholarship, to provide true Service, to set a standard for Sisterly Love and to define the noble concept of Finer Womanhood.
summary: The purpose of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is to embrace Scholarship, provide true Service, set a standard for Sisterly Love and define the noble concept of Finer Womanhood.

name: Zoology Club
description: The purpose of this club shall be to advocate for animals in a changing world, promote the general studies of animals and their environments that they inhabit, and to provide information about the possible applications of a degree in Zoology.
summary: The purpose of this club is to advocate for animals in a changing world, promote the general studies of animals and the environments in which they inhabit, and to provide information about the possible applications of a degree in Zoology.

name: Graduate Student Association of Biology
description: We represent graduate students in the Biology Program across multiple departments, and the Fisheries, Wildlife, and Conservation Biology Program in the Department of Applied Ecology. We plan scholarly and social events for the departments represented.
summary: We represent graduate students in the Biology Program across multiple departments, and the Fisheries, Wildlife, and Conservation Biology Program in the Department of Applied Ecology.

name: Student Leadership and Engagement
description: Student Leadership and Engagement’s mission is to facilitate and connect students to meaningful leadership development, campus involvement, and civic and community engagement opportunities. We promote student engagement as a critical component of the educational experience. We create and connect students to opportunities to gain knowledge, skills, tools, and experiences to practice socially responsible leadership and positively contribute to a world that is socially just for all people.
summary: Student Leadership and Engagement’s mission is to facilitate and connect students to meaningful leadership development, campus involvement, and civic and community engagement opportunities.

name: Vintage Church on Campus
description:
Vintage Church on Campus is an outreach and discipleship arm of Vintage Church. We want to invite students to connect with Jesus Christ and one another. Look for on-campus events and community groups that will meet throughout the semester.

About Vintage Church
Vintage Church is a church of doubters, seekers, and followers who are learning to follow and worship Jesus Christ. All are welcome with no prerequisite of church background or prior belief. Bring your faith and your doubt, your joy and your tears, your praise and your selfishness. As a church, we want to emulate Jesus’ call in Matthew 11:28 for all to come to him.

Our Mission
Vintage exists to make much of Jesus. We do that by making disciples who know the gospel, live the gospel and advance the gospel.

Our Vision
Vintage strives to be a vibrant worshiping community committed to seeing people become everything God designed them to be and seeing their city become everything God desires it to be. We will replicate these communities by planting churches across the Triangle and throughout the world.
summary: Vintage Church on Campus is an outreach and discipleship arm of Vintage Church. We want to invite students to connect with Jesus Christ and one another. Look for on-campus events and community groups that will meet throughout the semester.

name: The Sport Management Club at NC State
description:
This organization serves students at NC State University who are passionate about Sport Management and eager to expand their professional network within the campus community. Our mission is to create opportunities for learning beyond the classroom while fostering a sense of community both within and outside the major. As a club, we are grounded in three core pillars: professional development, academic enrichment, and social engagement.

 

Please join our GroupMe to stay up to date on future meetings and events!
summary: This organization is for students of the Sport Management major located in the within the Parks, Recreation, and Tourism Department of the College of Natural Resources.

name: Sounding Club at NC State University
description:
Sounding Club is a student led organization dedicated to providing meteorology enthusiasts with hands on weather balloon launching experience. Some launches are scheduled in advance, while others are left to the whims of the weather in the event of severe conditions. Our launch data can be used to compose local short range forecasts, which we send over to our friends next-door at the National Weather Service and NC State Climate Office. We also take a few field trips to explore different facets of technical meteorology in the professional world.

All launches are followed up with in-depth explanations of the collected data, so fear not if you've never heard of a Skew-T or a hodograph. 

We welcome all students to join us up on the 6th floor of Jordan Hall to launch some balloons and hope to provide everyone with some insight into how the wonderful weather works!
summary: Sounding Club provides students with hands on experience with weather balloon launches and equipment that aids in weather forecasting. The club meets for regularly scheduled launches as well as spontaneous extreme weather events!

name: Disc Golf Club
description:
The Disc Golf Club at NC State welcomes all students interested in learning and understanding the sport of disc golf. We will gladly accept players of all skill levels. If competition is your forte, you've come to the right place. Our team has produced 8 All-American athletes and has finished in the Top-10 at the National Collegiate Disc Golf Championships in Augusta, GA six times. Including our strongest (2nd place) finish in 2019.


Whether you're new to the sport or a seasoned veteran, there's a place for you in the club! We partake in organizing tournaments, on and off­-campus fundraising, hosting clinics, and have regular practice sessions.

 

If you have played any of the courses within an hour of Raleigh, you have most likely played on a course we have helped beautify! Unlike most clubs on campus, our involvement in the community doesn't stop with the student body. We have partnerships with various clubs and towns in the surrounding area, including: Cary Area Disc League (CADL), Town of Cary, City of Raleigh, Triangle Chain Posse (TCP), and the Town of Holly Springs.
summary: The Disc Golf Club at NC State University is composed of players of various experienced levels and includes both male and female participants. We play together both recreationally here in the Triangle and competitively throughout the nation!

name: Veterinarians as One Inclusive Community for Empowerment
description:
The purpose of Veterinarians as One Inclusive Community for Empowerment (VOICE) is:
1. To increase awareness and sensitivity to socio-cultural issues in the field of veterinary medicine
2. To celebrate multiculturalism within the profession of veterinary medicine and provide a campus environment that embraces diversity and promotes the success of students, particularly underrepresented students.
3. To promote the importance of cross-cultural awareness in veterinary medicine in order to meet the needs of our diversifying clientele
4. To promote the veterinary profession, provide leadership and mentorship to any youth, particularly underrepresented youth, interested in careers in veterinary medicine.
summary: VOICE works to increase the awareness of socio-cultural issues in the field of veterinary medicine and to create a campus environment that embraces the diversity of our student body.

name: Ignite at NC State University
description: Ignite NC State exists for the purpose of establishing Christian community, where students can be discipled and equipped to reach their generation with the love of Christ. We meet weekly on Sundays at 7:30pm for our large group gathering and several times throughout the week for small group bible studies, prayer meetings, and discipleship groups.
summary: Ignite NC State is a family of students on mission to connect our campus and generation to Jesus.

name: Men's Club Volleyball at NC State
description:
The Men's Club Volleyball Team at NC State is committed to being a competitive program at the club level both regionally and nationally. In addition, the Men's Club Volleyball team strives to  support and expand the volleyball community in and around NC State. We practice twice a week and compete in at least 3 tournaments a semester. Many show up never having touched volleyball and leave knowing everything. We take pride in the development of our members both as volleyball players and as people. We feel this helps better the community in the discipline of volleyball and around the Raleigh area.

Questions? Email us!
ncsumensvball@gmail.com

Follow or DM us on Instagram!

@ncsumvb
summary: The Men's Club Volleyball Team at NC State is committed to being a competitive program at the club level both regionally and nationally and broadening the volleyball community in and around NC State.

name: Student Chapter of the American Society of Laboratory Animal Practicioners at NC State
description: The purpose of the Student Chapter of the American Society of Laboratory Animal Practitioners of North Carolina State University is to foster educational activities in the veterinary specialty of Laboratory Animal Medicine and to familiarize the members with standards and regulations pertaining to this discipline.
summary: A student organization promoting education and involvement of veterinary students in laboratory animal medicine.

name: High-Powered Rocketry Club
description:
The NCSU High-Powered Rocketry Club gives undergraduate students the opportunity to gain real-world design and construction experience through participation in the annual Student Launch (SL) competition sponsored by the NASA Marshall Space Flight Center. The club is led by an experienced group of Aerospace Engineering seniors whose participation in the club doubles as their Senior Design Capstone Project. Student members get the opportunity to work alongside the Senior Design Team, a faculty advisor, and multiple club mentors (high-power rocketry enthusiasts and handymen) to research, design, construct, test, and launch high-power rockets.

The team has scored competitively in the SL competition in recent years, winning 1st for the apogee category of the 2019 competition. This award commemorates the team's accomplishment in reaching an apogee within 12 feet of the declared altitude. The team placed 5th overall in the 2020 competition. In the 2022 SL Competition, the club received awards for best looking rocket and social media while placing 2nd in payload design. Last year, in the 2023 competition, the team placed 4th overall among 59 national teams.

Along with competing in SL, the team has also participated in the NASA Centennial Challenge maxi-MAV competition where university teams were required to construct a platform for launching rockets that also acted as an autonomous ground unit that can identify and collect ground samples. The challenge was designed with future Mars missions in mind and each university had to meet strict challenge guidelines to qualify for prize money. In April 2015, the team won the $25,000 first place prize.

Information about the challenge and a video showcasing our 2015 win can be found here:

http://www.nasa.gov/directorates/spacetech/centennial_challenges/mav.html

 

Website: http://www.ncsurocketry.org

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/TachoLycos
summary: HPRC explores hobby rocketry and aerospace engineering to design rockets to compete in NASA's annual Student Launch competition. Join us!

name: Shelton Leadership Center Student Ambassador Program
description: The mission of the Shelton Leadership Center Student Ambassador Program is to be representatives of the Shelton Leadership Center and to enhance the values of honesty, integrity, compassion, diversity, and social responsibility through hands-on leadership with continuous student body engagement for current and future members of the Shelton family.  The vision is to foster a civically engaged community of values-based leaders through social networking, professional development, and selfless-service in order to impact individuals on the campus, local, and global levels.
summary: Connecting students who are interested in developing as values-based leaders through the mission of the Shelton Leadership Center

name: Carnivore Conservation Crew at NC State University
description: The Carnivore Team is a student-run organization that aims to familiarize students with the research, care, and husbandry of wild and captive carnivores. Our objectives are to offer hands-on experience to students, to conduct pertinent and valuable research, to educate the public, and provide optimal conditions for the animals under our care. This club is only open to current students within the College of Veterinary Medicine.
summary: Carnivore Team is a student-run organization that aims to familiarize students with the research, care, and husbandry of wild and captive carnivores.

name: Student American Veterinary Medical Association Chapter at NC State - CVM
description: SAVMA, the Student American Veterinary Medical Association Chapter at NCSU College of Veterinary Medicine, is a non-profit, student-run group modeled after its parent organization, the Student American Veterinary Medical Association. At NC State University, SAVMA is the umbrella organization for 31 other professional group student chapters and student clubs. SAVMA organizes fundraisers and social events, helps support educational opportunities at the school, and represents the student body to the college administration and the AVMA. SAVMA also provides funding for individual educational opportunities, such as travel to veterinary conferences, externships, wetlabs, and guest speakers.
summary: SAVMA, the Student American Veterinary Medical Association, Chapter at NCSU CVM, is a non-profit, student-run group modeled after its parent organization, the Student American Veterinary Medical Association.

name: Club Sports at NC State
description:
The mission of Club Sports is to provide competitive, recreational and instructional opportunities for the NC State community. We are committed to the development of successful and quality teams with a focus on enhancing the collegiate experience through leadership development, teamwork, sportsmanship, diversity, responsible and ethical conduct, healthy active lifestyles and fun. The NC State Club Sports program is committed to providing fair and equitable treatment for all club athletes.

With 44 clubs in which to participate, Club Sports are student-run organizations with an emphasis on student leadership, service and engagement. Club Sports are governed by the rules and regulations established for student organizations, University Recreation and NC State University. Club Sports are open to NC State University students, faculty and staff.
summary: The mission of Club Sports is to provide competitive, recreational and instructional opportunities for the NC State community.

name: American Meteorological Society Student Chapter Organization at NC State
description: The Student Chapter of AMS at NC State is devoted to exploring career and research opportunities within atmospheric sciences and other disciplines that incorporate meteorology for the benefit of society. Monthly meetings involve guest speakers from organizations such as the National Weather Service, broadcast media, and private sector corporations.
summary: A club designed for both students in the meteorology major and those with interdisciplinary connections. Members come to meetings in order to establish professional relationships and to listen to professional speakers in the field.

name: Animal Science Club at NC State
description: The Animal Science Club at NC State welcomes students of all majors and backgrounds who share an interest in livestock and animal agriculture. Our mission is to enhance knowledge, spark interest, and offer enriching opportunities for students at any level of experience or species focus. We promote leadership, scholarship, and organized animal science activities while serving both the campus and broader community. From running the Milk Booth and assisting with livestock shows at the NC State Fair to participating in Farm Animal Days, Ag Awareness Week, and Club Day, our members actively engage in outreach and service—and are even eligible for club-sponsored scholarships.
summary: The Animal Science Club welcomes all students interested in animal agriculture. We aim to educate both our members and the broader community about the importance and impact of animal agriculture.

name: Christian Veterinary Missions Fellowship at NC State
description: Christian Veterinary Missions Fellowship is a student fellowship chapter of the Christian Veterinary Mission (CVM) whose mission is to challenge, empower, and facilitate veterinarians to serve others through their profession while living out their Christian faith.
summary: We exist to minister to students as we pursue Christ, engage the College of Veterinary Medicine Community, serve the Yu'pik, Cherokee, and Navajo people, and impact the nations with the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

name: Research, Forecasting & Discussion Club
description:
The following is the club purpose as stated in the constitution:

  1. To connect undergraduates and graduate students to talk about current weather events, and break down atmospheric processes through in-person and online means. 
  2. Explore atmospheric research topics, case studies, and unique weather phenomena to deepen understanding of forecasting, climate, and atmospheric dynamics.
  3. Utilize Python and other forecasting and analysis tools to create visualizations and strengthen technical and data-driven skills
summary: This club provides an opportunity for both undergraduate & graduate students in meteorology to discuss current weather events, and practice analyses and forecasting techniques learned in classes.

name: Chi Omega
description:
It all began on April 5, 1895, at the University of Arkansas when four young women, with the help of a local dentist, established the secrets and symbolism that today bind over 360,000 women from 181 different collegiate chapters in the largest women's fraternal organization in the world. This small band of women founded Chi Omega after realizing a need for an organization that would foster both friendship and respect for the potential and inherent value of women.

Over the years, Chi Omega has provided its members with unique opportunities in leadership, scholarship, and lifelong friendship - striving to provide each Sister with a commitment to personal integrity, excellence in academic and intellectual pursuits, intergenerational participation, community service, leadership opportunities, and social enrichment.
summary: The Alpha Kappa Chapter of Chi Omega was established at North Carolina State University as a women's sorority in 1984 with the purpose of enriching our members' college experiences and making our campus and community a better place.

name: Student Chapter of the Society for Theriogenology
description: Therio Club is affiliated with the Society for Theriogenology. We aim to provide veterinary students with opportunities to gain hands-on experience with reproductive medicine while in school, enable opportunities for networking, and raise funds to support students in their pursuit of opportunities related to theriogenology.
summary: Therio Club develops opportunities for veterinary students to gain hands-on experience with reproductive medicine while in school.

name: Saudi Students Club at NC State University
description: As the number of Saudi students started to increase significantly in North Carolina State University, there was a need to provide a comfortable environment that would make them feel home! The club hosts events for yearly Saudi and Muslim holidays. In addition, the club aims to have a variety of social/cultural events that would help introduce the Saudi culture to the pack!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote services at NC State University community at large, provide professional development of our members, and to offer a social outlet to all those interested in Saudi Associations.

name: University Recreation
description: Department
summary: Department

name: ASABE at NC State
description:
The American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers (ASABE) promotes professional and educational development and provides community amongst BAE/BAET students. Bi-weekly meetings vary from social events, graduate student panels, and presentations from industry professionals. Additional events include the annual career fair, which brings in ~40 companies, sweetpotato sale and farm tour, stream clean up, barn building, Shack-A-Thon, and more!

Membership is open to any related major.

Department website: https://www.bae.ncsu.edu

Club website: https://asabestudentchapter.wordpress.ncsu.edu/
summary: The student chapter of ASABE at NC State promotes professional and educational development for its members through bi-weekly meetings and other events while encouraging a sense of community amongst BAE/BAET students.

name: Comparative Biomedical Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: We are the Graduate Student Association for graduate students that are a part of the College of Veterinary Medicine (CVM), including Comparative Biomedical Sciences and other graduate students working with faculty under the CVM. We serve as a link between the grad students and the University Graduate Student Association as well as the College of Veterinary Medicine. We also provide opportunities for graduate students to interact with one another educationally, scientifically, and socially.
summary: Graduate Student Association for graduate students that are a part of the College of Veterinary Medicine.

name: Forestry and Environmental Resources Graduate Student Association
description: The mission of the FER GSA is to improve the educational and social experiences of the graduate students in the Department of Forestry and Environmental Resources.
summary: This organization provides members an opportunity to organize seminars, meetings, field trips and other functions concerned with interests associated with Forestry and/or Natural Resources.

name: Graduate Christian Fellowship
description:
Graduate Christian Fellowship (GCF) includes graduate students, professional students, and post-docs from many different backgrounds and denominations, but who share and pursue a common purpose. Together we learn, grow, build community, serve, and follow our calling. We approach scripture with the same rigor that we approach our academic studies, and we seek to support each other through the various stages of academia.


We are primarily organized around a weekly Bible Study with fun events to build community and experiment with new ways to serve students. Check our website and calendar! Contact us for more information!
summary: The purpose of Graduate Christian Fellowship is to encourage and equip graduate students to live as Jesus Christ’s disciples, following him as they seek to be a redeeming influence among the people, ideas and structures of the university and professions.

name: Operations Research GSA at NC State
description: The Operations Research GSA at NC State serves Operations Research (OR) graduate students by fostering communication between students and faculty, establishing and maintaining a sense of identity and community within the OR program, and promoting events, interactions, and programs that enhance the quality of education for students and their outcomes after graduation.
