The Operations Research GSA at NC State is an educational non-profit.
summary: Our purpose is to enhance the quality of graduate education within the Operations Research Department of NC State by promoting involvement, networking, and communication among Operations Research graduate students and faculty members.

name: Saavan
description:
Saavan is an organization that supports South Asian arts including Carnatic and Hindustani music, classical and other performance arts. Its mission is to provide a platform for practice, teaching, and learning of these arts.

Please join our Discord here.

Saavan is currently holding weekly meetings on Mondays 6:30 to 8 pm. 
Details of the meetings and location can be found on Discord.
summary: Saavan is an organization that supports South Asian arts including Carnatic and Hindustani music, classical  and other performance arts. Its mission is to provide a platform for practice, teaching and learning of these arts.

name: Internal Audit Student Club
description: The Internal Audit Student Club provides students with a platform to explore the internal audit profession through networking with professionals and other students, presentations, and other events.
summary: IASC is an exciting student run organization for students interested in careers in both private and public audit.  We offer students an opportunity to discover the dynamic internal audit profession that is continually broadening in scope and importance.

name: MVMT Peer Educators
description:
MVMT Peer Educators is a student-led organization within the Women's Center that facilitates community workshops on issues of interpersonal violence and social justice. Our mission is to educate and expand knowledge on the topic of interpersonal violence on NC State’s campus (and beyond!) and support survivors of IPV through weekly meetings, educational presentations, and expressive workshops.

 

Spring 2026 weekly meetings are Thursdays from 5-6 PM in the Women's Center

 

To get involved, check out our Linktree!: MVMT Linktree
summary: MVMT - Peer Educating to End Interpersonal Violence

name: Arab Student Organization
description: The Arab Student Organization doubles as an affinity group for Arabs and an educational resource to the general student body at North Carolina State University regarding Arab culture. The organization is committed to promoting peaceful relations between Arabs and non-Arabs alike while celebrating the diversity that exists within Arab culture and heritage.
summary: The Arab Student Organization doubles as an affinity group for Arabs and an educational resource to the general student body at North Carolina State University regarding Arab culture while promoting peaceful relations between all human beings.

name: The Quarterdeck Society at NC State
description: The mission of this organization will be to develop midshipmen, Officer Candidates, and Marine Corps Enlisted Commissioning Educational Program candidates mentally, morally and physically and to imbue them with the highest ideals of duty, and loyalty, and with the core values of honor, courage and commitment in order to commission college graduates as naval officers who possess a basic professional background, are motivated toward careers in the naval service, and have a potential for future development in mind and character so as to assume the highest responsibilities of command, citizenship and government.
summary: We are the student organization associated with Naval ROTC at NC State.

name: DASA Co Curricular Implementation Team
description: Team will use this portal to become more familiar with the capabilities of CollegiateLink.
summary: Team will use this portal to become more familiar with the capabilities of CollegiateLink.

name: Pack Pullers
description:
The Pack Pullers are a student design club that designs and builds a 1/4 scale tractor each year in cooperation with the American Society of Biological and Agricultural Engineers (ASABE) International Quarter-Scale Competition. For more information visit asabe.org/iqs. We are open to students of any major but are housed within the Biological and Agricultural Engineering Department at NC State.

We receive a stock motor and set of rear tires, the rest of the tractor's designs are up to us. Our machine is designed and manufactured completely in house. If you're looking to get hands on engineering and manufacturing experience, this is the place for you!

 

Our weekly meetings are held every Monday at 5:30 pm Weaver 125. There is no experience necessary, we will teach you everything you need to know. There is a large soft-skills portion of our competition, so there is a place for everyone from design and fabrication to marketing and technical reports, and even electronics and data acquisition. 

 

We'd love to have you join us!
summary: Pack Pullers is a student organization in the Biological and Agricultural Engineering Department. Each year, students design, fabricate and test a new quarter-scale tractor for the annual ASABE International 1/4-Scale Tractor Student Design Competition.

name: Indonesian Student Association at NC State
summary: Indonesian Students’ Association in the United States, North Carolina State University Chapter known as PERMIAS NCSU fosters communication among Indonesian students, promote cultural exchange, and build networks through academic and social activities

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers at North Carolina State University
description:
Through the Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers at North Carolina State University (SASE at NCSU), we hope to enable and provide students with opportunities to connect with their community and culture, while also expanding their professional networks.

The national SASE chapter was founded in November 2007 to help Asian-heritage, professional scientists and engineers achieve their full potential. Organizations exist for many affinity groups — The National Society of Black Engineers, the Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers, and the Society of Women Engineers — and SASE serves a similar purpose for students representing all Asian cultures to connect and support each other.
summary:
SASE’s mission is to:
- Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world.
- Celebrate diversity on campus and in the workplace.
- Provide opportunities for members to contribute to their local communities.

name: Club Roundnet at NC State
description:
Spikeball club is for anybody looking for a good college sport that can be played in any open area. Spikeball is a new sport that has gained popularity at college campuses, around the United States, and around the world. We want to spread the sport across NC State and invite all students from avid athletes to casual first timers. We will have designated days where club members can get together and play. We have tournaments to raise money for different causes and winners can win prizes. We have spikeball sets that members may check out to play at anytime.

Please request to join the club Spikeball discord to stay up to date with events and pick up games. Link is below.

Follow our Instagram @roundnet.ncstate 

Join the Discord: https://discord.com/invite/d3edYAMT
summary: Spikeball is a competitive sport that involves two teams with two players on each team. The first team to 21 points wins. If you want to learn the rules come out and join us!

name: Raleigh Episcopal Campus Ministry
description: We are a ministry in the Episcopal tradition, but open to people from all denominational backgrounds and those who have yet to find a spiritual home. Through group worship, Bible study, and regular spiritual exercises, we seek to better follow the teachings of Jesus, cultivate spiritual well-being, and promote love for God, humanity, and the rest of creation. We are a proudly LGBTQ+ affirming group.
summary: Raleigh Episcopal Campus Ministry, a ministry of the Diocese of North Carolina, welcomes all college students in the Raleigh area. Come just as you are to our radically open and inclusive community.

name: CRANE Collective at NC State
description:
CRANE Collective stands for Critical Asian Narratives and Engagement. Our goals for the academic school year are:

1. Encouraging artistic, political, and social engagement within the Asian community

2. Serving as an umbrella organization, in means of community, for all Asian students at NCSU

3. Connecting and uplifting the Asian community at NCSU
summary: The CRANE Collective is the forefront of the Asian community at NCSU. We are committed to promoting crucial Asian narratives by means of education, action, and engagement by uplifting the on-campus and local communities.

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description:
The Fraternity of Phi Gamma Delta, also known as Fiji or Phi Gam, has 126 chapters and 18 colonies at the foremost colleges and universities of the United States and Canada. The Fraternity was founded at Jefferson College in 1848. Phi Gamma Delta offers much to our members through leadership development, educational programs, and lifelong friendships.

Phi Gamma Delta is dedicated to developing men of character within the academic setting, with the aim that they will become fully contributing members of society. In pursuit of this mission, the Fraternity promotes five core values: Friendship, which brings us together and is the basis of our brotherhood. Knowledge, the primary reason our members attend college and the key to a fuller, richer life. Service, in recognition of our obligation to give back to the campus and community that foster our growth. Morality, our commitment to do what is right in all our relationships. Excellence, the achievement of the full potential of the chapter and the individual members.
summary: Phi Gamma Delta IFC Social Fraternity

name: Multicultural Young Educators Network
description: The mission of MYEN is to: promote unity among diverse groups of students in the College of Education and in the university, prepare future youth advocates to be competent in diverse environments, and perform service projects related to education with a focus on cultural awareness.  We welcome members from any major at NC State University who have an interest in youth outreach, education and service.  We also host social events as a chance for members to connect. The organization meets once or twice a month in the fall and spring semesters. ALL students of all backgrounds and cultures are welcome to attend!
summary: The purpose of MYEN is to provide diverse students with a platform in which to collaborate, address challenges in education, and develop as culturally competent 21st century educators through social programming and community service activities.

name: American Nuclear Society at North Carolina State University
description:
The NC State section of ANS supports the academic, professional, and social development of its members. ANS hosts invited speakers from academia, industry, and government, professional development activities and workshops, and field trips to industry. It helps build a strong network that will become the foundation of future endeavors for members.

 

 

 

Have questions? send an email message to our planning committee.
summary: Connecting the nuclear industry with NC State's best!

name: PATHHEADS
description:
The Student Chapter of the American College of Veterinary Pathologists (ACVP) at North Carolina State University CVM aka PathHeads was formed by Kaori Sakamonto in 1997 with Dr. Mac Law acting as the faculty advisor. The club became an officially recognized student chapter in 2001. In 2002, faculty advisors for the student chapters were awarded a plaque at the New Orleans ACVP meeting. Today, PathHeads provides opportunities to students interested in veterinary pathology and related careers under the guidance of Dr. Danielle Meritet.

Pathheads encourages students to participate in the annual meetings of the ACVP. For the 2024 ACVP annual meeting in Seattle, NCSU CVM was able to send 2 PathHeads members.
summary: PathHeads club provides students with opportunities to explore the field of anatomic and clinical pathology. Wetlabs, annual trips to ACVP, and bi-semester lunch meetings allow students to explore the options available in the field of pathology.

name: Beekeepers Club
description: The objectives of the Beekeepers Club shall be to provide members an opportunity to organize meetings, seminars, field trips, and other functions concerned with common interests of the members of the Beekeepers Club. All levels of experience are welcome!
summary: The objective of the Beekeepers Club is to provide members an opportunity to organize meetings, field trips, and other functions with other specialists locally concerned with beekeeping.

name: Pack Abroad Ambassadors
description: PackAbroad Ambassadors is primarily a student outreach organization. Ambassadors reach out to fellow students through tabling, orientations, classroom presentations, meet and greets, the study abroad fair, departure conferences, zoom, and so much more. PAA provides the opportunity for NC State students to hear about study abroad programs from fellow students who have experienced them. Enthusiastic and passionate Ambassadors typically give a 10-20 minute presentation about the variety of NC State’s study abroad programs with special emphasis on personal experiences and stories. This is a valuable opportunity for students to hear a peer’s perspectives and ask questions. PackAbroad Ambassadors also meet monthly to share fun stories, get to know one another, and have guest speakers bring attention to further possibilities of global engagement!
summary: Pack Abroad Ambassadors is affiliated with the Study Abroad Office and aims to promote studying abroad to other students. PAA also offers opportunities to meet other students who have returned from studying abroad.

name: Sustainability Stewards at NC State
description:
The Sustainability Stewards at NC State are the university’s sustainability student ambassadors, moving NC State to a brighter, stronger and more sustainable future. the Stewards promote sustainable practices among their peers and the community through workshops, presentations and campus events. But most importantly, the Stewards are role models, inspiring campus to consider the impact that personal decisions have on the environment, community and the future.

The Stewards application period is typically in February-March each year, and there is a special application period every fall for first-year students. The process involves a written application and an interview.
summary: The Stewards are the university’s sustainability student ambassadors, moving NC State to a brighter, stronger and more sustainable future.

name: Musical Empowerment at NC State
description: Through free, one-on-one music lessons, Musical Empowerment nurtures meaningful relationships between collegiate mentors and children in under-served communities based on a shared love of music and learning that build a foundation of confidence, creativity and self-discipline to create successful individuals.
summary: A nonprofit that pairs an NC State student instrumental musician or vocalist with a child from a low-income family and provides the child with a free instrument, free weekly music lesson, and positive mentorship.

name: Wolf Trax Music Group
description: WTMG is the only student-run music management and promotions company on campus. We work with local artists to promote their music careers and book them shows around Raleigh, providing rare, real-world experience working in the music industry.
summary: WTMG is the only student-run music management and promotions company on campus. We work with local artists to promote their music careers and book them shows around Raleigh, providing rare, real-world experience working in the music industry.

name: Pre-Dental Club at NC State
description:
The Pre-Dental Club at NC State is an organization that serves to provide students at North Carolina State University with opportunities and information regarding all aspects of the dental field and the path to becoming a dental professional. The club strives for academic excellence, leadership, service, and community amongst its members. The mission of this club is to provide pre-dental students with service opportunities and knowledge in all aspects of the dental field and to serve as a network of future dentists in which members can confide while pursuing a career in dentistry.

 

Important: Please scroll down and read the "Welcome to the Pre-Dental Club at NC State!" article to learn how to join the club.
summary: The Pre-Dental Club at NCSU is an organization that serves to provide students at North Carolina State University with opportunities and information regarding all aspects of the dental field and the path to becoming a dental professional.

name: Pre-Med Club at NC State
description:
Dedicated to preparing the next generation of physicians through workshops, service, and advocacy

Join our GetInvolved page to be place on our email list and club roster

Follow us on Instagram @ncsupremedclub
summary: A pre-medicine professional group at NC State University with intent to prepare interested students for matriculation into MD and DO programs in the U.S.

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Association
description:
The Pre-PA Association is an organization at NC State University created to improve students' understanding of the PA profession. We strive to provide an enjoyable environment for students with similar professional interests to gather and explore their field in-depth. 

 

Each month, we invite a guest speaker to talk about their experiences firsthand! The guest speakers include practicing PAs, current students, and admission counselors. We incorporate workshops such as taking vitals, suturing, and CASPA application guidance to allow students a unique hands-on learning experience. 

 

Click here for our 25-26 membership application!
summary: Our aim is to prepare students for PA school by bringing in practicing health professionals, admissions counselors, and offering hands-on workshops.

name: Peer Career Coaches at Poole College of Management
description:
The Peer Career Coaches (PCCs) at Poole College of Management serve fellow undergraduate students as an extension of the college's Career Center. PCCs benefit from ongoing training and professional development opportunities related to career readiness. They then coach their peers on topics such as ePACK, interview prep, and resume and cover letter creation and review. In addition, PCCs provide workshops, host employers, and assist with larger Poole Career Center events. 

Membership offers great opportunities to hone networking skills and further develop business etiquette in a friendly environment. Members gain "insider access" to employers as well as career coaches.

Recruitment: Spring of each year, with occasional recruitment in the fall semester as needed

Eligibility: Students eligible to apply to be a Peer Career Coach must be undergraduates in the Poole College of Management, have a 2.5 GPA, and be interested in helping their peers with their career development needs. 

Meet the Current Members: Click here to meet the Peer Career Coaches.

Questions: If you have questions, please reach out to poole_careers@ncsu.edu
summary: The Peer Career Coaches serve Poole College of Management undergraduate students by providing career and professional coaching while connecting students with resources and opportunities to assist in the attainment of their professional goals.

name: Turning Point USA at NC State
description: Turning Point USA is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization that educates students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, limited government, and free markets. Through activities such as non-partisan debates, dialogue, and discussion Turning Point hopes to spread their message not only across college campuses but all over the nation. As an organization, Turning Point focuses strictly on economic issues and limited government.  We use materials such as buttons, stickers, and booklets to spread our message. Our hope is that by taking political policies that the younger generation might not pay attention to and relating them to pop-culture concepts we will be able to get their attention!
summary: Turning Point USA is one of the fastest growing youth movements in the country.  We use innovative campus activities and events as well as non-partisan, thought-provoking debate, dialogue, and discussion. To get youth involved in politics.

name: Masters of Accounting Student Organization
summary: We are a student organization comprised of NC State Jenkins Master of Accounting (MAC) Students who are organized for the purpose of representing all MAC students in the UGSA and ensuring program-wide continuity in career, academic, and social excellence

name: Benjamin Franklin Scholars
description: The Benjamin Franklin Scholars program is a community of highly motivated students who pursue degrees in both the College of Engineering and the College of Humanities and Social Sciences (or Economics). These students complete two courses specific to the Franklin program and take part in various activities throughout the year. Scholars must participate in at least three events per semester.
summary: The Benjamin Franklin Scholars seeks to provide support and supplemental experiences to dual degree students in the College of Engineering and College of Humanities and Social Sciences.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta at NC State
description: Alpha Epsilon Delta is the National Health Preprofessional Honor Society dedicated to the encouragement and recognition of excellence in preprofessional health scholarship. Our Society welcomes all students engaged in the pursuit of professional development, provides a forum for students with common interests and extends a program of service to benefit the college/university community. The next application cycle will open in August 2026.
summary: AED is the Pre-Health Honor Society at NCSU. We focus on serving the NCSU community by providing an avenue for those interested in healthcare to learn about the opportunities available to them, while meeting other like-minded students.

name: Pre-Optometry Subgroup
description:
We are a club of pre-professional students interested in optometry as a profession under the umbrella of the Pre-Health Club. We generally meet twice a month to hear from optometrists, optometry school representatives, or representatives from other eye-related programs. 
 
The club also participates in service activities. We partner with Prevent Blindness NC each year to provide free vision screenings for people at the North Carolina State Fair. We hold a glasses drive for the Lions Club all year, who take the donated glasses and provide them to people who cannot afford glasses otherwise. We also participate in Remote Area Medical's non-profit health clinics and have opportunities for members to participate in service events unrelated to healthcare within the Raleigh community.
 
Overall, the club provides students interested in optometry a place to interact with other people with the same interests. As a member, you can receive networking and shadowing opportunities. Our club's mission is to provide our members with the information, experiences, and resources they need to be prepared to apply to Optometry school.
summary: Pre-Health professional group of students interested in attending Optometry School after graduation from North Carolina State University.

name: DDS Pre-Dental Honor Society
description:
Delta Delta Sigma, DDS, is a pre-dental honor society that strives for academic excellence, leadership, service, and community amongst undergraduate students at North Carolina State University pursuing a career in dentistry. The mission of the honor society is to provide pre-dental students with clinical experience, valuable interpersonal skills, and a passion for serving those in our community and across the state of North Carolina.

*Service Hour Note* When logging service hours, please enter ALL meetings/events/opportunities as 1 HOUR 0 MINUTES, regardless of the time you spent to be approved. Thank you!

2025-2026 Application Link (you must fill out ONE of the following that applies to you in order to be accepted to the GetInvolved)

-NEW members: NEW Member Application Form

-RETURNING members: RETURNING Member Form
summary: A Pre-Dental Honor Society that strives for academic excellence, leadership, service, and community amongst undergraduate students at North Carolina State University pursuing a career in dentistry.

name: Voluntarios Ahora en Raleigh
description: VOLAR is a Spanish language service program that bridges NC State to the local community, fosters cultural diversity, and challenges students with authentic linguistic practice. We provide Spanish majors/minors and native speakers with opportunities to practice their Spanish while helping the Spanish-speaking community of Wake County. VOLAR has partnerships with various organizations and schools around the county where we provide translation services.
summary: VOLAR is a Spanish language service program that bridges NC State to the local community, fosters cultural diversity, and challenges students with authentic linguistic practice.

name: Material Advantage Student Chapter
description:
The purpose of this Chapter is to promote knowledge of metallurgy, ceramics, materials science and engineering and all its branches. Its goals include professional development and networking opportunities for members through hosting events involving company representatives, graduate students, and faculty members. This Chapter will also spread awareness about the Department of Materials Science and Engineering at NC State and current events in the field of materials science and engineering. 

 

Membership in the Material Advantage NC State Student Chapter will include association with the four main professional societies for MSE: ACerS, AIST, ASM International, and TMS.

Learn more: https://linktr.ee/material.advantage.ncsu
summary: Material Advantage is geared towards undergraduates in Materials Science and Engineering. Its goal is to aid in professional development, networking, and academic achievement by hosting company representatives, graduate students, and faculty members.

name: Chess Team at NC State
description:
Chess Club Will Meet Weekly on Tuesdays at 6 pm in Talley, near Talley Market.

We are a friendly group including chess players of all skill levels from beginner to expert. We host casual meetups as well as a weekly, free, USCF-rated ladder tournament and we also have a team to represent us in matches against other collegiate clubs. If you are interested, please join this group. We seek to provide opportunities for serious competitive players as well as a low-key place to play for casual players.

You don't have to join this page to come to the meetings, but I send emails about special events and changed meeting times to everyone in this page!
summary: We are the active chess group at NC State, open to players of all skill levels with both casual meetings and USCF-rated events.

name: InspirENGR
description:
Male, female, and non-binary students at NCSU (engineering and non-engineering majors) volunteer in programs catering to female elementary-high school students.  Programs include monthly engineering workshops in the fall semester primarily using KiwiCo kits. Volunteers work with students to construct engineering kits and obtain valuable skills such as soldering. The spring semester consists of a three-day long mentor program, where mentors are paired with a high school-aged student and focus on introducing the engineering opportunities at NCSU. The mentor program provides campus tours, guest speakers, research laboratory tours, and student organization-led engineering activities. InspirENGR also holds an Engineering Day in the spring for elementary and middle school students with an entire day of engineering activities. Outside of service-based programs, InspirENGR holds social and networking events to provide volunteers with valuable engineering skills to ensure success beyond their undergraduate careers. 

InspirENGR is proud to follow NC State's Non-Discrimination Policy
summary: NCSU student volunteers host monthly engineering workshops, a mentor program, and engineering day as service-based programs for younger, aspiring, female engineers in the local Raleigh community with the hopes of reducing gender disparity in engineering.

name: Pre-Speech, Physical and Occupational Therapy Club
description: The Pre-SPOT Club is a student-led organization here at NC State. The purpose of this club is to provide a forum for students with similar interests to gather and to explore more in-depth their interests in specific health professions fields. Club meetings will feature speakers, panels, volunteer opportunities and more, all relating to the fields of speech, physical or occupational therapy. Activities will aid students in pursuing graduate school and careers related to the listed fields.
summary: The Pre-SPOT Club is a student organization that provides members with information and opportunities related to their prospective professional school field.

name: SKY at NC State
description: SKY at NC State's mission is to bring meditation, breathwork, emotional intelligence, social connection, and service leadership to college students, faculty and staff. Check us out on instagram at @skyncsu
summary: SKY at NC State seeks to promote a stress free, happy & a socially beneficial way of living. We believe that joy grows when shared. We promote yoga and meditation as a means to be happy & organize social service activities to help us express that joy.

name: MEDLIFE Chapter @ NC State
description: MEDLife is a volunteer-run global health organization dedicated to creating sustainable change in medicine, education, and development both locally and globally. Our chapter provides students with opportunities to serve underserved communities, learn about healthcare inequities, and engage in hands-on volunteering. We host meetings, service events, and fundraisers throughout the year, and members also have the chance to participate in international service-learning trips with MEDLife.
summary: MEDLIFE is a volunteer-run global health organization whose mission is to help families achieve greater freedom from the constraints of poverty, empowering them to live healthier lives through equal access to healthcare, education and a safe home.

name: Energy that Works
summary: The N.C. Clean Energy Technology Center is a UNC System-chartered Public Service Center administered by the College of Engineering at North Carolina State University.

name: Psychology Club
description:
Our mission as a club is to help further each other’s understanding of psychology by fostering discussions through fun activities during meetings and around campus.

We hold monthly meetings, and occasional socials events, for our members. We recommend you reach us by email (psychologyclub1ncsu@gmail.com) or on Instagram (@psychologyclub__ncsu) if you'd like to get involved or have any questions!
summary: Our mission as a club is to help further each other’s understanding of psychology by working to foster discussions through fun activities and events during meetings and around campus.

name: Nuclear Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: This organization is the graduate student association for the Nuclear Engineering department at NC State. This group provides an active voice for graduate students in departmental affairs via regular meetings with department administration and gives graduate students an open forum for concerns and opinions. We are also responsible for organizing department-wide events, such as seminars, social gatherings, and other activities. Affiliated with the university-level Graduate Student Association (GSA), we also distribute GSA information to the departmental graduate student body.
summary: The purpose of the NE-GSA is to represent the graduate student body of the Nuclear Engineering department at NCSU. All graduate students in the NE department are members by default unless they opt-out.

name: Teacher Education & Learning Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of TELS-GSA is to represent the Department as graduate student ambassadors and to provide academic and social interactions for graduate students in the Department.
summary: Graduate student association for students in the Department of Teacher Education and Learning Sciences in the College of Education.

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon at NC State
description:
On November 19th, 2016, eleven men came together to found the Associate Chapter of Lambda Phi Epsilon International Fraternity. Committed to our five core values--authenticity, courageous leadership, cultural heritage, love, and wisdom--we dedicate ourselves to being "Leaders Among Men".

 

We take pride in our services to the community and university as we uphold our national philanthropy, Be The Match, which helps fight against leukemia internationally. Furthermore, we promote inclusiveness through the bonds of our brotherhood through various retreats and events that extend across the entire year. We also increase educational prowess in various areas, whether it be hosting professional development through workshops, or providing an insight into Asian cultures with different performances.

 

Today, Lambda Phi Epsilon is widely renowned as the preeminent international Asian interest fraternal organization, providing outstanding leadership, philanthropy, and advocacy in the community. At the start of each semester, we host a two-week long rush, which is prime time for you to come out and get to know more about our brothers. Further information can be found at our website and socials linked below:

 

Website:http://lphiencsu.weebly.com/

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ncsulphie/

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/ncsulphie
summary: Lambda Phi Epsilon Fraternity, Inc. at NC State is an Asian-interest, but not exclusive, international fraternity that aims to promote leaders among men through wisdom, love, cultural heritage, courageous leadership, and authenticity.

name: Adopted Students Union
description: The Adopted Students Union is committed to establishing an inclusive environment for all adoptees and individuals interested in learning about adoption. Our goal is to organize a variety of events, including cultural reconnections, social gatherings, and discussions about identity and self-worth. We aim to raise awareness about the adoptee community at NCSU.
summary: The Adopted Students Union is committed to establishing an inclusive environment for adoptees and those interested in learning about adoption. We coordinate various events to raise awareness about the adoptee community at NC State.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi
description:
Alpha Epsilon Pi was founded to provide opportunities for the Jewish college man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience. We have maintained the integrity of our purpose by strengthening our ties to the Jewish community and serving as a link between high school and career.

Our heritage stems from one source: young Jewish men banding together in allegiance. The role of Alpha Epsilon Pi has expanded since its inception in 1913. Initially, the Jewish fraternity served as a brotherhood of young men who came from similar religious backgrounds and who had experienced the same prejudices against their religious beliefs. Alpha Epsilon Pi soon broadened its role to include serving as the living quarters for some of its members. The fraternity became a home away from home, providing the same stabilizing and guiding values that students previously gained from their families. Armed with these values, Alpha Epsilon Pi faced changing conditions on the college campus and survived. Today, Jewish students search out Alpha Epsilon Pi because it is a Jewish Fraternity. In the fraternity’s 100-year history, over 93,000 men have worn the badge of Alpha Epsilon Pi and each year, over 2,500 undergraduates perform the Ritual of Initiation, which remains the same ritual adopted decades ago.

Perhaps of greater importance, Alpha Epsilon Pi develops leadership for the future of the American Jewish community. Tomorrow’s Jewish leaders are in our chapters today. These are the young men who must be counted upon to support Jewish causes and to prepare to be one of tomorrow’s Jewish leaders, so that they may aid themselves, their family, their community, and their people. Those students who enter the mainstream of non-Jewish life on the campus are far more likely to assimilate and to forsake their heritage. Working together with the Foundation for Jewish Campus Life/International Hillel, Alpha Epsilon Pi can play a vital role in helping reverse the growing trend among our young people to abandon Judaism at this critical time.

Throughout our history, the fraternity setting has served as a “learning laboratory”, a testing ground for young men who later become leaders in business, education, government, religion, and science. A goal of our fraternity is to help each student to develop character, to learn responsibility, and to develop a proper set of values through living together in brotherhood. Alpha Epsilon Pi prepares young men for their role in life as responsible citizens.

Therefore, our basic purpose is to provide the opportunity for a Jewish man to be able to join a Jewish organization whose purpose is not specifically religious, but rather social and cultural in nature. Alpha Epsilon Pi is a Jewish fraternity, though non-discriminatory and open to all who are willing to espouse its purpose and values.
summary: Our purpose is to provide the opportunity for male students to be able to join a Jewish organization whose purpose is not specifically religious, but rather social and cultural in nature. AEPi is a Jewish fraternity, though non-discriminatory.

name: Poultry Science Graduate Student Association
description: A chapter of the Graduate Student Association at NC State focusing on fostering community and collaboration among Poultry Science graduate students and supporting their specific academic and professional development needs.
summary: The purpose of this Association shall be to promote the academic and professional development of graduate students in Poultry Science.

name: FREEDM Systems Center
description: FREEDM Systems Center is the Future Renewable Electrical Energy Delivery and Management Systems Center, an NSF Engineering Research Center tasked with researching and developing the next generation technologies for the North American Power Grid.
summary: FREEDM Systems Center is the Future Renewable Electrical Energy Delivery and Management Systems Center, an NSF Engineering Research Center tasked with researching and developing the next generation technologies for the North American Power Grid.

name: Women in Business at NC State
description:
The Women in Business Club will seek to empower female students who are pursuing a degree or career in business. The club will provide a platform within Poole College of Management for women to voice their opinions and play an active role in the community. 

You do not have to be pursuing a business degree to join, our club is open to all students at NC State! The Women in Business Club will not discriminate against any members or potential members due to race, sex, religion, ethnicity, age, disability, or sexual orientation. 

We are here to inform our members of the importance of diversity, implementing team-building/club-strengthening activities, and hosting speakers to inform members on the topics of business, and specifically, women in business.

Please email us at wibatncsu@gmail.com if you have any questions. We look forward to meeting you!
summary: The Women in Business Club seeks to empower female students who are pursuing a degree or career in business. The club provides a platform within the Poole College of Management for women to voice their opinions and play an active role in the community.

name: Language Diversity Ambassadors at NC State
description:
The purpose of the LDA@NCSU is to promote awareness of linguistic diversity and linguistic discrimination on North Carolina State University’s campus as well as in society at large. Our desire is to engage with the university community in a variety of ways in order to provide greater linguistic understanding and appreciation for all.

Email if you want to be added to our listserv:
ldaatncstate@ncsu.edu
summary:
LDA's purpose is to promote awareness of linguistic diversity and linguistic discrimination on North Carolina State University’s campus as well as in society at large.
Email if you want to be on email list:
ldaatncstate@ncsu.edu

name: Career Development Center
summary: Career Development Center

name: Electrical and Computer Engineering Ambassadors
description:
Ambassadors represent the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department and NC State University as a whole, while exemplifying professionalism and campus pride.

Applications open each Spring semester.
summary:
Ambassadors represent the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department and NC State University as a whole, while exemplifying professionalism and campus pride.

Applications open each Spring semester.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma
description:
Dream Boldly. Live Fully. "Kappa Kappa Gamma is an organization of women, which seeks for every member throughout her life bonds of friendship, mutual support, opportunities for self-growth, respect for intellectual development, and an understanding of and an allegiance to positive ethical principles."

Eta Chi Chapter of Kappa Kappa Gamma found its home on NC State’s campus in the Fall of 2016 in a beautiful new house. Our chapter has quickly grown into a family of 170 amazing women.

Kappa Kappa Gamma's philanthropy is Mental Health Matters. Each semester, our chapter members vote which of our six organizations to donate our proceeds to. Additionally, all of our members have access to a free TalkSpace account. 

We host fun events each year in support of our philanthropy, like Mac and Keys, Miles for Mental Health and a Haunted House!

Eta Chi’s dedicated sisters also participate in several other campus fundraising events. We build a shack for Shack-A-Thon, a unique fundraising event that supports Habitat for Humanity. Our sisters also love creating a team for Dance Marathon, a 12 hour dance party to raise money for the Duke Children’s Hospital. We also participate in Service Raleigh, where we give back to our community by donating our time to various local projects.

Kappa is more than just an amazing organization; we are a fun-loving diverse group of talented young women. We are family. We are sisters. Our sisters are involved on campus, participating in various clubs, academic fraternities, and beyond. Eta Chi offers opportunities for professional development, personal growth, and networking, providing resources for effective communication, organizational skills, and emotional health. And yes, we know how to have fun -- backing the pack at football games, holiday parties, pumpkin decorating, work-out classes, rock climbing, movie nights, lip sync battles, and more. 

Dream boldy and live fully -- that is what the sisters of Eta Chi encourage each other to do. We cannot wait to meet you and welcome you to NC State, the place we call home. Go Pack!

 

Please go to http://www.ncstate.kappa.org for more information about our chapter!
summary: Kappa Kappa Gamma offers a member experience ranging from friendship to mentoring, from leadership to service and from campus activities to a lifetime of community involvement. We joined the Panhellenic Association at NC State in Fall 2016.

name: College Mentors for Kids, Inc. at North Carolina State University
shortName: College Mentors for Kids at NC State; College Mentors for Kids; College Mentors at NC State; College Mentors
description:
The mission of College Mentors for Kids at NC State is "Mentoring transforms lives: College Mentors for Kids connects kids to college students through weekly on-campus activities that inspire growth, confidence, and brighter futures." "Buddy pairs", consisting of one college and one elementary student, meet in groups for weekly activities on campus that focus on higher education and career, culture and diversity, and community service.  Through these activities and relationships with college students, children are exposed to higher education and the opportunities it has to offer.

** Weekly activities take place on Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays from 4:15 - 6:15. A mentor is responsible for consistent weekly involvement on ONE hour a week (every Tuesday, every Wednesday, or every Thursday at the same time)!**

Click here to sign-up! https://members.collegementors.org/application
summary: College Mentors for Kids connects kids to college students. "Buddy pairs", consisting of one college and one elementary student, meet in groups for weekly activities on campus that focus on higher education and career, culture and diversity, and service.

name: Intercollegiate Eventing at NC State
description: Established in 2016, the Club Eventing Team at NC State plans on growing in the future and becoming a well-known Intercollegiate Eventing team. Eventing is a three-phase equestrian sport involving the disciplines of dressage, cross-country, and show jumping. We host clinics, fundraisers, and travel together to various events, including recognized and unrecognized horse trials and other competitions. We do not supply horses to members, but you may still participate in social events if you do not have a horse.
summary: A competitive and recreational equestrian team that travels and competes at various recognized USEA events, both as individuals and in the Intercollegiate Team Challenges, as well as participates in clinics and fundraising.

name: Summit College at NC State
description:
Our vision is to be a community of disciples making disciples together through the local church. A disciple is someone who is with Jesus, is being changed by Jesus, and is on mission with Jesus. This is shown in Matthew 4:19 in which Jesus says, "Follow me and I will make you fishers of men."

Throughout the triangle 1500+ college students attend The Summit Church weekly. We desire to be the community of the Summit Church on the campus of NCSU. The following list is how we hope to serve and encourage members of the Summit Church, all students at NCSU, the surrounding Raleigh Community, and the world as a whole:

  • Sharing the knowledge of life in Christ Jesus with the NCSU community;
  • Helping people in their relationship with Jesus Christ and providing them with training for victorious Christian living, leadership, and excellence;
  • Introducing its members to a local church family and other missions or ministry opportunities;
  • Making a positive impact on the NCSU campus by developing students, equipping them with biblical solutions to contemporary problems so they can serve every facet of the NCSU community and ultimately the world.
  • Providing community service opportunities to serve the local community.
  • Serving as a support for Christian students who may be struggling or in need of support with living for Christ within a non-Christian environment.
  • Providing tutoring and mentorship to students to help them fulfill their purpose in life.
summary: Our vision is to be a community of disciples making disciples together through the local church.

name: College of Veterinary Medicine
description: The NC State College of Veterinary Medicine is where compassion meets science. Our mission, is to advance the veterinary profession and animal health through ongoing discovery and medical innovation; provide animals and their owners with extraordinary, compassionate medical care; and prepare the next generation of veterinarians and veterinary research scientists.
summary: The NC State College of Veterinary Medicine is where compassion meets science.

name: Minority Association of Pre-health Students (M.A.P.S.)
description:
Diversity. Inclusion. Excellence. 

MAPS (Minority Association of Pre-health Students) is the pre-medical portion of the national group "Student National Medical Association." SNMA is the oldest and largest organization focused on the needs and concerns of medical students of multiple nationalities. Its membership consists of more than 6,000 medical students, pre-medical students, residents and physicians. SNMA was established in 1964 by medical students from Howard University College of Medicine and Meharry Medical College School of Medicine. Due to their many initiatives since then, there has be a steady increase in minorities in medicine.

MAPS represents the undergraduate and post-baccalaureate students of SNMA. MAPS members are considered national Associate members and make up about one third of SNMA's membership. The members of SNMA are active in all fronts; we are creating diversity and making strides in all aspects of medicine and patient care.

MAPS is excited to serve students in minority groups on campus who are interested in health and learning more about improving professionally and academically in future career goals.

Primary Contact for MAPS:

Lilyanna Hopkins (President)

  • MAPS email: ncsumaps1@gmail.com
  • NCSU email: lshopkin@ncsu.edu
summary: The Minority Association of Pre-Health Students (MAPS) is a Pre-Med chapter of The Student National Medical Association (SNMA) and is an undergraduate organization that represents the interests of under served/represented minority pre-health students.

name: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air-Conditioning Engineers at NC State
description:
The student chapter hosts monthly meetings with engineering firms. Meetings generally cover what it is like to work as a mechanical engineer, some projects the firm has worked on and any opportunities for students with the firm. The chapter also occasionally hosts tours related to HVAC and energy around campus, like the solar house and Centennial Campus utility plant. 

There are is no membership fee for the student chapter, however, students are encouraged to join the ASHRAE national chapter and attend Triangle ASHRAE meetings.

The student chapter of the American Society of Heating, Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Engineers at NCSU (ASHRAE at NCSU) was founded on April 19, 1948, in the Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering. Students are encouraged to participate in the national ASHRAE design competition and take co-ops and internships with HVAC firms. See parent organization at https://www.ashrae.org/
summary: The student chapter of the American Society of Heating, Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Engineers at NCSU (ASHRAE at NC State) hosts meetings with local companies and tours related to HVAC, and Energy Efficiency.

name: Consult Your Community at NC State
description:
Consult Your Community (CYC) is a student-run nonprofit that organizes diverse teams to solve problems for small businesses and nonprofits in the Raleigh area. Students lead and complete pro-bono consulting projects that especially benefit women and minority business owners. We pride ourselves in our mission to give back to the Raleigh community while also giving students the opportunity to solve real business problems, meet face-to-face with clients, and work alongside a group of high-achieving peers from across the university!

 

We are looking for students from all areas of study who want to make a truly meaningful and lasting impact on local businesses!

 

Our Spring 2026 application has closed. If you are interested in joining our organization, please follow us on Instagram @cycncstate for more updates on the recruiting cycle and important events. You may also contact us by emailing ncstate@consultyourcommunity.org. We will begin recruiting again in the Fall!
summary: CYC is a student-run nonprofit that organizes diverse teams to solve problems for small businesses and nonprofits in the Raleigh area. Students lead and complete pro-bono consulting projects that especially benefit women and minority business owners.

name: Delight Ministries
description:
https://youtu.be/2cvqTTmYKtw

Delight meets on Wednesdays at 8! Follow us on Instagram, @delightatstate to see the exact dates and location
summary: Delight exists to invite college women into Christ-centered community that fosters vulnerability and transforms stories. We meet weekly to grow and learn through a Bible study, while having fun serving our campus and community through semester events.

name: App Development Club
description: The App Development Club teaches students about how to make apps for iOS and Android from scratch. Students will have the choice to learn about app development using Xcode (Swift language) or Android Studio (Kotlin). Students will also be able to learn front-end development and graphic design specifically for mobile devices. The ADC will hold brainstorming sessions weekly and will partner students together to work on apps collaboratively. Applications for the club open at the beginning of each semester. More information about the club and the link to apply can be found here and here.
summary:
🌟 2023 - 2024 Outstanding Growth & Development Student Organization
The App Development Club seeks to educate its members about the app development process and to enable them to obtain the skill set they need to become fully-fledged mobile developers.

name: All Music People Club
description:
We are a club that gives musicians at NC State a space to play improvised music together, network with each other, learn new instruments, and just enjoy music! We host weekly jam sessions on campus and have occasional performances in the Triangle area.

 

Our weekly jam sessions are informal and anyone can participate, regardless of musical background, experience, or instrument. We typically decide songs or forms we want to play during our meetings, so there is no prior knowledge or practice necessary, although it can be helpful to have at least basic knowledge of chords. These meetings are held on Fridays from 6:00 - 10:00pm on campus, typically in Price Music Center or Wolf Plaza. In order to accommodate different musical styles and volume levels, we typically split our meetings into 2 groups. This helps the jams to be more well-organized and allows everyone to be heard better.

 

Membership in this club is fluid, with no required attendance or dues. If you are interested in becoming a member, just email us and we can add you to our club GroupMe chat where you'll find more information about our upcoming meetings and special events! Many members use this chat to form bands, organize recording sessions, find people to go to concerts with, and just talk music.

 

Feel free to shoot us an email if you have any questions and maybe even stop by at one of our Wolf Plaza jams at the beginning of the semester to check it out!
summary: We are a club that gives musicians at NC State a space to play improvised music together, network with each other, learn new instruments, and just enjoy music! We host weekly jam sessions on campus and have occasional performances in the Triangle area.

name: The Helping Hand Project at North Carolina State University
description: The mission of the Helping Hand Project at NC State is to use 3D printing and innovative design solutions to provide recreational prosthetic devices to people with upper limb differences while providing a supportive community. In the U.S. alone, there are at least 125 cases of children born each year with missing fingers on one or both of their hands. This loss of function may be restored with a prosthetic device, however this solution can be costly. Traditional prosthetic devices can cost families thousands of dollars and are often outgrown by children. Meanwhile, 3D printed devices allow for easy scaling and assembly of new devices as children grow. We use 3D printing and other emerging technologies to build recreational prosthetic devices for children with limb differences. Volunteers students at NC State use available resources and donations to skillfully design and produce prosthetic devices at no cost to children or their families. Additionally, our support team strives to build a community for people with upper limb differences by planning social events like family campus visits and bi-annual family gatherings. This team also sends personalized care packages to the children so that they can see the progress of their hand and know that we are invested in them.
summary: The mission of the Helping Hand Project is to use 3D printing and innovative design solutions to provide recreational prosthetic devices to people with upper limb differences while providing a supportive community.

name: Data Analytics Club at NC State
description:
The Data Analytics Club is an undergraduate student-run organization associated with the Institute for Advanced Analytics at North Carolina State University. We provide resources for students interested in business and the field of data analytics. Students have access to guest speakers and leaders from the industry who are discovering insights, delivering business value, creating innovations using data, and recruiting in the field. Networking with these professionals is an opportunity to develop long-lasting relationships, and discuss the latest data technologies, concepts, and trends while learning how data analytics impacts and is incorporated into various fields.

 

To sign up for our mailing list and become a part of the club, please fill out this link. Follow us on instagram for further updates @dac_ncsu.
summary: The Data Analytics Club provides students access to guest speakers and leaders from the industry for the purpose of building a community and spreading knowledge related to how data analytics impacts and is incorporated into various fields.

name: Coasts, Oceans, Ports and Rivers Institute Student Chapter at North Carolina State University
description:
The COPRI student chapter at NC State serves as a platform to bring together graduate students interested in coastal and ocean engineering and related fields, and to facilitate their academic and professional development. To meet this goal, COPRI at NC State shall organize academic and social events to promote knowledge of the behavior and importance of the world’s coastlines. The COPRI student chapter at NC State shall encourage multidisciplinary collaborative efforts and provide opportunities to the student community to benefit from interactions with coastal engineers in academia and industry. COPRI at NC State shall promote a culture of professionalism among students and bring awareness of the state of the art in sustainable development practices for our coasts, oceans, ports, waterways, rivers and wetlands.

To join the COPRI national chapter please visit:

http://www.asce.org/coasts-oceans-ports-and-rivers-engineering/copri-membership/
summary: The COPRI Student Chapter at NC State serves as a platform to bring together graduate students interested in coastal and ocean engineering and related fields, and to facilitate their academic and professional development.

name: Food Recovery Network at NC State
description:
To strive toward both food justice and waste reduction, FRN guides college students through the process of recovering viable food leftovers from dining halls and donating to campus food pantries, local food banks, soup kitchens, and nearby shelters.  Wasting viable food is more than just a shame: 17% of landfill methane emissions come from wasted food, and significant amounts of energy and water are wasted in the production and disposal of untouched food.  In addition, 1 in 8 American households are food insecure, meaning they have trouble consistently accessing enough nutritious food. 

 

We want to change this by starting with NC State.  Since forming in 2015, the FRN at NC State chapter has redistributed over 16,000 pounds of leftovers from Clark, Fountain, University Towers, athletic events, RAVE catered events, and more. Although the Covid-19 pandemic dissolved the NC State chapter for a time, we strive to reintegrate this impactful and sustainability-driven service project back into the NC State community. We are looking for volunteers and those interested in making a difference on campus through sustainability and community service!

Sign up for service events here
summary: The Food Recovery Network is national organization that strives to reduce both food waste and hunger by redistributing college dining halls' would-be composted food to students and community members in need.

name: American Association of Textile Chemists and Colorists
description:
AATCC at NC State is a student chapter under the global parent organization AATCC. "Founded as the American Association of Textile Colorists and Chemists (AATCC), the Association continues to evolve to meet the needs of those in the ever-changing textile and material industries. AATCC has served textile professionals since 1921. Today, it provides test method development, quality control materials, education, and professional networking for a global audience." 

NC State's chapter aims to accomplish similar goals by providing professional networking opportunities while creating a community for all Wilson College of Textiles majors to take part in.
summary: AATCC is a professional organization with student chapters that provide testing standards and more importantly connections in the textile industry through various speakers and events. All majors at the Wilson College of Textiles are encouraged to join.

name: Criminology Club at NC State
description: The purpose of the Criminology Club is to provide students interested in the criminal justice field an  environment in which to further foster their interests, meet likeminded peers, and allow them the opportunity to  network within the field.
summary: The purpose of the Criminology Club is to provide students interested in the criminal justice field an environment in which to further foster their interests, meet like-minded peers, and allow them the opportunity to network within the field.

name: Operation Christmas Child Club
description:
Operation Christmas Child Club is a non-profit, year-round organization that collects, creates and packs necessities and children's items such as stationary, toiletries, stuffed animals, etc. into shoeboxes to give to children in third world countries around Christmas time.

Our mission is to make others aware of the assistance that children and families in underdeveloped countries need by promoting our club in a positive way. We work year-round to send shoeboxes with necessities to those in third world countries around Christmas time.
summary: Operation Christmas Child Club is a non-profit, year-round organization that collects, creates and packs necessities such as stationery, toiletries, stuffed animals, etc. into shoeboxes to give to children in third world countries around Christmas time.

name: The Tap Jam at NC State
description: The purpose of The Tap Jam is to provide tap dancers from all backgrounds and skill levels a place to express themselves, learn, collaborate, and perform. Tappers will meet weekly to work on technique, choreography, and to share personal experiences and pieces. One meeting at the beginning of the month will focus on helping beginners to learn and grow, without feeling overwhelmed by advanced choreography or the fast pace. The Tap Jam will also provide opportunities for members to perform for and share their love of percussive dance with the surrounding community.
summary: The purpose of The Tap Jam is to provide tap dancers from all backgrounds and skill levels a place to express themselves, learn, collaborate, and share their love of percussive dance with the surrounding community.

name: Veterinary Volunteer Service Corps
description: We typically put on community service events and charity fundraising events by partnering with other local organizations or entities such as animal shelters. We offer opportunities directly related to veterinary medicine or animal welfare as well as opportunities related to completely separate causes such as park clean ups. This is all an effort to help students become well rounded and expand their world perspective through the act of volunteer service.
summary: The purpose of VVSC is to provide an avenue for students, faculty, and staff of the North Carolina State University College of Veterinary Medicine to volunteer within the local community through various service projects and events.

name: SolarPack
description: Team of students working to build and race a solar vehicle to be used for energy efficient vehicle competitions, but with practicality in mind. The vehicle will be designed keeping up with US automotive design laws and will result in a street legal solar vehicle to be marketed and commercialized. Recruitment occurs at the beginning of each Spring and Fall semester through our in-person interest meetings and formal application.
summary: Team of students working to build and race a solar car to be used for energy efficient vehicle competitions. The vehicle will be designed with US automotive design laws in mind and result in a street legal solar vehicle to be marketed and commercialized.

name: Women's Veterinary Leadership Development Initiative at North Carolina State University
description:
The Chapter is affiliated with the national Women’s Veterinary Leadership Development Initiative (WVLDI). The Chapter aligns with the National WVLDI mission to support women and allies in achieving representation in leadership roles across all sectors—from practice ownership to academia and corporate boards.

The purpose of this student organization is to inspire and empower veterinary leaders to harness diversity and equity by creating an inclusive professional community where all members can deliver on the promise of the veterinary oath. Specifically, this Chapter aims to:

  • Support & Educate: Provide professional development, educational content, and leadership training for women and allies seeking leadership positions.
  • Mentor: Facilitate mentorship opportunities to build up the next generation of veterinary professionals
  • Engage: Give back to the local community through volunteerism and service projects.
  • Connect: Engage in meaningful dialogue and networking to support specific goals within leadership and professional development.
summary: Achieve leadership excellence in veterinary medicine that reflects the diversity of the profession. Empower veterinary students by equipping our members with tangible, practical skills in key areas such as interpersonal communication & management.

name: Circle of Sisterhood at NC State
description:
Vision

Making education a reality for girls and women around the world.

The Cause

For too many girls and women around the world, access to quality education is often limited. More than 60 million girls are out of school around the globe. Education is an answer to many of the global issues related to women – poverty, oppression, misogyny, brutality. Ultimately, more and more educated girls will mean stronger and healthier villages, communities, and entire countries.

Why Sorority Women?

Most sororities were founded in the 19th century because of very real obstacles to women achieving a college education. Sorority Founders bound together fiercely to fight for their right to higher education. Today, more than 160 years later, and millions strong, we vow to continue the legacy of our Founders by standing together again to help girls and women go to school around the world. Since the Circle of Sisterhood’s establishment in 2010, sorority communities across the U.S. have engaged in this global humanitarian effort to remove barriers to education for girls in the developing world. This movement by sorority women has already had an impact in 22 countries on 4 continents. 4 Sororities across North America will continue to help domestic U.S. charities in significant ways. The Circle of Sisterhood is a way for our entire community to contribute to the global community – together. We are a mechanism by which all sorority women can stand together across affiliation, age, color, and creed to make a difference in the lives of millions of girls and women. Collectively, we can do much more than we could as individuals or individual groups. We can give others an equal chance for rewarding lives and livelihoods through schooling, the learning of a trade, or even a college degree. One person can make a small difference. But as a community of educated women, our collective efforts will be transforming for generations to come.
summary:
The Circle of Sisterhood will leverage the collective influence of sorority women to raise financial
resources for entities around the world that are removing educational barriers for girls and women
facing poverty and oppression.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
description:
The Black Graduate Student Association (BGSA) was chartered on January 17th, 2017 at NC State University. BGSA provides inclusive professional and personal development opportunities for Black graduate/professional students, avenues for civic engagement, and access to academic and social support.  BGSA offers structured and informal opportunities for mentorship, peer connections, service learning, social capital, and professional growth. 

BGSA's Objectives:

  1. Develop a support system for Black students enrolled in graduate and professional programs.
  2. Create networking opportunities for Black students and alumni.
  3. Encourage members to maintain personal, academic, and professional growth.
  4. Host workshops, guest speakers, meetings, and other activities that add value to members' personal and professional lives.
  5. Serve our members and facilitate success in the daily endeavors of fellow graduate/professional students
summary: NC State’s Black Graduate Student Association promotes positive cultural awareness, social support, and professional/academic development for Black graduate and professional students.

name: Cosplay and Costuming Club at NC State
description:
The club offers information about cosplay, conventions, and the art of costuming. Main focuses include how to craft costumes, sewing, armor making, prop making, and more. We provide lessons, demos, and workshops. All levels are welcome!


Please join our Discord server for more information!

Meeting Location: 111 Lampe Room 243

Day: Thursday

Time: 7:30-9:30 PM
summary: The purpose of this club is to provide a place for people to talk about all things regarding cosplay, cosplay creation, and conventions! They will be able to learn, socialize, and give and receive help on their work.

name: SOL Garden
description:
SOL Garden is North Carolina State University's on-campus garden, established and operated by student volunteers. We follow agroecological practices, choosing organic whenever and wherever possible. The garden is located right behind Fountain Dining Hall in Lee Park. The garden is is always a work in progress, so there is always room for new ideas and innovations.

SOL Garden hopes to encourage others to make more sustainable choices everyday when it comes to food and environmental well-being. We encourage use of the garden for classroom experiments and projects and we rent raised beds each semester, which provides an opportunity for experienced gardeners to garden away from home and new gardeners to gain knowledge and experience. It is also a great way to make new friends with fellow plant-lovers on campus.

We  love sharing the space with others and are always looking for other students to get involved. Find us on instagram @solgarden.ncstate, or share interest in becoming a member right here on our Get Involved page.
summary: SOL Garden is an educational and demonstrative garden where students, faculty, and visitors can learn about small-scale agriculture/sustainable gardening, as well as a place of serenity and community.

name: Anthropology Club at NC State
description: A welcoming community that discusses the four subcategories of anthropology including: physical, linguistic, cultural, and archaeology. Throughout the year we hold weekly meetings and take trips as a group. We study humans - dead or alive!
summary: A welcoming community that discusses the four subcategories of anthropology including: physical, linguistic, cultural, and archaeology. Throughout the year we hold weekly meetings and take trips as a group. We study humans - dead or alive!

name: The Origami Club at NC State
description:
We are a campus-wide organization of students who are enthusiastic about origami, or paper folding. Everyone is welcome, from beginners to experts. We will be meeting every week to hang out and fold some awesome models together! Find our socials here. Email origamiclub@ncsu.edu if you have any questions!

Meetings for the Spring 2026 semester are held on Mondays and Thursdays from 7-8pm in Fox Labs, room 204.
summary: We are a campus-wide organization of students who are enthusiastic about origami, or paper folding. Everyone is welcome, from beginners to experts (though we are mostly beginners). Come join our chill group for some stress relief!

name: The League of Poker
description:
The League of Poker seeks to develop and promote the poker community at NC State by providing opportunities to discuss strategy, play poker, and meet fellow poker players. We will host in-person and virtual poker events so that all can learn to play and put their skills to the test in friendly competition.  All experience levels are welcome!

Click here to join our club discord here!
summary: The League of Poker seeks to develop and promote the poker community at NC State by providing opportunities to discuss strategy, play poker, and meet fellow poker players.  All experience levels are welcome!

name: Society for Health Systems
description: The purpose of the Society for Health Systems is to promote the use and awareness of systems engineering, analysis, and process improvement in the healthcare field by providing appropriate education and career opportunities to North Carolina State University students. This organization is a committee that operated under IISE but focuses specifically on Health Systems Engineering.
summary: The name of this organization is Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers Society for Health Systems Committee. This committee will utilize the acronym SHS in all publicity, materials, and correspondence.

name: Engaged Service Scholars Program
summary: TBD

name: Give Kids the World at NC State
description:
Origin:

Give Kids the World at NC State is based on the Give Kids the World Village in Florida. They partner with the Make a Wish foundation to send kids with terminal illnesses and their families on a week expense paid vacation so they don't have to focus on their illness. They get to run around and be kids!

Who we are :

Our organization works to bring that inspiration and hope back to campus to the students and to the surrounding areas of the triangle. We do service activities with other organizations and raise funds to support GKTW and those that share a similar mission. We work to embody the motto of Give Kids the World that says, "Where Happiness Inspires Hope." 

Join us! ::)

We want to inspire others and spread hope. We are currently trying to revive Give Kids the World's chapter at NCSU after it died down after COVID. Follow us on our Instagram below to see more of what we do!
summary: Spread awareness of Give Kids the World Village through fundraising and outreach events while overall promoting service and positive attitude.

name: CHAARG at North Carolina State University
description:
We liberate girls from the elliptical ++ show them that fitness can [+ should!] be fun by partnering with local fitness studios. Our mission is to empower every CHAARG girl to be the best version of herself.

 

CHAARG aims to ignite a passion in college-aged girls for health + fitness. Too many college girls rely solely on the elliptical for their exercise. CHAARG liberates girls from the elliptical + shows them that fitness can [+ SHOULD] be fun! Since every girl has a different preference of what fun is, we create opportunities for our CHAARG girls to “find their fit.”

 

Once a girl joins CHAARG, she is welcomed into a supportive + empowering community of 10,000+ girls! When you workout, you feel good — but, when you surround yourself with people to support you, you feel even better. CHAARG combines good friends + a good workout to keep every girl feeling her happiest + healthiest self.

 

This year we'll be taking classes like aerial yoga, kickboxing, goat yoga, rock climbing, pilates, cycling, and so much more!
summary: Our mission is to empower every CHAARG girl to be the best version of herself.

name: Tourism Management Association at North Carolina State University
description: Tourism Management Association at North Carolina State University is to provide a network for students who plan to enter the tourism industry as professional managers, or simply express an interest in exploring the impacts tourism has on global industry. Members share a common goal of helping each other understand the professions of event services, attraction management, destination marketing and management, and hospitality. Similarly, members are to promote the tourism industry in the community and engage in corporate partnerships and philanthropy. Members will actively work towards professional development, networking within the tourism industry, and skill building in an effort to prepare themselves for careers in tourism. The Association and its members are also responsible for increasing awareness within the student body of career opportunities in the tourism industry.
summary: Tourism Management Association at NC State University serves to provide a network for students who plan to enter the tourism industry as professional managers, or simply express an interest in exploring the impacts tourism has on global industry.

name: Period@NCState
description: PERIOD at NC State is a university chapter of PERIOD. The Menstrual Movement, a national organization dedicated to the belief that menstrual care is a basic human right. Our chapter focuses on education, advocacy, and service by fundraising and partnering with local organizations to distribute menstrual products to unhoused and underserved communities while raising awareness on campus.
summary: PERIOD at NC State is a student-led organization working to end period poverty by providing menstrual products, education, and advocacy. We promote menstrual equity, dignity, and access for students in the NC State community and the Raleigh community.

name: Canvas Club at NC State
description:
The Canvas Club is designed to bring artists together to paint in all mediums and styles, anywhere from beginners to advanced, to explore their creative interests, have fun painting, and develop their knowledge in a supportive and artsy environment. Our meetings give student artists a space to unwind with their paintings, and gives beginners the opportunity to grow with their interest in painting!
