Our meetings are biweekly, and for more information, you find follow our Instagram @canvasclubatncstate, or join our GetInvolved to join our email list!
summary: The Canvas Club is designed to bring painters together to paint in all mediums and styles, anywhere from beginners to advanced, to explore their creative interests, have fun painting, and develop their knowledge in a supportive and artsy environment.

name: Parkour Freerunning and Tricking at NC State
description:
Organization Description

Exploring and understanding bodily movement through disciplines of Parkour, Freerunning, and Tricking

Parkour is a movement discipline in which you train to overcome obstacles in the safest, most efficient way possible.

Freerunning is a term coined for a style of movement that evolved from parkour. Freerunning is trained not to overcome but to create artistic/aesthetic movement with the use of obstacles.

Martial Arts Tricking is an aesthetic blend of kicks, flips, and twists that a practitioner chains together in “combos” to create a unique art all it’s own. Sampling and using movements from martial arts, break dancing, and tumbling, tricking has many different styles.
summary: Exploring and understanding bodily movement through disciplines of Parkour, Freerunning, and Tricking

name: Melee At NC State
description:
Welcome to Melee at NCSU! Our goals are to promote involvement in the Super Smash Bros Melee eSport, learn about the game, promote friendly competition, and to encourage a positive and inclusive social atmosphere for everyone. Various online and offline tournaments, fests, and sessions will be held to help new members learn the game and to provide a competitive environment for members and students interested in the games. Outside of NC State, there are many monthly tournaments around Raleigh and NC that Melee players can join in order to improve and test their skills. Information about these tournaments will be regularly updated on the Melee at NCSU Discord server.

Learn more about melee here: melee.tv
summary: Melee at NC State's goals are to promote involvement in the Super Smash Bros Melee eSport, learn about the game, promote friendly competition, and to encourage a positive and inclusive social atmosphere for everyone.

name: Ski Club at NC State
description:
We are the recreational Ski and Snowboard club! In the spring semester, we organize 6-7 regular season trips to the mountains in Vermont, Snowshoe, Wintergreen, and Boone. This is a non-competitive, for fun only club with the goal of making snow sports more accessible to students in the Southeast region. Come out and join us on the slopes! 

Go to our website for more information: https://www.skiclubatncsu.com/
summary: We are the recreational ski and snowboard club! We organize 5-6 weekend ski trips in the spring semester to mountains near us such as Snowshoe, Wintergreen, and NC mountains. Interested? Sign up to receive information: https://forms.gle/gRzhz6wMoi2Mu7waA

name: Themed Entertainment Association at NC State
description:
Our club is dedicated to celebrating the magic and wonder of theme parks while also delving into the fascinating world of the theme park industry. We aim to provide a platform for students who share a passion for theme parks to come together, explore, and learn more about this dynamic field.

 

Here, we offer a diverse range of activities and events tailored to both entertain and educate our members. From exciting trips to various theme parks, where we get to experience the thrills firsthand, to engaging educational presentations that delve into the history, design, and business aspects of theme parks, there's something for everyone to enjoy.

 

One of our main goals is to bring exposure to the theme park industry through interviews with industry professionals, conferences, trips, and networking opportunities. We believe in providing our members with valuable insights into the inner workings of the theme park world, helping them gain a deeper understanding of this unique and multi-faceted industry.

 

In addition to our educational endeavors, we also organize fun activities and social events, creating a vibrant and welcoming community for theme park enthusiasts to connect and bond over their shared interests. Whether it's movie nights, game tournaments, or design contests, there's always something exciting happening within our club.

 

Join us and embark on a journey filled with adventure, knowledge, and unforgettable memories. Come be a part of our vibrant community as we explore the magic of theme parks together!
summary: Welcome to the Themed Entertainment Association @ NC State! Whether you're a passionate fan or aspiring to work in the industry, join us to share excitement, insider tips, career insights, and unforgettable park experiences. Come join us on this journey!

name: Epsilon Eta Environmental Fraternity Iota Chapter at North Carolina State University
description: Epsilon Eta's mission is to advocate for environmental justice and sustainability at NC State and the surrounding area. We welcome all students who have an interest in the environment and wish to make our communities more sustainable and eco-friendly. Our chapter wide philanthropy is the United Nation's 13th Sustainable Development Goal, Life on Land, and we organize many volunteer events dedicated to this cause. We are certified as a sustainable student organization through Sustainability at NCSU and a Well Wolfpack Certified Organization at the silver level. We also arrange unique opportunities to volunteer with local animal sanctuaries and we have adopted Lake Raleigh on NCSU's centennial campus. Epsilon Eta also collaborates with career development center to sponsor helpful career development workshops. Furthermore, we organize exciting outdoor social events including hikes, camping, and more. If you're interested in joining us please reach out and we'll give you information about our upcoming rush schedule!
summary: Epsilon Eta is NC State's only sustainability and environmental science fraternity. We are a co-ed organization that focuses on environmental community service, career development, and outdoor socials. We welcome students from any major!

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America at NC State
description:
Young Democratic Socialists of America (YDSA) at NC State is a chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA), a national organization working to establish democratic socialism in America.
We are a leftist organization that aims to educate and organize the NC State and greater Raleigh community toward equality, justice, and democratic socialism. We understand that true human liberation is impossible under capitalism, therefore we seek social change which extends democracy into all aspects of life – social, political, and economic.

Our mission is to empower students with the knowledge, support, and opportunity necessary to become lifelong, principled socialist organizers.

Our vision of socialism is in direct opposition to all forms of oppression and discrimination. Therefore, we are profoundly democratic, feminist, pro-trans, anti-racist, and anti-imperialist. We are home to a broad range of leftist thought, but forbid any ideological concessions that profoundly degrade our basic principles. Our work involves collaboration with other student organizations, the DSA, and all local groups seeking to improve the human condition.

( Please fill out this Google Form to get connected )
summary: YDSA at NC State is a youth chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA). We are a leftist organization that aims to educate and organize the NC State and greater Raleigh community.

name: Studio 804 Ballet Company
description: Studio 804 Ballet Company began as a club in 2017 initiated by four suite mates who were interested in ballet, but had no opportunities on campus to be more involved. Today, we have grown to incorporate a wide range of students, majors, dance styles, and campus opportunities. Our goal was to create a club that gave students the opportunity to dance and perform in a safe and accepting environment where creativity, passion, and fellowship are encouraged.  The club evolved from a small group of students who were hoping to succeed, to a group of individuals who are dedicated to dancing and creating beautiful movement. Studio 804 is entirely student led and has a winter and spring showcase annually at the end of each academic semester and participates in various promotional performance opportunities throughout the semester. Studio 804 also teaches fun, themed community classes at a beginner level throughout the semester the open to all at NCSU!
summary: Studio 804 Ballet Company reaches to serve all dancers by offering an advanced level, audition based, all-style dance company with classes throughout each week and a performance each semester as well as beginner level, fun monthly classes open to all!

name: Zero Waste Wolves
description: Supported by the Office of Waste Reduction and Recycling, NC State’s Zero Waste Wolves is a recognized student organization committed to reducing waste in their personal lives, on campus, and in the community. Through events, workshops, information sessions, and programs, the Zero Waste Wolves strive to spread the message about the environmental benefits of zero-waste efforts. Check our Instagram or GroupMe to know when the next meeting is!
summary: The Zero Waste Wolves aim to divert waste from landfills through campus projects, events, collaborations, and education. We love hosting events about how to be more sustainable on campus.

name: Global Corps
description:
Mission: Members of Global Corps serve as an extension of the Global Programs Office in Poole College of Management to educate Poole students about the value of global experiences and the variety of opportunities available on campus and abroad. Global Corps members organize and participate in information sessions, advise students on the study abroad process and experience, and create and implement marketing efforts. 

 

Recruitment: The application for the 2023-2024 Academic Year will open in Spring 2023. 

 

Application: Visit go.ncsu.edu/globalcorpsapply to complete the application.  

 

Eligibility: Students eligible to apply to Global Corps must be students in the Poole College of Management, have a 2.5 GPA and have either studied abroad or have plans to study abroad in the future. 

 

Meet the Current Members: Click here to meet the student members of Global Corps.

 

Questions: If you have questions, please reach out to poole_globalcorps@ncsu.edu.
summary: Global Corps serves as an extension of the Global Programs Office in Poole to educate Poole students about the value of international experiences with a specific focus on study abroad programs.

name: Geospatial Graduate Student Organization
description: This organization is designed to support students in their study of geospatial science and analytics. The organization provides educational, networking, and other professional development opportunities to connect students in the geospatial community and advances the interests of interdisciplinary geospatial research and education.
summary: This organization is designed to support students in their study of geospatial science and analytics.

name: Club Beach Volleyball at NC State
description:
!!!2025 TRYOUT INFORMATION POSTED ON WEBSITE!!!

 

Club Beach Volleyball at NC State competes through the Eastern Collegiate Volleyball Association (ECVA). We practice 3 times a week and participate in 3+ tournaments a semester. We break our season into 2 semesters with opportunities to tryout in the fall and spring. 2 practice days will be focused on improving volleyball skills and the 3rd will be game play, attendance is required for all 3. We typically host 2 open gyms to help people get a sense of what to expect from the club and its members and get some touches in before tryouts. We have 3 tryout days, with at least one on the weekend to accommodate people's schedules. You are only required to go to one and there will be cuts. Our team prides itself on being a welcoming and supportive community that will improve our players to be the best they can be, and we simply love playing beach volleyball! If you have any questions please reach out via our email or message us on Instagram where we post most of our announcements!
summary: Club Beach Volleyball at NC State, a student organization, focuses on developing versatile volleyball players, capable of competing at the regional and national level. Tournament format will be beach/ men's/women's doubles.

name: Student Film Society at NC State
description:
The Student Film Society at NC State is a club open to anyone who is passionate about film! SFS is dedicated to helping build a community among individuals who want to get involved in activities that range from film appreciation, discovery, discussion and production. We host FREE weekly screenings for members and guests, with a selection ranging from cult-classics to newly discovered films. For those looking to get super involved with the club, you will have the opportunity to attend our club socials, receive resources to sign up for film related opportunities and potentially even be provided with FREE advanced screening tickets! For students who want to get more involved in film making, we host a semesterly Short Film Festival!

Our primary form of communication is through Instagram (@ncsufilmclub) and email (studentfilmsocietyatncsu@gmail.com). If you’d like to be on the mailing list, please fill out the following form: https://forms.gle/4pa6BuxaDorTahzK7
summary: SFS at NCSU is a club for those who are passionate about film and want to get involved in film discussion, appreciation and production. SFS is dedicated to sharing the love of film to students through weekly screenings and film-related opportunities!

name: Bangladeshi Student Sports Club
description: Bangladeshi Students Sports Club at NC State aims to promote soccer, cricket, badminton, table tennis, board games and other fun sports among the students of NC state. The goal of this club is to aid students maintain a healthy and active lifestyle aside their busy work schedule. As its initiative the club plans to arrange matches during weekends and competitive tournaments each semester. The club will also try to provide students with necessary equipment for their sports activities.
summary: The Bangladeshi Students Sports Club at NC State is dedicated to promoting the soccer and cricket among the students of NC state as well as in the neighboring university. The club will arrange weekly matches and competitive tournaments in each semester.

name: The Smash Ultimate Club at NC State
description:
The purpose of this club shall be to provide a conduit for on campus involvement in the esport of Super Smash Brothers Ultimate. The club shall also focus on helping each of its member learn about and improve at the game through improvement sessions, friendly competition, and Smash Ultimate related events. Through these purposes the club shall also strive to provide an encouraging and fun social atmosphere where players of any skill level can learn, grow, and enjoy themselves.

Join our Discord at https://discord.gg/Eyef4Jr

https://www.youtube.com/c/NCSUSmashBros
summary: Providing a place for NC State students (and others) to play, learn about, and improve at Super Smash Brothers Ultimate while creating a fun, social environment. Join our Discord at https://discord.gg/Eyef4Jr

name: Radiology Club at NC State CVM
description:
SECTION 1: To endeavor to improve the stature and increase the knowledge of students of veterinary medicine interested in the field of veterinary diagnostic imaging.

SECTION 2: To elevate the standards of veterinary diagnostic imaging.

SECTION 3: To emphasize the importance of imaging in any field of veterinary medicine.

SECTION 4: To promote understanding and good will among its members.

SECTION 5: To stimulate interest, activity, and advancement in the field of veterinary imaging.

SECTION 6: To encourage cooperation, participation, and membership in the Student Chapter of the American Veterinary Medical Association.

SECTION 7: ALL POWER CLAUSE: To do everything and anything reasonably and lawfully necessary, proper, suitable or convenient for the achievement of the above stated, or for any of them, or for the furtherance of the said purposes.
summary: To endeavor to improve the stature and increase the knowledge of students of veterinary medicine interested in the field of veterinary diagnostic imaging.

name: Biomathematics Graduate Student Association
description:
The Biomathematics Graduate Student Association is an educational non-profit organization that exists to represent and advocate for graduate students enrolled in the biomathematics program at North Carolina State University. We obtain funds through a variety of methods. These include fundraisers, requesting funds through North Carolina State University’s Student Government Association, rebates from the North Carolina State University’s Graduate Student Association, various grants provided by the University, and soliciting endorsements from industry companies located in the Triangle area.

 

These funding methods provide us with an opportunity to fulfill our organization’s goals. We achieve them by creating a supportive network inside the biomathematics graduate program and by providing a forum for graduate student opinion and discussion. This allows us to provide a voice for our graduate students' opinions in all matters of mutual interest to graduate faculty and students. We also aim to increase the visibility of the program and its students and develop collaborative efforts that will benefit current and future students.  Another main goal is to nurture the cross-disciplinary attributes of the students and department as a whole by promoting professional interest and fellowship among graduate students.
summary: We are the Graduate Student Association chapter representing the Biomathematics Graduate Program at NC State.

name: Cosmetic Chemistry Club
description:
The Cosmetic Chemistry Club at NC State is the only student organization on campus for students interested in learning about cosmetics and the industry behind them. We are an interdisciplinary club and open to students of all majors. We seek to provide opportunities to learn the science behind skincare and beauty products, as well as provide professional development opportunities through company visits and guest speakers. The Cosmetic Chemistry Club is a great way to meet other students, build your knowledge of cosmetics, and prepare for a career in the cosmetic industry in a fun, collaborative way.

Email cosmeticsncstate@gmail.com for questions! Follow our Instagram and subscribe to our email list through our LinkTree!
summary: We are a student organization where students learn all about the science behind beauty products. All majors welcome!

name: Women in Physics at NC State
description: The purpose of this organization is an intersectional approach to the advancement of women in the physics department through fellowship, career development and academic enhancement. We welcome people of all genders (cis, trans, and non-binary).
summary:
The purpose of this organization is an intersectional approach to the advancement of
women in the physics department through fellowship, career development and academic
enhancement. We welcome people of all genders (cis, trans, and non-binary).

name: Forestry and Environmental Resources Leadership Board
description: The Forestry and Environmental Resources Leadership Board is made up of student leaders within the Department of Forestry and Environmental Resources. Board members create events and programs to benefit the FER community, such as the Spring Mentor Program, the Focal Area Fair, and the External Learning Experience Panel. Membership in this organization is through application only.
summary: We are an on-campus organization focused on fostering community and making connections within the Department of Forestry and Environmental Resources through the creation of student-run events and programs for current FER students.

name: Phi Kappa Tau
description: Since receiving their charter on February 9, 2019, the Chi Chapter has been devoted to pioneering positive change in the dynamic of the Greek community by committing to brotherhood, service, and leadership through deliberate and thought-provoking action. The teamwork demonstrated by these group of men, who each have brought their own passion and diverse backgrounds, has been nothing short of amazing.
summary: Phi Kappa Tau is a mens social fraternity that was founded in 1906. The Chi Chapter at NC State re-chartered on February 9, 2019.

name: Office for Institutional Equity and Diversity
summary: The Office for Institutional Equity and Diversity (OIED) is committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible, and diverse intellectual and cultural campus experience related to the mission of North Carolina State University (NC State).

name: Forest Biomaterials GSA
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be:

  1. To represent the graduate student body in the Forest Biomaterials Department,
  2. To provide a forum for Forest Biomaterials graduate student opinion,
  3. To voice Forest Biomaterials graduate student opinion in all matters of mutual interest to graduate faculty and students, and
  4. To promote professional interest and fellowship among Forest Biomaterials graduate students.
summary: The mission of the FB GSA is to represent the graduate student body in the Department of Forest Biomaterials, providing a forum for students to express their opinions and promote professional development and fellowship among the students.

name: The National Parks Club at NC State
description:
We are focused on raising awareness of the importance of national, state and local public lands and advocating for their conservation through volunteering, discussion, and recreation. This club aims to create a fun, welcoming environment for people of all backgrounds to bond over a common interest.

 

Giving back is an important aspect of conservation so we are engaged in service around the region every month. Building an appreciation with nature begins with exposure so we take monthly day hikes and a weekend trip every semester so students can build connections to nature and then advocate for it.

 

If you are interested in getting involved-check out our social media (linked below) or shoot nationalparksclub@ncsu.edu an email. 

 

There are no requirements to join-show up to anything when you can! Check out our google calendar linked below. 

Read more about our early days in this Technician article

National Parks Club additional information
summary:
Together we foster student connections to nature and public lands through service and education, and recreation.  

Meetings are to be announced. Dates of upcoming events can be followed on our club Instagram page @nationalparksclub

name: Digital ShapRs at NC State University
description: The purpose of this club is to equip Poole Students with the mindset and tools they need to help shape the multi-faceted digital future. As mankind is about to experience the most profound, far-reaching, and interdisciplinary changes in its history, the dynamics of change and their complexity make it hard for individuals, but also for entire institutions to adapt accordingly. Among others, innovation areas such as Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Big Data, or Blockchain are likely to have severe impacts on the way tomorrow’s managers conduct business. Staying ahead of the emerging megatrends is inevitable for business people to be able to shape the economy, technology, and, lastly, society as a whole. By raising awareness of these trends, bringing in speakers, fostering professional networks, and creating a platform for critical discourse, Digital ShapRs will help to shape tomorrow’s digital leaders.
summary: Digital ShapRs will equip Students with the mindset and tools needed to excel in and shape the multi-faceted digital future. We are about to experience the most profound, far-reaching, and interdisciplinary changes in its history. Let's be ready for it!!

name: Citizen Science Club at NC State
description: Citizen Science Club works to promote inclusion and expand on interdisciplinary measures in the scientific community. Our biweekly meetings feature presentations from guest speakers whose work involves citizen science and may present opportunities for members to be involved in their research or club training and participation in citizen science projects. Events outside our usual meetings involve more social outings like visits to the North Carolina Museums for science related showings and group volunteering! One of our current outreach projects is our collaboration with Centennial Middle School where we go as guest speakers to talk about the importance of citizen science.
summary: Citizen Science Club was created to promote science as interdisciplinary endeavor, to welcome and involve students of any degree in scientific research as well as science communication, and to advocate for diversity in science.

name: National Organization of Minority Architect Students
description:
NOMAS has a mission of increasing influential voices, promoting the quality and excellence of minority design professionals. By increasing the number of student members in this organization, we add strength in the voice with which we can speak against apathy, bigotry, intolerance, and ignorance; against abuse of the natural environment; and for the un-empowered, the marginalized and the disenfranchised.

We want to build a strong organization and develop a showcase for the excellence and creativity which have been ignored for so long. 

Our goals are to increase the level of participation in the social, political and economic benefits afforded the citizens of this nation and to tear down the barriers that make full participation unattainable.

  1. Foster communications and fellowship among minority architect students
  2. Act as a clearing house for information and maintain a roster on practitioners
  3. Create and maintain relationships with other professionals and technicians
  4. Be an effective source of motivation and inspiration for minority youth.
summary: NOMAS has a mission of increasing influential voices, promoting the quality and excellence of minority design professionals.

name: Red Terrors Soccer Support Club at NC State
description:
The Red Terrors at NCSU is a student led organization to support NC State Men's and Women's Soccer Teams. We create an incredible atmosphere at soccer games by standing behind the goals to lead chants, wave flags, use red smoke, and cheer for NC State! Whether you're new to the sport of soccer or a seasoned veteran, come join the party with us at every men and women's soccer games!

President: Caleb Huff

Vice President: Thomas Sutton

Treasurer: Thompson Jones

Secretary: Caleb Moss
summary: Student Soccer Support Group At NC State University

name: Squash Club
summary: Squash Club is aimed at putting together a fun group to play squash casually and competitively. We have practices and tournaments where we play against colleges from around our area.

name: Filipino American Student Association
description:
Kumusta!

The Filipino American Student Association is a cultural organization for those interested in Filipino culture. Our purpose is to bring awareness of Filipino culture to our local communities through meetings and events.

Keep up with us on Instagram feel free to shoot us a message!
summary: FASA is be a non-profit organization composed of individuals interested in Filipino culture. Our purpose and mission is to bring awareness of the Filipino culture among the individuals at NC State, as well as the surrounding community.

name: Pakistani Student Association
description:
We, the Pakistani students of North Carolina State University, establish PACKSA, the Pakistani Student Association. This organization is established in order to foster an inclusive environment for NC State students while specifically highlighting the history and culture of Pakistan. Our objectives are to promote the culture and heritage of all provinces in Pakistan: Punjab, Kashmir, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK), Sindh, as well as raise funds for Pakistani causes (Refugees, etc.), host cultural awareness events, promote awareness of poverty/issues in Pakistan, and to create an environment where we can link students to resources of the NCPAA (North Carolina Pakistani Association) through our student-led organization.

 

Our organization is in no way limited to Pakistani or South-Asian students. It is open to all, and we aim to provide a fun and diverse atmosphere for those who would like to be a part of it.
summary: The goal of PACKSA is to promote awareness for the culture and customs of Pakistan and to bring a sense of community for Pakistanis at NC State, while working on the recognition and assistance of poverty-stricken parts of Pakistan.

name: Grand Challenges Student Organization
description:
The Grand Challenges Student Organization was created in support of the Grand Challenges Scholars Program at NC State. Centered on the 14 Grand Challenges of Engineering set out by the NAE, our organization strives to support students in their pursuit of generational achievement. Through professional development opportunities with corporate partners, peer-to-peer research spotlights, deliverable-focused workshops, and community building events, we elevate the mission of the GCSP at NC State.

Membership in GCSP is required for membership in this student organization: these can be granted semi-concurrently.
summary: The Grand Challenges Student Organization provides professional, academic, and social learning opportunities on engineering topics related to the Grand Challenges of Engineering.

name: KPACK
description:
KPACK is an organization that aims to share the love of dancing, particularly choreography specific to Korean pop (KPOP) music. We participate in performances, film dance covers, and host community workshops. Community workshops are open to everyone and allow anyone who is interested in K-pop dance. We aim to build a sense of community through Korean music and culture, and other similar interests. KPACK hopes to become a power "PACK", improving our dance skills together and enjoying Korean music. If you are interested in working with us, send us an email at kpackncsu@gmail.com!

Our auditions for official members are held at the beginning of each semester, so if you are interested in joining us, please look out for audition information on our social media. You don't have to be a KPACK member to attend our community workshops!

Join our email list for our weekly newsletters!

Join our Discord server to get connected to KPOP fans at NC State!
summary: KPACK is an organization that aims to share the love of dancing, particularly to Korean pop music. We participate in performances, film dance covers, and host free community classes.

name: Black Campus Ministry
description: Black Campus Ministry is a space for students to learn how to be unapologetically Black and unapologetic followers of God and his word in all parts of their lives at NC State. We meet weekly on Wednesdays at 6 p.m. To get connected with us feel free to join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/47257038/8Ua9Sa
summary: Black Campus Ministry is a space for students to learn how to be unapologetically Black and unapologetic followers of God and his word in all parts of their lives at NC State.

name: Pokémon Club at NC State University
description:
Pokémon Club at NC State University is an official club for a large, campus-wide community that has come together over several years to connect, help each other, and welcome new people who play the popular mobile game, trading card game, console games and are fans of the Pokémon series as a whole.

Join our discord to interact with other trainers and make new friends! https://discord.gg/sXzza3g

Please do not forget to sign up for the club role to get notified of club meetups by clicking the button in #welcome! 

Find all of our important links here: https://linktr.ee/ncsupogo
summary: Our organization is a community for a popular all-age-friendly franchise and is composed of NC State University students, faculty, graduates, and local community members. Find all of our important links here: https://linktr.ee/ncsupogo

name: Liquid Rocketry Lab
description:
Liquid Rocketry Lab is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, student-led engineering-focused team with a goal to design, fabricate, test, and launch a liquid-fueled rocket to reach the Karman Line (100km altitude), the official 'edge of space'. This would make us the first college team in the world to achieve such a feat. Liquid Rocketry Lab aims to achieve this goal through research focusing on high-powered liquid-fueled rocket systems including but not limited to: Research in the rocket structures and supersonic aerodynamics, guidance navigation and control systems (GNC), recovery methods, and of course rocket engine design and manufacturing. We are a dedicated group of students at NC State University with a diverse background in rocket science and engineering as a whole. This is a great place to practice skills and gain experience that you may not otherwise get a chance to as an undergraduate.

If you are interested in joining the team, please fill out our rolling application on our website, which can be found by clicking "Join The Team" on the landing page. Both business and engineering rolling applications are available, but the default application is the engineering application.


Liquid Rocketry Lab is generously supported by Valworx
summary: The purpose of LRL is to conduct research and construct novel, high performance rocket systems and to advance the North Carolina rocketry community through highly ambitious projects. Want to join the team? Fill out our application on our website!

name: Architectural Graduate Student Association
description:
AGSA Architectural Graduate Student Association

The purpose of this student organization is to facilitate the development of a strong graduate student community within the School of Architecture at the College of Design.
summary:
Architectural Graduate Student Association 

The purpose of this student organization is to facilitate the development of a strong graduate student community within the School of Architecture at the College of Design.

name: Pride Student Veterinary Medical Community
description:
Pride Student VMC serves to connect, support, and empower LGBTQ+ people and allies within the academic veterinary community through:

- fostering an understanding of what it means to be an LGBTQ+ person within the veterinary profession

- educating veterinary and graduate students on the importance of and contributions made by the LGBTQ+ minority within the veterinary profession

- encouraging healthy, supportive relationships between LGBTQ+ people and allies

- advocating for the respect and equity of seen and unseen LGBTQ+ people
summary: Pride Student VMC (formerly Broad Spectrum Veterinary Student Association) serves to connect, support, and empower LGBTQ+ people and allies within the academic veterinary community.

name: Women in Textiles Leadership Development Network
shortName: WithIt Chapter at NC State University
description:
WithIt at NC State is dedicated to empowering students in the textiles and home furnishings industries through leadership development, education, mentorship, creative exploration, and meaningful networking. Our chapter creates space for both professional growth and hands-on engagement with textiles, interiors, and craft-based design.

By joining our chapter, students can:

  • Build connections with industry professionals and peers

  • Gain insight into career paths in textiles, home furnishings, interior design, and related creative fields

  • Participate in hands-on activities, workshops, and craft-focused events that explore textile and interior design processes

  • Grow as emerging leaders while developing both creative and professional skills

  • Help carry forward WithIt’s mission of fostering female leadership and advancing the future of the industry
summary: WithIt at NC State is the student chapter of Women in Textiles, supporting leadership, mentoring, education, and networking in the textiles and home furnishings industries.

name: Gaelic Athletic Association at NC State
description:
The NC State Collegiate Gaelic Athletic Association was established in the summer of 2018 to encourage, support, and promote Gaelic sports at North Carolina State University. The goals of the organization are to introduce the sport of Gaelic Football to students at NC State and build a social group and atmosphere that is invested in and celebrates Irish culture.

The target audience for the organization is as follows: anyone! Whether you are interested in learning a new sport or just learning the ways of Irish society please come to any of our practices to watch, though we strongly encourage anyone with the slightest interest to participate.
summary: This was established in the summer of 2018 to promote Gaelic sports at NCSU. The main goal of the club is to introduce Gaelic Football to NC State and create a community that celebrates Irish culture here in Raleigh

name: Veterinary Anesthesia and Analgesia Club at NC State
description: The Veterinary Anesthesia and Analgesia Club at NC State has a mission of defining and promoting the highest standards of clinical practice of veterinary anesthesia and analgesia by providing future veterinarians with advanced training opportunities and supplemental education in the clinical practice of veterinary anesthesiology, and promoting scientific research and education in veterinary anesthesiology and analgesia.
summary: The Veterinary Anesthesia and Analgesia Club at NC State is a club devoted to promoting the highest standards of clinical practice of veterinary anesthesia and analgesia by providing future veterinarians advanced training and supplemental education.

name: Pi Kappa Phi Fraternity
description:
Pi Kappa Phi, commonly known as Pi Kapp, is an American Greek Letter secret and social fraternity. It was founded by Andrew Alexander Kroeg Jr., Lawrence Harry Mixson, and Simon Fogarty Jr. on December 10, 1904 at the College of Charleston in Charleston, South Carolina.

 

Our chapter designation at NC State is Tau. We were founded at NC State in 1920.
summary: Pi Kappa Phi, commonly known as Pi Kapp, is an American Greek Letter secret and social fraternity. It was founded by Andrew Alexander Kroeg Jr., Lawrence Harry Mixson, and Simon Fogarty Jr. on December 10, 1904 at the College of Charleston.

name: Best Buddies at NC State University
description: Best Buddies International is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment and leadership development for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD). Best Buddies is the world’s largest organization dedicated to ending the social, physical and economic isolation of the 200 million people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD). Our programs empower the special abilities of people with IDD by helping them form meaningful friendships with their peers, secure successful jobs, live independently, improve public speaking, self-advocacy and communication skills, and feel valued by society.
summary: Best Buddies International is a nonprofit organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment, and leadership development for people with disabilities.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE)
description: Our chapter stands as the oldest Hispanic organization on campus, founded in 1996. Today, SHPE enjoys a strong but independent network of professionals of student chapters throughout the nation. The concept of networking was the key basis for our organization, and therefore we consistently strive to send student members to SHPE conferences. As a chapter we strive to contribute positively to minority students' recruitment and retention in STEM fields through an emphasis on the following pillars: Academic development, professional development, leadership development, chapter development and community outreach.
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development. Through our membership, SHPE will inspire the next generation of STEM innovators.

name: Hindu YUVA at NC State
description: Hindu YUVA is a socio-cultural organization that aims to preserve, practice, protect, and promote Hindu culture and heritage, by bringing together Hindu youth on the NC State campus. Our goals include creating opportunities for members of the NC State community, to understand and practice Hindu Dharma, or the Hindu way of life. Conducting regular programs that focus on all the aspects of personality development (physical,mental and spiritual). We aim to develop strong character and leadership skills in our members, emphasizing values such as self-discipline, self-confidence and a spirit of selfless service for the society. We do this by  doing service activities in and around campus to benefit the local community as well as raising awareness about issues affecting Hindus around the world.
summary: Hindu YUVA is a socio-cultural organization that aims to preserve, practice, protect, and promote Hindu culture and heritage, by bringing together Hindu youth on the NC State campus.

name: Club Flag Football at NC State
description:
8/07/2025 - Alex London (President)

Thanks for taking the time to stop and read through our page! It may seem a little empty here at the moment, but we are working on getting rosters updated for the season, along with other information.

Club Flag Football at NC State fields both a Men's and Women's team. We hold tryouts for each team once a semester. Tryouts are tentatively scheduled for BOTH Wednesday, August 27th (6-8pm), and Friday, August 29th (3-6 pm) on Miller Fields. (For those who have signed up be on the lookout for an email)

I highly encourage you to come out to Rec Fest. This is a great opportunity to speak with club members from both our Men's and Women's teams. It is also a great opportunity to see what else Well-Rec has to offer!

If you would prefer to fill out the interest form (this is mainly so we can contact you later, so ensure your information is correct!) they can be found below:

Men's Team Interest Form: Click Here
Women's Team Interest Form: Click Here

We aim to attend 2 Regional tournaments (UGA at Athens, GA, and VT at Blacksburg, VA) along with 1 National Tournament (Rice University/Houston Texans at Houston, TX). We will likely have other scrimmages or tournaments pop up throughout the semester.
Dates for the above-mentioned tournaments can be found here: NIRSA Tournaments

If you have any questions or just want to reach out, feel free to contact me directly at salondon@ncsu.edu

Thanks for your time, and I hope to see you at tryouts!
- Alex London (President)
summary: We are a motivated group of men and women who love the game of flag football. We practice a few nights a week and play in local and national tournaments across the US. Contact Alex London at salondon@ncsu.edu for more information on how to join.

name: NC State Chapter of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description: Looking to join our org? Email us or contact one of our social media pages to get more info!
summary: The mission of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) is to ensure the political, educational, social, and economic equality of rights of all persons and to eliminate race-based discrimination.

name: F1RST at NC State
description: F1RST is a student-led organization on NC State’s campus aimed at creating community among first-generation and low-income students. Our mission is to provide social, academic, and professional support and development for these individuals. We often collaborate with First in the Pack and TRIO - two of the university’s initiatives to aid first-generation, low-income, and minority students. This organization is meant to prepare students for success beyond their collegiate careers.
summary: F1RST is a student organization aimed at creating community among first-generation and low-income students at NC State.

name: Foundation For International Medical Relief of Children
description: FIMRC is an international non-profit that aims to improve healthcare in developing countries. The goal is to educate communities on preventative measures and provide them with adequate resources to maintain their health. The purpose of this student organization is raise money for medical projects, volunteer at FIMRC clinical sites, serve the community, and raise awareness. It is required by the organization that four community outreach projects are conducted each year, and that the chapter raise funds and supplies for FIMRC projects worldwide.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is raise money for medical projects, volunteer at FIMRC clinical sites, serve the community, and raise awareness.

name: National Society of Black Physicists at NC State
description: We promote the professional well-being of African-American physicists and physics students within the NC State and the local community. Activities we participate in include outreach, conferences, professional development workshops, cultural events, and community-building activities. You are not required to be a physics major to join.
summary: Promote the professional well-being of African-American physicists and physics students within the NC State and the local community.

name: Pickleball Club at NC State University
description:
Pickleball is the fastest-growing sport in America! We hold 4 weekly practices from 6-8 that are come and go as you please. We host social events, internal club tournaments, collegiate matches, and we travel for DUPR hosted Collegiate tournaments across the country. We are also currently TOP 10 in the country. 

 

Open Play Times:

Sundays, Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays from 5PM to 8PM

Everyone is welcome to come to open play! No paddle, partner, or past experience required!

We meet at the pickleball courts behind Carmichael Gym.

 

ncsupickleball.org
summary: Club Pickleball at NC State University will promote competitive and casual play of pickleball for men and women of all skill levels. We go to DUPR Collegiate tournaments, host our own tournaments and events, and have social events!

name: Biology Club at NC State University
description: The purpose of the Biology Club is to promote interest in biological sciences, increase awareness of biology, and encourage fellowship among students and faculty of the Life Sciences. Our biweekly meetings include faculty research presentations, discussions of current events, social events, and special topics in biology. For more info,click here!
summary: The purpose of the Biology Club is to promote interest in biological sciences, to increase awareness in biology, and to encourage fellowship among students and faculty in the Life Sciences through service and speakers.

name: Ice Hockey Club at NC State
summary: The Ice Hockey Club at NC State is a club which competes in the ACHA Division 2, and is a member of the ACCHL, and plays against teams across the East Coast and nationally.

name: STEM Education Graduate Student Association
description:
We represent master's and doctoral students from all programs within the STEM Education Department. Our mission is to:

  • Build a supportive and inclusive community for graduate students in STEM education.

  • Encourage collaboration across disciplines and programs.

  • Champion innovative approaches and solutions developed by graduate researchers.

  • Elevate and showcase the work and voices of our members.
summary: We represent master's and doctoral students from all programs within the STEM Education Department.

name: Puppetry Arts Club
description: Welcome to Puppetry Arts Club at NC State! Puppetry Arts Club is dedicated to creating opportunities for all student artists. We welcome students who enjoy hand building figures, stop motion animation, performance, costume and set design and more - our community is open to all creators. You're invited to be a part of our community!
summary: Puppetry Arts Club is dedicated to creating opportunities for all student artists. We welcome students who enjoy puppetry, performance, and stop motion animation, and more - our community is open to all creators.

name: MIx: Motivation and Inspiration
description:
Want to become a member of our TEDxNCState Planning Team?  Fill out our interest form today!  The application aims to place you in the department you'd like to work in most and tell us a little about yourself! You can find our interest form here: https://forms.gle/SVDKjsCZmp5ghB9F7 

MIx aims to promote inspiration through hosting TEDxNCState, a TED affiliated annual speaker series. With an opportunity for networking, participants will gain an interdisciplinary perspective showcasing how one can make a difference in the community.

For more information regarding MIx, email mixncstate-org@ncsu.edu, check out our Instagram @tedxncstate, or visit our website at www.TEDxNCState.com.
summary: MIx aims to promote inspiration through hosting TEDxNCState, a TED affiliated annual speaker series. With an opportunity for networking, participants will gain an interdisciplinary perspective showcasing how one can make a difference in the community.

name: NC State University
description:
The purpose of the Student Association for Fire Ecology is to
provide students from diverse backgrounds with an open forum on fire ecology through which
research can be shared, networks formed and funding and information resources accessed. At NC State we are focused on raising interest in controlled burning in the SE. We are also focused on promoting and introducing students to the different careers in Fire Management.
summary:
The purpose of the Student Association for Fire Ecology is to
provide students from diverse backgrounds with an open forum on fire ecology through which
research can be shared, networks formed and funding and information resources accessed.

name: Agronomy Club at NC State
description:
The Agronomy Club at NC State works to promote an interest in agriculture, preserve our farmland and natural resources, and encourage service for our local and global community.

We meet every 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month in 2405 Williams Hall at 6:30 PM. 

If you have any questions or concerns please reach out to our primary contact or NCSUAgronomy@gmail.com
summary: The Agronomy Club at NC State works to promote an interest in agriculture, preserve our farmland and natural resources, and encourage service for our local and global community.

name: Club Paintball @ NC State
description: The Paintball Club is a recreational and competitive club formed in 2006. The club offers opportunities for members to play paintball recreationally throughout the year. The club also has a speedball team that began competing in the National Collegiate Paintball Association (NCPA) in the 2012-13 season. In the team's inaugural season, the team placed 10th overall in the nation taking 4th place at nationals. Players of all skill levels are welcome and having your own equipment is not necessary to join. During the 2013-14 season, the team took 1st place at nationals finishing the season in 4th place. Players of all experience and skill levels are encouraged to join.
summary: The Paintball Club is a recreational and competitive club formed in 2006. The club offers opportunities for members to play paintball recreationally throughout the year. Members may also compete on the traveling speedball team.

name: The Agroecology Club at North Carolina State University
summary: To build a close community, educate, and provide service to not only club members, but also the University, and the local Raleigh area, in order to share the ideas and values of agroecology and sustainable food systems.

name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space
description:
The primary mission of the NCSU SEDS chapter is to further the exploration and development of space through means of:

    • Competitive and innovative challenges, such as the SEDS National Design Challenges
    • Innovative projects designed and created by the organization members with or without the partnership of the NCSU faculty
    • Educating the general public on the importance of progress in the STEM fields relating to space, and advocating for the space industry as a whole
    • Interacting with institutions involved in the furthering of space exploration and technology via collaboration, seminars, tours, and conferences
summary: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at NC State (SEDS at NC State) is a student organization with the goal of equipping every engineering student with the skills and opportunities to engage in and impact the future of space.

name: Global Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene Student Chapter
description: Goal: to educate students on emerging issues in water, sanitation, and hygiene globally and build connections with the Global WaSH faculty cluster as well as other non-profits and NGO’s in the triangle.
summary: Global WaSH Student Chapter is a platform for promoting awareness about Global WaSH issues and research to students from multiple disciplines, and collaborating with non-profit organizations worldwide by bringing practical and affordable solutions.

name: Poultry Science Club at NC State
description: The Poultry Science Club at NC State engages members through outreach, service, and professional development opportunities. The Poultry Science Club at NC State has monthly meetings that are held on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of every month. The club participates in outreach and service events through the Fall and Spring semesters to interact with the community. The Poultry Science Club also travels to Atlanta, GA for the annual International Production & Processing Expo to explore internship and job opportunities in the agriculture and poultry fields.
summary: The Poultry Science Club at NC State engages members through outreach, service, and professional development opportunities.

name: Construction Management Association of America at NC State University
description:
The Construction Management Association of America is an industry association dedicated to the practice of professional construction management. Their mission is to promote the profession of construction management and the use of qualified CMs on projects.

CMAA was formed in 1982 to establish standards for managing construction projects. Construction management is rooted in traditional project management practices and also requires a unique body of skills and professional knowledge to perform successfully. CMAA is delivery method neutral; seeking to ensure that all professionals are prepared to succeed regardless of the scope, scale, or complexity of any project or program. 

Today, CMAA has nearly 16,000 members including individual professionals, professional service companies, and construction owners in the public and private sectors, as well as academic and associate members.
summary: An industry association dedicated to the practice of professional construction management

name: Crossfit Club at NC State
description:
We workout at 12th State Gym on Monday (7:30-8:30pm), Wednesday(7:30-8:30pm), and Friday (12:00-1:00pm)! 

Visit our Instagram (crossfitclub_ncsu) for updates!

Here is our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/108126090/VBdNPG4H

And this is our GroupMe for the carpool:  https://groupme.com/join_group/108126223/cvwOUYs9

The NC State CrossFit club seeks to enhance the lives of students through CrossFit. We aim to unite a group of like-minded people in an environment where the values of community, kindness, and continuous improvement are encouraged. In preparing ourselves to succeed in life, this is a great opportunity for leadership, learning, and service. This challenging, time efficient, and extremely effective physical training program has advanced the concept of fitness and has become a powerful alternative to traditional fitness routines. As a new and intense training opportunity for CrossFit veterans and rookies alike (and all NC State students!), the system is highly practical and beneficial. 

CrossFit is a form of physical training that utilizes constantly varied, high-intensity, functional movement patterns. As CrossFit describes it, the workouts are designed to “prepare the body for any physical contingency—not only the known, but also the unknown.”
summary: CrossFit Club seeks to unite a group of like-minded people through an environment which values community, health, and challenging yourself. CrossFit promotes holistic fitness through constantly varied, high-intensity, functional movements.

name: Financial Literacy Club at NC State
description:
Our goal is to help students of all backgrounds and majors increase their financial literacy, so that they may become financially independent now and especially after graduation. We will achieve this by helping to develop financial discipline, develop healthy financial practices, gain a better understanding of the economy, and learn investment types (stocks, bonds, mutual funds, 401(k), etc).

Please Note: The information provided by the Financial Literacy Club at NC State is provided for educational purposes only and should not be relied on as investment, legal, tax or other advice as it does not take into account the particular needs of any specific investor.
summary: The Financial Literacy Club at NC State aims to increase the financial literacy and financial independence of our members. Topics include economics, investments, credit/loans, wealth management, etc. The club is open to all students, regardless of major

name: Korean American Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization will be to provide students an opportunity to learn more about Korean and Korean American culture. KASA will host events throughout the academic year to promote Korean/Korean American culture and share related political or social issues on campus and beyond.
summary: KASA strives to embrace Korean culture by hosting fun events on NC State campus. We hope to provide a community of students where both Korean and non-Korean students are able to connect with the rest of the NC State community.

name: Fencing Club at NC State
description:
The fencing club is a student organization within club sports that will promote fencing. We hope to help competitive fencers improve and accept all beginners from the ground up, as well as spread the word that fencing is a fun sport.

 

Club Information:

We practice on Monday's from 9:00PM -11:00PM and Wednesday's from 8:00pm - 11:00pm at Carmichael Gym in room 1240. All nonmembers are welcomed to come to try out the club for 4 free practices before having to pay club dues.

 

Updated 2025

President: Grace Rheinecker

Vice President: Cole Zoutewelle

Treasurer: Owen Lewis

Secretary: Riley Remy

Safety Officer: Jacob Ewy
summary: The fencing club is a student organization within club sports that will promote fencing. We hope to help competitive fencers improve, as well as spread the word that fencing is a fun sport.

name: The Thomistic Institute at North Carolina State University
description:
The mission of The Thomistic Institute at North Carolina State University is to foster intellectual and theological discussion on the teachings of the Catholic church and how these teachings relate to modern society, with an emphasis on the teachings of St. Thomas Aquinas. Speakers from the greater Thomistic Institute will be brought to campus to speak on these topics. The target audience/member is any NC State student with an interest in the above.

The greater Thomistic Institute exists to promote Catholic truth in our contemporary world by strengthening the intellectual formation of Christians at universities, in the Church, and in the wider public square. The thought of St. Thomas Aquinas, the Universal Doctor of the Church, is our touchstone.  The TI is an academic institute of the Pontifical Faculty of the Immaculate Conception at the Dominican House of Studies in Washington, D.C.
summary: The Thomistic Institute exists to promote Catholic truth in our contemporary world by strengthening the intellectual formation of Christians at universities. The TI brings in speakers every semester to speak on a wide variety of topics.

name: BAPS Campus Fellowship
description:
An organization approved by the Bochasanwasi Shri Akshar Purushottam Swaminarayan Santha, Inc. (BAPS), a leading denomination of the Hindu faith in North America, and will represent itself in a way that is consistent with the guidelines set forth by BAPS

 

For further information, please contact:

 

Rishabh Patel

rhpatel3@ncsu.edu

919-434-9134
summary: The BAPS Campus Fellowship purpose is to unite students on NC State’s campus and provide opportunities to learn about Hindu heritage, culture and a more Swaminarayan way of life.

name: Oriental Orthodox Club at NC State
description: Oriental Orthodox churches include the Coptic, Syriac, Ethiopian, Eritrean, Indian, and Armenian churches, but anyone is welcome to attend!
summary: Oriental Orthodox Club is a religious club based as one of the denominations of Christianity. There will be guest speakers, outings, Bible studies, community service activities, and more!

name: Farm Bureau Collegiate Young Farmers & Ranchers Chapter at NC State
description: The Farm Bureau Young Farmers & Ranchers (YF&R) Program is for young women and men between 18 and 35 years of age. It provides opportunities to develop leadership traits, public speaking skills and exposure to current issues in agriculture from state and national leaders. Members can experience these opportunities through attending bi-monthly meetings with industry leaders, participating in the Farm Bureau Collegiate Discussion Meet, and representing NC State at local, state, and national conferences. YF&R members support agriculture by serving on special Farm Bureau committees, county Farm Bureau Boards of Directors, and testifying as experts addressing concerns of the farmers and ranchers at public hearings. If having an impact on the future is of interest to you, Farm Bureau will reserve a special place for you in the YF&R program.
summary: A Young Farmer and Rancher organization on campus.  Open to anyone 18-35.

name: The Textile Association of Graduate Students
description:
Mission Statement: To create a platform for the Wilson College of Textiles graduate students to (1) share ideas in an inclusive environment, (2) network and socialize and (3) learn from each other.

Membership: Every Wilson College of Textiles graduate student is an automatic member of TAGS. Members are encouraged to participate in all events and are eligible to run for officer positions. Positions are determined at the end of each spring semester, however, volunteers can participate at any point.  Officers participate in formal meetings weekly as well as all semester events in addition to external events. Please contact an officer if you are interested in learning more!

TAGS is the Wilson College of Textiles’ chapter of the NC State GSA.  A representative from TAGS sits on the GSA council and represents the interests and concerns of the students to the university. TAGS is also represented in the Tompkins Textile Student Council, the student government of the Wilson College of Textiles.
summary: TAGS is the Wilson College of Textiles chapter of the NC State Graduate Student Association. TAGS represents the interest and concerns of graduate student of Wilson College to the GSA and Tompkins Textile Student Council.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description: The Minority Association of Pre-Health Students is a pre-medical chapter of the national organization, Student National Medical Association (SNMA). SNMA is the oldest student organization representing the interests of medical students of color and serving the needs of underserved populations. Its membership consists of more than 5,000 medical, pre-medical, physician, corporate, and institutional members nationwide. SNMA was established in 1964 by medical students from Howard University College of Medicine and Meharry Medical College School of Medicine. A goal of MAPS is to increase underrepresented student matriculation to all professional health related programs, with an emphasis on medicine. MAPS at NC State is excited to serve students from minority groups who are interested in health and learning more about improving professionally and academically, with the goal of diversifying medicine and patient care.
summary: The Minority Association of Pre-Health Students is a pre-medical chapter of the national organization Student National Medical Association (SNMA), to represent the interests of pre-medical students of color and serve the needs of underserved populations.

name: Pershing Rifles Company L-4
description: We the members of Company L-4, National Society of Pershing Rifles are a group of motivated future leaders in the United States. We hope to create bonds and bolster camaraderie between the members of our organization as well as provide Drill and Ceremony based services to not only NC State but to the Triangle area as a whole. Our standard is Excellence and we accept nothing less. "No Slack". We are open to all Students and Cadets of any branch.
summary: Pershing Rifles Company L-4 is a Student Military Organization that exemplifies professionalism and excellence on campus. We forge Military Officers and Civilians that lead with distinction. We are open to all students and cadets of all military branches

name: Turtle Rescue Team at NC State
description: Our mission, at Turtle Rescue Team, is to provide the highest quality medical, surgical, rehabilitation, and husbandry for sick and injured wild turtles, reptiles, and amphibians in the hopes of returning them to their natural habitat. We strive to give our members the best hands-on experiences possible to further enrich their veterinary training at the DVM and undergraduate levels. Through community and school outreach programs, we strive to show North Carolinians the importance of conserving and protecting our native wildlife and ecosystem, spread the word about the services we offer, and offer educational experiences for interested high school students through partnerships with several area schools.
summary: The Turtle Rescue Team is dedicated to medical, surgical, rehabilitation, & husbandry for injured turtles & reptiles. We provide hands-on learning, leadership, & research experiences for vet students & undergrads under the tutelage of world-class vets.

name: Divine Youth Association
description: It shall be the purpose of this organization to engage in devotional activities to develop God loving mentality, inculcate moral and devotional values in young adults and to train and engage young adults throughout the world; to arrange, hold, and conduct public lectures, classes, conferences, youth camps, discussions, seminars, publications, libraries, research, retreats, tours, visits, and like, all for the benefit of young adults; and to conduct all other activities for proper administration of the organization. DYA strives to create a divine relationship among the university student community through the principles of devotion to understand more about our way of life and way of thinking and lead ourselves towards a wholesome integrated life with pure, powerful, progressive and sensitive mind.
summary: DYA strives to create a divine relationship among the community through the principles of devotion to learn more about our way of life and way of thinking and lead ourselves towards a wholesome life with pure, powerful, progressive and sensitive mind.

name: Women’s Ice Hockey Club at NC State
description: Still a young team, we won the ACCHL championships in 2022 and want to keep growing the women's program! We have weekly practices on Sundays, off-ice training, and games on certain weekends. We can help you find some equipment, and no prior experience is required!
summary: We are a women's ice hockey organization open to NC State students of all skill levels.

name: Color guard/Winter guard at NC State
description: Color guard/Winter guard at NC State is an organization who aims to spread the awareness and opportunities provided by the activity of color guard. Students involved will be given the opportunity to develop the skills and technique required to compete on an international level. The color guard operates as a section of the NC state marching band in the fall semester but begins training in the early winter months for the internationally competitive winter guard team. The winter guard competes both locally in the AIA circuit (Atlantic Indoor Association), as well as at the national/international level in WGI (Winter guard International).
summary: Color guard/Winter guard at NC State is an organization who provides performance and competitive opportunities to students both locally and nationally through the sport of the arts, also known as color guard.

name: Higher Education Doctoral Association
description: HEDA will promote the sharing of research, guidance through the Ph.D. process, professional development, and a sense of community among doctoral students in the Higher Education program at North Carolina State University.
summary: HEDA will promote the sharing of research, guidance through the Ph.D. process, professional development, and a sense of community among doctoral students in the Higher Education program at North Carolina State University.

name: African American Cultural Center
description:
Mission

The African American Cultural Center is an expressive and abundant haven for the NC State community.

Through a model of student success, well-being, and belonging, we provide mentorship, resources, and cultural programming to empower all NC State students to excel and embrace their identity.

Our work is grounded in the diasporic traditions of storytelling, innovation, and communal care.

Vision

The African American Cultural Center is a beacon of success, where dreams flourish and diasporic heritage is preserved.

We create opportunities for the NC State community to be valued as authentic, emergent, and expansive.

We acknowledge always, we are free.
summary: The African American Cultural Center is an expressive and abundant haven for the NC State community.

name: Student Veterans Alliance of NC State
description: The Student Veterans Alliance of NC State is an organization dedicated to offering new opportunities to Veterans and their families. Our mission is to promote community values through our organization and bring together Veterans and military affiliated students to harbor diverse social and professional groups. We will develop innovative experiences and new leadership roles for members to excel and promote themselves through the Alliance. All members are encouraged to bring their talents to the table and strive for success.
summary: NC State University’s Student Veteran Alliance is a nationally recognized chapter of Student Veterans of America. The SVA is a student group dedicated to advocating for and creating community among our student veterans and their families.

name: Acts 2 Fellowship
description:
We're a Christian group on campus trying to live out our faith in a close-knit community based on Acts 2:42-47. We love studying the Bible, discussing tough questions, playing Catan or football, and eating diverse foods from pupusas to kimchi. Whether you are investigating Christianity for the first time or hoping to grow in your faith, come join us!

check linktr.ee/a2fncsu
summary: We're a Christian group trying to live out our faith in close-knit community. We love studying the Bible, discussing tough questions, playing games, and eating diverse foods. Whether you're new to Christianity or hoping to grow in your faith, join us!

name: Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering Ambassadors at North Carolina State University
description:
The MAE Ambassadors aim:

  1. To foster social interaction between the public, faculty, and undergraduate students associated with the MAE department at NC State.
  2. To organize social events with the purpose of creating a stronger community within the MAE department or furthering the opportunities of members of the MAE Department.
  3. To provide support to MAE undergraduate students in academic, career and collegiate planning.
  4. To provide information to the community, visitors, undergraduate students, faculty, and staff about the MAE department.
summary: The MAE Ambassadors foster interaction between the public, faculty, and undergraduate students associated with the MAE department at NC State.

name: Students of Life Sciences at NC State
description:
SOLS' purpose is to share the passion of service in life sciences with the community through outreach and engagement and to build a community within life sciences, with special attention on engaging first-year students, on the basis of common interests.

 

Contact us at: solsncstate-org@ncsu.edu

Follow us on Instagram: @ncstate_sols

Join GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/91288262/jp0nZVUD
summary: SOLS' purpose is to share the passion of service in life with the community through outreach and engagement and to build a community within life sciences, with special attention on engaging first-year students, on the basis of common interests.

name: Aces and Aros
description:
Aces and Aros is a club serving the needs of students who are questioning, who identify as members of the asexual and/or aromantic communities and their allies at NC State.

We hold bi-weekly meetings for A-Spec and questioning people to share their experiences and discuss topics related to the community.

Ask us for an invite to our Discord!
summary: Aces and Aros is a club serving the needs of students who are questioning, who identify as members of the asexual and/or aromantic communities and their allies at NC State.

name: Science Olympiad Alumni Organization at NC State
description: We are group of students passionate about giving back to Science Olympiad here at NC State. We do that through running our own Division B Invitational and helping out at local tournaments for all Divisions. We have fun socials every month ranging from game nights to fun icebreaker activities. No prior experience or past participation is necessary to get involved in our club. If you have a passion for STEM education, connecting with past Science Olympiad competitors, enjoy collaborating with others, and are excited to make a difference throughout NC, then this is the club for you!
summary: We provide support at Science Olympiad tournaments as well as host our very own invitational through test writing, event leading, and general volunteering.. You do NOT need to be an SO alumni to join nor do you have to be a STEM major. We welcome all!

name: Persian Culture and Language Association
description: The Persian Culture and Language Association doubles as an affinity group for those from Persian speaking countries including Iran, Afghanistan, Tajikistan, parts of Uzbekistan, parts of China, and parts of Kurdistan, etc. This organization serves as an educational resource to the general student body at North Carolina State University regarding the Persian culture and language. This organization fosters an open-minded pursuit to educate and appreciate Persian language and culture through cultural, educational, and social events. The Persian Culture and Language Association promotes the appreciation of diversity by empowering a diverse community to converge and celebrate Persian culture and language.
summary: The Persian Culture and Language Association doubles as an affinity group for those from Persian speaking countries including Iran, Afghanistan, Tajikistan, parts of Uzbekistan, parts of China, and parts of Kurdistan, and more.

name: Society of Sales Engineers at NC State
description: https://salesengineers.wixsite.com/website
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to educate students about technical sales by giving them the skills and networking opportunities with industry professionals to have successful careers as sales engineers.

name: Pack Recovery at NC State
description:
Pack Recovery provides a safe, supportive, and inclusive community for collegiate students who identify as in Recovery and students who identify as sober-curious. The purpose of the organization is to help reduce stigma around substance use and other problematic behaviors, and to help students at any level of recovery, with no requirement for a specified length of recovery necessary.

 

Note: More members are involved in Pack Recovery than those shown on the roster, for privacy reasons.
summary: The purpose of Pack Recovery is to provide a safe, supportive, and inclusive community for collegiate students in recovery for substance use disorders.

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math
description: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (oSTEM) is a professional and social organization for LGBTQ+ students and allies in STEM fields. Various meeting topics, events, and programming focus on aspects of professional development and support as well as community bonding for LGBTQ+ students and allies in STEM majors entering STEM fields. Visit our website for more information: https://ostem.wordpress.ncsu.edu/. If you want to get in contact with us for any reason, fill out this form: go.ncsu.edu/ostem-contact-form
summary: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (oSTEM) is a professional and social organization for LGBTQ+ students and allies in STEM fields.

name: Bi/Pan
description:
The purpose of this student organization is to serve the needs of students who identify along the bisexual/pansexual and biromantic/panromantic spectra and their allies. Bi/Pan meets every Wednesday at 7 p.m. and provides a safe space for bisexual, pansexual, flexible, queer, curious, or questioning students to feel affirmed, address challenges and build community through networking and solidarity via topic-led discussions and sharing experiences among other students with similar identities. 

Bi/Pan now has a discord server! Join us here: https://discord.gg/pFtWnSyk
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to serve the needs of students who identify along the bisexual/pansexual and biromantic/panromantic spectra and their allies.

name: Pride Alliance
description:
