The Pride Alliance is NC State University's student group dedicated to promoting equality and unity for Gay, Lesbian, Bisexual, Transgender, Queer, Intersex, Questioning, and Straight Ally students, faculty, and staff on our campus and in our community. Our goal is to be supportive and proactive via educational and social events for our diverse campus body. The group is a safe and supportive space for people of all sexual orientations, gender identities, cultural backgrounds, life stages, and beliefs.

Meetings occur every Thursday bi-weekly from 6-8PM at the LGBTQ Pride Center.

Join our e-mail list! (We send out a newsletter the week of every meeting): Newsletter Signup Form
summary: The Pride Alliance is NCSU's student group dedicated to promoting equality and unity for Gay, Lesbian, Bisexual, Transgender, Queer, Intersex, Questioning, and Straight Ally students, faculty, and staff on our campus and in our community.

name: Internal Audit Students Club
description: The Internal Audit Students Club (IASC) is open to all accounting and non-accounting students who are interested in learning more about the internal audit, advisory and assurance professions. Club meetings are industry professional led with open-forum setting, so students gain a bird’s eye view of what internal auditors are currently doing as well as technical aspects of the career. Additionally, students can engage with the Institute of Internal Auditors organization professionals who share their education and career development journeys. IASC opens students to a broader range of professional networking, industry insights, and career development opportunities.
summary: The Internal Audit Students Club (IASC) is open to all accounting and non-accounting students who are interested in learning more about the internal audit, advisory and assurance professions.

name: Platform Magazine
description: Platform Magazine was founded in 2016 as PackFashion Magazine. When founded, the primary intention of PackFashion was to be a student-lead, run organization and publication. Throughout the years and rebranding process, Platform has continued to develop as a student organization and creative community. It is the goal of Platform to always maintain a focus on student voices, maintain a consistent publication, and support a community of creatively-minded individuals. Visit our website to learn more about ways you can get involved!
summary: Platform Magazine is NC State's fashion and culture magazine. Platform Magazine is a creative community and a voice for artists and creatives from all corners of NC State's campus.

name: Theta Chi Fraternity
description:
Founded on the principle of friendship through the mutual assistance of every member, Theta Chi aims to provide its members with an enjoyable and fulfilling collegiate experience that also prepares them for rewarding, successful lives after graduation.

 

Status: Organizational Probation through December 31, 2023. Violations: Alcohol and FSL Social Events Policy
summary: Theta Chi is one of the oldest men’s college fraternities in North America. Founded in 1856, more than 185,000 members have enjoyed the Fraternity’s traditions, ideals, and fellowship.

name: Swine, Poultry and Aquaculture Club
description: This organization will be known as the Swine, Poultry, and Aquaculture Club which includes the North Carolina State University College of Veterinary Medicine Student Chapter of the American Association of Avian Pathologists and North Carolina State University College of Veterinary Medicine Student Chapter of the American Association of Swine Veterinarians.
summary: The Swine, Poultry, and Aquaculture Club (SPAC) is a club for veterinary students interested in working with or learning more about any of the species covered by the club.

name: Junoon
description: Junoon is a competitive dance team at NC State that strives to provide an accepting community in which individuals can express their passion for dancing and share their interests and talents with others. We indulge in various different styles of dance including Bollywood, Bhangra, Raas, Hip Hop, Contemporary, and more. Junoon aims to provide opportunities through this fusion dance style for individuals to engage, explore, and immerse themselves in South Asian cultures and dance through competitions as well as performances at or around NCSU.
summary: The purpose of Junoon is to provide students of North Carolina State University an opportunity to express themselves through dance in a fusion dance style as a dance team.

name: Navarasa at NC State
description: Navarasa is NC State's annual intercollegiate premier Indian Classical Dance Competition, with all proceeds going to charity. Teams travel from across the country to compete, and placing at Navarasa qualifies teams to compete in the National Indian Classical Dance Competition (Origins). Navarasa aims to spread awareness and educate the community about the South Asian culture. Throughout the year, we fundraise in order to raise money to hold the event and to donate to charity as well.
summary: Navarasa is NC State's annual intercollegiate premier Indian Classical Dance Competition, with all proceeds going to charity. Navarasa aims to spread awareness and educate the community about the South Asian culture.

name: Chordination A Cappella
description: Chordination is NC State's newest mixed-voice, competitive a cappella group. Chordination strives to create an inclusive, supportive, and fun environment that emphasizes diversity, cohesion, and community through our shared love of music. By sharing our passions, we aim to bring joy and connection to the Wolfpack community while abiding by all North Carolina State University policies and guidelines. Chordination is a family of talented musicians who work as one to create amazing music! If you are interested in taking part in our organization, please reach out for audition details.
summary: Chordination is NC State's newest mixed-voice, competitive a cappella group! Proudly representing NC State as '21, '23, and '25 ICCA Quarterfinal Champions, Chordination aspires to positively represent the Wolfpack community while sharing our music.

name: Her Campus NC State
description:
"At Her Campus Media we work every day to empower young women with the tools, passion, confidence, and training they need to succeed, no matter what they dream of doing."

With 318 campus chapters across 44 states and nine countries run by more than 10,000 contributors, Her Campus is the biggest media source for college-age women. Best of all, it is run entirely by different chapters of women in college. Her Campus network receives more than 16 million monthly unique visitors and more than 90 million monthly multi-platform content views. 

Why join Her Campus? Not only is it a great career opportunity, but it is fun too!

For someone interested in journalism, there are countless stories of women getting into the industry. Contributors have been offered jobs and internships with Glamour, Vogue, Buzzfeed, Vanity Fair, Seventeen, Marie Claire, Harper’s Bazaar, People magazine, W magazine, Teen Vogue, InStyle, Lucky, Esquire, O Magazine, MTV, The Washington Post, Digitas and Ogilvy, among others. If you aren’t interested in pursuing that kind of career, it is still a great way to be able to say and show published works.

Members can be involved in writing, social media, or marketing groups. With Her Campus we are able to give you experience in any area you want to learn more about.

Not only will it benefit your resume, but it will also give you a network of supportive girls to be friends with forever.

We are currently not accepting new members, but we welcome you to join us next semester! Please email us to be put on a list! PLEASE DO NOT CREATE AN ACCOUNT on the website until registration opens. Thank you!
summary: Her Campus is an international organization where college women write articles that are published on the online blog Her Campus. Join this group to write articles about anything you want, learn about business and social media, and make great friends!

name: Marine Science Club
summary: The objective of Marine Science Club is to promote marine science on NC State's campus by giving passionate community members a place to gather, as well as furthering their knowledge of marine science.

name: PCM
summary: Our purpose is to help students of all backgrounds discuss and answer questions of faith and spiritual issues.

name: International Society of Tropical Foresters at NC State
description: The NC State Chapter of the International Society of Tropical Foresters (ISTF) is a student-led organization that acts in concert with the College of Natural Resources to enhance the international perspective of forestry studies at NC State University. The chapter objectives include the advancement of science, technology, education, and practice of professional forestry in the tropics and to apply the knowledge and skills of the profession to benefit society as a whole. The NC State chapter regularly interact with other chapters across the globe. ISTF at NC State meet monthly and typically feature speakers from the professional forestry community that work in and around the tropics.
summary: Student-led organization which focuses on management of and research on tropical forests

name: Sports Science Club at NC State
description: The purpose of the Sports Science Club at NC State is to create a community, provide professional development, and coordinate community service opportunities for NC State students minoring in Sports Science and/or who are interested in the sports science field. This organization is geared towards students who are interested in careers relating to athletic training, biomechanics, exercise physiology, fitness, health care, nutrition, sports medicine, sports science, strength and conditioning, personal training, and physical therapy. This club will hold monthly meetings that focus on roundtable discussion, reviewing, and presenting on various topics in the sports science field. Students will have the opportunity to take their own initiative to present and educate the club on their own self-chosen topics that they are interested in. Professionals within the area will be invited into meetings periodically to answer questions about their career and/or sports science related questions. This club will also work to coordinate community service and fundraising efforts for a local charity or cause.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to create a community, provide professional development, and coordinate community service opportunities for NC State students minoring in Sports Science and/or who are interested in the sports science field.

name: Figure Skating Club
description: Our organization is a competitive club sport at NC State. The Club Figure Skating is a loving community of brothers and sister skaters here to push and inspire one another. Our biggest goal is to compete at nationals one day, but to get there we need to push ourselves and work as a group to do so. We are welcome to all levels!
summary: To spread collegiate figure skating across not only NC State, but as well as the rest of North Carolina and the east coast, to provide a supportive environment for any skater at any level, and to encourage athleticism, work ethic, and sportsmanship.

name: Quantum Computing Club at NC State
description:
The Quantum Computing Club at NC State is a community of students interested in learning about and developing a skillset in quantum information and computing. Quantum computing can be a difficult field to start in, so the club is designed to break down the barriers to entry by providing members with a collaborative learning environment and opportunities to learn quantum programming with hands-on workshops and hackathons. We also work closely with industry and university partners like Duke and UNC Chapel-Hill to expand the scope of research, learning, and professional development opportunities for all members.

To learn more, follow our Instagram https://www.instagram.com/ncsuquantum/
summary: The Quantum Computing Club at NC State is a community of students (from beginners to advanced) interested in learning about and developing a skillset in quantum information and computing. Join us for hackathons, workshops, and fun events!

name: Pack Robotics at NC State
description:
Pack Robotics is NCSU's VEXU team. We work together to design, fabricate, build, and program two competition robots to participate in the yearly Vex VRC game. We travel to events throughout the season and compete with teams across the country. We also partner with local high school teams and leagues to volunteer and mentor our nearby robotics community.

 

We use Discord as our main platform for engagement. Join through this link.
summary: Work with Vex Robotics at a college level and participate in VEX U competitions.

name: National Organization for Rare Disorders at NC State
description: The National Organization for Rare Disorders Student Chapter at NCSU aims to provide students in related biological sciences and biomedical fields with valuable networking, volunteering, and research opportunities in the rare disease space. Our mission is to primarily raise awareness, educate and advocate for the research & challenges regarding rare diseases, as well as to create a safe space for those affected by them in our growing Wolfpack community. By partnering with local non-profit organizations, biotechnology companies, & research labs on and off campus, we plan to invite representatives to share their experiences and involvement in the rare disease network, as well as promote research and internship opportunities to aspiring students who are interested in a career within this field.
summary: Our mission is to provide a safe space for students within our community to share their experiences and challenges with rare diseases. Additionally, we aim to educate students as well as introduce career building opportunities within this field.

name: The Black Artist Coalition
description: The goal of this student organization is to celebrate and uplift black artists across all artistic disciplines. We seek to create a space that honors the importance of art in black history and culture while providing a voice for the artists of today. This purpose will be fulfilled through educational and uplift programing that highlights the talents of organization members and also by welcoming diverse voices.
summary: The goal of this student organization is to celebrate and uplift black artists across all artistic disciplines. We seek to create a space that honors the importance of art in black history and culture while providing a voice for the artists of today.

name: Kesem at NC State University
description:
The purpose of this student organization is to serve children through and beyond a parent’s cancer. Through various activities throughout the year we support children that have been affected by a parent’s cancer all culminating in a week-long summer camp for said children. 

National Statement of Purpose: "Kesem is a nationwide community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond their parent's cancer. Kesem's flagship program, Camp Kesem, operates free summer camps for children who have been impacted by a parent's cancer.” 

JOIN OUR GROUPME FOR UPDATES: https://groupme.com/join_group/102443279/aeD4eLZv
summary: Kesem is a nationwide community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond their parent's cancer. This ends with a week-long free summer camp for these children run by NC State students.

name: Technical Association of Pulp and Paper Industry
description: We the members of the Technical Association of the Pulp and Paper Industry – Student Chapter, desiring to promote education and social activities, to unite the students in the Pulp and Paper curriculum, and create an everlasting peer network, do hereby establish this Constitution for the Technical Association of the Pulp and Paper Industry.
summary: We desire to promote education and social activities, to unite the students in the Pulp and Paper curriculum, and create an everlasting peer network.

name: Society for Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics & Native Americans in Science
summary: SACNAS is an inclusive organization dedicated to fostering the success of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans, from college students to professionals, in attaining advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership in STEM.

name: Student Veterinary Emergency & Critical Care Society at NC State
description: We are the NC State Chapter of the National SVECCS at the College of Veterinary Medicine at NC State, and we promote the education and involvement of veterinary students in all aspects of emergency and critical care medicine.
summary: We are the NC State Chapter of the National SVECCS at the College of Veterinary Medicine at NC State, and we promote the education and involvement of veterinary students in all aspects of emergency and critical care medicine.

name: Crop and Soil Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: As the Crop and Soil Sciences chapter of the NC State Graduate Student Association, we are made up of graduate students seeking degrees in Crop or Soil Science. We seek to create a shared community for graduate students in the department and to advocate on their behalf.
summary: Graduate student association for the Crop and Soil Sciences department

name: Underrepresented STEM Graduate Student Organization
description:
USGSA works towards the following goals:

  • Provide support for minority/underrepresented science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) graduate students and, in doing so, build a community from all STEM graduate programs and departments.
  • Provide a platform for graduate students to discuss their concerns.
  • Disseminate information of interest or importance to its members.
  • Advocate and foster unity within the graduate students through seminars, social gatherings, and other activities.
summary: USGSA aims to improve diversity in STEM fields, provide support to students from underrepresented groups, and create a more inclusive community for NC State graduate students.

name: Strength Club at NC State University
description:
The purpose of this student organization is to create a community of like-minded individuals that already possess advanced knowledge of Powerlifting and Weightlifting, including those aspiring to compete at local, state, and national levels. Due to the high incidence of injury in the sport, the club is being restricted to members who already have 2-5 years of lifting experience or a starting Wilks Score of an intermediate lifter (a Wilks Score of at least 300).

The objectives of the club are as follows…

  1. Educate club members on proper form and technique involved with particular exercises.
  2. Encourage fellow members of the club to better themselves in and outside of the gym.
  3. Create a positive and social environment that club members will enjoy being a part of.
  4. Establish lasting connections and friendships that will last throughout the time a club member is at NC State and beyond.

If you are interested in joining, please reach out to our club secretary who is listed below, and we will get back to you!
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to create a community of like-minded individuals that already possess advanced knowledge of Powerlifting and Weightlifting with aspirations of building a increasing their strength.

name: Nepali Students' Association
description:
The Nepali Students Association (NSA) has a deep belief in the solidarity of the community and is an effort to reinforce the connection among Nepalese students at NCSU. We as a team are sincerely committed to North Carolina State University's Nepalese students to resolve the challenges they encounter throughout and beyond their studies. The organization also provides support and guidance for newly arriving students and their families for a successful transition into university's environment. Having a vibrant community, proud culture and a deep societal connection, we believe in fostering and spreading knowledge of Nepal and its cultural heritage through social and cultural events. We believe in the connection with other cultures as well, hence we collaborate and participate in events of mutual interest with other organizations on campus and throughout the City of Raleigh.

Interested becoming a Member Click to Join us.
summary: NSA is an association of students aimed to aid Nepalese students at North Carolina State University throughout their journey at NCSU and also to foster and spread the knowledge of Nepal and its cultural heritage through social and cultural activities.

name: Sierra Club Student Coalition
description: The purpose of The Sierra Club Student Coalition is to provide an environment where students with a passion for environmental justice can come meet to learn, grow and work with the local North Carolina Capital Group of The Sierra Club. The main goal of this club is to contribute to helping the environment! We mainly volunteer for local organizations by helping out with their projects and causes.
summary: The Sierra Club Student Coalition is affiliated with the NC Capital Group of the Sierra Club. We are a club where students with a passion for environmental justice can learn about local environmental issues and work alongside the Capital Group.

name: Latinx Veterinary Medical Association at NC State
description: The Latinx Veterinary Medical Association is a club from the College of Veterinary Medicine whose main purpose is to raise awareness of the Latinx communities in the veterinary profession. We strive to make an inclusive and accepting environment for all who desire to join us. Our goal is to create a space for the Latinx community at the veterinary college to provide them with professional resources and a place to connect with other Latinx students. Even though our focus will be geared toward the Latinx community, it is open to all who wish to learn about these amazing ethnicities.
summary: Latinx Veterinary Medical Association is a club at the College of Veterinary Medicine whose main purpose is to raise awareness of the Latinx communities in the veterinary profession. We strive to make an inclusive and accepting environment for all.

name: MannMukti at NC State
description: The NCSU chapter of the national organization MannMukti (www.mannmukti.org). We discuss important mental health issues that are prevalent in the South Asian community. We welcome students from all backgrounds and encourage everyone to attend our meetings and events!
summary: MannMukti@NCSU is a club at NC State that aims to destigmatize mental health in the South Asian community through conversation, learning, and connection. We aim to foster a friendly, safe space to check in, advocate, and discuss mental health topics.

name: Pack United
description: Pack United is a registered student-athlete led non-profit organization at NC State. Through our three pillars: awareness, education and action, we strive to promote social impact in the community. Our primary initiatives are food and housing insecurity, civic engagement, and educational equity.
summary: Pack United aims to bridge the social justice, food and housing insecurities, educational equity, and civic engagement  that exist in the community through our three pillars of awareness, education, and action.

name: 180 Degrees Consulting at NC State
description: 180 Degrees Consulting (180DC) is the world’s largest university based consultancy, providing non-profit organizations around the world with high-quality, extremely affordable consulting services. We are committed to working with organizations to develop innovative, practical and sustainable solutions to the challenges they are facing. Learn more about our branch here at NC State & 180DC Global.
summary: The world’s leading university-based consultancy with a mission to achieve the largest social impact while providing affordable, high quality consulting services for nonprofits and social enterprises through practical and innovative solutions.

name: Office of Strategic Brand Management
description:
• Protect the trademarks, service marks, logos, symbols and indicia that belong to the University and that are associated with the values, purpose, and traditions of NC State.
  • Promote the University through brand identity and public awareness that comes from the use of the trademarks.
  • Support NC State students by using trademark royalties collected from the sale of licensed products to provide athletic, merit, and study abroad scholarships, as well as need-based financial aid programs.

The Trademark Licensing office provides guidance, resources, and examples for licensees, fans, students, and university departments so that they can use NC State trademarks in accordance with pertinent policies, rules, and regulations.
summary: Office of Strategic Brand Management

name: New Student Programs
description:
New Student Programs addresses the holistic needs of each new NC State student to create a foundation of success.

We achieve this through:

  • Cultivating strategic partnerships across the university
  • Promoting an environment of personal responsibility
  • Fostering inclusivity through a shared campus identity
  • Partnering with parents and families
  • Preparing and empowering student leaders to serve the campus
  • Striving to be innovative in meeting the needs of our community
summary: New Student Programs addresses the holistic needs of each new NC State student to create a foundation of success.

name: Fighting Game Club at NC State
description:
Weekly Meetings on Wednesdays @ 6:30PM @ 111 Lampe Drive Room 353. JUST COME! YOU DON'T HAVE TO REGISTER OR ANYTHING

CHECK THE DISCORD LINK BELOW FOR UPDATES!

The FGC at NC State provides a community for people who take an interest in video games in the fighting genre, running events such as tournaments and casual meet-ups!

 

We host weekly meetings for fighting game players to play games. Anyone is free to bring in and play any game they want.

 

We also host themed tournament for players in the Raleigh area. In the past we've ran games such as Guilty Gear, BlazBlue, and many more! We often even play versus games that aren't typically called fighting games, for example, we have played and hosted tournaments for Mega Man Battle Network, Sonic Adventure 2 Battle, Garry's Mod, and more.

 

 

Join the Discord to be notified of events:

FGC Club at NCSU Discord

SWorld Twitter

SWorld YouTube 

archives:

Shadow World Twitter

Shadow World YT (for tournament replays)
summary: The FGC at NC State provides a community for people who take an interest in video games in the fighting genre, running events such as tournaments and casual meet-ups.

name: Hosea College
summary: The goal of Hosea College is to encourage a genuine love for Christ by connecting relationally with students and helping them grow alongside one another in a challenging and meaningful way.

name: Students for Life of America at NC State
description:
Students for Life of America at NC State is a non-partisan, pro-life group with the mission to recruit, train, and mobilize the pro-life generation at NC State. We seek to teach students about life from conception to death, as well as offer resources for mothers in need.  

We work to make a positive impact on the NCSU and Raleigh communities through effective education and supportive services. We aim to advocate for the interests of the vulnerable and marginalized.
summary: Students for Life is a non-partisan, pro-life group with the mission to recruit, train, and mobilize the pro-life generation at NC State. We seek to teach students about life from conception to death, as well as offer resources for mothers in need.

name: Asian InterVarsity
description:
Asian InterVarsity (AIV) exists to be a community of Asian Americans who grow in and experience what following Jesus means for our whole selves, our communities, and our campus. 

To see more of AIV in detail, follow us on Instagram where you can see FUN & EXCITING events of what we do as a community!
summary: AIV exists to be a community of Asian Americans who grow in and experience what following Jesus means for our whole selves, our communities, and our campus.

name: BattlePack Robotics Club at NC State
description: BattlePack Robotics aims to provide NC State students with the opportunity to apply in-class engineering concepts to the practical design, construction, and testing of combat robots; engage with the wider community of engineering hobbyists; and provide a constructive, fun environment geared toward producing the next generation of engineering hobbyists.
summary: We're a robotics club geared toward combat robotics! We design, prototype, and build robots to compete in local fighting competitions. We pride ourselves on our community, and our supportive team breeds ingenuity, leadership, and friendly competition.

name: Ultraviolet
description:
Ultraviolet is a student-run organization serving the needs of sapphic individuals at NC State. Our objective is to create a community for sapphic and questioning students through fun activities, events, and the occasional discussion.

We welcome anyone who may fall under the sapphic umbrella, including lesbian, bisexual, pansexual, queer, unlabeled, questioning, WLW/NBLW, or otherwise sapphic-identifying. Ultraviolet is trans and non-binary inclusive. 

 

Please view our Linktree to access our official website, Instagram page, Discord server, weekly newsletter, and other relevant information.
summary: Student organization geared towards sapphic individuals at NC State!

name: UNICEF at NC State
description: UNICEF at NC State is a chapter of UNICEF USA, which works to fundraise, advocate, and build community for the safety, protection, and education of children all over the world. At NC State, we want to empower our students into becoming advocates for children all over the world and build our local community to become more active as global citizens.
summary: UNICEF at NC State is a chapter of UNICEF USA, which works to fundraise, advocate, and build community for the safety, protection, and education of children all over the world.

name: Ag Institute Club
description: The purposes of this organization shall be to develop those traits of character, personality, and leadership essential to the success of Agricultural Institute students in their chosen profession.  The club shall lend itself to the support and advancement of the Agricultural Institute.
summary: The purposes of this organization shall be to develop those traits of character, personality, and leadership essential to the success of Agricultural Institute students in their chosen profession.

name: Alpha Pi Mu
description:
Alpha Pi Mu (APM) was founded in 1949 by Industrial Engineering (IE) students at Georgia Tech. It was created to be an organization that would allow students to exchange ideas and develop their professional networks. By 1959, Alpha Pi Mu became a part of the Association of College Honors Societies (ACHS), making it the only nationally accepted honors society for Industrial Engineering.

 

Our NC State University chapter is one of over 60 active chapters that exist today. Our mission is to recognize junior and senior students who have demonstrated academic excellence in the Department of Industrial Engineering and encourage them to further their knowledge and develop a strong professional network. 

 

Membership is by invitation only to third year IE students in the top one fifth of their IE class and to fourth year IE students in the top one third of their IE class. Members work to improve the surrounding community by performing acts and duties that bring a greater understanding of Industrial Engineering to their community. On top of our community focus, NC State's chapter aims to help its members in various capacities that provide value to their career and educational aspirations.
summary: Alpha Pi Mu is a national honor society for Industrial and Systems Engineering students.

name: College of Education Education Council
description: Ed Council serves as the umbrella organization for all College of Education (“CED”) affiliated student clubs and organizations. Ed Council provides governance, leadership development, and funding opportunities for the student clubs and organizations within the College of Education. Ed Council works in partnership with the College of Education leadership and administration to provide an inclusive, student-centered, teacher preparation-focused experience for all undergraduate students within the College.
summary: The Education Council serves as the umbrella organization for College of Education-based clubs and organizations and serves as an internal governance board.

name: Embedded Machine Learning Club
description:
Hi there! We’re the Embedded Machine Learning Club here at NC State!

LINK TO OUR CALENDAR

The three ‘E’s of our objectives are Education, Experience, and Effect. These play into our overall goal of increasing awareness and skills related to embedded systems and machine learning throughout the student body. 

 

  1. Education - To raise awareness about Machine Learning (ML) by conducting outreach and to advance member knowledge and skills in ML with internal education.
  2. Experience - To provide members a hands-on opportunity to gain technical and leadership skills, increase professional development, build career networks, and propel internship search.
  3. Effect - To provide impactful ML solutions to community partners, including research groups and other club organizations, and to host and participate in ML-related competitions.

 

We provide workshops in Python (the most popular language for Machine Learning), vision processing (a very popular application for machine learning), and embedded systems (the actual hardware these products are deployed on), as well as many more to come. 

 

We have several projects themed around autonomous robotics and embedded systems going on at any given time, and we also plan to participate as a club in one big calendar year competition. One of our main projects is F1Tenth, an incredibly exciting competition for autonomous racing at 1/10th the scale of an actual vehicle. We also have smaller projects and resources available for learning embedded systems and machine learning. 

 

This club is not only a great place to learn about the concepts surrounding embedded machine learning but also expand your network: not only for these types of systems but also in ECE and CSC in general! 

 

To stay up to date on meetings, projects, and events please join our Discord

 

We are always looking for new members and would love to have you join our ranks!
summary: The Embedded Machine Learning Club provides growth for students in the machine learning and embedded systems space through our three values: Education, Experience, and Effect, which include professional skill building, competitions, networking, and more.

name: Student American Society Of Landscape Architects
description:
SASLA plans several technical workshops, social events and brown bag seminars with practitioners, and provides important input and advice to the faculty about the curriculum, courses, and the overall quality of the program.

SASLA also manages the Department’s Professional Development Mentor Program which pairs students with practitioners and the Peer-to-Peer Program which pairs incoming students with second and third-year students. Women in Landscape Architecture (WxLA) is a student group in association with the SASLA that reaches out to the professional community to strengthen networking opportunities for women in the profession. WxLA hosts networking events regularly throughout the school year, attended by NCSU students and faculty, with local professionals.
summary: The Society’s mission is to advance landscape architecture through advocacy, communication, education, and fellowship.

name: ACM SIGGRAPH Student Chapter at NC State
description: The ACM SIGGRAPH Student Association's  mission is to promote the generation and dissemination of information on computer graphics, animation and interactive techniques, and foster a membership community whose engagement and values furthers research, innovation and professional practice in these realms.
summary: The ACM SIGGRAPH Student Chapter at NC State is an interest group for computer graphics, animation, computational media design, interactive techniques and related activities.

name: Music Makers Club
description:
The Music Makers Club is a community for those interested in writing and producing original music. Our members operate in a wide range of genres: hip-hop, jazz, pop, electronic, and everything in between. This semester we will be making songs as well as hosting collaborations, competitions, crash courses, and more!

Music Makers Club meetings occur weekly on Thursdays at 8:00pm in Broughton 2408. For more information, reach out to the email provided.

(NEW!) Soundcloud: https://on.soundcloud.com/ZJmrJ
summary: A group for those interested in writing and producing original music of any kind. Pop, jazz, rap, noise... you name it, we make it.

name: Women in Electrical and Computer Engineering
description:
Women in ECE (WiECE) is an organization led by women and female-identifying students who are studying Electrical & Computer Engineering at NC State University. 

The organization was created to provide a safe and welcoming space for female-identifying students and their advocates to learn and grow as leaders in ECE & STEM. WiECE provides social, academic, and professional networking and mentoring opportunities for BS, MS & Ph.D. women and female-identifying students in the department. 

Additionally, the group aims to connect students and community members with STEM opportunities for engagement and enrichment to the academic community.
summary: Our goal is to support women and non-binary students in the ECE department by providing opportunities in academic, professional, and personal development, while being inclusive of the greater community.

name: Triangle Area American Society of Biomechanics Student Chapter
description: The purpose of this ASB Student Chapter is to foster connections among ASB-affiliated students and faculty within and across universities in the Triangle Area. In doing so, the ASB Student Chapter will provide comprehensive intellectual and professional development among students from a wide range of disciplines that contribute to the field of biomechanics. The ASB Student Chapter aims to construct a supportive environment where students of all levels and from all disciplines gain valuable leadership skills, forge new friendships, and foster new collaborative opportunities.
summary: The purpose of this ASB Student Chapter is to foster connections among ASB-affiliated students and faculty within and across universities in the Triangle Area.

name: Black Student Union at NC State
summary: The Black Student Union at NC State is an organization dedicated to community building, advocacy, and development for the Black community at NC State.

name: Industrial Designers Society of America Student Chapter at North Carolina State University
description: The Industrial Designers Society of America student chapter at NC State exists to provide students with long lasting peer and professional relations, as well as supplementary workshops to enhance skills taught in and out of the classroom.
summary: The Industrial Designers Society of America student chapter at NC State exists to provide students with long lasting peer and professional relations, as well as supplementary workshops to enhance skills taught in and out of the classroom.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America at NC State
description: ALPFA at NC State empowers students through community building, professional development, and access to transformative internships/jobs, preparing well-rounded leaders to excel in the workforce and create a lasting impact in their communities.
summary: ALPFA works to empower and develop Latino men and women as leaders of character for the nation, in every sector of the global economy. By networking and professional leadership development opportunities with companies seeking Latinx talent.

name: Ethiopian-Eritrean Student Association
description:
The NCSU Ethiopian-Eritrean Student Association (EESA) is a student group at North Carolina State University. The purpose of the organization is to intentionally build a safe and encouraging space for Ethiopian-Eritrean students to connect with one another. Additionally, we strive to bring awareness and understanding of Ethiopian-Eritrean culture to the NC State community. 

Follow us on Instagram and Tiktok @NCSUEESA!

Stay updated by joining our GroupMe: search NCSU EESA
summary: Our purpose is to intentionally build a safe and encouraging space for Ethiopian-Eritrean students to connect with one another.

name: Mock Trial Club at NC State
description:
Mock Trial is a competition in which students simulate a real, lower court trial. A team is made up of 6 to 10 members, and the club as a whole may be made up of multiple teams. Auditions are held between late August and early September, and the competition season lasts from September to late February.

The American Mock Trial Association (AMTA) serves as the governing body for intercollegiate mock trial competition. Through engaging in trial simulations in competition with teams from other institutions, students develop critical thinking and public speaking skills, as well as a knowledge of legal practices and procedures. AMTA sponsors both regional and national-level competitions, and provides engaging and complex case materials for use by registered teams.

Mock Trial at NC State allows students to work together as a team to dig deep into the case materials provided by AMTA and argue the case at competitions. Teams will prepare for competition by working diligently to create witness portrayals, direct examinations, cross examinations, opening statements, and closing arguments. Between theatrical performances and real application of the law and rules of evidence, there is something for everybody!
summary: Mock Trial is a competition where students simulate a real trial. Members work as a team to dig into case materials and prepare arguments to present at competition against other schools in regional and national competitions.

name: Muslim Interscholastic Tournament
description: The Muslim Interscholastic Tournament (MIST) is a 3-day tournament held in several regions across the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom. The tournament gives young Muslim high school students a chance to showcase their talents and creative abilities in various competitions, including non-religious competitions like short film, mobile app development and poetry to more Islam-centric competitions such as a religious knowledge test and memorization of the Quran.
summary: We inspire high school students to collaborate and compete, develop creative skills, and learn from professionals, while educating them about Islam and Muslims.  MIST envisions a vibrant community of creativity, knowledge, and mutual respect.

name: Student Chapter of the National Association for Black Veterinarians at NC State
description: The mission of the NABV is to "ADVOCATE, provide SUPPORT, and cultivate an inclusive COMMUNITY for black individuals in veterinary medicine at every level of the profession through the organization, members and leadership."
summary: The mission of the NABV is to "ADVOCATE, provide SUPPORT, and cultivate an inclusive COMMUNITY for blacks in veterinary medicine at every level of the profession through the organization, members and leadership."

name: Physiology Graduate Student Association -
summary: Student association for the Master of Physiology program.

name: Rafting Club at NC State
description:
We take multiple whitewater rafting trips out each semester. Come explore the Appalachian Mountains with friends and shred some gnar!

Rafters of all skill levels are able to join. Come check us out if you're passionate about the outdoors and are interested in exploring the rivers of the East Coast!

______________________________________________________________________

DM us on instagram @raftingclubatncstate to sign up or ask questions!

Join our GroupMe here
summary: We take multiple whitewater rafting trips out each semester. Come explore the Appalachian Mountains with friends and shred some gnar! DM us on instagram @raftingclubatncstate to sign up or ask questions!

name: ECE Graduate Student Association
description:
ECE GSA seeks to represent graduate students in the Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering. Its mission is to:

  • Communicate with the university administration and department faculty and administration on behalf of electrical and computer engineering graduate students.
  • Encourage faculty-student interaction.
  • Facilitate student-initiated academic activities.
  • Provide a forum for graduate student opinions in all matters of mutual interest to faculty and graduate students.
  • Promote professional interest and fellowship among current graduate students and the ECE alumni.
summary: ECE GSA seeks to represent graduate students in the Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering.

name: Code Red at NC State University
description:
NCSU Code Red is a stomp and shake cheer student led organization. Stomp and Shake is a style of cheer commonly done at HBCUs. Code Red officially became an organization January 2022, but has already made a huge impact.

 

The purpose of NCSU Code Red is to expose students to a different style of cheer while educating them on a piece of black culture. NCSU Code Red will also be a place for students who may have felt held back from our official cheer team requirements and allow them to participate in something new or even continue doing an activity they love. We strive to create an exciting environment for our members to showcase their talents. 

 

We want to create a sense of sisterhood, give students who may have felt held back by the requirements of our campus cheer teams an opportunity to cheer, create a new multicultural organization, and establish a better sense of diversity as far as what students can participate. We also aim to develop strong character, positive attitudes, and responsible citizenship while instilling confidence in our goals, and values.
summary: Code Red is a student founded stomp and shake cheerleading team which is a style commonly done at HBCU's.

name: Unicycle Club
description: We are a group of passionate unicycle enthusiasts who enjoy riding around campus and biking trails, and dabble in the juggling arts too! All are welcome to join, even if it's just to learn how to ride a unicycle. We usually do group rides around main campus on Sunday afternoons whenever members are available.
summary: We are a group of passionate unicycle enthusiasts who enjoy riding around campus and biking trails, and dabble in the juggling arts too! All are welcome to join, even if it's just to learn how to ride a unicycle.

name: Grappling Club at NC State University
description:
The purpose of this student organization is to teach members about grappling techniques and self-defense while emphasizing discipline, respect, and self-improvement. The Grappling Club at NC State University will provide instruction with regards to self-defense, using Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu and wrestling techniques and etiquette. Members will gain an understanding of the main fundamentals of BJJ.

Members are REQUIRED to have signed this Waiver PRIOR to their first meeting. You may email an electronically signed copy to us, or bring a hardcopy to your first practice.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to teach members about grappling techniques and self-defense while emphasizing discipline, respect, and self-improvement.

name: EMGSA at NC State
description:
The purpose of this organization is to enhance the quality of graduate education within the Engineering Management Department at NC State by promoting involvement, networking, and communication among Engineering Management graduate students and faculty members.

This organization strives to provide a platform for social interaction among the members of the Engineering Management Department of NC State and within the Raleigh, North Carolina community.

This organization strives to foster a sense of identity and community within the Engineering Management Department of NC State.
summary: The Graduate Student Association for the Engineering Management Program at NC State. Responsible for taking care of the student body of the EM program, we offer events and support for the EM students.

name: Women's Weightlifting Club at NC State
description: An inclusive space for women to lift or learn to lift!
summary: We are an all women weight lifting club that is open to women that have experience lifting as well as no experience. Feel free to reach out with any questions!

name: Badminton Club at NC State
description:
The Badminton Club at NC State aims to provide students the opportunity to pursue the sport of badminton and further hone their skills through competitive and casual play. We welcome all levels of experience!

‎

Join our Discord for more information: Discord Server
summary:
The Badminton Club at NC State aims to provide students the opportunity to pursue the sport of badminton and further hone their skills through competitive and casual play. All levels welcome!

Join our Discord for more information: bit.ly/ncbadminton

name: Black Students Board at NC State
description: The Black Students Board fosters unity among students of the African diaspora by providing a social outlet that creates space for holistic development, advocacy, and culturally focused programs. As one of NC State University’s first African-American organizations, it is our belief that the strength of our people originates from the knowledge of our past and if we want to go far, we must go together.
summary: The Black Students Board fosters unity among students of the African diaspora by providing a social outlet that creates space for holistic development, advocacy, and culturally focused programs.

name: Student Chapter of the Association of Asian Veterinary Medical Professionals at NC State
description: A student chapter at the NCSU College of Veterinary Medicine dedicated to forming a community for Asian veterinary professionals and educating allies. We strive to inspire and support Asian veterinary professionals through club meetings, Q and As, events, and outreach. This club is open to current students within the college of veterinary medicine and NCSU graduate students.
summary: A student chapter dedicated to forming a community for Asian veterinary professionals and educating allies. We strive to inspire and support Asian veterinary professionals through club meetings, Q and As, events, and outreach.

name: Earth, Wind, and Yarn at NCState
description:
Earth, Wind, and Yarn at NC State is NC State's first casual textile hobby club on campus! Our mission is to provide a space where members can explore a variety of yarn crafts, hone their skills in a craft they already enjoy, and share their hobby with a community full of yarn lovers. If you are interested in receiving updates via group chat, use this link to join us on discord!

https://discord.gg/4pHzHpayQb
summary: Earth, Wind, and Yarn at NCState is a student organization designed to cultivate connections between students who share a passion for yarn crafts, provide space to explore and create, and spread yarn love with the greater community

name: The District Dance Collective
description: The District Dance Collective strives to provide a welcoming and safe environment for dancers of all levels to learn and excel in various styles of dance by providing classes and workshops on-campus to help members achieve their personal dance goals.
summary: The District Dance Collective is an organization committed to providing beginner, intermediate, and advanced level dance classes to the dance community at NC State. Classes included styles like jazz-funk, hip-hop, whacking, contemporary, heels, and more!

name: Triangle Bengali Association at NC State
description: Triangle Bengali Association at NC State (TBA) is a cultural organisation formed by Bengali Students studying in the Triangle area. We organize Sharodiya and Bashanta Panchami on a small scale every year. We are recently trying to expand our initiatives by organizing other events like Poila Boishakh and Rabindra Jayanti too. We cordially invite all living in or around the Triangle area to join us in our events. We are also proud to announce that we have recently started our own Sharodiya magazine named ছাতিম.
summary: The organization aims to unite all Bengali-speaking students at NCSU (India and Bangladesh). This will organize events and festivals indigenous to the Bengali community, like Bengali New Year, Durga Pujo, Saraswati Pujo, and host cultural programs.

name: BridgeUSA at NC State
description:
BridgeUSA at NC State is a chapter of BridgeUSA, which is a youth-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that creates spaces on high school and college campuses for open discussion between students about political issues. Political division is one of our biggest challenges in the United States. Today’s youth are being taught that tribalism is better than unity and that having conversations across our differences is impossible. Spoiler Alert: This isn’t true!

We began in 2016 at the universities of Notre Dame, CU-Boulder, and UC Berkeley in response to growing polarization on campus. Our work emphasizes the importance of empathy and understanding, ideological diversity and solution-oriented politics. By engaging America’s youth in constructive discussions, we are equipping the next generation of leaders with the skills necessary for navigating conflict, finding solutions across differences and building bridges in their communities.

We are currently located on over 50 college campuses and 24 high school campuses in the U.S.
summary: BridgeUSA is a political organization that encourages active discussion to get real solutions. We are leading the battle against hyperpartisanship by encouraging and hosting dialogues on issues that matter.

name: Student-Made at NC State
description: We provide an outlet for creative NC State student-entrepreneurs to join a supportive network, connect with their campus community and the greater NC community as they grow their business, and gain the experience needed to pursue a viable business. This is achieved through in-person events and an online shop that has been designed to assist students as they develop their personal creative and business style. If you would like to join please fill out this quick form https://www.studentmadencstate.com/join-student-made
summary: Student-Made will provide an outlet for creative student-entrepreneurs to connect with their campus community as they grow their business with the support of a network of artist who promote and sell work through pop-up events and an online store.

name: Student Affiliates of American Chemical Society
description: The Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society (SA-ACS) is the official ACS organization for undergraduate students interested in the chemical sciences. Members participate in a wide range of programs and activities that enhance their college experience and prepare them for successful careers.
summary: The Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society (SA-ACS) is the official ACS organization for undergraduate students interested in the chemical sciences.

name: IgniteGen Missions Team at NC State
description: IgniteGen Missions Team exists to help students gain an awareness of global poverty, and to bring faith-filled solutions through an annual missions trip in the US or the Dominican Republic.  Students will receive training in Christian beliefs and discussions of life's meaning, faith and purpose.  Expectations in cross cultural environments will also be discussed.  Fundraising efforts for the missions trip and to benefit local service organizations will be held throughout the year.  We will share meals together, serve others, and above all HAVE FUN!!! International students are welcome for Nation Nights and Holiday Host Homes events.
summary: IgniteGen Missions Team organizes a summer missions trip in the US or Dominican Republic. We will give students awareness of poverty in other cultures and how to create solutions for lasting change. Students will receive training on mission activities.

name: Classics Club
description: We are a club dedicated to promoting the study of Ancient Greek and Roman history, culture, and language. All majors are welcome, and no background knowledge is required. We discuss this study in both scholarly and social settings through a variety of different venues and activities.
summary: We are a club dedicated to promoting the study of Ancient Greek and Roman history, culture, and language. All majors are welcome, and no background knowledge is required.

name: Asian Business Student Union
description: ABSU aims to be an Asian-Interest, but not Asian-exclusive organization to recruit and help students gain professional development, academic/career guidance, and personal growth while at NCSU. We aim to create/expand an alumni network between our members and allow them to continue to stay in contact with one another to help them build towards a successful future.
summary: We are a student-initiated organization, serving as a resource to minority students focused around the Poole community. ABSA serves as a campus-oriented organization with the aim to expand to a local, national and international level.

name: Bioinformatics Graduate Student Association
summary: Graduate Student Association for the Bioinformatics Graduate Program. This program is comprised of graduate student pursuing studies in Bioinformatics at North Carolina State University.

name: TBC at NC State
description: TBC at NC State exists to help support The Blood Connection's mission to support our healthcare partners with adequate, safe, cost-effective blood supplies and services. We will raise awareness of the need for blood donation and cultivate lifelong passions for sustained donation.
summary: TBC at NC State exists to help support The Blood Connection's mission to support our healthcare partners with adequate, safe, cost-effective blood supplies and services. We will raise awareness of the need for sustained blood donations.

name: Five Rivers of NC State
summary: A club that works with Trout Unlimited and focuses on conservation and fly fishing. Our goal is to connect the angler to the importance of conservation to ensure healthy fisheries for years to come.

name: First Love Church at North Carolina State University
summary: Our desire is to know the Lord Jesus and make Him known; with the goal of living a transformed life that will impact our neighbors, campuses, nations and the world for our Master Christ Jesus.

name: Blockchain at NC State
description: The Blockchain Club at NC State is dedicated to bringing together students and community members to learn, research, and connect with the blockchain industry. The club’s mission is to foster education, hands-on experience, and professional networking, empowering members to participate in and contribute to the rapidly evolving blockchain ecosystem.
summary: A student-led community at NCSU with opportunities for education, research, and project-building in blockchain technology.

name: National Association of Black Accountants (NABA)
description:
NABA, Inc. is a nonprofit membership association dedicated to bridging the opportunity gap for Black Business Leaders in accounting, finance business, and entrepreneurship. Representing more than 200,000 Black Business Leaders in these fields, NABA advances people, careers, and the mission by providing education, resources, and meaningful career connections to professional and student members. 

We work with and through our members, corporate and educational partners, policymakers, and partner organizations who are committed to professional and academic excellence possess a sense of professional and civic responsibility and are concerned with enhancing opportunities for Black Business Leaders. NABA’s goals include, but are not limited to, the following:

  • To promote and develop the professional skills and advancement of our members.
  • To encourage and assist Black students entering the accounting, finance, and business professions.
  • To provide opportunities for members to fulfill their civic responsibilities.
  • To promote public confidence in our members and the services they provide.
  • To represent the interests of current and prospective Black Business Leaders.
  • To ensure long-term financial stability and provide adequate resources to implement chapter, regional, and national programs.
summary: NABA, Inc. is a nonprofit membership association dedicated to bridging the opportunity gap for Black Business Leaders in accounting, finance business, and entrepreneurship.

name: National Science Teaching Association Preservice Teacher Chapter at NC State
description:
The mission of NSTA is to “Transform science education to benefit all through professional learning, partnerships, and advocacy.” To do this, we:

  • acquaint pre-service teachers of science with the support resources available from NSTA.
  • provide additional professional development in science education to preservice teachers.
  • acquaint preservice teachers of science with others like themselves at NC State  and across the United States and Canada.
summary: We aim to “Transform science education to benefit all through professional learning, partnerships, and advocacy."

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description:
OCF transforms the lives of college students by guiding them along the path to Jesus Christ through His Church, cultivating a campus community of worship, witness, service, fellowship, and education

Our goal is to actualize the Orthodox Christian life in the hearts, minds, and souls of college students by facilitating unified campus ministry efforts—reaching Orthodox college students on campuses throughout North America.

OCF does this by working continuously to improve in the areas of leadership training, high school to college transition, and college to parish transition through new and innovative programs as well as partnerships with other organizations in the Orthodox world.

OCF’s programs and partnerships offer an environment for students interested in deeper spiritual experiences. Every year, Real Break, College Conference, and similar events are hosted to enrich the spiritual lives of students These programs stand apart as premier service and educational opportunities available to any college student.

Most importantly, OCF continues to provide a home for students on college campuses across the continent
summary: OCF offers college students a way to find a rich community in Christ, inspiring a life-long love of God and neighbor.

name: The Hindi-Urdu Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to allow students to practice their Hindi/Urdu skills outside of the classroom with other students who are seeking to improve their knowledge and presentation of the language. There will be various activities organized by the organization such as movie nights, cultural nights, and other fun activities relating to cultural food, clothing, traditions and celebrations that will allow members to embrace Indian culture while expanding their knowledge.
summary: This club will allow students to practice Hindi/Urdu through various entraining activities while also exploring and expanding their knowledge of both the language and the cultural aspects that go along with it.

name: Triangle Molecular Simulation Society at NC State
description: TriMolS at NC State is a student chapter dedicated to promoting the filed of molecular simulation among students, researchers, and professionals. This society focuses on advancing knowledge and skills related to computational modelling and simulations of molecular systems, including areas such as materials science, chemistry and biophysics. The chapter organizes seminars, workshops, and networking events, providing a platform for students to collaborate share their research and connect with experts in the field TriMolS serves as a bridge between academia and industry, fostering interdisciplinary collaboration and professional development. Through hands-on training sessions, guest lectures by leading scientists, and community outreach, the society aims to enhance computational skills and scientific communication among its members. TriMolS also supports student-led initiatives, encouraging innovation in molecular simulation research. By creating a dynamic and inclusive environment, the chapter empowers students to explore emerging trends, develop expertise, and contribute to the broader scientific community.
summary: TriMolS at NC State is part of a broader network connecting researchers across the Triangle area to advance computational molecular science, through seminars, workshops and networking events.

name: French Ambassadors
description: This group is for students interested in Communications and French Studies. Students will engage in club events and create social media content relating to French Studies on campus. We will host events and outreach with both SKEMA and NCSU in order to continue and further the French-American connection.
summary: This group is for students interested in Communications and French Studies. Students will engage in club events and create social media content relating to French Studies on campus.

name: Pack disAbility Advocacy Club
email: paaccess-org@ncsu.edu (please use this or email full board; do NOT use primary contact alone)
description:
Pack disAbility Advocacy Club (PAAC) works to promote a supportive community for people with all types of disabilities at NC State — visible and invisible, including mental health challenges. We strive to create a culture of inclusion that extends beyond compliance and erases stigma. Through this club, we will:

1. Provide a community for students with disabilities, and promote their academic and professional success.

2. Train students with and without disabilities to advocate for themselves and others, and put these skills into practice.

3. Advocate for proactive accessibility in the NC State campus and community.

In the WolfPAAC, disability advocacy is what we think and do.

 

How to connect with us:

1. Join for free on this GetInvolved page to become an official member and/or email us to get connected.

2. We have a Discord server you can join via the NCSU Discord hub or by emailing us for a link from a ncsu.edu email address.

3. You can also receive updates via on Instagram (linked below), Google calendar (also below), GroupMe, or WhatsApp. (Email us to be added on GroupMe or WhatsApp.)
summary: PAAC aims to create a supportive community for students with and without disabilities in our club and throughout the WolfPack as well as promote opportunities for disability advocacy.

name: Defensive MMA
description: Learn to defend yourself with a practical combination of many different martial arts. Learn situational awareness, train your flight/fight/freeze response, and learn to protect yourself when needed. We have boxers, wrestlers, BJJ practitioners, and more! Beginner friendly! Join to learn new skills, hone your craft, or simply get a good workout in! For Spring 2026, we meet in room 1240 of Carmichael gym from 7 to 9 pm every Friday.
summary: Learn practical self-defense skills in a beginner-friendly environment! Expand upon your pre-existing skillset, or start from scratch! It's a great workout and tons of fun! Come to hang out and leave with an irreplaceable skill.

name: Pack Climbing
description: An all-encompassing student organization for climbers at NC State. A community for Outdoor, Casual, and Competitive Climbers. PLEASE FOLLOW OUR INSTAGRAM FOR UPDATES AS THAT IS OUR MAIN FORM OF COMMUNICATION. FIND US @Pack_Climbing
summary: If you have an interest in rock climbing, this is the place for you. A diverse and supportive group of climbers here for good burns and great vibes. established to accommodate casual, outdoor, and competitive climbing at any skill level.

name: Windhover
description: WINDHOVER, NC STATE’S LITERARY AND ARTS MAGAZINE, STRIVES TO SERVE THE CREATIVE COMMUNITY OF NC STATE THROUGH ITS ANNUAL PUBLICATION THAT INCLUDES ART, FILM, MUSIC, POETRY AND PROSE. OUR MAIN GOALS ARE TO PROVIDE A WELCOMING ENVIRONMENT FOR PROGRESSIVE AND OUT-OF-THE-BOX THOUGHT PROCESSES AND TO ENCOURAGE ALL ARTISTS TO SUBMIT WORK THAT REFLECTS THE CORE OF THEIR CREATIVITY. WITH THIS FREE PUBLICATION, WE HOPE TO BRING TOGETHER NC STATE STUDENTS, FACULTY, STAFF AND ALUMNI FOR A CELEBRATION OF THE ARTS.
summary: NC State's Literary and Art Magazine

name: Roundabout Magazine
description:
What is Roundabout Magazine?

Roundabout is a general interest magazine that focuses on student life and culture. We publish three times a year: for Fall, Spring and New Student Orientation. You can see examples of our previous issues here. If you are a Freshman, you have likely received our orientation issue in the mail! 
 
How Can I Get Involved?  
 
We are happy to accept all majors and experience levels. The primary ways you can contribute are with writing, graphic design and photography.
 
What's the Time Commitment Like?
 
Because Roundabout publishes so infrequently, we have all-staff meetings every two weeks and there is plenty of time before deadlines. This means that we are excellent if you want to work for other clubs or Student Media outlets at the same time (or if you're just super busy). Our editorial staff does have weekly meetings, however. Being an editor is better if you want to become exclusively involved with Roundabout. 
 
Will I be Paid?
 
Yes indeed, you will be paid for content that you submit to Roundabout after you complete correspondency training ($15 per articles, photos, and graphics, $30 for cover photos). Editors are given an hourly wage. There is also the "glory pay" of having your name and effort printed thousands of times. 
 
If you have other questions or if you're interested in joining, email roundabout-editor@ncsu.edu
summary: The newest NC State Student Media outlet, Roundabout is a glossy general interest magazine that covers student life, arts, and culture.

name: Pack the Poll Ambassadors
summary: Pack the Polls

name: Network of Enlightened Women at NC State
summary: Network of Enlightened Women is a national organization that cultivates a community of conservative women and expands intellectual diversity on college campuses through its focus on education, service, and career development.

name: Alpha Xi Delta
description: Founded in 1893 at Lombard College in Galesburg, Illinois, Alpha Xi Delta is one of the oldest women’s fraternal organizations in the United States. Alpha Xi Delta is a progressive organization dedicated to the personal growth of women. Through membership in Alpha Xi Delta, Sisters are inspired to realize their potential through the ideals our Founders instilled in our Fraternity: Sisterhood, leadership, knowledge and service to our communities.
summary: Alpha Xi Delta

name: Discomfort Club
description:
We are a club that focuses on helping people step outside of their comfort zones (in a safe and responsible way) in order to grow as people, and overcome personal barriers. 

We want to provide opportunities for people to try new things, while being supported by other members in the club. Each meeting we would choose one thing that is uncomfortable for someone in the club, and do it as a group. 

After each uncomfortable experience we will come together and talk about what we took away from this experience, what we learned.
summary: We are a club that focuses on helping people step outside of their comfort zones (in a safe and responsible way) in order to grow as people, and overcome personal barriers. Each week we would do something uncomfortable as a group, and reflect on it.

name: Mexican American Student Association
description: Mexican American Student Association is a cultural organization that promotes cultural and social awareness, community service, and exploration of the Mexican culture. Our organization serves to provide Mexican Americans and their community allies the ability to obtain a sense of belonging where they can build lifelong connections with others of the same interest rooted in the knowledge of Mexican culture.
summary: Mexican American Student Association is a cultural organization that promotes cultural and social awareness, community service, and cultural exploration of Mexican culture.

name: Multicultural Student Affairs
description:
Multicultural Student Affairs (MSA) researches, designs, and implements culturally responsive initiatives, events and programs that promote the pursuit of academic success, retention, and graduation of students.

MSA is a place for students of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences to come together to learn more about themselves and each other.

The MSA Suite (Suite 4261 in Talley Student Union) is a place for community and joy. You can find connection, music, conversation, and fellowship happening in the space at any time.

MSA is not a club or organization to join, but a space for building community. Students interested in getting involved with Multicultural Student Affairs can stop by the MSA suite in Talley Student Union, attend an MSA-hosted event, or reach out via email or social media.
summary: Multicultural Student Affairs (MSA) researches, designs, and implements culturally responsive initiatives, events and programs that promote the pursuit of academic success, retention, and graduation of students.

name: Women's Center
description:
The NC State Women’s Center is one of four Campus Community Centers (along with the LGBTQ Pride Center, Multicultural Student Affairs, and the African American Cultural Center) housed under the Division of Academic and Student Affairs (DASA).

 

The mission of the Women’s Center is to build and create a community of authentic and engaged allies and leaders to pursue gender equity and social justice; enhancing the campus climate through education, advocacy, support and leadership development.

 

The Women’s Center is not a club or organization to join, but a space for building community. Students interested in getting involved with the Women’s Center can stop by the suite in Talley Student Union, attend a Women’s Center-hosted event, or reach out via email or social media.
summary: Get Involved Page for the Women's Center

name: LGBTQ Pride Center
description:
The LGBTQ Pride Center is one of Student Success centers housed under Division of Academic and Student Affairs (DASA). The LGBTQ Pride Center serves all students. 

The Pride Center strives to increase the NC State campus community’s understanding of connection by working collaboratively to raise awareness, promote respect, and create a culture of joy.
summary: The LGBTQ Pride Center serves students of all sexual orientations, gender identities and gender expressions. The Pride Center strives to increase the NC State campus community’s understanding of belonging and promote awareness.

name: Bell Tower Capital Management
description: Bell Tower Capital Management is NC State University’s student-run undergraduate investment fund,  managing a long-only equity portfolio under the guidance and review of University Faculty, and is wholly-funded as a component of the NCSU Endowment. We were founded in 2017 by a handful of passionate students originally managing a paper-traded portfolio. Fund membership is highly selective, but applications are open to all NC State Students who demonstrate an interest in asset  management. Bell Tower members gain practical experience in financial modeling, equity analysis, and portfolio management, in addition to joining a community of students passionate about keeping up with current  events, broad economics and financial markets.
summary: Bell Tower Capital Management is NC State University’s student-run undergraduate investment fund, managing a long-only equity portfolio under the guidance and review of University Faculty, and is a fully-funded component of the NCSU Endowment.

name: Department of Sociology and Anthropology and Criminology Student Ambassadors of NC State University
description: As Student Ambassadors, we strive to create a welcoming environment for current, new, and prospective students in our department and the university as a whole. We work directly with the Director of Advising to host information sessions, plan events for the department, volunteer at University Open House, assist with graduation proceedings, and more. Each Ambassador is also assigned a Student Connections Group that they will email at least once per month to provide information about events, club meetings, etc., as well as to check in on students and connect with them. For more information, please feel free to contact any of the officers, or read our student-written newsletter articles! **Students must submit an application to our Director to join this organization. Stephanie will send out application information via email in Spring 2026.**
summary: The Student Ambassadors are a team of undergraduates majoring in sociology, anthropology, or criminology that work to establish connections with current, new, and prospective students, and represent the department at information sessions and other events

name: German Ambassadors Club
description:
We are a club open to anyone who shows interest in the German language or culture. Events may look new and different from previous years, and are meant to be interactive, engaging, interesting, and fun! Students will not be required to attend every meeting/event, but they are AWESOME so you will probably want to come to them anyways. All students are encouraged to join, whether you speak German, are learning German, or don't know a word of German!

The German Ambassadors Club will work alongside the National German Honorary Society (Delta Phi Alpha (DPA)) to create supplementary and complimentary events. Students are encouraged to be members of both the DPA and the German Ambassadors club.
summary: This club is open to anyone who shows interest in the German language or culture. Events may look new and different from previous German clubs, and are meant to be interactive, engaging, interesting, and fun!

name: NCSU Wrestling Club
description:
NC States Folk-style Wrestling Club

For More Info: https://lounge.live/lounges/5iveo7cz?ic=wbjpb
summary: A student led organization focused on folk style wrestling for all skill levels. We hope to spread the interest of wrestling and continue to grow in wrestling knowledge.

name: Sustainable Materials and Technology Student Association
description:
