SMTSA is an organization that is focused on professional development, team building, and highlighting potential opportunities for students with Sustainable Materials and Technology majors, minors, and prospective students.

Check us out on Insta @smtsa_!
summary:
SMTSA is an organization that is focused on professional development, team building and highlighting potential opportunities for students with Sustainable Materials and Technology majors, minors, and prospective students. 

Check us out @smtsa_!

name: The Recreational Volleyball Club
description:
Current times nets are set up: (all courts are located in Carmichael Wellness and Rec Center)

Mon - Thu: 5-11 pm at Court 10

Fri - Sun: Gym opening - closing at Court 5

This is an organization to meet new students, have fun, and learn about the sport of volleyball, while also contributing back to your community. Our members range from brand-new volleyball players to club players. All students no matter their background are welcome to join.

Before a pickup, players communicate within the 'I Love Volley' GroupMe to estimate the number of players going to pickup on that day. This is done via poll. Members play sets of 15 points. Teams' play order is based of King of the Hill format unless there are more than 4 teams (30+ players) present. 

Teams are selected by you - you choose who you play with. Occasionally, if the more experienced players gather on one team, officers will scramble teams to encourage mingling, skill improvement, and fairness. Players come and go due to personal schedules, so naturally teams are shuffled throughout the period of the daily gathering.

You are NOT required to show up at the start time, nor required to stay the entire period of the gathering. This is a casual organization which allows maximum flexibility with the member's schedules.

Rule enforcement during a volleyball game is dependent on the perceived skill levels. A referee (Typically someone sitting a game out due to losing in the king of the hill style format) will determine which rules to use, and enforce them when broken. This way, new players can have fun during their play, but also slowly learn the rules of volleyball.

Many members already have their own equipment such as: volleyballs; knee pads; cranks (to adjust nets); and basketball court appropriate shoes. You are encouraged to bring your own equipment as the club does not own rental equipment. NCSU Wellness and Recreation offers volleyball rentals, however numbers are limited. The volleyballs used in gameplay are: "Mikasa V200W Official FIVB", and "Molten FLISTATEC Volleyball". If you want to buy your own, you may do so, however the club is not responsible for lost equipment. There is no dress-code that you must follow to attend, however it is encouraged to wear short sleeves, gym shorts, socks, and gym shoes for maximum comfort and mobility during gameplay.

Taunting during a pickup is limited to jokes between friends only. Do not spew hate speech, insensitive jokes, or any manner of speech that may offend another player. If someone creates drama, verbally assaults, or is caught saying anything racist, homophobic, or any other hate speech, they will be forced to disassociate from the organization.

There are currently no fees required to be a member. Occasionally tournaments will be held, and they will require entry fee. All proceeds from tournaments will be pledged or immediately donated to a charity organization.
summary: Looking for a place to play volleyball? RVC is the place for you. No membership fees. No experience required. Daily pickups that are not mandatory. Great place to make friends and develop or maintain your volleyball skills.

name: Archery Club at North Carolina State University
description:
This is a club for students to learn how to do archery, get competitive, hone their skills, and practice with friends. This safe shooting environment will allow students who have taken the archery class at NCSU to continue to shoot, as well as students of any experience level to participate.

Groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/100582494/uXG2cD52
summary: This is a club to participate in archery with other students.

name: The Self-Love Club
description: The Self-Love Club at NC State University is dedicated to promoting self-love, mental health awareness, and charitable giving. Our mission is to create a safe, inclusive space where individuals can openly express their feelings and know they are not alone. We recognize that isolation can contribute to depression, but we also believe in the power of community to uplift and heal. Through connection, conversation, and compassion, we remind one another that our voices matter, that we matter. We also aim to empower our members with opportunities for personal growth, self-discovery, and meaningful engagement with causes that reflect our values.
summary: This organization inspires honest conversations about mental health on campus in the safe space of club members. This club is focused on loving yourself and promoting mental health/positivity on campus. We are focused on giving back to the community.

name: Crocheting for a Cause
description: At Crocheting for a Cause, we are a student-led organization that comes together to connect, create, and change the community.
summary: At Crocheting for a Cause, we are a student-led organization that comes together to connect, create, and change the community.

name: Telugu Velugu - The Telugu Student Association
description:
Telugu Velugu is the Unified Telugu Student Association of NC State University founded by Dr. Vanadeep Kaluvagunta. a graduate student in the Department of Marine, Earth, and Atmospheric Sciences in September 2021 as a WhatsApp group and eventually was registered successfully as a student organization on the 18th of January, 2023. The principal aims of Telugu Velugu include - the promulgation of Telugu Cultural Unity among the students comprising a constructive confluence of undergraduate, graduate, and alumni components by building an effective network, which enables students to stay in touch with their beautiful Telugu Culture that is thousands of years old and empowering them through facilitation of invaluable exchanges that would help them build their academic excellence, future career paths in the United States. by providing them with a great moral support making them feel home away from home, and also forging collaborations with Telugu, Indian and other professional associations in RTP area and across USA as we believe that expanding peer professional and industry networks is an essential requirement for improving outreach and for achieving success in the United States.

As a student organization, we also conduct events throughout the year. During the Fall ­Semester, we start with the Telugu Language Day on August 29, followed by the Vijaya Dasami festival during September – October, and Kartika Poornima falls during November- December. The spring semester begins with the major festival Sankranti which occurs at the end of the second week of January. This will be followed by the major celebration of the Telugu New Year Ugadi in March – April followed by the Sree Rama Navami celebration in April. Besides, we will plan occasional get-togethers aimed at increasing familiarity and bonding among students coming from diverse backgrounds. Furthermore, there would be periodical career awareness lectures, and professional meetups along with a welcome party for new incoming Telugu students and a farewell for the graduating students.

Apart from organizing events, Telugu Velugu intends to work closely with the Office of International Students (OIS), MATRI – The Indian Graduate Student Association, EKTAA-South Asian undergrad Student Association, and other establishments at NC State University to serve the student community efficiently. We currently have 250 plus prospective members in total and we plan to increase this number significantly over time.

The organization was founded in January 2023. The core executive team consists of executive members with different roles assigned along with a group of Member Officers who are recruited to support the Executive Committee initially. Competent members among these member officers would then be part of the next executive committee when the existing committee is dissolved after the committee members graduate. This is done to ensure that the organization grows while making it easier for the team members to focus on their academics and other responsibilities and also to train the prospective members to take these responsibilities up in the future at the right time.

Telugu Velugu means the ‘Brilliant Light of Telugu’ that denotes the glory of the ancient and evergreen Telugu language and culture while indicating the tremendous inherent potential and skills possessed by Telugu people who staunchly believe in upholding the cherished principle of ‘Unity in Diversity’ while maintaining their unique cultural identity by staying in touch with their roots.
summary: Telugu Velugu is the Unified Telugu Student Association of NCSU, whose principal aims include promoting Cultural Unity among Telugu students, comprising undergraduate, graduate & alumni by effective networking for achieving academic & future career goals

name: Pack Motorsports FSAE
description:
Welcome to Pack Motorsports FSAE. We are a student run organization, and we design, build, and compete with a Formula style race car in the annual collegiate design competitions sponsored by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE). Our program is housed under the College of Engineering at N.C. State, but any member of the student body, graduate or undergraduate, can be a member regardless of major. This organization gives students the unique opportunity to design and build a new race car every year. Students receive a wealth of hands on engineering experience working on something exciting. Engineering students can apply what they learn in the classroom to a real project and develop important skills that are applicable to mechanical, thermal, and electrical systems. 

New member onboarding is conducted in the beginning of each Fall semester, and more specific recruiting is done in the beginning of each Spring semester. For more information, look out for updates on our Instagram page. Please do not request to join via this page, as we will not accept the request.
summary: Welcome to Pack Motorsports FSAE. We are a student run organization, and we design, build, and compete with a Formula style race car in the annual collegiate design competitions sponsored by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE).

name: Hike More, Worry Less at NC State
description: The “Hike More, Worry Less” club at NC State was formed to promote being in the outdoors and having an opportunity to meet other students and make new friends. The club is made for anyone and everyone from all different backgrounds and experiences.  Now, why the “worry less” part? Well, due to NC State's recent issues with mental health, me and my buddies wanted to address this issue. We've created an environment to promote worrying less and taking a break from all the stressors of school. Please don't apply to join on this site, just follow us on Instagram to get updates on meetings. Feel free to pop in whenever we have one.
summary: Dedicated to Hiking More and Worrying Less and promoting students to get their minds off of school. We meet once every 2 weeks on Fridays and have outings the next day. Please don't join on this site, just follow us on Instagram and come to a meeting =D

name: Hack_NCState Planning Committee
description:
This student organization represents the Hack_NCState Planning Committee. We plan, coordinate, and run the event. The planning committee is split into five subcommittees: Sponsorship, Logistics, Software, Media, and Finance, all of which perform essential tasks before, during, and after the event to make sure everything runs smoothly. Being part of the Hack_NCState Planning Committee gives you the unique opportunity to work alongside fellow students and company sponsors to host a weekend full of innovative software solutions. If you would like to join us in running this event, consider applying!

Hack_NCState is NC State's largest, all-inclusive hackathon competition. Since 2018, the Hack_NCState Student Organization has hosted this 24-hour event, which is sanctioned by Major League Hacking. It is open to undergraduate and graduate students. Participants can work individually or in small teams to develop a creative software solution to any problem they want to address within our various themes and tracks. Hack_NCState has grown steadily year over year and has attracted 200-300 participants annually. For more information, please visit the Hack_NCState website at https://hackncstate.org/ and reach out to the planning committee at hackncstate@ncsu.edu.
summary: This student organization represents the Hack_NCState Planning Committee, which organizes and runs NC State's premier 24-hour hackathon where students compete to develop innovative software solutions.

name: Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter at NC State
description: Habitat for Humanity at NC State is a service organization that is dedicated to working towards providing affordable housing for people in our local communities. We partner with Habitat for Humanity of Wake County to assist with build days on local sites and volunteer in their ReStore centers. We also run Shack-A-Thon, a week-long fundraising event here at NC State every year. During Shack-A-Thon, we work with other student organizations to build and man shacks in the Brickyard to raise money and awareness for housing insecurity.
summary: Habitat for Humanity at NC State is a service organization affiliated with Habitat for Humanity of Wake County dedicated to the mission of establishing affordable housing in local communities.

name: FOUR at NC State University
description:
FOUR at NCSU is a community of students that seek to grow in their relationship with God. Our mission is to lead students from where they are to a deeper experience with Christ. 

 

In Mark 2:1-5, we see a story of Jesus coming back home from traveling and as people hear that He is home, they gathered in a large group where He was staying. Whenever there was a crowd Jesus often took the opportunity to spend time with those people. As He is teaching the crowd, the Bible talks about how there are 4 men who carried a paralytic man to Jesus. The thing was, there was no room, not even near the door of the home where Jesus was staying. These 4 men knew that if they could take this paralytic man to Jesus, Jesus could transform his life. There was something special about Jesus that caused these 4 men to be persistent in seeking the care of this paralytic man. So, the 4 men took him to the roof, dug a hole in the roof, and lowered him down to where Jesus was teaching. The Bible says, “When Jesus saw THEIR faith, he said to the paralyzed man, “Son, your sins are forgiven.” Our entire mission of FOUR comes down to this: leading students from where they are to a deeper experience with Jesus. We can provide some of the answers, but Jesus has every answer that we need. 

 

If you are looking for true authentic community, then this is the place for you. We are nowhere close to perfect, but Jesus is. We all find ourselves at different places in our spiritual journey with God. The best place to start growing is by knowing where you are and taking that next step in faith with others. We want to help build an atmosphere that allows for students to grow in their faith every single day of their lives. 

 

Join us every Wednesday night at 7pm for our FOUR at NCSU Gathering.
summary: FOUR at NCSU is a gathering of students who seek to grow in their relationship with God through student community, bible study & worship, and serving.

name: Field Hockey at NC State
summary: Field Hockey at NC State provides a competitive field hockey team in which NC State students can participate in the National Field Hockey League. The objective of the team is to be a safe, fun, and healthy atmosphere in which players can enjoy the sport.

name: Campus Thrift Store
description: The Campus Thrift Store aims to bring a fun shopping experience to students while enforcing the importance of sustainability and longevity in the world of fashion and textiles. We hope to also provide cheap or accessible clothing options to those in need, without having to rely on corporations who may have negative impacts both environmentally and socially. We also provide opportunities for students to work on their entrepreneurial, marketing, graphic design, and educational skills, as the proper functioning of organization relies on many components.
summary: Providing affordable, accessible, and sustainable clothing to students through our permanent thrift space, vintage markets, and other pop-up events on campus.

name: The History Club at NC State
description: An organization that promotes the study of history and community building through fun events and discussions. We encourage students with an interest in historic topics to join us and meet like minded peers, as well as learn a little bit about a new topic each meeting.
summary: An organization that promotes the study of history and community building through fun events and discussions.

name: NC State Real Estate
description: Please visit fill out the membership form located on the club website to be added to our mailing list
summary: The goal of Real Estate club is to expose students from all backgrounds to careers in real estate, trends in real estate, meet with industry professionals, and equip members with resources they need to succeed

name: Student Business Sustainability Association
description:
At the Student Business Sustainability Association (SBSA), we believe in making changes from within. If we can bring in passionate individuals into all levels of operations, businesses will be at the forefront of change. At NC State, there is rising group of students who aspire to make a difference to our society. All they need is a community of similarly committed people for support and development. By offering career-oriented events, our club aims to prepare the aspiring impact-driven leaders to discover how they can utilize their existing talents or discover new skills to influence businesses toward a sustainable future.

 

Ready to take part? Join us today: Interest Form
summary: Inspiring the next generation of business sustainability leaders.

name: Phi Sigma Rho Sorority
description: Phi Sigma Rho is a social sorority for women and non binary students in STEM majors.
summary: Phi Sigma Rho Sorority is a social sorority for students who identify as female or non-binary pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree in Engineering and Engineering Technology (STEM).

name: Nigerian Students Association at NC State
description: NSA at NCSU
summary: This is a student led organization that provides integration, interaction and mentorship support for prospective and current Nigerian students in North Carolina State University.

name: Nutrition Club at NC State University
description: The Purpose of the Nutrition Club is to offer a range of opportunities for student life enrichment, including professional development, social events, service opportunities, and community outreach both on and off campus. While the club's primary focus is Nutrition majors and minors, it is open to any NC State student interested in this field. Our activities aim to build a supportive community, enhance professional skills, and promote awareness about Nutrition and health within and beyond the university.
summary: A club for students at NC State who are interested in learning more about the field of nutrition, serving in the community, and meeting other students with similar interests.

name: SolidWorks User Group at NC State
description:
SolidWorks User Group at NC State is a user group for any student at NC State. Whether you are using SolidWorks for a student design team, a personal project, or just have a passion for the software, all are welcome to join.
The SOLIDWORKS User Group Network (SWUGN) is run for our users, by our users. Its mission is to empower people who use SOLIDWORKS and 3DEXPERIENCE Works products to collaborate with, learn from, and teach others.
Connect and meet up with one another anytime, anywhere, on any device for in-depth technical conversations and face-to-face networking!

Discord: https://discord.gg/6QzHYa4CH8

SWUG Community: https://community.swugn.org/solidworks-user-group-at-nc-state/
summary: The SOLIDWORKS User Group Network (SWUGN) is run for our users, by our users. Its mission is to empower people who use SOLIDWORKS and 3DEXPERIENCE Works products to collaborate with, learn from, and teach others.

name: Swifties at NC State
description: The purpose of this student organization is to provide currently enrolled students who love Taylor Swift, with the opportunity to come together and create a space of Swift appreciation. This space will seek to teach and be welcoming of those who do not know much about Taylor or the fandom itself. Our goal is to find comfort and relatability within her lyrics in unison while also being happy, free, confused and lonely at the same time in our college years.
summary: Part of the Taylor Swift or "Swiftie" fan base here at NC State.

name: Mountain Bike Club at NC State
description:
Hi! We're the Mountain Bike Club at NC State. Our goal is to provide a fun environment for riders of all skill levels through trips to mountain biking destinations and weekly local rides. We're not out here to set KOM's on Strava, but we are here to get outside and have some fun on bikes. 

 

Interested in joining? Drop an email to ncsumtb@gmail.com and we'll point you in the right direction.

 

Still not sure? If you've got a working bike, know how to ride it, and a helmet, come to one of our free interest rides at the beginning of the semester! Again, send an email to ncsumtb@gmail.com to let us know, and we'll get you sorted.
summary: The MTB Club at NC State is a club for casual riders, with local rides and adventure trips, focused on creating a fun environment for riders of all skill levels.

name: Code Black
description:
Code Black is dedicated to solidifying the presence of black students in NC State's computer science department by providing opportunities for community development, professional success and departmental interactions.  It will hold professional devlepment meetings with business contacts, organize a hack-a-thon in honor of black history month and hold social events through out the school year.

Social Events the first Thursday of every month!

 

Join the Code Black Discord: https://discord.gg/AspNDMYYj5
summary: Code Black is dedicated to solidifying the presence of black students in NC State's computer science department by providing opportunities for community development, professional success and departmental interactions.

name: Magical Moments Foundation at NC State
description:
Magical Moments Foundation is a wish-granting organization for children with craniofacial disorders. We work to make a difference in these children’s lives, spread awareness of the lifelong harm caused by appearance-related stigma, and promote acceptance of facial diversity.

 

Children with facial differences often endure the pain of teasing, bullying, and isolation— pain that can leave emotional scars that last a lifetime. Children that face the world with a difference also endure the challenge of a more silent, but just as damaging, form of stigma: that of unconscious bias. And so, many children with differences grow up in a world that tells them that their dreams must be smaller than those of their peers.

 

Magical Moments is working to change all of that. We create magical experiences that restore hope and provide comfort, inspiration, and encouragement to children with facial differences that are the result of genetic disorders, congenital anomalies, cancer treatment, severe trauma and burns. We help children understand that they are not at all limited by their appearance, but only by the size of their dreams! Through the magical moments that we create, we not only make a difference in children’s lives, but we also promote awareness of the lifelong harm caused by appearance-related stigma and create more widespread acceptance of facial diversity within our communities.

With your help, we can grant wishes that teach children with facial differences to believe in themselves while teaching the world to see and celebrate the beauty in every child!
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to spread awareness about the children the Magical Moments Foundation works with through hosting fundraising events and donation opportunities.

name: Pack Pals at NC State
description:
We all have acquaintances, but what about real friends? No one knows the challenge of making new friends like a college student. The transition from home to college is intimidating, especially with the pressure to branch out and make new connections. Pack Pals is a safe space and organization where all students can come together to make positive relations with one another. We provide the invitation for connection and companionship, as well programming structured around nurturing these relationships.

At weekly meetings we discuss making connections, opening up and challenging the limits of your comfort zone. Connection is a fundamental aspect of the human experience; Pack Pals seeks to foster that in our community. 

The end goal is for every student who joins to leave the semester with one close friend they made. Whether that friend be in or out of the club, we want students to learn the skills necessary for socializing and developing friendships.

We are working on getting the club more involved with new leadership and other organizations, we will use instagram, discord and emails as a way to keep members updated. We plan to collaborate and table on campus as well to advertise! Please join the discord and follow the instagram if you wish to stay connected :)

Discord 

Instagram

Facebook coming soon!
summary: We all have acquaintances, but what about real friends? No one knows the challenge of making new friends like a college student.

name: Backpacking Club at NC State
description:
We are a student-led organization that goes on overnight hiking trips. We want to provide people with the opportunity to get outdoors, meet new people, get some exercise, and develop the skills needed for backpacking. No prior backpacking experience is necessary.

 

We are no longer taking new members for Spring 2026. Check back in the fall for more information about joining the club!

 

Follow us on Instagram at @ncstatebackpackingclub for reminders and trip photos!
summary: We are a student-led organization that goes on overnight hiking trips. We want to provide people with the opportunity to get outdoors, meet new people, get some exercise, and develop the skills needed for backpacking. No prior experience necessary.

name: The Irish Dance Club
description: The purpose of this student organization is to spread the joy and culture of Irish dance to people through practice, performance, education, and competition.
summary:
The purpose of the Irish Dance Club is to spread the joy and culture of Irish dance to people through practice, performance, education, and competition.

All experience levels are welcome!

name: Blossom Together at NC State
description: Blossom Together stands upon a philosophy for happiness. We base our efforts with dreams of a utopia, where every individual woman and man lives their lives dedicated to the happiness of others, where neither poverty nor famine roams the land, where every newborn is given an opportunity to read and achieve. We have decided that if such a utopia were to exist, it would first have to blossom. The effort works to support the blossoming and growth of this philosophy. Blossom Together at NC State aims to bring the values and motives of the Blossom Together organization to North Carolina State's campus and support the original organization's cause for a better world for all.
summary: Blossom Together is a student-led humanitarian organization that supports living standards in developing African countries. The organizations mainly focuses on three branches of support: food, water, and health.

name: T-Files
description: The mission of T-Files is to provide a space the centers the lived experiences of transgender, non-binary, gender non-conforming, and gender questioning students to explore and affirm their identities, build community, and create a welcoming environment at NC State. Through participation, students can freely share their experiences, foster positive relationships, and become advocates for one another’s safety, health, and well-being.
summary: This student organization is intended to connect and build community among transgender students at North Carolina State University.

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association at NC State
description:
NC State University's chapter of the National Student Speech Language Hearing Association (NSSLHA), the only national student organization for pre-professionals studying communication sciences and disorders (CSD) recognized by the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA). Supporting and forming a community for students looking to get into careers in speech-language pathology, audiology, etc.

 

National NSSLHA, established in 1972 and headquartered in Rockville, Maryland, supports nearly 11,000 members and 350+ chapters at colleges and universities nationwide.
summary: The national student organization for pre-professionals studying communication sciences and disorders (CSD) that works to provide the tools to navigate academic careers and prepare for professional ones in the fields of speech pathology, audiology, etc.

name: NRF @ NC State
description: NRF at NC State is NC State's chapter of the National Retail Federation Foundation (NRF Foundation). We are a student organization that helps students gain valuable career development and networking skills through events such as guest speakers, resume workshops, mentorship, and more. As a student chapter of the NRF Foundation, we aim to educate more students about opportunities through the NRF Foundation and provide them with opportunities to connect with professionals in the fashion, retail, or textiles industries.
summary: NC State's chapter of the NRF Foundation for students looking to learn career development skills and gain networking opportunities with professionals in primarily the fashion, textile, and retail industries. All NC State Students are welcome!

name: Ornithology Club at NC State
description: The purpose of the Ornithology Club is to provide a group for those who share a common interest in wild birds and ornithology. This club is for birdwatchers, birders, conservationists, researchers, and anyone who is interested in the conservation, research, and study, and appreciation of wild birds. Email rpwanden@ncsu.edu for any questions you may have.
summary: Provide a group for those who share a common interest in wild birds and ornithology. Email rpwanden@ncsu.edu for any questions you may have or if you want to join!

name: NC State Athletics
description: NC State Athletics needs you to help cheer on our men and women's student-athletes to victory! Listed on this page are all of the home events taking place on or around campus and exciting promotions that are coming up this season for all of our sports! The Strength of the Wolf is the Pack!
summary: NC State Athletics needs you to help cheer on our men and women's student-athletes to victory! Listed on this page are all of the home events taking place on or around campus and exciting promotions that are coming up this season for all of our sports!

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
description:
In 1898, nine young women at the Virginia State Female Normal School (now Longwood University) formalized their friendship by forming Zeta Tau Alpha. Away from home for the first time, these teenagers longed for companionship while they studied to be teachers. Fearing their unique friendship would fade without a formal bond to sustain it, they met in secret by candlelight in the bathroom of their dormitory. At the start, they used only three question marks as their group name. The first minute book of regular meetings states Zeta Tau Alpha was organized Oct. 15, 1898.

Learn more about how Zeta Tau Alpha was founded.

We now have more than 297,000 initiated members, 172 active collegiate chapters and more than 225 alumnae chapters across the country.

The Zeta Tau Alpha Foundation is the philanthropic and fundraising arm of the Fraternity. The Foundation leads our efforts in breast cancer education and awareness, provides around $1 million in scholarships annually, and sponsors educational and leadership opportunities for our members and others.

The ZTA Fraternity Housing Corporation is the largest in the Panhellenic world and provides safe, secure and competitive housing for all our collegiate members.

Explore the national ZTA website to find additional information about our Nine Key Values, policies, dedication to inclusion, programming and more.
summary: ZTA sisterhood begins in college and extends far beyond four years. At Iota Phi, we are proud to be both a part of the Wolfpack and North Carolina Zetas! Since 1989, we have been home to some of the brightest and most wonderful women at North Carolina!

name: Student Parent Association
description: The Student Parent Association (SPA) offers opportunities for support by connecting students who are pregnant and/or parenting with each other, sharing information about helpful campus-based and community-based resources, and collectively advocating for policies, programs, and practices that support the well-being and retention of student parents. SPA establishes a network of student parents at the undergraduate and graduate level at NC State University.
summary: The Student Parent Association (SPA) aims to connect, support, inform, and advocate for NC State students who are pregnant and/or parenting. Anyone is welcome to join (undergraduate students, graduate students, postdocs, etc.).

name: Students Supporting Free Moms at NC State
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to coordinate reservations, marketing, and working with administration to ensure the smoothness of Free Moms staying and bringing their love to NC State!

name: Rising Leaders
description:
Rising Leaders is a new tradition at NC State through the Student Leadership and Engagement Office that provides an opportunity for first-year students & first-year transfer students to develop intentional leadership skills, become more involved in the Raleigh community through service learning, and build authentic relationships with others in the program. 

Rising Leaders allows students to have a mentor who has previously completed the rising leader program to help embark on your first-year at NC State! Rising Leaders is for anyone who is looking to further develop their leadership skills, give back to their community, and develop impactful relationships with other first-year students and mentors of the program.

Check out this link here to learn more about Rising Leaders: https://sle.dasa.ncsu.edu/leadership/rising-leaders/
summary: Rising Leaders is ran through Student Leadership and Engagement that provides an opportunity for first-year students & first-year transfer students to develop intentional leadership skills through service learning and mentorship.

name: Country Swing Club
description:
Join the group me!

https://groupme.com/join_group/95834333/LJst5lbl
summary: Country Swing is an up and coming version of Swing Dancing. Our focus will be to teach and exercise the moves of Country swing dancing to appropriate music. Through this, people will collaborate and dance  using the moves taught.

name: Fungi Appreciation and Mycology at NC State
summary: A student organization aimed at creating a community of fungi enthusiasts, from budding amateurs to hardened mycologists. We provide an inclusive space for learning, discussing, and working with mushrooms, fungi, and mycology.

name: NC State University Chapter of the Diabetes Link
description: We are a group of students who's goals is to create a supportive environment for students on campus living with diabetes. We also bring awareness to the condition by hosting outreach and education events. Throughout the semester we will host a variety of social and outreach events to help foster a community for those on campus with diabetes!
summary: This is a chapter level of the Diabetes Link. We are a group of young adult students at NC State who have some form of diabetes. We support each other and fundraise for diabetes organizations.

name: Queer Business Student Association
description:
The purpose of this student organization is to provide unique professional development opportunities and build a community for LGBTQIA+ identifying business students at NC State University. We welcome all students affiliated with the Poole College of Management, as well as students who career goals relate to business (students studying Agriculture Business, Sports Management, Textile Product Management and Marketing, and more!) By creating this space we hope to empower our members and provide them with meaningful tools, experiences, and connections to take with them into their careers.

We look to successfully implement this through events including but not limited to: mentorship opportunities, networking events, guest speakers, volunteer opportunities with local LGBTQ+ groups, attending queer-affirming conferences, resume workshops, and social events.

 

Website: https://qbsancsu.wixsite.com/ncstateqbsa

Instagram: qbsa_ncsu
summary: The goal of the Queer Business Student Association is to provide unique professional development opportunities and foster community for LGBTQIA+ identifying business students at NC State.

name: Marksmanship Club at NC State
description: The mission of this student organization is to create a community which provides members with opportunities to learn or improve upon their marksmanship skills, regardless of prior experience. The Marksmanship Club has a primary focus on education and will foster connections between members and instructors for the purpose of furthering their education and marksman training in a wide variety of disciplines. These include, but are not limited to, general safety practices, laws and regulations, firearms mechanics and engineering, ballistics, competition, and sporting purposes.
summary: Pack Marksmanship is dedicated to creating a safe community where all students can have access to education, training, and events in the shooting sports. From beginner to expert, we strive to foster relationships and events that increase knowledge.

name: Africa Prosperity Project
description:
The purpose of the Africa Prosperity Project is to create a lasting impact by empowering students of African descent to give back to their home countries and support underprivileged communities within their area. Our core mission is to foster a sense of responsibility, empathy, and global citizenship among students while making a difference in the lives of those in need.

Our primary focus will be on helping underprivileged communities in various African countries. Each semester, we will concentrate on a particular country and partner with an elementary school in that region. Through fundraising efforts and collaborative initiatives, we will provide essential personal care packages, educational supplies, and learning resources to the youth, empowering them with the tools they need to succeed. While we are dedicated to making a difference in Africa, we must also acknowledge the importance of giving back to the local community. In Raleigh, we will engage in exciting community service projects, addressing issues like education, poverty, and hunger, to promote positive change right at home. We will organize a series of creative and engaging fundraising events throughout the semester, open to the entire student body and local community. We believe that promoting an understanding and appreciation for diverse cultures is crucial to creating a harmonious world. Our club will organize events, workshops, and cultural exchanges that cultivate fellowship among all students, specifically those with African backgrounds, invoking mutual respect and understanding. To maximize our impact, we will collaborate with other student organizations, non-profit organizations, and educational institutions. By forging partnerships, we can pool resources, exchange expertise, and collectively work towards sustainable solutions that bring about meaningful change.
summary: The purpose of the Africa Prosperity Project is to create a lasting impact by empowering students of African descent to give back to their home countries and support underprivileged communities within their area current areas.

name: The National Association of Minority Landscape Architects at NC State University
description: The National Association of Minority Landscape Architects (NAMLA) is a 501(c)(3) organization founded in Los Angeles, California in 2020. The premise for starting the organization is based on increasing minority representation at all levels of landscape architecture practice and academia. NAMLA plans to do this by providing educational and career development assistance to minorities while confronting the structural racism that has disproportionately kept people of color from having decision-making roles that determine how our landscapes are programmed, designed, and taught.
summary: The National Association of Landscape Architects aims to increase minority representation at all levels of Landscape Architecture practice and academia through education, career development, and leadership.

name: Data Science Club
description: The Data Science Club offers a jumping off point for students from any background to learn how to make use of data in their particular fields. Regular meetings will feature domain-focused presentations on relevant data science concepts and skills; other common programming includes outreach events and data science competitions. Students can join as full members or just drop in to relevant activities as they please; any student with an interest in data is encouraged to participate!
summary: The Data Science Club at NC State is a student organization that highlights data science applications for all disciplines. It aims to bring together students from all majors with interest in data to take part in activities and technical workshops.

name: Chinese Learning Society
description:
说明: 

Our mission is to inspire lifelong learning and cultural appreciation, empowering students to connect with Chinese-speaking communities around the world. Whether you’re preparing for study abroad, academic pursuits, or simply expanding your horizons, the Chinese Learning Society is open to you. Through language exchanges, study sessions, cultural events, and study abroad opportunities, we aim to build a community of students who are enthusiastic about improving their language skills and deepening their understanding of Chinese traditions and society.

summary: The NCSU Chinese Learning Society is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering a passion for learning Chinese language and culture. We provide a supportive and engaging environment for learners of all levels, from beginners to native speakers.

name: Pediatrics and Family Medicine at NC State
description:
PFM (Pediatrics and Family Medicine at NC State) is a community that is open to all undergraduate and graduate students at NC State University, regardless of major, degree, minor or age. As a student organization, our hope is to foster a community that inspires an academic and feasible pursuit of family medicine and pediatrics. Our group strives to promote awareness of and interest in these two fields through education and mentoring. Additionally, PFM is here to encourage undergraduate students with an interest in pediatrics and family medicine to educate others. Our organization will allow students to learn from a variety of health professionals including but not limited to optometrists, medical doctors (MD and DO), nurses, dentists and physical therapists. PFM is excited to serve NCSU students who are interested in health through virtual meetings.

Founder: Jawad Haque
summary: Our student organization seeks to provide information and experiences related to pediatrics and family medicine to undergraduate students at NC State University.

name: Poole Student Advisory Board
description:
The Poole College of Management's Student Advisory Board (PSAB) is an organization created directly under the Dean's supervision at the Poole College of Management. The PSAB's mission is to provide a diverse and holistic representation of the undergraduate student body in the decision-making processes of the College. It directly advises the Deans on current issues facing the student body and conduct research and engagement initiatives to improve our academic experience at Poole College. 

 

With only three years since its founding, PSAB has: 

- Presented various research projects to the Dean and the Poole Advisory Board. 

- Created and disbursed awards to recognize outstanding faculty and staff. 

- Established an annual Graduating Class Gift that turns into a scholarship for new Poole students. 

- Implemented and facilitated Poole's first student-led Professional Mentorship Program. 

- Assist Poole College of Management choose graduation class speakers.

 

This semester we are continuing our efforts and maintaining our initiatives, and we are excited for the ways we will grow in upcoming years!
summary: The Poole College Undergraduate Student Advisory Board (PSAB) supports and advises the Poole College of Management when developing innovative curricula and impactful educational programs and strategies for supporting undergraduate students.

name: Spanish Ambassadors
description:
Purpose

To cultivate a community within Spanish majors/minors and faculty and recruit for the Spanish section through community and outreach events. Spanish Ambassadors will plan and organize events related to Spanish for NC State students. 

 

Please contact the advisor to learn how to get involved.
summary: To cultivate a community within Spanish majors/minors and faculty and recruit for the Spanish section through community and outreach events. Spanish Ambassadors will plan and organize events related to Spanish for NC State students.

name: Healthy Hands Initiative
description: Healthy Hands Initiative is the third chapter of The Healthy Hands Initiative 501 ( c)(3) nonprofit organization based out of UNC-Chapel Hill. The mission of this nonprofit seeks to provide innovative clean water solutions to the Bokaro district of northeast India in order to reduce the rates of prevalent, preventable diseases and work towards promoting social welfare and healthcare in our targeted areas. Throughout the next few years, HHI plans to expand their reach to more underprivileged communities in India as well as trying to establish a base in other countries.
summary: The Healthy Hands Initiative's mission is to provide sustainable clean water solutions to underserved families and students in India to reduce the rates the rates of prevalent diseases.

name: Latine Graduate Student Association
description: The LGSA is dedicated to promoting the success of Latine graduate students at NC State University. The organization's purpose is threefold. First, LGSA aims to build a community of Latine scholars at NC State University by hosting a forum for academic exchange and social interaction, offering support to new students, and encouraging the recruitment of prospective Latine students. Second, LGSA seeks to build a network outside of NC State University, connecting with national organizations, political organizations, and other academic institutions. Third, LGSA works to build a bridge between NC State University and Raleigh Latine communities.
summary: The LGSA is dedicated to promoting the success of Latine graduate students at NC State University.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi at NC State University
description: The vision of Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity at North Carolina State University is to foster a community of individuals interested in pursuing careers within the legal and government fields.
summary: The vision of Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity at the North Carolina State University is to foster a community of individuals interested in pursuing careers within the legal and government fields.

name: Raleigh Red Rollers
description: The purpose of this student organization is to allow students with an interest in bowling a time, place, and group to bowl with in order to develop their skills as a bowler. Another goal of the Raleigh Red Rollers is to establish a community of support and fellowship among members.
summary: A bowling club for NC State students with an interest in bowling

name: Food, Bioprocessing and Nutrition Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: FBNS GSA was started in Fall 2022 semester to support FBNS graduate students. Our goals are to build leadership and communication skills, the FBNS grad student community, career skills, and raise our visibility within the department and university. We also aim to expand mental health support for grads and create a better connectedness between our multiple campuses. So far, we've organized multiple community-building events (such as educational field trips), career-building events (such as a CliftonStrengths workshop and access), and continuously communicated about mental health resources. We hope to continue these goals and pursue new ones to support our grad students as best as possible.
summary: FBNS GSA is a three-year-old organization focused on supporting graduate students within our department. Our goals are to build leadership and communication skills, the FBNS grad student community, career skills, and raise our visibility.

name: Lebanese Club at NC State
summary: The objective of the Lebanese club is to educate the Wolfpack community about the various aspects of Lebanon, encompassing its traditions, cuisine, and history. This serves as an excellent means to unite individuals of Lebanese descent on campus.

name: Developmental Ultimate Club
description:
We strive to provide an inclusive, competitive, positive, and constructive environment for any and all players. We aim to improve the fitness, ultimate frisbee skills, and game knowledge of any player regardless of initial skill level. Our goal throughout the fall will be to develop new players to allow them chances to get on-field experience and grow as players. The focus in the spring will shift towards refining a team to compete at the top tier of developmental teams in the Atlantic Coast Region. We will strive to provide opportunities for everyone to play throughout the year and to reward the effort and skill displayed.

Sponsored by ARK Consulting Group, PLLC: https://www.arkconsultinggroup.com/
summary: The Developmental Ultimate Club welcomes all individuals seeking a community and improvement in ultimate frisbee skills. Everyone, regardless of prior experience, is encouraged to join us—even if you've never thrown a frisbee before.

name: Queer, Trans, Black, Indigenous, People of Color
description:
Meets 4:30 - 6:00 pm every other Friday at the Pride Center!

Next meeting: November, 14th
summary: A student organization dedicated to creating community and events centering the lived experiences of Queer, Trans, Black, Indigenous, People of Color (QTBIPOC) at NC State.

name: Global Medical Brigades at NC State
description: Global Brigades is a non-profit organization committed to resolving health and economic disparities through empowerment. A holistic model that focuses on Water & Sanitation Infrastructure, Sustainable Health Systems, and Economic Development is implemented to help communities rise out of poverty. Global Medical Brigades consists of both students and medical professionals that work alongside communities in Ghana, Panama, Honduras, Greece, and Guatemala to implement sustainable health systems. Students that are part of the GMB Chapter at NC State will have the opportunity to sign up as a volunteer for a 7-day Medical Brigade to one of the locations listed above. During the brigade, students will learn about the community's healthcare needs and shadow/work with licensed health care professionals in mobile clinics. A majority of the brigade will be spent volunteering in the mobile clinics. These clinics assist hundreds of community members by providing consultations, medication, triage, and educational workshops. Throughout the academic year, students will prepare for the brigade by fundraising to meet our donation goal.
summary: Global Brigades is a non-profit organization committed to resolving health and economic disparities. The Global Medical Brigades Chapter at NCSU is part of a movement that aims to implement sustainable health systems in under-resourced communities.

name: Entomology Club
description: The Entomology club is targeted toward students who share an interest in insects and other related arthropods. The club is open to not only those planning a future career in entomology or related fields but also those who enjoy entomology as a hobby or have a keen interest in the subject. As a club, our mission is to lead meetings and participate in local events with the intent of sparking curiosity about the wonderful world of insects. We hope to achieve this mission by introducing participants to active professionals in fields of entomological research, as well as participation in hands-on activities both in the classroom and out in the field. We hope you will join us on this adventure as we explore the diverse world of insects all around us.
summary: A club targeted towards students with a current interest in insects or students interested in expanding their knowledge involving insects. This is a club for people interested in entomology as a hobby or a career.

name: Sigma Alpha Mu
description: IFC Fraternity
summary: Since 1938 Sigma Alpha Mu’s Mission is to foster the development of collegiate men and our alumni by instilling strong fraternal values, offering social and service opportunities, and encouraging academic excellence at NC State University.

name: History Grad Student Association
description: The History Graduate Student Association at NC State is an organization devoted to the professional development of NC State's history and public history graduate students. We promote history and public history interests within our department and at our university. In addition to representing the History Department in NC State's graduate student government, HGSA organizes professional and social events such as conferences, presentations, and field trips. Our goal is to provide professional development while fostering a strong academic and social community within our department.
summary: The History Graduate Student Association at NC State is an organization devoted to the professional development of NC State's history and public history graduate students.

name: North Carolina State University - ECS Student Chapter
description: The purpose of this organization is to act as a branch of the National Electrochemical Society. Specifically, this organization desires to bring about the goal of the national organization on a university level. This goal is outlined in the national by-laws as “The mission of the Society is to advance theory and practice at the forefront of electrochemical and solid-state science and technology, and allied subjects. To encourage research, discussion, critical assessment, and dissemination of knowledge in these fields, the Society holds meetings, publishes scientific papers, fosters training and education of scientists and engineers, and cooperates with other organizations to promote science and technology in the public interest.”
summary: ECS North Carolina State University Student Chapter is the local chapter of the National Electrochemical Society. We focus on encouraging research, discussion, critical assessment, and dissemination of knowledge in the field of electrochemistry.

name: Baking Club at North Carolina State University
description: The purpose of the Baking Club at North Carolina State University is to provide a space for students who are passionate about baking to come together, share their love for baking, learn new skills, and create delicious baked goods. The club aims to foster a sense of community among members, promote the art of baking, and organize events that allow members to showcase their baking talents. Additionally, the club recognizes the therapeutic nature of baking in relieving stress and aims to provide a relaxing and creative outlet for its members.
summary: The purpose of the Baking Club at North Carolina State University is to provide a space for students who are passionate about baking to come together, share their love for baking, learn new skills, and create delicious baked goods.

name: Volunteer Organization for Youth
description: The Volunteer Organization for Youth (VOY) encourages community involvement among students. Often, students do not engage in service simply because opportunities are not well-advertised, so VOY eliminates this obstacle by making volunteering options readily available. Specifically, the organization focuses on solving modern problems relevant to members, specifically crises youth in the community face. We believe that, by engaging in volunteering early in life, students can ignite a lifelong passion for service.
summary: The Volunteer Organization for Youth (VOY) encourages community involvement by making volunteering options readily available and addressing issues that are valuable to members, specifically crises youth in the community face.

name: NC State Student Chapter of Delta Waterfowl
description:
At Delta, the future of hunting and management of wetlands and waterfowl are university students. Students who do not understand hunting and hunters might support policy and management decisions that do not sustain waterfowl and their required habitat at the same levels desired by hunters and conservation biologists. These wetlands and associated uplands are important for a myriad of other wildlife species including game and nongame. Nationwide, there is a shift away from game management in many of the state, provincial, and federal agencies. Therefore, as a direct result of these concerns, Delta launched the University Hunting Program in August 2017 and the Fisheries, Wildlife, and Conservation Biology Program at NCSU started the NCSU University Hunting Program affiliated with Delta Waterfowl in 2022.


Consistent with the objectives of Delta Waterfowl, the NCSU Student Chapter objectives are:


1. To officially become the NCSU Student Chapter of Delta Waterfowl aligned with Delta Waterfowl..
2. To further the scientific and educational interest of wildlife conservation and waterfowl management.
3. To recognize and represent the field of Waterfowl Ecology and Biology for all school and campus activities.
4. To represent NCSU as a leader in the arena of wildlife conservation, waterfowl management, and education.
summary: Delta Waterfowl is a leading conservation group that works to produce ducks through intensive management programs and conservation of breeding duck habitat. Delta conducts vital waterfowl research while protecting waterfowl hunting in North America.

name: Quarterbacks Club
description:
This club is meant to provide an outlet for those who aren't looking for a serious endeavor and just want to hang out, meet new people, and throw a football.

Fall 2025: Every Other Tuesday 6-7 PM starting August 26th
summary:
This club is meant to provide an outlet for those who aren't looking for a serious endeavor and just want to hang out, meet new people, and throw a football.

Fall 2025: Every Other Tuesday 6-7 PM starting August 26th

name: The Striking Club at NC State
description:
This student organization teaches members about different striking techniques, centered around boxing, kickboxing, and Muay Thai. The learning of these techniques will foster discipline in members of the organization, respect for others, and respect for themselves, all while building a community of members equally interested in martial arts. Members will have the opportunity to learn intermediate and advanced techniques as well.

 

Members are required to sign this waiver. Please print and sign (not digitally) or ask a coach for one at practice for us to keep in our records. 

Join our https://groupme.com/join_group/91590840/qGAOewlk for club announcements and updates!

Contact us for any more details using any of these methods below: 

Official Club Instagram | @fight.ncsu

Official Club Email | thestrikingclub-org@ncsu.edu

Meeting times for Spring '26 semester: Monday/Thursday 6:15pm to 7:15 pm
summary: The Striking Club teaches its members technique centered around boxing, kickboxing, and Muay Thai. Whether your goals are more physical fitness, general recreation, or self defense, come to a class for a good lesson and workout

name: Stem Program to Reach Our Underrepresented Teens
description: We are an outreach group with NC State’s Biological and Agricultural Engineering (BAE)  called Sprouts (STEM  Program to Reach Our Underrepresented Teens). In collaboration with the BAE Graduate Student Association, we seek to bridge the gap between K-12 learning and college education through Science, Technology, Engineering, Agriculture, and Math (S.T.E.A.M.) workshops. The goal of Sprouts is to expose students to environmental engineering topics they might not be familiar with through once a month learning sessions. We will establish a basis of knowledge of Bio and Ag Engineering topics, while fulfilling some of North Carolina's essential standards for education. We want to show students that Biological and Agricultural Engineering is a broad field with plenty of opportunities for their innovative young minds.
summary: Our mission is to broaden participation within Biological and Agricultural Engineering through the edification of students.

name: Celebration Fellowship at NC State
description: Celebration Fellowship is dedicated to the purpose of serving the spiritual, academic, career, and social well-being of members. Our goal is to actively support Christian and non-Christian students at NC State through spiritual programs.
summary: Celebration Fellowship is dedicated to the purpose of serving the spiritual, academic, career, and social well-being of members. Our goal is to actively support Christian and non-Christian students at NC State through spiritual programs.

name: Grill Club At North Carolina State University
description: The purpose of this student organization is to create a space for students who share a passion for grilling to come together to create delicious food while learning new techniques and skills. The Grill Club at NC State aims to open the opportunity for a sense of community among members and a chance to bond over their passion for grilling. Additionally, events will be held for members to showcase their skills and engage with our local community. Grill Club creates an environment for therapeutic and stress relieving benefits by allowing students a creative outlet and community.
summary: This organization aims to create a community with students with a shared passion for grilling. Become a Grill-Master at Grill Club!

name: Isshin-Ryu Karate at NC State
description: The purpose of this student organization is to teach members the art of Isshin-Ryu karate through various drills, katas, sparring, and application techniques. Isshin-Ryu at NC State will not only provide self-defense training in a safe and inclusive environment, but also promote confidence, self-efficacy, and leadership skills. Our goal is to empower our members to become the best version of themselves.
summary: In a safe and inclusive environment, this organization will teach members the art of Isshin-Ryu karate through various drills, katas, sparring, and application techniques.

name: Students for Decarbonization at NCSU
description:
email list insta

Join either the email list or insta for up to date info!

See our website for our recent actions.
summary: A group of students advocating for the decarbonization of NC State, including the secession of all fossil fuel investments, military industrial complex investments, and expansion of fossil fuel infrastructure.

name: Thandav
description: The purpose of Thandav at NC State is to encourage cultural awareness, social service, and sustainment of Indian Classical dance while also being nationally recognized and competitive.
summary: The purpose of Thandav at NC State is to encourage cultural awareness, social service, and sustainment of Indian Classical dance while also being nationally recognized and competitive.

name: User Interface User Experience Tech Club
description:
Keywords (separated by commas): Product Design, Web/Mobile/Wearable Device Development, Accessibility, Wireframe, User Research, Usability Testing, Extended Reality (XR) such as Virtual (VR)/Augmented (AR)/Mixed reality (MR)

Current Events

Google Sites: https://sites.google.com/ncsu.edu/uiuxtechclub/home
Google Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/u/0?cid=dWl1eHRlY2hjbHViLW9yZ0BuY3N1LmVkdQ

Stoat Chat: 

https://stoat.chat/login/

Join code: D0d3BMYm

 

Check out our Google Calendar for the latest updates (room change, cancellation, zoom link update, etc.).

We utilize our Google Sites website, Google Calendar, our Stoat chat social forum, and club emails to send out info instead of using Get Involved (posts and events require approval and this can take days; it is much faster for us to work through our own outreach ecosystem).

For information about content and club activities please visit our server in Stoat or refresh our club calendar!

Mission

The mission of the UI UX Tech Club is to:
• Promote the understanding of UI and UX by providing workshops
• Encourage creative endeavors in designing UI and UX by providing opportunities to experiment
• Provide a forum for learning about and discussing recent developments in UI and UX
• Empower student endeavors in UI and UX
summary:
UI UX Tech Club is a student organization that focuses on exploring user interfaces and user experiences centered around digital technology.
This includes accessibility, mobile phones, computers, tablets, wearables, and Virtual/Augmented/Mixed reality.

name: Basspack Fishing Club at NC State
description: Competitive bass fishing club here at NC State. We have won 3 national championships and we are striving for more.
summary: Bass fishing club here at NC State. We have 3 club tournaments every semester. Dues are $30, which gives you access to our discount codes that are given to us by our sponsors.

name: Folklorico Club at NC State
description: NC State Folklorico Club promotes and celebrates the rich cultural heritage of Folklorico dance throughout Latin America. Through education, performances, and community engagement, we aim to preserve and share traditional Folklorico dance styles, fostering a sense of unity and appreciation for diversity among its members.
summary: Folklorico Club at NC State promotes and celebrates the rich cultural heritage of Folklorico dance throughout Latin America.

name: Real Estate
description: The Real Estate Club at NC State was created for students passionate about real estate. We offer valuable insights, knowledge, and hands-on experience through engaging events, workshops, weekly meetings, and networking opportunities. Whether you're a seasoned real estate veteran or a beginner, our supportive community provides a platform to learn, connect with industry professionals, and gain practical skills. Join us for a journey into the world of real estate, where education meets application, and where you can build lasting connections with like-minded individuals. Elevate your understanding of real estate and navigate the investment landscape with confidence.
summary: The Real Estate Club at NC State is for students who are passionate about real estate investing, careers, and education. Learn, connect, and navigate the real estate landscape with us!

name: Latinos Unidos en Textiles
description: The Latinos Unidos en Textiles exists to promote and celebrate the rich cultural heritage of Hispanic textiles. The Latinos Unidos en Textiles strives to create a welcoming environment that encourages collaboration, learning, and the sharing of knowledge
summary: The Latinos Unidos en Textiles exists to promote and celebrate the rich cultural heritage of Hispanic textiles. The Latinos Unidos en Textiles strives to create a welcoming environment that encourages collaboration, learning, and the sharing of knowledge

name: Chabad at NC State
summary: Your home away from home, Chabad provides a welcoming Jewish community for students, with cultural and educational programs to explore traditions and connect with peers in a warm and inclusive environment.

name: Navarasa at NCSU
description: Navarasa is NC State's annual intercollegiate premier Indian Classical Dance Competition, with all proceeds going to charity. Teams travel from across the country to compete, and placing at Navarasa qualifies teams to compete in the National Indian Classical Dance Competition (Origins). Navarasa aims to spread awareness and educate the community about the South Asian culture. Throughout the year, we fundraise in order to raise money to hold the event and to donate to charity as well.
summary: Navarasa is NC State's annual intercollegiate premier Indian Classical Dance Competition, with all proceeds going to charity. Navarasa aims to spread awareness and educate the community about the South Asian culture.

name: Armenian Student Association
description: The continuing Nagorno-Karabakh war has taken a toll on the people of Armenia and those in the diaspora, creating a further need for community. One hundred twenty thousand people have been displaced to the Syunik region, and they still need help. The unfortunate ignorance of the American media has caused many to look past this recent genocide and move on to international relational interests. Through the anthropology of Armenia, we will educate those interested in our country's beautiful culture. Through the fundraisers, we will raise money to send to the displaced and make their lives slightly easier. Through films, we will learn more about the Armenian arts and how our people were entertained in the past. Through meetings, we will come together in a safe space and discuss the place we all know and love. In Raleigh, the Armenian population is small; however, as William Saroyan once said, "For when two of them meet anywhere in the world, see if they will not create a New Armenia."
summary: Armenian Student Association is a club for both Armenians and those wanting to immerse themselves in the country's anthropology. Through fundraisers, movies, music, food, and history, we will create a home in Raleigh and raise awareness about Armenia.

name: Comparative Medicine Institute Associate Members
description:
Please consider joining the Comparative Medicine Institute Larger Network!! Sign up form here.

 

The purpose of this student organization, made up from graduate students and postdoctoral scholars, is to encourage and support the mission of the Comparative Medicine Institute: to “...strive to foster interactions between clinical and basic research groups within the university community by developing mechanisms that encourage and facilitate those collaborations.” The CAM represents the voice of all graduate students and postdoctoral scholars that are participating in interdisciplinary research teams formed under the Comparative Medicine Institute.
summary: CAM seeks to encourage interdisciplinary research collaborations for graduate students and postdoctoral scholars through events hosted by the Comparative Medicine Institute.

name: Unitarian Universalist Campus Ministry
description:
We are a group of students on campus who are affiliated with the Unitarian Universalist Fellowship of Raleigh. We meet once a week, Wednesdays at 5pm, and each of our meetings includes a check-in and discussion that follows a theme of the month. Our purpose is to provide a space on campus where students can explore their faith while also connecting with other students. Being a Unitarian Universalist is not necessary to join, we are open to students of any religion, however our meetings do often involve discussion of the Unitarian Universalist faith. Please fill this form out if you are interested (or have questions) and we will reach out to you!

Meetings are weekly on Wednesdays at 5pm!
summary: We are a group of students on campus that meet each week and explore Unitarian Universalism and our faith. We are open to all NC State students. Our meetings usually consist of a check-in and discussion surrounding different monthly themes.

name: InterVarsity Graduate Christian Community
description: InterVarsity Graduate Christian Community at NC State is a chapter of InterVarsity Christian Fellowship/ USA, a national campus ministry organization that establishes and advances Christian communities of students and faculty.
summary: InterVarsity Graduate Christian Community's purpose is to encourage and equip graduate students "and faculty" to live as disciples of Jesus, following him as they seek to be a redeeming influence among the people, ideas and structures of the university.

name: Shutterbugs at NC State
description:
Welcome to Shutterbugs at NC State, your own photography club! 📸🐺
Capture the moments that define your Wolfpack experience. Join us for photography adventures, workshops, and a community passionate about visual storytelling. Whether you're a beginner or a seasoned pro, all are welcome to share, learn, and explore the art of photography together. Let's frame memories and freeze time through the lens!

Discord link - https://discord.gg/rNQB5Zsqw

#ncstateshutterbugs
summary:
Welcome to Shutterbugs at NC State, your own photography club! 📸🐺
Capture the moments that define your Wolfpack experience.
Join us for photography adventures, workshops, and a community passionate about visual storytelling

name: ASL Club at NC State
summary: Welcome to the ASL Club at NC State, where we celebrate and promote the rich language and culture of American Sign Language! Whether you're a fluent signer or just starting to learn, our club provides a welcoming and inclusive space for all.

name: Quantitative Finance Club
description: Dedicated to the exploration and promotion of opportunities at the intersection of finance and technology  by providing opportunities for education, mentoring, and networking in fields positioned at this intersection.
summary: The Quantitative Finance Club is dedicated to promoting the leadership of individuals interested in the intersection of finance and technology, fostering an inclusive environment that encourages networking, mentoring, and learning opportunities.

name: Artificial Intelligence Club at NC State University
description: The purpose of this student organization is to provide a platform where individuals with an interest in AI come together to share knowledge, collaborate on projects, and discuss advancements in the field. We will provide skill development in the form of workshops and tutorials. The AI club will facilitate networking events where we will invite speakers and attend conferences.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to provide a platform where individuals with an interest in AI come together to share knowledge, collaborate on projects, and discuss advancements in the field.

name: NC State Chapter of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
summary: The NC State Chapter of the NAACP seeks to promote political activism, advance social and economic justice, and to foster a sense of cultural pride and campus ownership amongst minority students on the campus l and surrounding communities.

name: Korean Media Club
description: The purpose of this student organization is to form a community for people who share the common interest of Korean media. This may include things such as K-pop, K-dramas, and more. It will give these students a place to talk about things they are fans of and the chance to form connections with others based on these interests.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to form a community for people who share the common interest of Korean media. This includes things like K-pop, K-dramas, and more. It will give these students a place to build connections based on shared likes.

name: RoboPack at NC State
description:
We are a competition team that participates in the annual Student Design Robotics Competition hosted by the American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers. We build an autonomous robot that performs an agricultural task.

Gain hands on experience with design, assembly, coding, and testing! If you are interested in joining shoot us an email at: baerobopack@ncsu.edu.
summary: We are a competition team that designs, assembles, and tests a robot suited to perform an agricultural task for the Student Design Robotics Competition hosted by the American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers.

name: Ukrainian Association at NC State
description:
The Ukrainian Association at NC State (UA@NC) is a vibrant and inclusive student organization dedicated to celebrating and promoting Ukrainian culture, heritage, and traditions within our university community. As a dynamic group of students, we aim to foster a sense of belonging and pride among Ukrainian students while also welcoming those interested in learning more about Ukrainian culture.

At UANC, we organize a diverse range of events and activities throughout the academic year, including cultural showcases, language exchanges, traditional dance performances, culinary experiences, and educational seminars. Through these initiatives, we provide opportunities for members to connect with one another, share experiences, and deepen their understanding of Ukrainian customs and values.

Our organization serves as a platform for cultural exchange and dialogue, facilitating connections between Ukrainian students and individuals from various backgrounds.

Whether you're Ukrainian or simply interested in learning more about Ukrainian culture, we welcome you to join us at UANC. Together, let's celebrate our rich heritage, build meaningful connections, and create lasting memories on campus and beyond. Dare to discover the beauty and depth of Ukrainian culture with us at the Ukrainian Association at NC State!
summary: UANC celebrates Ukrainian culture through events, activities, and seminars, fostering connections and understanding among students of all backgrounds. Whether Ukrainian or curious, join us to explore, connect, and celebrate Ukrainian heritage at NCState!

name: CVM Office of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
description:
