NC State College of Veterinary Medicine strives to be the premier institution for training and preparing future leaders in veterinary medicine and research sciences. We are committed to excellence. Diversity and inclusion are vital to our goal. We recognize that a diverse community generates diverse thought necessary to successfully create relevant and effective solutions to complex challenges. We choose to be a culture that values diversity, inclusion, respect, empathy and equality for all.

Diversity at the NC State College of Veterinary Medicine is defined as the active incorporation throughout our college of people from various backgrounds, perspectives, thoughts and beliefs. Diversity is not limited to race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age or physical ability but rather represents the wide range of visible and invisible differences and similarities that make each of us unique.
summary: NC State College of Veterinary Medicine strives to be the premier institution for training and preparing future leaders in veterinary medicine and research sciences. We are committed to excellence. Diversity and inclusion are vital to our goal.

name: Imago Dei Church
description: Imago Dei Church is located in North Raleigh. We desire to see lives changed by the Gospel of Jesus Christ through the means of gospel, community, and missions.
summary: We desire to see lives changed by the Gospel

name: Forensic Science Club at NC State
description: The purpose of this student organization is to allow students interested in forensic science to further their passions by creating an environment where they can meet like-minded peers and provide networking opportunities within the professional field.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to allow students interested in forensic science to further their passions by creating an environment where they can meet like-minded peers and provide networking opportunities within the professional field.

name: Supply Chain Club
description: Our mission is to support undergraduate students of the Poole College of Management by offering career guidance, professional support, and educational resources focused on Supply Chain. We accomplish this by facilitating connections with resources, scholarships, contacts, and opportunities, enabling students to achieve their professional aspirations.
summary: The Supply Chain Club provides resources and contacts for business administration majors concentrating in Operations and Supply Chain Management. We hold meetings to gain information about SC oriented companies and set up tours with well-known companies.

name: A Moment of Magic at NC State
description: With empathy and kindness, we provide a safe space for children to engage in meaningful, fun activities that enhance their social-emotional development. Led by NC State student volunteers, our in-person social wellness activities are age-friendly, medically safe, and easy to implement in various settings. In-person experiences are provided directly to families or in partnership with hospitals and health care centers, and nonprofit organizations. Some examples of in-person activities a Moment of Magic includes are engagement in music, literature, art, and play. We are looking for new members to join our team! If you are interested, please fill out this Interest Form. If you have any questions or want to learn more, feel free to email us at ncsu@amomentofmagic.org
summary: A Moment of Magic is a national 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that focuses on providing social wellness activities for vulnerable and underserved children.

name: Boxing Club at NC State
summary: Our goal is to spread the science of boxing to other students on campus, with the potential of letting students compete in collegiate competitions should they wish to.

name: Mu Epsilon Delta
description: Mu Epsilon Delta is a national co-ed pre-health fraternity dedicated to preparing members for successful healthcare careers through the development of scholarship, fraternity, and service. We emphasize excellence in health science, promoting a holistic model of healthcare through cooperation and collaboration across pre-health disciplines, educators, and professionals. Our supportive community empowers future healthcare leaders through networking events, community service projects, workshops, and academic support. We are committed to serving local, national, and global communities, advocating for equal opportunity, and fostering brotherhood to make a significant impact on the Wolfpack community.
summary: Mu Epsilon Delta is a national co-ed pre-health fraternity that prepares members for health careers through service and growth. We promote collaboration across pre-health disciplines to support future healthcare leaders.

name: Student Chapter of Carolina Adapts Toys for Children at NC State
description: CATCH works to provide adapted toys to children with special needs who are unable to play with most commercially manufactured toys. Utilizing engineering, creativity, and innovation, we modify toys to give back to our local community.
summary: CATCH works to provide adapted toys to children with special needs who are unable to play with most commercially manufactured toys. Utilizing engineering, creativity, and innovation, we modify toys to give back to our local community.

name: Latino Association of Computer Science
description: LA/CSC is dedicated to strengthening the presence of Latino and Hispanic students in NC State's computer science department by creating social recognition, giving guidance to incoming and existing students, and developing opportunities for professional growth.
summary: The Latino Association of Computer Science promotes the development of Latino and Hispanic students in Computer Science.

name: Women in the Shop at North Carolina State University
description:
Mission Statement

Women in the Shop’s mission is to create a community that encourages and mentors women in the College of Design to learn technical skills and become confident in the shop.

Goals

WITS’ long-term goal is to increase visibility of women in the shop. We want to support, promote and retain women in design by providing opportunities for technical growth, personal and professional development, social engagement, and outreach.

Plan

With the College of Design’s Material Lab as a resource, we will work on new projects together, visit factories and manufacturing sites, and have workshops on using equipment and new tools.
summary: Women in the Shop's mission is to create a community that encourages and mentors women in the College of Design to learn technical skills and become confident in the shop.

name: Crafting Curations
description: Crafting Curations is a club dedicated to bringing together NC State's student body through arts and craft events. The hope is this will allow students to unwind from the stress of school and get a chance to be creative while meeting new people.
summary: Crafting Curations is a club dedicated to bringing together NC State's student body through arts and craft events. The hope is this will allow students to unwind from the stress of school and get a chance to be creative while meeting new people.

name: AutoHaven Enthusiasts At NC State
summary: Car enthusiast club set to show the car community at NCSU and their passion for cars specific to their own automobiles.

name: Associated General Contractor
description: The Associated General Contractors (AGC) of America is the leading national professional organization representing both public and private commercial construction. To prepare the industry for the future, AGC is dedicated to promoting the construction industry at institutes of higher education through research, conferences, and scholarships. The NCSU student chapter was chartered on April 28, 1930, as AGC’s first student chapter.
summary: The Associated General Contractors is committed to promoting construction, student involvement, academics, philanthropy, and overall student wellbeing. The organization accomplishes its mission through meetings, volunteering, student competitions, etc.

name: BioTech Syndicate Club
description: This student organization aims to cultivate a dynamic network and community for students passionate about biotechnology. It focuses on bridging interdisciplinary gaps, providing volunteer opportunities, participating in hands-on competitions, and hosting poster sessions to showcase innovative projects. Additionally, we organize company visits, events, and professional development workshops to enhance members' resumes and provide career opportunities and guidance. Through these initiatives, we aim to foster collaboration, creativity, and professional growth among our members in the field of biotechnology.
summary: This student organization aims to cultivate a dynamic network and community for students passionate about biotechnology.

name: One Health Club
description: We connect vet students with veterinarians who are pursuing careers in One Health. We alsoalert students of opportunities, scholarships, and potential career options.
summary: CVM focused on introducing veterinary students to topics and careers in One Health.

name: Jawaani at NC State
description: Jawaani is NC State's premier fusion A Cappella group, geared towards providing a supportive community and bringing together a wide range of cultures from different parts of the world in the name of music. Our mission is to create a fun, yet professional atmosphere for singers to come together and immerse in learning/creating fusion music with the ultimate goal of performing at various exhibitions and eventually at competitions.
summary: Jawaani is an A Capella group geared towards bringing together a wide range of cultures from different parts of the world through music. Our mission is to create a fun, professional environment for singers to come together and immerse in creating music.

name: The Alt Protein Project at NC State
description:
APP at NC State is a student organization formed with an aim to create a vibrant ecosystem of alternative protein in and around the campus. The purpose of the organization includes, but is not limited to:

  1. Generating awareness and enthusiasm in the campus regarding alternative protein
  2. Creating an inspiring and inclusive community to explore opportunities around alternative protein
  3. Connecting researchers and students across multiple departments of the university working on alternative protein 
  4. Exploring options to include alternative proteins module in relevant existing courses or create a new course in coordination with university faculty and stakeholders
  5. Forming a strong connect with industry partners to organize guest lectures, conferences and exploring any internship and/or career opportunities
summary: The Alt Protein Project at the NC State is a student chapter of the Alt Protein Project initiative of the Good Food Institute (GFI) and formed with an aim to create a vibrant ecosystem of alternative protein in and around the campus.

name: The Agricultural Communicators Program at NC State
description: Undergraduate students will be involved in a two-year cohort model that offers professional development and exposure to the agriculture industry through the invited guest speakers at bi-weekly meetings and networking events.
summary: Training and equipping undergraduate students to communicate complex agricultural, food, and life science issues to the public.

name: Ethnobiology Club at NC State University
description: The Ethnobiology Club at NCSU aims to bring together the student body across disciplines to better understand biocultural diversity, how society interacts with the ethnoecological relationships of the world, and to promote the research, education, and training within ethnobiology.
summary: The Ethnobiology Club aims to bring together the student body across disciplines to better understand biocultural diversity, how society interacts with the ethnoecological relationships of the world, and to promote the research, education, and training.

name: ISSA Chapter at NC State
summary: ISSA is the Community of choice for International Cybersecurity Professionals dedicated to advancing individual growth, managing technology risk and protecting critical information and infrastructure.

name: Competitive Bowling Club at NC State
description: This organization is designed to be a way for bowlers at NC State to compete against the top bowling schools in the country while improving their skills in a team setting. This club helps build friendships through the social and competitive sport of bowling. This team is a competitive entity, however, and strives to build success on and off the lanes for members.
summary: This is an organization that is compiled of the best competitive bowlers enrolled at NC State. It serves as an  opportunity to compete against other schools at a high level.

name: Student Conduct Board at NC State
description: The mission of the NC State Student Conduct Board is to promote and adhere to our six principles: accountability, compassion, education, fairness, integrity, and restorative efforts. The Student Conduct Board seeks to create a safe, respectful, and inclusive campus community. Board members educate this community on policies and expectations, while also adjudicating cases involving suspension and expulsion related to Academic and Non-Academic misconduct.
summary: The NC State Student Conduct Board is an organization that seeks to create a safe, respectful, and inclusive campus community by upholding university and department values. The Board adjudicates conduct violations on behalf of the NC State community.

name: Neuroscience Club at NC State
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to promote interest in neuroscience at North Carolina State University, inform career and involvement experiences within the field, and provide opportunities to build relationships with faculty and students.

name: Veterinary Artists Alliance
description: Our mission is to foster empathy, promote ethical reflection, explore cultural perspectives, advocate for well-being, and serve the community. Through discussions, workshops, and outreach initiatives, we aim to cultivate compassionate, well-rounded veterinarians who prioritize the welfare of animals and humans alike.
summary: The VAA Club at NCSU College of Veterinary Medicine is dedicated to integrating the humanities into the veterinary education experience.

name: Students Leading Collaborative Computing Education
description:
Students Leading Collaborative Computing Education (SLCCE) is a volunteer organization dedicated to creating opportunities for adolescent students through engagement and experiential learning with Computer Science (CSC) and Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE).

SLCCE members engage with middle and high school students through CSC and ECE-centric hands-on projects, workshops, and community outreach activities. SLCCE creates opportunities for students and organization members to enhance their technical knowledge, professional skills, and technical portfolio.

Interested? Apply today.
summary: Students Leading Collaborative Computing Education (SLCCE) is a volunteer organization dedicated to creating opportunities for adolescent students through engagement and experiential learning with Computer Science and Electrical and Computer Engineering.

name: Black & Latino Biomedical Engineering Community
description: N/A
summary:
This community is dedicated to building interactions between Black and Latino undergraduate and graduate Biomedical Engineering (BME) students. 

For community communication, please join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/67449565/j7nHX0ym

name: Botewa
description: Betewa Dance Team, is a branch of ASU that focuses on blending modern and traditional dancing styles of every part of AFRICA.
summary: Botewa is an Afro/Caribbean dance team

name: Poole Women in Finance and Investing
description:
Empower. Lead. Innovate. Achieve.

Poole Women in Finance and Investing is dedicated to empowering women in the finance industry through camaraderie, networking, and education. PWIFI aims to provide a supportive environment where members can connect, share knowledge, and grow together. We focus on building camaraderie by fostering a strong sense of community and support among members, creating lasting relationships and professional connections. Through networking opportunities, we connect women across the finance and investing sectors to expand professional networks and discover new career opportunities. Our club offers workshops, seminars and hands-on activities to develop marketable skills essential for success in the finance industry. We provide comprehensive learning materials and expert-led discussions to explore financial theories, formulas, and strategies. Additionally, we guide members in pursuing financial designations and certifications, enhancing credentials, and advancing careers.

The goals of PooleWIFI are as follows:
1. Cultivate a group of driven women who have a passion for finance and career development
2. Build credibility and knowledge with fundamentals 
3. Create network between exterior professionals and group members
4. Learn from other club members, advisors, and guest speakers to gain new perspectives and understanding
summary:
Poole Women in Finance and Investing is dedicated to empowering women in the finance 
industry through camaraderie, networking, and education.

name: Queer Educator's Alliance
description:
The purpose of this student organization is to provide an inclusive community dedicated to fostering meaningful discussions, sharing experiences, and creating a sense of belonging for Queer-identifying education majors at NC State. We believe in celebrating diversity and embracing individuals from all walks of life. Queer Ed is more than just a club; it's a vibrant community where we deeply care about educational equity and strive to support students of all creeds. Whether you identify as Queer or not, joining this community will help you understand more about supporting Queer individuals and more about your own identity. We aim to teach future Queer educators how to work with their future students and engage in productive dialogue around Queer issues.

We look to successfully implement this through events including but not limited to: social events, mentorship opportunities, volunteer opportunities with local LGBTQ+ organizations, intentional professional development, educational workshops, and more. Our organization works with faculty and staff to offer faculty meetings, networking opportunities, and support.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to provide an inclusive community dedicated to fostering meaningful discussions, sharing experiences, and creating a sense of belonging for Queer-identifying education majors at NC State.

name: Council of Global Health Scholars Chapter at NC State
description:
We at the Council of Global Health Scholars are a collection of doctors, community builders and problem solvers dedicated to building the next generation of healthcare professionals through service, community, and invention.

Our team has spent over 20 years building health infrastructure around the world, training thousands of doctors and allied health professionals, and developing curricula that we know to differentiate those who seek to be among the best. 

Unlike some honor societies, we do not wish to merely honor those with outstanding academics – but rather, outstanding students who are able to demonstrate an ability to put their passion, inventiveness, and resourcefulness to work, in order to make an impact. Our experience guiding countless students through successful matriculation into medical schools around the country has given us a keen sense of what differentiates an applicant. Our experience working closely with executives at the highest levels of the worlds largest companies has given us a keen sense of what makes for a leader, and we seek to bring that to our Fellows.

The heart of our organization lies in our Fellowship program. Each year, we select a cohort of dedicated students who demonstrate exceptional promise and passion for healthcare innovation and service. These Fellows are entrusted with the responsibility to design and develop healthcare innovations that address pressing needs in communities around the world.

As a Fellow of CGHS, you gain unique access to resources, mentorship, and support to turn your ideas into tangible solutions. Through hands-on experience and collaboration with like-minded peers, you’ll have the chance to improve health outcomes for underserved populations around the world.
summary: The Council of Global Health Scholars strives to make long lasting sustainable innovations to help the betterment of the global health system. This organization provides opportunities for students to widen their knowledge on global health.

name: Girl’s Anime and Gaming Club
description: The Girl’s Anime and Gaming Club is dedicated to creating a safe and welcoming environment for girls who share a passion for anime, gaming, and similar hobbies. Our mission is to provide a space where members can freely enjoy their hobbies, do activities and host events together, and connect with like-minded individuals.
summary: A place for girls and others to feel safe and nerd out

name: Lutheran Campus Ministry-Raleigh.
summary:
The purpose of Lutheran Campus Ministry-Raleigh is to conduct, support, and interpret a Lutheran
Ministry in higher education. This ministry seeks to assist people in academic settings throughout
Raleigh, and especially at NCSU.

name: Frigid Sound
description: This is a group of musicians that will rehearse and perform at IcePack hockey games! We play stand tunes between puck drops as well as some during intermission.
summary: This is a group of musicians that will rehearse and perform at IcePack hockey games! We play stand tunes between puck drops as well as some during intermission.

name: Health Link Society at NC State
Professional Engineers of North Carolina
description: The NCSU PENC organization aims to provide information and resources to students that want to take the P.E. exam. We are partnered with the PENC Triangle Chapter to allow members to connect with and form mentorships with professionals in their perspective fields.
summary: A college chapter of the professional organization, Professional Engineers of North Carolina (PENC), this organization serves to inform member about the P.E. and connect them with professionals in their perspective field.

name: Student Chapter of the Association of Shelter Veterinarians at NC State CVM
summary: SCASV is a veterinary student organization created to promote the importance of the specialty of shelter medicine within the veterinary field and to raise student awareness of issues pertaining to homeless animals and pet overpopulation.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances - Veterinary Outreach at NC State
description:
VAW Global Veterinary interns work closely with community partners to implement veterinary clinics, as well as provide assistance to existing animal shelters, across low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). In the Veterinary Outreach program, interns learn about healthcare in a global context. Through shadowing local veterinarians, interns will also learn about veterinary procedures, treatment courses, clinic operations, vaccinations, and deworming.

Interns will prepare for their trip throughout the year by attending team training sessions, participating in local community service projects, and developing team-building activities, led by their Team Leader.
summary: The VAW Global Veterinary Outreach Program allows student interns to learn about healthcare in a global context. Through shadowing local veterinarians, interns will also learn about veterinary procedures, treatment courses, and clinic operations.

name: Actuarial Science Club at NC State
description:
The purpose of the NC State Actuarial Science Club is to spread important information regarding the career of an actuary through the lens of both the Casualty Actuarial Society (CAS) or the Society of Actuaries (SOA). Potential events of the club include, but are not limited to:

  1. a) Group study sessions for actuarial examinations;
  2. b) Online or in-person workshops by those currently in an actuarial role in their current employment;
  3. c) Resume building and mock interview opportunities.
summary: The purpose of the NC State Actuarial Science Club is to spread important information regarding the career of an actuary through the lens of both the Casualty Actuarial Society (CAS) or the Society of Actuaries (SOA).

name: Student Leadership Council
description:
SLC represents the student body of the FREEDM Systems Center. Its mission is to:
           ● Communicate with the FREEDM staff and faculty on behalf of students.
           ● Encourage faculty-student interaction.
           ● Facilitate student-initiated academic activities.
           ● Provide a forum for student opinions in all matters of mutual interest to faculty and students.
           ● Promote professional interest and fellowship among current students.
           ● Create a bridge between the current students and the alumni.
           ● Foster industrial connections and outreach programs.
summary: FREEDM SLC seeks to represent the undergraduate and graduate students at the FREEDM Systems Center in the Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering at North Carolina State University.

name: Beta Gamma Sigma Chapter at NC State
summary: Beta Gamma Sigma is an international business honor society with over 600 collegiate and 50 alumni chapters in the US and abroad.  BGS Global Headquarters is in St. Louis, Missouri. The NC State Chapter of Beta Gamma Sigma was formed in 2001.

name: Alpha Psi Omega (Psi Kappa Cast)
description: The services we provide to University Theatre include program and patron support for auditions, performances, and social events. Principle social events are the annual Winter Social and end-of-year Hammy's Award Show. Membership involves commitment to regular service throughout the academic year from load-in and strike to hosting concessions to planning events.
summary: A theatre honors society with the object of promoting respect for, providing service to, and advancing the performing arts at North Carolina State University.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances - Global Health at NC State
summary: VAW Global Health Alliances is a national organization that aims to bring medical care, dental care, and health education to communities around the world, as well as give students the opportunity to work closely with professionals to develop their skill.

name: Students for the Advancement of Global One Health
description: The purpose of this student organization is to foster interdisciplinary education and action amongst students and the surrounding community aligning with Global One Health principles. Global One Health is defined by the World Health Organization as "an integrated, unifying approach that aims to sustainably balance and optimize the health of people, animals, and ecosystems.” Global One Health aims to solve many of the world's most pressing problems through the collaboration between peoples from all walks of life looking to make a difference, no matter how big or small. Change starts with us, and at NC State, the the Students for the Advancement of Global One Health gathers like minded individuals to start conversations about these problems and come up with real solutions focused on key One Health issues, including addressing climate change and health disparities, driving food and water solutions, and combating infectious diseases. This organization will engage with these issues through regular club meetings, external events, and collaboration with community members.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to foster interdisciplinary education and action amongst students and the surrounding community aligning with Global One Health principles: "To optimize the health of people, animals, and ecosystems".

name: Bridging the Mind
summary: This organization focuses on serving individuals and families of individuals with severe mental disorders. We aim to educate ourselves and the community on these disorders and direct people to resources such as counseling, therapy, education, diagnosis.

name: Sales Club at NC State
summary: The Sales Club at NC State aims to foster a dynamic community of students interested in the field of sales and business development.

name: Black Psychology Club
description:
Our goal is to create a space where Black psychology and social work majors or minors can find support and community. We hope to hold engaging conversations surrounding the psychology field that can inspire. We also plan to hold events where our members can network, mindfulness meetings where people can come and color or paint, and much more. We believe that it is important to create a community that can support and uplift one another

Our Instagram is @blackpsychologyclub

Interest form
summary: Black Psychology Club aims to create as safe and collaborative space for black students majoring in  psychology and or social work at NC State.

name: Skate Club of NC State
summary: A club for those interested in skateboarding of all forms at North Carolina State University, used as a meeting place for those invested in a common interest.

name: Cultura Sin Fronteras at NC State
description: The purpose of Cultura Sin Fronteras is to celebrate the diversity of Hispanic cultures from all regions through inclusive cultural events and volunteer opportunities. While focused on promoting Hispanic culture, our organization is open to all, embracing and highlighting various cultural backgrounds' rich traditions, experiences, and perspectives. We aim to foster community engagement, raise awareness about different cultural identities, and provide opportunities for positive social impact through community service.
summary: An organization that celebrates the diversity of Hispanic cultures from all regions through inclusive events and volunteer opportunities that foster community engagement and embrace traditions from all backgrounds.

name: Packonomics at NC State
description:
Packonomics is committed to supporting students interested in

economics. Our mission is to connect economic principles with real-world applications by

offering group discussions, guest lectures, and events designed to foster networking,

critical thinking, and leadership skills among students. 

 

Join the GroupMe to stay in the loop on club events!

GroupMe
summary: Packonomics creates opportunities for economics students to explore economics beyond the classroom and make connections.

name: Global Affairs Association at NC State University
description: NCSUGAA engages in Model UN as well as offers members opportunities to get involved with the Carolina International Relations Association at UNC-Chapel Hill, including the opportunity to contribute to UNC’s Journal of Foreign Affairs and participate in programs such as MoMUNtum. Members learn about global affairs through the unique combination of JFAC and Model UN. This includes learning about the United Nations system and its current events as well as debate skills critical for a future in diplomacy.
summary: NCSUGAA engages in Model UN as well as offers members opportunities to get involved with the Carolina International Relations Association (CIRA) at UNC-Chapel Hill, including contributions to UNC's Journal of Foreign Affairs and programs such as MoMUNtum

name: Revolution Campus Ministry
description:
Our campus ministry's focus is to restore first-century Christianity in this generation. We do this in a multitude of ways, ranging from personal Bible studies, to small 15-minute group discussions, to full-on Sunday church services. We operate alongside our mother organization Restored Church Raleigh to try to show as many people as we can the scriptures and help them make the Bible the standard of their life!

"All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training in righteousness, so that the servant of God may be thoroughly equipped for every good work." - 2 Timothy 3:16-17 NIV
summary: We are a campus ministry that teaches the Bible as the standard for our lives! We have weekly events that range from personal Bible studies to large group events!

name: The Product Club at NC State
description:
The Product Club at NC State (TPC) is a student-driven community designed to empower aspiring product leaders with the skills and experience needed to thrive in product management and innovation. TPC focuses on bridging the gap between theory and practice by offering workshops, hands-on projects, and networking opportunities in areas like product marketing, design, development, and sales.

With a special emphasis on emerging technologies such as AI, XR, IoT, and Blockchain, TPC provides a collaborative environment where students can innovate, scale ideas, and build the expertise to lead in the tech-driven world.
summary: The Product Club at NC State (TPC) is a vibrant student organization dedicated to equipping future leaders with the skills and knowledge to excel in product management and related fields. TPC provides a platform for hands-on experience and learning.

name: The Last Reformers
description:
Weekly Meetings: Monday 7:00pm ~ 8:00pm 

Our Primary meeting room for the semester will be Cox 204 but it may differ depending on the week! Get in contact with us to stay updated!

Looking into studying something specifically? This form allows you to pick a topic of your choice in whatever time or environment is best for you!

https://forms.gle/QJGdznkxXaAZP4UM6 

 

Full Summary of the Club

This is a Non-Denominational Bible Study Club open to anyone! Our aim to focus on aspects of the Bible that are less understood such as the New Covenant and the prophecies that connect the Old and New Testaments into one perfect message (Luke 24:27). By fully understanding and following the teachings of Christ, we aim to reform our character and live in step with God's teachings.

As Jesus described His people as the salt and light (Matthew 5:13-16), we strive to reflect His teachings on our campus.

To do this, we promote:

  • Individual Bible studies
  • Weekly group fellowships and meetings
  • Meaningful volunteer work and service opportunities

Through studying, practicing, and sharing God’s words, we hope to grow spiritually and positively impact our community.
summary: We develop an accurate understanding of prophecies and teachings within the Bible, using various verses and history itself in order to understand and follow biblical teachings that some may be unaware of while serving many communities.

name: Brain Computer Interface Club
description: We strive to develop technical skills through brain-computer interface projects. Such skills include Python and Arduino C++.
summary:
The BCI Club aims to foster the current generation of engineers to restore autonomy for motor-impaired patients. 

https://linktr.ee/ncsu_bci

name: Student Energy Club
description:
The NC State Student Energy Club is a student-led organization dedicated to advancing innovative solutions and collaboration toward a clean energy future. We focus on connecting students across diverse departments, fostering hands-on experience through projects, and building networks with industry leaders and academic experts. Through events like Energy Week, symposiums, and forums, we advocate for and highlight energy research across campus while creating opportunities for students to learn from guest speakers and energy professionals. Our initiatives include hosting weekly planning meetings, organizing recruitment events, publishing a monthly newsletter featuring interviews and job opportunities, and collaborating with energy clubs at other universities. We aim to develop essential skills in communication, task management, and writing, all while promoting a united effort to address today’s critical energy challenges.

Follow us on our social media and check out our latest content!
Website: ncsuenergyclub.org
Contact: studentenergy@ncsu.edu
Instagram: @studentenergyclub
Substack: substack.com/@studentenergyncsu
summary: We are a student-led energy club focused on advancing ideas for a clean energy system. Our mission is to connect NC State's energy community, uniting path-breaking innovations across campus to address today’s critical energy challenges.

name: Beyond the Pack
description:
Beyond the Pack is North Carolina State University’s first digital-only, student-led independent news organization, providing coverage on issues that extend beyond the University’s immediate scope.

Founded in the fall of 2024, the organization aims to foster creativity within journalism, encouraging diverse voices and perspectives and exploration within the field. 

Visit our website to learn more about ways to get involved!
summary: Beyond the Pack is North Carolina State University’s first digital-only, student-led independent news organization, providing coverage on issues that extend beyond the University’s immediate scope.

name: National Marrow Donor Program
description: The National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP) is a non-profit organization dedicated to saving lives through cellular therapy. It connects patients with leukemia, lymphoma, and other life-threatening blood disorders to volunteer bone marrow and stem cell donors. By increasing awareness and recruiting donors, the NMDP ensures more patients can find matches for life-saving transplants. A school chapter program would allow students to actively support the NMDP's mission by spreading awareness about the need for donors, hosting donor recruitment drives, and educating peers about the life-saving impact of bone marrow transplants. Through these efforts, students can make a real difference in their communities and help give patients a second chance at life.
summary: The National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP) is a non-profit that facilitates bone marrow and hematopoietic cell transplants from volunteer donors to treat leukemia and other life-threatening blood diseases.

name: CrossPoint Fellowship
summary:
The purpose of this student organization is to provide a weekly or biweekly Christian fellowship meeting for students on North Carolina State University Campus. 

Email: crosspointncsu@gmail.com

name: North Carolina Speech and Debate Association
description: The NC State Speech and Debate Association combines a team of 10 students with other club members to create a community of students with shared interests. The team is focused on competing, while club members focus on developing rhetorical and analytical skills through campus events and collaborations with other campus organizations.
summary: The purpose of NC State SADA is to provide a welcoming community for NC State students to cultivate their rhetorical and public speaking skills, while engaging in friendly competition both within and outside of the University.

name: Operation Monarch
summary: A community support organization that is a collaborative force between other organizations and objectives that aims to help the NC State community and students at large, from fighting food insecurity to advocacy and awareness activities, we are there.

name: Young Forwardists at NC State
description:
Are you upset about gerrymandering? Do you think that American politics is dysfunctional?

The Young Forwardists at NC State are a chapter of the Forward Party's youth committee. Together, we advocate for our generation and the issues that truly matter to us. 
Founded by former presidential candidate Andrew Yang and former Governor Christine Todd Whitman, the Forward Party is a new political party founded on the hope of giving power back to the American voter. The Forward Party will ensure that politicians adequately and transparently represent their community. 

The Young Forwardists at NC State will work closely with the North Carolina Forward Party to engage with the community, increase awareness on/near college campuses, and increase youth involvement in politics. 

Use this link to hear from some of the North Carolina Forward Party's members on why the Forward Party is important.
summary: NC State's chapter of the Forward Party's youth committee. The Forward Party is a new movement and political party built on ensuring that politicians represent their community's interest. We will put the power back in the hands of the American voter.

name: Collegians for Christ at NC State
description: Collegians for Christ at NC State provides an extracurricular activity to those students and/or faculty interested in a more in-depth study of the Bible in a Christian environment that welcomes discussion and questions. Collegians for Christ does not focus on a worship service but rather a relaxed Bible study setting where students can engage deeply in studying the Bible. Collegians for Christ provides opportunities for students to participate in Christian growth in the areas of study, prayer, fellowship, witness, and missions.
summary: Collegians for Christ at NC State offers a welcoming environment for students and faculty to engage in Bible study, discussions, and questions. The group focuses on study, prayer, fellowship, and witnessing, without formal worship services.

name: Remote Area Medical Student Chapter at NC State University
description: Our main purpose is to help RAM provide FREE medical, dental, and vision care, in communities where access to healthcare is limited or non-existent by helping set up, operate, and break down pop-up clinics. This will allow NCSU undergraduate students interested in healthcare fields to be exposed to common procedures that could change the lives of patients and network with professors and students of schools that volunteer at these clinics. Follow us on Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/ramncsu?igsh=MTZiZjdub2QwNHc3Zg==) and join our GroupMe (https://groupme.com/join_group/105165099/qKYcjb09) to stay up to date on upcoming clinics, meetings, workshops, panels, fundraising events, and more!
summary: Our main purpose is to help RAM provide FREE medical, dental, and vision care, in communities where access to healthcare is limited or non-existent by helping set up, operate, and break down pop-up clinics.

name: Poole Undergraduate Student Hub Advocates
description:
The Poole Undergraduate Student Hub Advocates (P.U.S.H.) at NC State is a self-governed student organization dedicated to fostering an inclusive and supportive community within the Poole College of Management. By celebrating cultural diversity and empowering students to embrace their differences, P.U.S.H. aims to create a welcoming space where students feel supported, build connections, and find a sense of belonging.

Advocates actively engage with peers in the Student Hub, located on the first floor of Nelson Hall, through meaningful interactions, event support, and advocacy efforts. In collaboration with the Assistant Director of Student Affairs and Advocacy, P.U.S.H. members organize events, maintain a safe and clean environment, and provide resources to promote equity and community-building. The organization is committed to professionalism, inclusivity, and personal and collective growth, guided by principles of nondiscrimination and respect for all.
summary: PUSH Advocates foster inclusiveness in PCOM by celebrating diversity and empowering students to embrace differences. Through advocacy and engagement, we create a supportive hub where students feel welcome, build connections, and find belonging.

name: Fiber Society Student Chapter at NC State
description:
The NC State Student Chapter of Fiber Society (NCSFS) serves as a platform for students passionate about fiber science, engineering, and technology to connect, collaborate, and explore their potential. It embodies the broader mission of the Fiber Society by uniting individuals who share a common interest in advancing fiber science and technology. The chapter welcomes students from diverse scientific, engineering, and design backgrounds for the purpose of fostering interdisciplinary research and innovation in fiber and fiber-related work. Moreover, the chapter aims to provide a platform for academic and professional growth through networking, mentorship, and peer collaborative opportunities. Broadly, the function of NCSFS can be acronymized as REC.

 

  • Research: provide practical tools for effective research
  • Empower: career readiness and personal growth
  • Collaborate: hassle-free and inclusive nature of support
summary: NCSFS promotes the advancement of fiber science by offering resources and opportunities that foster learning, collaboration, and innovation among its members.

name: Pack Global Case Club
description: The purpose of Pack Global Case Club is to provide a dedicated platform for students to further develop their case-solving abilities in a collaborative, competitive, and growth-oriented environment. The club creates structured opportunities for students to sharpen their analytical, presentation, and strategic thinking skills through consistent practice, workshops, and peer feedback, preparing them to compete with confidence on national and global stages.
summary: The purpose of Pack Global Case Club is to provide a dedicated platform for students to further develop their case-solving abilities in a collaborative, competitive, and growth-oriented environment.

name: Design Council at NC State College of Design
description: We are a student-led organization dedicated to representing and advocating for all students within the College of Design. Our mission is to serve as a bridge between students, faculty, and staff, ensuring that student voices are heard in discussions that impact their academic experience. Beyond advocacy, we strive to build a strong, inclusive community by organizing events that bring students together, foster collaboration, and celebrate creativity. Through our initiatives, we aim to enhance the student experience, create meaningful connections, and support a dynamic and inspiring learning environment for all.
summary: We are a student-led organization that advocates for all College of Design students to faculty and staff while fostering community through events.

name: Environmental Justice Club at NC State
description: Environmental Justice means that vulnerable communities should not have to be subjected to disproportionate amounts of pollution or environmental hazards. Our organization believes in the 17 Principles of Environmental Justice adopted by the First National People of Color Leadership Summit in 1991. Specifically, we believe that the Earth is sacred and must be protected, land use should be ethical, people should not be exposed to toxic chemicals, all workers deserve a safe workplace, indigenous self-determination, opposition to corporate destruction and opposition to military occupation.
summary: EJC works on and off campus to spread ideals of environmental justice. We believe that EJ can be addressed in all aspects of society. We work to educate people about EJ and advocate for underserved communities in the process.

name: National Wild Turkey Federation at NC State
description:
We are a student chapter of the National Wild Turkey Federation. The NWTF is a national nonprofit conservation and hunting organization that has worked for the conservation of the wild turkey and the preservation of our hunting heritage.

 

Our purpose is to promote the wise conservation and management of upland habitat and wildlife both in North Carolina and across North America. Furthermore, the NWTF Chapter at NC State will actively support conservation through fundraising, education, and introducing others to outdoor activities that support wildlife management.
summary: We are a student chapter of the National Wild Turkey Federation. The NWTF is a national nonprofit conservation and hunting organization that has worked for the conservation of the wild turkey and the preservation of our hunting heritage.

name: ORaCEL Social Association
email: we do not have an email address yet, but vice president is bvassem@ncsu.edu
description:
ORaCel Social Association is, founded to improve the well-being and interactions of the members. The official goal of this organization is to create regular social and networking events for the ORaCEL community in order to boost inter-group and inter-departmental communication, foster cross-discipline friendships, and provide an outlet for the stress of research.

What OSO does
- Provide a voice for its members to solve problems facing our members
- Organizing social and cultural events. To improve the well-being of its members, foster communication
  and improves cultural awareness.
- Provides forms to improve communication of it’s members
- Hosting workshops to share information among the members.
summary: ORaCel Social Association is founded to improve the well-being and interactions of the members. By creating regular social and networking events for the ORaCEL community.

name: Philosophy of Law Club
description:
Upcoming Fall 2025 Schedule:

Oct 27, 2025 6:00 PM - 7:30 PM Talley Student Union, 3285 
Nov 13, 2025 6:00 PM - 7:30 PM Talley Student Union, 3210

Follow us on Instagram! @ncsuphilosophyoflaw

Join our GroupMe or email ncsuphilosophyoflawclub@gmail.com for more information!

The Philosophy of Law Club provides a platform for NC State students to critically engage with legal philosophy, ethics, and justice. The club fosters intellectual discussions, hosts guest speakers, and creates opportunities for members to explore the intersection of law and philosophy.

For our up-to-date calendar, click here: Google Calendar Link
summary: Student-led organization dedicated to exploring legal philosophy, justice, and ethics through discussions, guest speakers, and events.

name: Premier MCAT Prep
description:
Premier MCAT Prep is a student-driven tutoring service dedicated to helping college students excel on the MCAT through collaboration, proven strategies, and personalized support. Our in-person sessions at NC State foster an interactive learning environment where students can engage in discussions, share insights, and tackle challenging concepts together. By incorporating structured study plans and test-taking techniques used by top scorers, we aim to make MCAT prep more effective, approachable, and less overwhelming.

 

Beyond in-person sessions, we extend our reach through YouTube, offering free video resources that break down key topics and share high-impact strategies. By partnering with experienced peers and campus organizations, we create a community of motivated pre-med students working toward a common goal. Whether through live discussions or online content, Premier MCAT Prep is committed to providing the guidance and support students need to maximize their potential and achieve success on test day.
summary: Student-driven tutoring service that offers interactive in-person sessions and free online resources to help students master the MCAT with proven strategies. We make MCAT prep more effective, approachable, and supportive through community collaboration.

name: Psychology Peer Mentors at NC State
description:
Our Peer Mentors are current Psychology students who understand what it's like to navigate college life. They also offer guidance, advice, and support in various areas through one-on-one student meetings and networking events. 

Here’s how the Peer Mentors can assist students:

  • Pack Planners & Registration Support: Need help navigating your Pack Planner or figuring out course registration? Our Peer Mentors can walk you through the process and offer tips.
  • Student Life Questions: From campus events to student organizations, your Peer Mentors are a great resource for anything related to student life.
  • Course Recommendations: Wondering which courses to take next? Peer Mentors can share their personal experiences and offer insights into what courses they found valuable.
  • Networking & Student Perspectives: Peer Mentors are also available to help you make connections and provide a student’s perspective on your college journey.

It’s important to note that Peer Mentors are not a replacement for an academic advisor. They are fellow students who can offer guidance from their own experiences. They are here to provide a unique student perspective and be another resource for students as they navigate their time at NCSU.
summary: Our Peer Mentors are current Psychology students who understand what it's like to navigate college life. They also offer guidance, advice, and support in various areas through one-on-one student meetings and networking events.

name: The Scholars Network at NC State
description:
The mission of The Scholars Network at NC State is to foster a sense of community for Black and/or African American Students within the College of Social Sciences and Humanities, to contribute to their professional and scholarly development. The Scholars Network seeks to support its members in three ways: 

Career and Professional Development:  Hosting Career and Professional Development events such as career panels, professional development retreats, interview preparation, and resume workshops. 

Community: Providing students a safe space to connect with like-minded individuals on campus.

Scholarship: Providing academic support for individuals, recognizing their academic achievements, and connecting them with high impact experiences.
summary: The Scholars Network at NC State seeks to foster a sense of community for Black/African-American Students within the College of Social Sciences and Humanities, and to contribute to their professional and scholarly development.

name: Whitewater Club at NC State
summary: Student Organization with the goal of connecting NC State students to the world of whitewater paddling. Whether Rafting, kayaking, or canoeing, the club will provide opportunities to practice skills travel to rivers to explore them.

name: Wuffle Ball Club
description: In recent years, everyone's favorite childhood game of Wiffle Ball has been taken to new heights thanks to leagues that originated on social media, like MLW (Major League Wiffleball). The scaled down and fast-paced version of baseball has even allowed the league to host games in sections of football arenas. "Wuffle Ball," or NC State's version of Wiffle Ball, is a club for physically active students looking to compete in a fun, family friendly environment here on campus. The club will follow the same rules as the popular leagues on social media and feature several teams, all vying for a postseason trophy.
summary: A Wiffle Ball Club for physically active NC State Students.

name: Women's Coalition
description: https://www.canva.com/design/DAGS6wb0B1w/JHzIxqir8JePZf_TynIjPQ/view?utm_content=DAGS6wb0B1w&utm_campaign=designshare&utm_medium=link2&utm_source=uniquelinks&utlId=hc11304fa16
summary: We are an organization focused on community outreach, sisterhood, and service. We aim to create a safe space for women to discuss women's issues and the female experience.

name: Experimental NanoSatellite Club
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to build interest in satellites at North Carolina State University through the design and production of a cube-satellite for potential launch into low-Earth orbit. Our goal is to engage students in aerospace engineering and foster skills critical to careers in space technology. Building a legacy of NCSU satellite production starts with PackSat.
summary: The Experimental Nanosatellite Club at NC State, known as PackSat, is dedicated to developing practical, hands-on experience in designing, building, and launching cube-satellite missions.

name: Womens Club Wrestling
description: This club gives an opportunity and environment for girls to come together and learn one of the most ancient sports in a supportive, fun, and hardworking environment and team. For most beginners we will begin with folkstyle wrestling, a more prevalent style in North America and easier to pick up, before we transition into freestyle wrestling, the professional and international style of wrestling, where intermediate and advanced wrestlers will be at. We are all working to learn and grow here, so come with a professional and determined mindset. We have a lot coming up so be ready to shoot right in!
summary: This club offers a supportive and hardworking space for girls to learn wrestling, starting with folkstyle for beginners and freestyle for advanced levels.Members are expected to come with a determined mindset as the team grows, and exciting things ahead!

name: Clash of Clans Club
description: Clash of Clans is a popular social and strategy game where people join together as a clan to compete against other clans and engage in various activities. Clash of Clans Club at NC State organizes meetings, events, lessons, and more to bring together all Clash enthusiasts and grow as a community. We are also in the process of initiating an intercollegiate league.
summary: Clash of Clans Club at NC State organizes meetings, events, and more. We bring together all Clash enthusiasts to grow as players and as a community.

name: Exercise is Medicine Club at NC State
description: The Exercise is Medicine Club works to encourage healthy habits in the NC State community through exercise education, activities, and community. The student-led club promotes health education accessibility among NC State students, including guest speakers, group fitness events, and a walk/run 5k every semester.
summary: Exercise is Medicine Club at NC State is a student-led club with the goal of educating and engaging the NC State community in positive exercise and healthy habits.

name: International Relations Club
description: Our world today faces global challenges that require global-minded citizens to address. The globe has entered a "power balancing" stage, where competition between rival superpowers has shattered the post-Cold War global order. NC State is lucky enough to have academic programs, students and faculty dedicated to solving this crucial issue. We seek to gain a greater understanding of these issues and to discuss them in depth. We also hope to grow various networking opportunities, with faculty and IR professionals alike. Students can join the club by following us on Instagram @ncstateIR or by signing up for our email list. Our primary contacts are ncstateIR@gmail.com  or hkafle@ncsu.edu
summary: Club dedicated to fostering understanding of international relations and conflict. Focuses on an academic approach to this issue, including the larger historical questions at play. Hosts networking events with those in International Relations Community.

name: Ghana Students' Association at NC State
description:
• Integration
    • Hosting welcome events and cultural orientations for incoming Ghanaian students.
    • Building bridges between Ghanaian affiliates and the wider NC State community.

  • Interaction
    • Organizing social, academic, and cultural activities to deepen cross-cultural exchange.
    • Creation of networking opportunities with campus groups, faculty, and alumni.

  • Mentorship
    • Pairing prospective and new students with experienced mentors for academic and personal support.
    • Offering workshops and resources on navigating university life and career development.
summary: Fostering a vibrant Ghanaian community at NC State by facilitating integration, encouraging interaction, and providing mentorship for prospective and current students, alumni, and all campus members with an interest in Ghanaian culture and experiences.

name: Women in Political Science
description: Women in Political Science is committed to fostering an inclusive and empowering environment where individuals of all gender identities can build confidence, strengthen their leadership skills, and develop lasting social connections within the political science community. Our long-term vision is to create a network of supportive peers that encourages women to pursue academic and professional opportunities in politics and public service with self-assurance and resilience.
summary: \Women in Political Science Club aims to provide an empowering and supportive social space for students interested in political science, government, and women/gender minorities’ place within these fields.

name: Pickup Soccer Club
description:
The Pickup Soccer Club is a way for students to connect and come together to play soccer. From small casual games to 11v11 competitive games, anyone can show up to play.

Joining is as easy as joining the club GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/109155002/GoCRQQ8Q
summary: The Pickup Soccer Club is a way for students to connect and come together to play soccer. From small casual games to 11v11 competitive games, anyone can show up to play. Join the GroupMe to find a game: https://groupme.com/join_group/109155002/GoCRQQ8Q

name: Eco Design Outreach Club
description: Eco Design Outreach Club aims to unite students from diverse fields of study to collaborate on projects that promote sustainability across campus. This club encourages eco-conscious design thinking and interdisciplinary teamwork. Through hands-on projects, workshops, lectures, and field trips, members will have opportunities to connect, think critically, and creatively to explore sustainable solutions.
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to bring together students from diverse areas of study to learn and collaborate. Our goal is to create projects that are focused on sustainable design to improve areas on campus and provide hands-on learning

name: NCPMI Student Club at NC State
description: To empower students in North Carolina with leadership, project management, and professional development skills. Through mentorship, hands-on experiences, and networking, we foster growth and innovation. We strive to bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world applications, preparing future industry leaders
summary: Empowering NC students with leadership, project management, and professional skills through mentorship, hands-on experiences, and networking. We bridge the gap between academics and real-world needs to prepare tomorrow’s industry leaders.

name: Plant-Based Pack
description:
The Plant-Based Pack is a student organization dedicated to creating a more compassionate and sustainable world through advocacy of plant-forward living. Up to 90 billion land animals are killed a year for food, causing harm to animals and the environment. Our group’s work is focused on addressing this by

 

1.        Raising awareness on the impact our food choices have on animals and the planet through outreach and tabling events

 

2.        Supporting individuals interested in transitioning to a plant-based lifestyle

 

3.        Working to increase plant-based options in the dining halls

 

4.        Building a supportive community of individuals with shared values through various events (potlucks, animal sanctuary visits, movie screenings, etc.)

 

Join our emailing list: 

https://forms.gle/VgpSo51gmQoxHqY77

Join our Discord:
https://discord.gg/Mhs3SH2X8d

*Everyone is welcome, whether you are vegan, vegetarian, omnivore, or just veg curious!
summary: The Plant-Based Pack is dedicated to reducing animal suffering by advocating for plant-based diets, supporting those transitioning to vegan/vegetarian lifestyles, increasing plant-based dining options, and fostering a supportive community.

name: Society of Entrepreneurs at NC State
description:
The Society of Entrepreneurs is a community for anyone with an entrepreneurial spirit – whether you’re just beginning to explore what entrepreneurship means or have already made it your full-time pursuit.

At our core, we’re about learning through doing, growing through failure, and supporting each other every step of the way. Through interactive activities, hands-on business experience, and real conversations with founders who’ve been there before, we create a space where members learn from one another and push each other to get better every day.

We also host pitch competitions where you can win capital and prizes, bring in guest speakers to share unfiltered stories from the journey, and offer networking opportunities that turn ideas into action.

More than anything, we’re a community built to help you turn your drive into impact, together.
summary: The Society of Entrepreneurs is a place for anyone interested in anything entrepreneurship-related. Come network, find other founders to work with, learn how to start a business, pitch competitions, and meet tons of motivated people.

name: Letters for Smiles Club at NC State
description: The purpose of this student organization is to spread positivity, love, and support through letters. The club will hold workshops that allow any NC State students to write kind letters to various organizations and people - ex.: companionless/isolated elderly, terminally ill patients in hospitals, cancer patients, NCSU faculty, NCSU students, etc. The letters will be overviewed, organized, and packed to send to organizations or people. Writing workshops and events will be hosted weekly/bi-weekly and will be available to all public (NC State students). If possible, the club will also try to host fundraisers with the workshops to help the organizations the letters will be sent to.
summary: Letters for Smiles is an organization that centers around spreading positivity through writing and encouraging students on campus to write letters and notes.

name: Women In Sport at NC State University
summary: The Women in Sport Club at NC State is an inclusive organization open to all who are passionate about supporting women’s sports and empowering those pursuing careers in the sport industry. We promote leadership, career development, and networking!

name: Black Male Collective
summary: The objective of the Black Male Collective (BMC) is to empower individuals who identify with or support the lived experiences of Black men by fostering academic success, leadership development, cultural affirmation, and personal growth.

name: Health Mentor Association at NC State
summary: An organization aimed to provide volunteering and mentorship to prehealth students on campus.

name: Artificial Intelligence & Us
description: AI & Us is a student club exploring AI through a humanities lens. We focus on how AI affects society, ethics, culture, and us! No STEM or tech background needed, just curiosity and a desire to understand AI's impact on the human experience.
summary: AI & Us is a student club exploring AI through an everyday humanities lens. We focus on how AI affects ethics, culture, and us! No STEM or tech background needed, just curiosity & a desire to understand AI's impact on the human experience & vice versa.

name: Bake it Forward at NC State
description: Bake it Forward at NC State aims to combine the fun aspect of baking or buying baked goods and putting it towards giving back to our community! Students have the option to bake their own treats, buy baked goods, or donate money which will then purchase baked goods!
summary: Students coming together to donate baked goods to various local organizations

name: Chamber Music Club at NC State
description: The Chamber Music Club at NC State is a group of musicians and music lovers who want to find others to play with, listen to others play, and/or perform music they have been working on. The mission of this club is to connect musicians at NC State and represent the university's music community. Members of the club may receive help in finding others to play with and/or work on their own repertoire to perform. We will have several events and concerts throughout each semester so that there are plenty of chances to perform. Membership is free and open to anyone.
summary: The Chamber Music Club at NC State seeks to connect the university's music community and provide opportunities for musicians to perform individually or with small ensembles without fear of judgement.

name: Classic Robotics Lab
summary: Classic Robotics Lab is a student-led organization at NC State focused on designing, building, and researching advanced robotic systems.

name: College of Natural Resources Community Connect
description: CNR Community Connect aims to foster meaningful connections between students in CNR, helping new students find their academic and professional niche. From working in policy to pursuing fieldwork full-time, CNR Connect matches new students with a range of diversely experienced & established students pursuing the new students’ career/field of interest to form meaningful connections through facilitated events and 1:1 meetings. The mentorship program provides new students a direct line to identify opportunities for professional development and experiential learning.
summary: We aim to foster an inclusive environment and establish a 1:1 mentorship program to connect new CNR students with professional development and experiential learning opportunities through authentic friendships.

name: Disciples Student Fellowship
summary:
Disciples Student Fellowship (DSF) at NC State is affiliated with the Christian Church
(Disciples of Christ), a mainline Protestant denomination committed to justice, unity, and
inclusion. Students can experience spiritual growth and fellowship.

name: Forestry Club
description:
The Forestry Club at NC State provides students with the opportunity to compete in timbersports events, comprised of both physical and technical events, throughout the Southeastern United States to encourage leadership, teamwork, competition, sportsmanship, professionalism, skill development, and fellowship among the students within NCSU and other members of the Southeastern Conclave.

 

The Forestry Club at NC State competes annually at the Cradle of Forestry in America, the Annual Rolleo in Schenck Forest, TimberBeast at Virginia Tech, and as a founding member at the ASFC Conclave.
summary: The Forestry Club at NC State provides students with the opportunity to compete in timbersports events, comprised of both physical and technical events, throughout the Southeastern United States.

name: Harmful Algal Blooms, People, & the Environment
summary: Harmful Algal Blooms, People, & the Environment (HABPE) is a student-led organization at NC State that raises awareness of harmful algal blooms in North Carolina. We connect science with community through outreach, education, and inclusive engagement.

name: International City/County Management Association (ICMA) Student Chapter at NC State University
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to cultivate student interest in and commitment to the field of local government and public service. Our chapter seeks to provide a platform for undergraduate and graduate students across all disciplines.

name: Japanese Student Association
description: JSA at NC State is a community for Japanese students and anyone interested in Japanese culture. We host fun, cultural, and social events to connect people and celebrate the beauty of Japan through food, traditions, language, and more!
summary: JSA at NC State is a community for Japanese students and anyone interested in Japanese culture. We host fun, cultural, and social events to connect people and celebrate the beauty of Japan through food, traditions, language, and more!

name: Mahjong Club at North Carolina State University
description: We teach multiplayer mahjong to people of all backgrounds and play matches to improve game knowledge and skill. We meet on campus every other Tuesday from 6 pm to 8 pm. Click here to add our meetings to your calendar and here to join our Discord server.
summary: We teach multiplayer mahjong to people of all backgrounds and play matches to improve game knowledge and skill. We meet on campus every other Tuesday from 6 pm to 8 pm. There are no dues or required meetings.

name: NC C-CAPE Club
summary: Student organization for members of the North Carolina Center for Coastal Algae, People and the Environment. All students who are interested are welcome.

name: Friends of Medecins Sans Frontieres at NC State
description: NC State MSF Chapter is designed to help raise and provide funds to support healthcare workers working in underserved communities and under-resourced areas. We want to create a space where students can come together to raise awareness about global healthcare disparities and lack of access to essential medical services in underprivileged regions. While we can’t help out on the field, we want to build a community that is committed to advocacy and ethical engagement to help healthcare professionals and would love to be granted the opportunity to help raise and provide funds for those who can help in the communities that need/ lack access to adequate healthcare.
summary: The NC State MSF Chapter aims to support healthcare workers in underserved and under-resourced communities by raising funds and increasing awareness about global healthcare disparities.

name: Girl Up at NC State
description: Girl Up at NC State is a Club. Our mission is to raise awareness about gender conflicts and more subtle yet ever-present discrimination on the basis of sex (such as pink tax, domestic violence, female infanticide, etc.). We hope to create a safe space for individuals of any gender to discuss and learn about issues related to the discrimination of females simply on the basis of their gender.
summary: Girl Up at NC State (GUNCS) is a Club. Our mission is to raise awareness about gender conflicts and more subtle yet ever-present discrimination on the basis of sex (such as pink tax, domestic violence, female infanticide, etc.).

name: The Wellness Society
description:
The Wellness Society is a student organization focused on holistic health and wellness, aiming to create a community of students that utilizes different events and activities to foster nutrition education, mental health, and movement. Our focus areas include self-care, nutrition, movement, and stress relief. Our mission is to make health and wellness accessible and fun for students. We host a variety of fun events, from yoga classes to smoothie shop days to vision board making nights, so there is a place for everyone!

Use the below link to access our GroupMe and other forms!

http://linktr.ee/ncsuwellnesssociety
summary: Our mission is to implement events, programs, and workshops to educate and empower students at NC State. We aim to help students prioritize self-care, nutrition, movement, and healthy lifestyle choices by creating a community of like-minded individuals.

name: Pack Cancer Organization
description:
The organization makes a sustainable impact by

  • providing educational, professional, and networking opportunities for students;
  • organizing community-based initiatives that aid patients and families;
  • promoting collaboration between on-campus organizations for growing and cultivating a strong pack community
  • fundraising for national and local nonprofits; and
  • spreading cancer awareness.
summary: The Pack Cancer Organization engages college students in the fight against cancer through education, community, and giving.

name: Pack Performance Leaders
description: The Pack Performance Leaders is a group for student leaders who are enrolled in one of the courses within the Department of Performing Arts and Technology. This group is for students who are ambitious, show strong leadership potential, and are ready to plan and assist with different events hosted by the department.
summary: An organization for students currently enrolled in a course within the Department of Performing Arts and Technology who seek to actively support and contribute to the growth of the department.

name: PackPolish
description: PackPolish is a student-run community service organization dedicated to uplifting and connecting with the elderly through weekly visits to Waltonwood Assisted Living. Members provide nail care, including painting and nail stamping, while engaging residents in meaningful conversation. The organization promotes creativity, dignity, and intergenerational connection, offering students a hands-on opportunity to serve, listen, and build relationships that enrich both their lives and the lives of those they visit.
summary: PackPolish is a student-led club that visits Waltonwood weekly to paint nails, practice nail stamping, and connect with residents, fostering relationships and promoting well-being.

name: Second Chance Initiative at NC State University
description: The purpose of this organization is to inform and engage NC State University students, faculty, and staff about prison education efforts and the broader issues faced by justice-impacted individuals. This student organization aims to raise awareness about the barriers incarcerated individuals encounter– both during and after incarceration– including limited access to education, systemic injustices, and the cycle of recidivism. Through discussions, educational events, and community outreach, SCI members will explore the social and ethical concerns surrounding prison institutions. By fostering informed dialogue and advocacy, the organization seeks to promote meaningful collaboration between members of NC State’s campus community, the North Carolina Department of Adult Corrections (NCDAC), and the North Carolina Community Colleges System (NCCCS). The organization is committed to supporting ways to provide education opportunities for justice-impacted individuals. The club encourages participation by current NC State students, faculty, and staff.
summary: The Second Chance Initiative (SCI) organization at NC State University aims to inform and engage NCSU students, faculty, and staff about prison education efforts and the broader issues faced by individuals impacted by the justice system.

name: Solar District Cup Club at NC State
description:
The Solar District Cup Club at NC State brings together students from diverse disciplines to design innovative, real-world solar and energy storage solutions as part of the U.S. Department of Energy’s annual Solar District Cup competition. Our mission is to equip students with hands-on experience in renewable energy engineering, financial modeling, distribution system planning, and energy equity analysis through collaborative design challenges.

Each academic year, our team selects a real-life campus or urban district and develops a complete proposal to transform it into a resilient, net-zero energy district using rooftop and carport solar PV, battery energy storage, and advanced system design tools like Helioscope, SAM, Aurora, and PVsyst. Students gain valuable skills in solar technology, microgrid planning, interconnection, policy compliance, and technical communications.

In addition to competition preparation, the club hosts technical workshops, guest speakers, and outreach events to promote clean energy awareness on campus and beyond. Whether you're an engineer, business student, designer, or policy enthusiast, the Solar District Cup Club offers a chance to work on impactful projects, build your professional network, and shape the future of renewable energy.
