Students who actively compete through the full academic year are eligible for an all-expenses-paid trip to RE+, the largest clean energy industry conference in North America. Held annually in the fall, RE+ brings together over 40,000 professionals across solar, storage, EV infrastructure, and grid technologies. Attendees gain exclusive access to cutting-edge technology showcases, workshops, career opportunities, and direct networking with top companies and national energy leaders—making it a launchpad for future clean energy careers.
summary: The Solar District Cup Club empowers students to design renewable energy systems and compete nationally, applying engineering, policy, and finance to solve real-world clean energy challenges.

name: NC State Skypack
description:
A club devoted to the sport of skydiving, involving both experienced and inexperienced jumpers. The aim of this club is to help people escape their comfort zones and develop friendships within the amazing sport of skydiving.

Zero previous experience is required to join. This club is to help make the sport more accessible by providing opportunities for groups of friends and fellow club members to go as a group and share their new experience of a tandem skydive.

For experienced and licensed jumpers, we will plan on hosting group jumps allowing friends and fellow club members the opportunity to connect with fellow students and enjoy the sport together.
summary: A club devoted to the sport of skydiving, open to both experienced and inexperienced jumpers. The aim of this club is to help people escape their comfort zones and develop friendships within the amazing sport of skydiving.

name: Spectra at NC State
description: This is the NCSU chapter of the greater Spectra organization. We aim to build community for the LGBTQIA+ community in the mathematical science. We are open to both undergraduate and graduate students from all majors who are interested in the mathematical sciences. We aim to provide greater understanding of the contributions of LGBTQIA+ individuals to the mathematical sciences, as well as mentoring opportunities and encouraging LGBTQIA+ individuals as they prepare for careers in the mathematical sciences.
summary: Association for LGBTQIA+ mathematicians.

name: The Silicon Den: Tapeout Club at NC State
description:
The Silicon Den is a student-led organization at NC State dedicated to hands-on integrated circuit (IC) design. Members work on both digital and analog projects, designing custom silicon chips from the ground up. We guide students through the full development cycle—from RTL and schematic entry to mixed-signal integration, tapeout submission, and post-silicon validation.

Our projects provide a unique opportunity to apply classroom knowledge to real-world hardware challenges using industry-standard tools and design flows. Members gain practical experience in ASIC workflows, circuit simulation, physical design, and verification, while learning to collaborate across domains.

Whether you're interested in processor design, analog front-ends, mixed-signal interfaces, or verification infrastructure, The Silicon Den offers a project-based, team-oriented space to learn, build, and grow. We welcome students of all experience levels who are passionate about semiconductors and hardware design.

Please contact us at silicon-den-org@ncsu.edu for any questions, concerns or professional requests.

Request to join our mailing list via our Google Group at https://groups.google.com/a/ncsu.edu/g/group-silicon-den-org.
summary: A student-led ECE organization at NC State focused on digital (RTL) and analog (layout/schematic) IC design. Members gain hands-on experience in full-chip ASIC design, verification, and mixed-signal integration from concept to tapeout.

name: The Tetris Club at NC State
description: Welcome to The Tetris Club at NC State! This club is intended to create a local community for Tetris players of all skill levels to interact and grow. We mainly focus on modern guideline games with the goal of fielding a team for the Collegiate Tetris League; With that being said, we gladly welcome those interested in Classic, TGM,Puyo Puyo, and other variants and genres of puzzle games.
summary: The Tetris Club at NC State is intended to create a local community for Tetris players of all skill levels to interact and grow. We mainly focus on modern guideline games with the goal of fielding a team for the Collegiate Tetris League.

name: Neurotech at NC State
description: Neurotech at NC State is a student-run organization committed to spreading education and building meaningful projects at the intersection of neuroscience, biomedical engineering, electrical engineering, machine learning, materials science, and more. We aim to create a community of students who are passionate about applying technology to improve the lives of those around them. Furthermore, we hope to serve as a platform for students to learn, collaborate, and build innovative projects that interface with the brain and nervous system.
summary: A club focused on broadening access to neural engineering concepts with an emphasis on user-focused design, clinical awareness, and education.

name: The Wii Club
description: Instagram: ncsuwiiclub
summary: The Wii Club is a place where new or fellow enthusiasts of the Nintendo Wii console can relive their late 2000s and early 2010s years and enjoy playing games from their childhood. Everyone is welcome!

name: NC State Student Chapter of Ethics Bowl Club
description:
The NC State Student Chapter of Ethics Bowl (NCSSCEB) is a club designed to allow students to be exposed to ethical questions, compete with students from other universities, and learn from one another/ understand new perspectives via sharing our stances on the ethical dilemmas presented during the case studies for the competitions. 

We seek to:

  • Discuss complex, real-world, ethically ambiguous dilemmas
  • Learn how to thoughtfully craft an argument and argue/ argue respectfully 
  • Inculcate critical thinking skills
  • Fundraise for competition fees, transportation, etc. 
  • Prepare for inter-collegiate competitions
summary: The NC State Student Chapter of Ethics Bowl (NCSSCEB) is a club designed to allow students to be exposed to ethical questions and learn from one another/ understand new perspectives on ethical dilemmas.

name: Study Center
summary: Cultivates Christian life and thought at NC State and the surrounding college communities, offering the welcome, wisdom, and truth of Christ.

name: CubePack
description: CubePack will promote cubing at NC State, make it more popular and competitive by creating pathways towards officially recognized competitions, and establish connections between individuals interested in twisty puzzles. We strive to bridge the gap between beginners and competitive cubers by fostering improvement among solvers, and we plan on encouraging people to take up other puzzle-related activities as well. Join the discord to be a part of the community and receive the latest updates! https://discord.gg/mFdyg2s2D3
summary: CubePack is an organization at NC State University that promotes cubing and other puzzle-related activities by building a community that will allow cubing to become more popular and competitive.

name: White Coat Prep at NC State
description: White Coat Prep at NC State is a student-led organization for pre-med students who want a supportive and non-competitive space to study for the MCAT together. Through peer collaboration, shared resources, and creative study strategies, we focus on academic growth and encouragement. Whether you're just starting your pre-med journey or actively preparing for the MCAT, we welcome you to join our community!
summary: White Coat Prep is a student-led organization committed to collaborative MCAT preparation, wellness, and peer support, creating a balanced and fun environment for pre-med students at NC State.

name: Kendo Club at NC State
description:
Kendo is a sword fighting martial art originating in Japan. In kendo you wear protective equipment and keiko (spar/ practice) with others. Kendo is very standardized, so there is only one official system and ranks are recognized all around the world. However, everyone develops their own unique style as they progress.

Kendo comes from Japan's samurai culture and history. In the 18th century, Naganuma Shirozaemon-Kunisato invented the shinai (bamboo sword) and an early version of bogu (armor), and samurai began to travel between schools to find new opponents. Before World War II, kendo was much different than it is today. Now, kendo is less of an art of war and more of a competition which has strong roots in its traditions and history. Kendo combines fierce, dynamic competition with traditions and etiquette (rei)--making an environment which is very different from the everyday life that most of us are familiar with.
summary: Kendo is a sword fighting martial art originating in Japan. In kendo you wear protective equipment and keiko (spar/ practice) with others. Kendo is very standardized, so there is only one official system and ranks are recognized all around the world.

name: Students for Sustainability at NC State
description: Students for Sustainability at NC State brings students together every other week to clean up trash and promote recycling in parks near NC State and on campus. This organization helps to foster environmental stewardship and community service. Members can make a tangible impact while enjoying the outdoors and meeting like-minded peers.
summary: This club meets biweekly to clean up trash and promote recycling on campus and in nearby parks, encouraging environmental care and community service while connecting like-minded students.

name: GI Health and Advocacy Club
description: The purpose of this student organization is to raise awareness about gastrointestinal (GI) health and promote a better understanding of chronic GI conditions such as Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and other related disorders. The organization is dedicated to supporting students living with these conditions by fostering peer networks and providing a sense of community. It also seeks to educate the campus through events, guest speakers, and outreach efforts. In addition, the group aims to advocate for improved policies and campus support services, while organizing community service and fundraising initiatives to support GI health research and awareness.
summary: A student organization focused on raising awareness of GI health, supporting students with chronic GI conditions, educating the campus community, and advocating for better policies and research funding.

name: Girls Go Government at NC State
description: Through engaging workshops, seminars, and mentorship programs, Girls Go Government creates a supportive setting where girls can explore their interests, participate actively in their communities, and grow as leaders. By linking college members with high school students, we aim to promote mentorship, provide access to resources, and motivate young women to get involved in public service and civics.
summary: Our goal is to inspire and educate young women about government or related opportunities, from local to state levels. The GGG Collegiate Affiliated Club offers support and guidance to high school students as they explore careers in government.

name: IEEE Technology and Engineering Management Society (TEMS) NC State Student Branch Chapter
description:
To empower NCSU Students by fostering a vibrant community that bridges academia and industry driving engagement, connection, and professional growth in a rapidly evolving technology & management landscape.

  1. Engage students through real-world learning and leadership opportunities
  2. Build strong connections with faculty and industry professionals
  3. Support career growth through events, mentorship, and networking
  4. Explore new trends in engineering management and innovation
summary: To empower NCSU Students by fostering a vibrant community that bridges academia and industry driving engagement, connection, and professional growth in a rapidly evolving technology & management landscape.

name: LiveSound
description:
LiveSound is a student organization created to unite musicians and engineers who share a passion for live music and performance production. The club fosters collaboration between performers and technical crew, creating a space where both groups can learn from and support one another. 

Throughout the year, LiveSound will host various events including open mic nights, live performances, and full-scale concerts. Whether you're passionate about performing, running sound, or just helping make a show happen, there’s a place for you.

All majors, backgrounds, and experience levels are welcome
summary: A club for musicians and tech enthusiasts to connect! Join as a performer in student bands or as part of the tech crew running sound, lights, and staging for live shows. All skill levels welcome!

name: SVYM Health Student Chapter at NC State
description: SVYM Health Student Chapter at NC State aims to explore rural healthcare and support the Swami Vivekananda Youth Movement (SVYM) in addressing health challenges in rural Karnataka. Students will learn about SVYM’s institutional and community health initiatives as well as ways they can become involved. Students will also engage in fundraising events to support these efforts. They will have the opportunity to work on projects with SVYM either in person or remotely. Students who pursue in-person projects will also have the opportunity to shadow doctors at Vivekananda Memorial Hospital (VMH), a hospital dedicated to serving tribal and rural populations at subsidized rates.
summary: A student-led chapter focused on supporting the health sector of the Swami Vivekananda Youth Movement in overcoming health barriers in Karnataka, India.

name: Bilingual Education Student Organization at NC State
description: The Bilingual Education Student Organization (BESO) at NC State is a student-led chapter affiliated with the National Association for Bilingual Education (NABE). BESO advocates for educational equity and excellence for bilingual and multilingual students in a global society. Our mission is to build a community of future bilingual educators and professionals by fostering opportunities for networking, professional development, advocacy, and resource-sharing. We support student engagement in the College of Education and beyond to advance bilingual education across schools and communities.
summary: BESO at NC State supports future bilingual educators by promoting advocacy, networking, and professional growth. As a NABE chapter, we aim to advance bilingual/multilingual education and equity in schools and communities.

name: American Choral Directors Association at North Carolina State University
description: We are the collegiate chapter of American Choral Directors Association. The association functions as a way for choral directors to connect and grow, as well as being a home for all who are interested in choral music. The national organization gives us a lot of latitude in how we operate so we decided to use this latitude to make the club into a non-auditioned choir. We are also planning to do other events as well but those will be determined later.
summary: We are NC State's collegiate chapter of ACDA with the goal of strengthening the choral community on campus, featuring a non-auditioned mixed choir 🎶

name: Omani Student Association at NC State
description: Our mission is to create a welcoming space where students can connect with each other and celebrate our traditions while supporting one another academically and socially. We organize cultural events that go under the Omani theme with its food, colors, and traditions. We share clips and videos of the events through our organization's social media platforms
summary: a student-led organization dedicated to supporting Omani students on campus and sharing Omani culture with the broader university community.

name: Construction & Development Club at NC State University
description: The Construction & Development Club at NC State connects students interested in construction, civil engineering, and sustainable development. Members work on hands-on projects, network with professionals, and gain real-world experience in the industry.
summary: The Construction & Development Club at NC State connects students interested in construction, civil engineering, and sustainable development. Members work on hands-on projects, network with professionals, and gain real-world experience in the industry.

name: Fiction Book Club at NC State University
description: The Fiction Book Club at NC State University is a club dedicated to creating community for those who love to read books. This club will have a lot of reading without the pressure of academics, and give a space for people to find others who share their interests. There will be a theme every month, so there will be a wide variety of fiction books being read. Members will vote on what books are being read. Along with occasional literacy projects, such as fundraising literacy programs, or reading books to elementary students.
summary: Every week, members will meet to discuss fiction books. There will be one book per month that the members voted for. The goal of club is to encourage discussion, literacy, and build a community full of book lovers!

name: The Kings Chapel at NC State
shortName: The Kings Chapel at NC State
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to make Jesus Christ known on college campuses! We plan to do that through student outreach events and campus Bible studies.

name: Pack Attack Motorsports
summary: Our mission is to provide students with hands-on engineering experience by designing, building, and competing with race vehicles at the highest collegiate level. This differs from Formula SAE in which we go wheel-to-wheel racing.

name: Litigation Club at NC State
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to engage students in the study and practice of litigation through mock trials, workshops, and community partnerships. This organization seeks to develop skills in legal analysis and civic engagement.

name: First Gen Business Collective at NC State
summary: The First Gen Business Collective is dedicated to empowering first-generation business students by breaking down barriers to success. We provide access to professional development, financial and academic resources, and community support.

name: Biostatistics and Biomathematics Society at NC State
description: The Biostatistics and Biomathematics Society (BBS) at NC State is a student organization dedicated to promoting enthusiasm for biostatistics and biomathematics and supporting students interested in these interdisciplinary fields. BBS explores what careers in biostatistics and biomathematics are like and helps students prepare by sharing information on related academic programs, internships, and research opportunities. Through meetings, events, and collaborations, the organization aims to foster community, mentorship, and networking with peers, faculty, and professionals, creating a supportive environment for students to learn about applications of mathematics and statistics in biology and health sciences.
summary: BBS at NC State promotes enthusiasm for biostats and biomath by exploring what careers in these fields are like and helping students prepare through information on related programs, internships, research opportunities, and networking with others.

name: Open Door Project
description:
The Open Door Project is a student-led community service organization that will work towards expanding STEM education and supporting underserved communities throughout the Greater Triangle area. The mission of ODP is to deliver hands-on STEM workshops, youth outreach programs, and high-impact service events that promote education, equity, and opportunity. The greatest resource we provide is a team of NCSU students who are looking to make a real change in our community. Members gain meaningful community service experience, develop leadership skills, and build strong connections with STEM-based organizations that support their academic and professional growth.

 

ODP has an established record of success, highlighted by its highly active and accomplished chapter at UNC Charlotte. The UNCC chapter has led some of the organization’s most impactful initiatives and set the standard for chapter excellence. The president of the current chapter previously served as a board member for the UNCC chapter, bringing invaluable experience, insight, and proven leadership to guide the organization’s next stage of growth.
summary: Open Door Project is a student-driven organization dedicated to expanding STEM access and delivering community service programs that support underserved populations in the Greater Triangle region.

name: Lions Club at NC State
description:
The Lions Club at NC State is a student-driven service organization affiliated with Lions Clubs International, the world’s largest volunteer service network. Our chapter empowers NC State students to address community needs through hands-on service, health and vision support initiatives, campus outreach, and global humanitarian projects.

We focus on impact-driven service opportunities, professional development, leadership training, and meaningful collaboration with local Raleigh Lions Clubs and nonprofit partners. Our mission is to develop student leaders who serve with integrity, support underserved communities, and contribute to LCI’s global pillars: vision, diabetes awareness, hunger relief, environmental service, and support for childhood cancer initiatives.
summary: The NC State Lions Club is a student-led service organization dedicated to improving campus and community life through volunteer work, leadership development, and global humanitarian service as part of Lions Clubs International.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances - Medical Outreach
summary: VAW Global Medical Interns work closely with community partners to implement primary medical clinics across low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). In the Medical Outreach program, interns learn about healthcare in a global context.

name: 1911 Consulting
summary: 1911 Consulting provides consulting services to various departments and faculty initiatives across the university in order to increase efficiency and output of programs.

name: YWAM Campus Fellowship
description:
Help students develop a biblical worldview by exploring how Scripture speaks to all areas of life, including identity, culture, justice, and calling.

Teach Christian apologetics by equipping students to understand, explain, and defend the Christian faith with clarity, humility, and love.

Provide a safe, respectful space to wrestle with hard questions young adults are asking, including but not limited to:

Sexuality, gender, and relationships

Feminism and modern social movements

History of religions and worldviews

Suffering, evil, and the problem of pain

Science, faith, and reason

Lead students toward a living relationship with Jesus Christ, encouraging personal faith, repentance, and ongoing discipleship. And meaningful service to the community.

Offer Bible study, discussion, prayer, and community that help students anchor their lives in the Word of God and live missionally on campus and beyond.
summary: We work to help individuals develop a biblical worldview by exploring how Scripture applies to every aspect of life. We also teach apologetics and how to explain  the Christian faith. This is a safe place to ask every deep question that you might have.

name: Safewater Club
description: Safewater Club is a subset of NC One Water, the North Carolina focused association jointly under the American Water Works Association and the Water Environment Federation. This club is focused specifically on fostering and supporting North Carolina State University graduate and undergraduate students interested in the water and wastewater fields. Additionally, we provide members opportunities for education, training, service, and leadership in the field of water and wastewater treatment with the goal to protect public health and the environment.
summary: Safewater Club is the North Carolina State University specific chapter for NC One Water, an organization dedicated to water and wastewater training education and service, and includes all those interested or engaged with industry/academic goal in water.

name: National Ground Game At NC State
description: “Every debate, every chapter, every voter registration effort is designed for results. We exist to take back the youth vote and secure Democratic victories, giving the next generation the tools, networks, and leadership they need to shape the future of American democracy.” Through live debate events, regular chapter meetings, open discussions, guest speakers, social cohesion activities (game nights, album listening parties, election or major political event watch parties, ice cream socials etc.), and any other fun, exciting, or effective means, NGG At NC State aims to be the premier destination on campus for open, candid debate and consensus-building that directly translates to major Democratic electoral victories, and we also want to strive to help people better understand and care for their community and the world around them.
summary:
The purpose of National Ground Game At NC State is to serve as the official NC State chapter of 
National Ground Game (NGG). Our mission: take back the youth vote, train organizers, and deliver measurable results at the ballot box.

name: ZamoPack Association of Zamorano Alumni at North Carolina State University
description:
This student organization serves as the North Carolina–based chapter of the Association of Zamorano Alumni in the United States (AGEAP-USA). 

 

The purpose of this student organization is to cultivate a supportive and professional community for Zamorano University alumni and North Carolina State University students with interests in agriculture, natural resources, marine and atmospheric sciences, and engineering. Through networking, collaboration, and academic exchange, the organization seeks to strengthen the ZamoWolfpack presence at NC State and across North Carolina.
summary: This student organization serves as the North Carolina–based chapter of the Association of Zamorano Alumni in the United States (AGEAP-USA).

name: Association for Computing Machinery @ NC State
description:
ACM aims to promote excellence, education and opportunities for students in the computing field. We do this through hosting faculty and industry speakers, student-led workshops, and corporate events.

If you are interested in joining ACM leadership, please fill out the committee member application form :

https://forms.gle/4Rx6b2AiT76QvAAQ7

If you are interested in just attending our events, join the Discord below for announcements! Our events focus on computing students.

https://discord.gg/5DzjFzhgGT
summary: Promoting excellence, education and opportunities for students in the computing field.

name: Guiding Eyes for the Blind at North Carolina State University
description:
The objectives of this club organization are to educate the NC State community and the greater public about GEB and puppy raising, including but not limited to:

 

Increase campus and community awareness about guide dogs and how these pups help individuals:

  • Develop student leadership on the NC State campus 
  • Greeting puppies on campus appropriately while in training
  • Recruiting future members 
  • Our presence in the community and on campus 
  • Campus rules 
  • Becoming an Active General Body Member 
  • Becoming a sitter or raiser to prepare pups for Guide dog training
summary: The objectives of this club organization are to educate the NC State community and the greater public about GEB and puppy raising.

name: Behavior Medicine and Welfare Club at NC State
summary: The Behavior & Welfare Medicine Club is a hybrid veterinary student organization focused on animal behavior and welfare. Through virtual and in-person events, members learn evidence-based strategies to improve animal well-being in veterinary practice.

name: Clash Royale at NC State
description:
Clash Royale at NC State is a esports organization for both competitive and casual Clash Royale players at NC State. The organization fields competitive teams for intercollegiate collegiate competition while also providing a welcoming environment for students interested in learning, improving, or playing socially.

Through organized practices, scrimmages, tournaments, and community events, the club promotes strategic thinking, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Clash Royale at NC State aims to create a community where players of all skill levels can engage with the game, connect with fellow students, and represent NC State in collegiate esports.
summary: Clash Royale at NC State is an esports organization for both competitive and casual Clash Royale players at NC State. The club competes in intercollegiate leagues while also hosting practices, event, and activities open to players of all levels.

name: ITS ON US at NC STATE
description:
It’s On Us was founded in September 2014, as a public awareness campaign of the Obama-Biden Administration, following recommendations from the White House Task Force to Prevent Sexual Assault that noted the importance of calling everyone into the conversation on sexual assault prevention.

Since then, It’s On Us has grown into the nation’s largest nonprofit program dedicated to college sexual assault prevention, training a national network of student-led campus chapters to become peer-educators; developing suites of prevention education programs; and conducting and publishing research to expand the field of knowledge on violence prevention and inform our work.
summary: A student movement designed to change the culture around sexual assault on campus.

name: EDM Club at NC State
description:
Club Discord Server:

https://discord.gg/Y6FbR3ua6
summary: Our club plans to foster growth in EDM music by connecting with students and Alumni in NC State.

name: The Common Front
description:
We are a community-rooted collective dedicated to building power through mutual aid, political education, and accessible networks of support. Grounded in leftist values of solidarity, cooperation, and material care, our organization brings people together to share resources, skills, and knowledge that strengthen our collective capacity to resist harmful systems and create alternatives. 

With our network, members can connect with others committed to grassroots change, exchange support, and collaborate on local projects that meet immediate needs while working toward long-term solutions. This will set the precedent of long-term collaboration for the sake of protecting the broader community.

Our goal is simple: empower everyday people with the tools, community, and confidence to care for one another and build a more just, equitable world from the ground up.
summary: A platform that provides mutual aid, education, and a network of communication. This space will be used to encourage different organizations on campus to communicate with each other to create a stronger community overall, pursuing a broader agenda.

name: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine at NC State
description:
The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is committed to supporting current and future healthcare professionals and scientists in efforts to combat educational and professional racial inequities by promoting attainability through educational resources, unity, and mentorship. The purpose of this student organization is to increase the recruitment and retention of students pursuing broad careers in medicine, while simultaneously promoting the advancement of those already established in these fields.

The National Society of Black women in Medicine has a National affiliation among colleges/universities. With eight collegiate chapters already established in North Carolina, and over seventy collegiate chapters established nationwide.
summary: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is committed to supporting current and future healthcare professionals and scientists by promoting attainability through educational resources, unity and mentorship.

name: American Medical Student Association at NC State
description: AMSA at NC State is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting pre-medical and health-focused students through advocacy, service, and professional development. Our chapter provides opportunities for clinical exposure, community health outreach, and mentorship. We emphasize health equity, ethical medicine, and patient-centered care. AMSA at NC State fosters a collaborative community for students pursuing careers in medicine and healthcare.
summary: AMSA at NC State is a student-led org supporting pre-med and health students through service, advocacy, and professional development. We offer clinical exposure, mentorship, and community outreach while promoting health equity and patient-centered care.

name: H2GO at NC State
description: Members will explore the production of hydrogen and they will collaborate to construct devices to produce hydrogen in a clean and efficient manner. The main way we will produce hydrogen is through electrolysis.
summary: The purpose of H2GO at NC State is to produce clean hydrogen gas and corresponding hydrogen fuel cells in order to power and understand clean transportation.

name: Global Health & Education Leadership Club at NC State
description:
The Global Health & Education Leadership Club at NC State is a student-led organization focused on advancing global equity in healthcare and education through service, leadership, and advocacy. The club is jointly affiliated with BlinkNow Foundation, which supports education and health initiatives in Nepal, particularly at Kopila Valley School, and Operation Smile, a global nonprofit providing cleft surgery and comprehensive care worldwide.

Through fundraising, educational programming, and awareness campaigns, the organization works to address disparities in global health and education while fostering cross-cultural understanding and student leadership. Funds raised by the club are distributed evenly between BlinkNow and Operation Smile unless otherwise designated for approved initiatives. Open to students of all majors and backgrounds, the club emphasizes inclusivity, collaboration, and meaningful global impact.
summary: The Global Health & Education Leadership Club at NC State is a student organization focused on global health, education, and service. We partner with Operation Smile and BlinkNow to fundraise, raise awareness, and support global initiatives.

name: SHPEtinas at NC State
description: Our chapter’s purpose is to empower women in STEM by providing a community on campus and by helping them navigate their careers, professionally and academically, through a male-dominated field. We strive to create an encouraging and supportive environment for women from minority groups. Those who actively contribute to the mission, goals, and objectives of SHPEtinas are more than welcome in this space.
summary: The purpose of SHPEtinas is to build a community that empowers women in STEM and help them navigate through a male-dominated work and learning environment while accomplishing their own goals.

name: Scale and Coin at NC State
description: Founded in 2025, Scale and Coin is NC State's premier business society. Our founding class forms a community of scholars and professionals. Members compete and work together to develop essentials skills in the business world across several industries including IB, corporate finance, NPOs (philanthropy), and Business Tech. Once a month, we will host professionals in consulting, finance, etc., will visit to offer industry advice to students.
summary: Scale and Coin is NC State's premier business society.

name: World Tea Association at State
description:
The World Tea Association at State is a student-led organization chartered by the World Tea Association, which supports student chapters at universities including UNC, Duke, and Wake Forest. Our organization is dedicated to exploring tea as a cultural, educational, and community-building practice.

 

Through guided tastings, Gong Fu Cha sessions, and matcha whisking workshops, we provide hands-on opportunities for members to engage with traditional tea practices from around the world. In addition to experiential learning, we emphasize mindful consumption and an appreciation for the science, history, and production of tea.

 

We are committed to accessibility, sustainability, and meaningful connection. Our teas are thoughtfully sourced from reputable producers, including small-scale farmers in Asia and established organizations such as the New York Tea Society, to ensure quality, authenticity, and ethical practices. With these resources, we strive to create safe spaces where members can engage with tea, encouraging curiosity, fostering open-mindedness, and cultivating cultural awareness on campus.
summary: The World Tea Association at State is a student-led organization that explores tea as a cultural and educational practice. Through tastings and workshops, we create safe, inclusive spaces for learning, connection, and mindful tea appreciation.

name: Middle Grades English Language Arts and Social Studies Club
description: The Middle School English Language Arts & Social Studies (MSL) Club at NC State serves as a community-building and professional support organization for students in the Middle Grades Language Arts and Social Studies program. The MSL Club connects students across cohorts and licensure stages to foster collaboration, mentorship, and shared experiences within the major. Through social events, academic and professional development opportunities, and program-specific initiatives, the MSL Club supports students as they grow as reflective educators. The organization works in partnership with the College of Education to promote a supportive, inclusive environment that strengthens teacher preparation, encourages peer connection, and celebrates the unique identity of future middle grades educators.
summary: The MSL Club is a student-led organization, partnering with the College of Education, that connects middle grades language arts & social studies education majors through community-building, mentorship, and professional development opportunities.

name: The Climate Journal Club
description: The purpose of The Climate Journal Club is to facilitate interdisciplinary conversations around climate change by discussing a range of writings from academic articles to blog posts. The club aims to provide a space for STEM-based students to learn and discuss climate change concepts outside the scope of their classes.
summary: A discussion-based club focused on climate change issues outside the scope of STEM classes. This club is primarily composed of natural science graduate students but is open to all NCSU students.

name: North Carolina Access Project
description: This organization serves as North Carolina’s central hub for nonpartisan, easy-to-understand political information. It collects, organizes, and presents data on elections, campaign finance,  and public policy in a clear, accessible format for citizens, educators, and civic leaders. By translating political data into practical insight, the organization empowers North Carolinians, from Dare to Cherokee County, to better understand how government operates, who influences it, and how decisions are made at every level of the state.
summary: To serve as North Carolina’s source for nonpartisan, easy-to-understand political information that empowers and educates citizens from Dare to Cherokee County.

name: Students Demand Action NC State University Chapter
description:
When lawmakers offered thoughts and prayers but little meaningful action after the 2018 mass shooting at Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida, high school and college students across the country came together to make our voices heard. Students Demand Action started in 2016 as a pilot program and, with a sense of urgency, we launched it as a national initiative within two weeks of the Parkland shooting. Because what could be more urgent than fixing the errors in our system that have cost so many young people their lives? Students Demand Action now has 1,000 groups across the country and active volunteers in every state and D.C.

Students Demand Action NCSU is one of dozens of chapters within NC, and we work with every community in NC to demand action and change on all fronts. We believe in a better and safer North Carolina, and we are working daily to make it happen! Joining over 1000 other chapters of Students Demand Action, we have opportunities to volunteer, testify, lead, and learn, all within one club. We have supporters from all across the country, and we work to provide opportunities to create change at every level!

 

https://www.instagram.com/reel/DOPGeCikkG6/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA%3D%3D
summary:
We’re young activists committed to ending gun violence in our communities.
We’ve grown up in the midst of America’s gun violence crisis. Now we’re coming together across the country to rise up and demand action to end gun violence.

name: NextGen Nurses
summary: To prepare students interested in nursing with their future career paths with opportunities in leadership, hands-on clinical field, community service, and engagement with healthcare professionals.

name: RC Aeronautics Club at NCSU
description:
The purpose of the RC Recreational Aeronautics Club is to create a relaxed, hands-on space where members can learn by doing and learn from each other. We focus on designing, building, flying, and testing RC aircraft while openly sharing knowledge, mistakes, and improvements along the way. The club emphasizes documenting each step of the process, from initial concepts and materials to flight testing and iteration, so everyone can better understand how and why things work.

 

Above all, this club exists as a non-competitive, low-pressure environment where curiosity comes first. Whether you’re brand new to RC planes or already experienced, members are encouraged to ask questions, try new ideas, experiment, and help others without judgment. The goal is to have fun, build cool aircraft, and grow together through collaboration and shared learning.
summary: RC Recreational Aeronautics Club is a student-led, non-competitive space for learning by building, flying, and testing RC aircraft together. We focus on safety, collaboration, documentation, and hands-on experience for all skill levels.

name: Brazilian Club at NC State
description: The Brazilian Club at NC State seeks to connect with students on campus to share Brazilian culture, empower student involvement, and unite an entire community away from home.
summary: Striving to connect Brazilian students at NC State with the entire campus community, sharing our culture and cultivating a welcoming environment away from home.

name: SGI Campus Club at NC State
summary: NC State SGI is a campus organization for students interested in the principles and activities of Soka Gakkai International, a global lay Buddhist organization, providing a space for dialogue, growth, and respect for the dignity of all individuals.

name: Comic Book Club at NC State
description: In this club we read and discuss different comic books. We vote on books to read as a group and read them in-between meetings. During the following meeting, we have a group discussion about the story. We also discuss and connect about comic book related media, such as movies, video games, and TV shows. Special events include things like movie screenings and trivia nights. For meeting updates and the latest info, follow our Instagram, @comicclubncsu.
summary: A space where students can gather to discuss all kinds of comic books of all genres. We just want to talk about our shared interest in a welcoming, safe space. :)

name: Muslim Dawah League at North Carolina State University
summary: Sharing the message of Islam in accordance with the Quran and Sunnah.

name: SCUBA Club at NC State University
summary: A student run organization that provides a space for certified divers and those intrested in diving to connect and create a community

name: Windpack at NC State
description:
Windpack is a student-led club at NC State. We want to be the ones designing and presenting meaningful solutions for the future of clean energy. 

Each year we compete in a national competition sponsored by the Department of Energy Competition called the Collegiate Wind Competition. For this competition, there is an engineering side and a project management side. Our engineering team builds a 45cm tall model wind turbine, where members gain experience in blade design, drivetrain development, power electronics, and testing. Our project development team develops a wind farm project plan (much like Duke Energy does) that covers site selection, energy production estimates, financial modeling, permitting considerations, and community impact. We take pride in our teams which collaborate across many majors. We prepare students for both engineering (Mechanical Design, Electrical Power, Energy Systems) and non-engineering careers (actuarial science, data science, and environmental science). We travel to the DOE competition at the end of the year to test our turbine in-person. The 2026 competition will be hosted at Penn State.
summary: For a national DOE competition, we build a model wind turbine and develop a wind turbine project plan.

name: Lift Challenge at NC State
summary: A student team tasked with competing in the DARPA Lift Challenge.

name: The Association for Information Systems
description:
The Association for Information Systems (AIS) Student Chapter at NC State University is a professional organization focused on preparing students for careers at the intersection of business and technology. We provide hands-on opportunities for students interested in data analytics, information systems, consulting, and technology-driven industries.

Members participate in case studies, industry projects, and skill-building workshops throughout the semester. AIS students compete in regional and national competitions, including the AIS National Conference and the AIS Americas Competition, where teams present real-world business and technology solutions. We also attend conferences to network with professionals, connect with other universities, and gain exposure to emerging trends in information systems.

Through competitions, conferences, and professional development programming, AIS helps students build practical experience, leadership skills, and strong industry connections.
summary: Connects business and technology by preparing students for careers in data analytics, information systems, consulting, and tech-driven industries through professional development, certifications, competitions, and networking opportunities!

name: NC State Student Chapter of INFORMS
summary: This is the student body of INFORMS at NC State University. INFORMS is the foremost organization of operations research, analytics and management science researchers and practitioners.
